steps.m: A-L-M (three special letters at the start of this chapter)
steps.n: These are three special letters that appear at the start of some chapters in the Quran. Each letter is said separately, like spelling out a word in English. While scholars have discussed their deeper meanings, what's most important is knowing how to say them: 'Alif' (like saying 'A'), 'Laam' (like saying 'L'), and 'Meem' (like saying 'M').
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' - this is a unique word that became a proper name for the One True God

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of this verse)
steps.n: Added the shadda to emphasize the 'l' sound and added the 'u' ending to show this is the subject of the verse. The hamza at the start became a connecting hamza (ٱ) because it's in the middle of speech.
This is the basic negative word that means 'no' or 'not'
Added fatha to show how it's pronounced
The long alif (آ) makes the negation stronger and absolute. This word works with إِلٰهَ to say 'there is absolutely no god' as part of the testimony of faith.

steps.m: to be devoted to, to take as an object of worship

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to worship' into noun meaning 'object of worship'

steps.m: god (object of لَآ/no in لَآ إِلٰهَ)
steps.n: Added alif with overbar (ٰ) to elongate the sound, and fatha at the end because it's the object of لَآ (no/there is no)
This is a primary particle that isn't derived from a root. It's used to make exceptions in sentences.
Added kasra (ِ) under the first letter to help with pronunciation
Added shadda (ّ) on the second letter to show it's pronounced strongly
Added alif (ا) at the end to complete the word. In this ayah, it introduces an exception - there is no god except Him (هُوَ).
This is a pronoun that means 'he'. It's used to talk about one male person who isn't right next to you.
Added the vowel marks ُ (damma) and َ (fatha) to show how to pronounce the word correctly. In this ayah, this word refers back to Allah (اللَّهُ) mentioned just before it.

steps.m: to be alive, to have life

steps.n: Added fatha and combined the repeated ي into one letter with shadda to show intensity
Added ال to make it definite and specific, showing this is a divine attribute
The word has a damma (u-sound) at the end to show it's describing Allah as the Ever-Living One
Root letters combined to make a noun meaning 'people/folk'
Changed middle letter to ya with shadda to show something that is always and forever maintaining everything
Added definite article to specify this is referring to Allah
Added damma to show this is describing Allah in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he sent down repeatedly (subject of the sentence)
steps.n: Added shadda (double letter mark) on the middle letter to show this was done multiple times or with emphasis. This word starts a new sentence describing what Allah did.
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of or over something else
The ى changes to يْ when we add something to the end. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: upon you (object of نَزَّلَ - He sent down)
steps.n: Added ك at the end to show this is happening to 'you'. This word connects to نَزَّلَ (He sent down) to show what was sent down upon.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels

steps.m: book (object of نَزَّلَ - he sent down)
steps.n: This pattern is often used for things that result from an action - a book is the result of writing. The word كِتَاب is in accusative case because it is the object (what is being sent down) of the verb نَزَّلَ (to send down).

steps.m: the book (object of نَزَّلَ - he sent down)
steps.n: Adding ال (al-) at the start makes it 'the book' instead of just 'book'

steps.m: the book (object of نَزَّلَ - he sent down)
steps.n: Added the accusative case ending َ to show this is the object being acted upon. The alif is written as ٰ when it comes after certain letters.
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to make a noun meaning 'truth' or 'reality'
Added ال to specify we're talking about absolute truth, not just any truth

steps.m: with the truth (describes how نَزَّلَ/sent down was done)
steps.n: Added بِ to show this describes how Allah sent down the Book - He sent it with/in truth. The ِّ at the end is added because of the بِ at the beginning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make basic past tense verb
Adding مُ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra makes it describe someone who does the action
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger, showing this isn't just simple confirmation but strong verification
Adding tanween fatHa (ًا) shows this word describes how the Quran (الكتاب) was sent down (نَزَّلَ)
Combined the preposition لِ (for/to) with ما (what) to create a compound word meaning 'for what'
Added shadda to emphasize the ل. This word connects to مُصَدِّقًا (confirming) in the ayah, showing what is being confirmed.

steps.m: to be between or separate things

steps.n: Adding vowels changes this from a root into a usable word meaning 'between'
Root letters joined together to make the word for 'hand', which can mean physical hand or having power over something
Added ي with fatha before it to make the dual form
The sukoon shows this word will connect to something after it

steps.m: his two hands (possessed by مَا in لِّمَا)
steps.n: Added هِ to show possession. This word appears in the phrase بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ (between his hands) where مَا is the possessor.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb

steps.m: he caused to send down (the Torah and Gospel)
steps.n: This form shows causing something to happen. Here it shows Allah caused the Torah and Gospel to be sent down.

steps.m: and he caused to send down (the Torah and Gospel)
steps.n: Adds و to connect this with the previous statement about sending down the Book
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation of this borrowed Hebrew word. The fatha shows the 'a' sound and the sukun shows where to pause.
Added ة at the end to make it a feminine noun, which is common for borrowed religious terms in Arabic.
Added ال at the beginning to make it definite (like 'the' in English). The ت gets a shadda because it comes after ال.
Three changes: 1) اَل becomes ٱل with a waṣla, 2) ا becomes ىٰ (alif maqṣūra with small alif), and 3) added fatha at end to show it's the object of the verb أَنزَلَ (sent down).
This is a borrowed word from Greek 'evangelion', referring to the Christian Gospel. Since it's a borrowed word, it doesn't come from an Arabic root.
Added ال to specify we're talking about the specific Gospel, not just any gospel
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it (the Torah)
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object (thing being sent down) in the phrase أَنزَلَ ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةَ وَٱلْإِنجِيلَ (He sent down the Torah and the Gospel)
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it combines with قَبْلُ to mean 'from before'
The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word is connecting to what comes after it
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning 'before'
Added damma at the end to show this word is in the nominative case

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Changed from basic root meaning 'guide' into a noun meaning 'guidance' by adding marks (damma and sukun)
Added tanween and alif maqsura at end to make it a complete abstract noun

steps.m: to be social, friendly with others

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'people' by adding a long 'aa' sound
Added لِل which combines 'for' (لِ) and 'the' (ال)
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on ن makes the word stronger/more emphatic
The ل has a shadda (double letter) and the word ends with kasra (-i sound) because it connects to what comes before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
This is a verb in Form IV that makes it mean 'to cause to descend' or 'send down'. As a verb, it has an implied subject (Allah) who is doing the action of sending down.
Added 'and' (و) at the beginning

steps.m: to distinguish between right and wrong, to separate truth from falsehood

steps.n: The ان ending makes it mean 'something that does a lot of distinguishing'
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'

steps.m: the criterion (object of أَنزَلَ - He sent down)
steps.n: The َ at the end shows this word is receiving the action of being 'sent down' in the verse
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly' in English. In this ayah, it emphasizes the statement about those who disbelieve.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to connect descriptions to people or things
Added ال at the beginning and doubled the ذ with shadda to make it specific

steps.m: those who (plural, referring to those who disbelieved in كَفَرُوا)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to make it plural. This word connects to كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved) which comes right after it in the ayah

steps.m: to reject, deny, or cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people did this action

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident

steps.n: Adding ة changes this into a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
Added the feminine plural ending to show multiple signs
Added بِ at the beginning to show these signs are being used as tools or means
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to 'Allah' after it, showing these are specifically Allah's signs
Added emphasis to show this is the ultimate, supreme deity

steps.m: of Allah (The signs/verses belong to Him)
steps.n: Changed to show possession/belonging by adding a kasra (the small line under) at the end
This is a basic particle meaning 'for' or 'to' that shows direction or purpose
Added هُمْ (them) to show who the action is for. This word connects to عَذَابٌ (punishment) in this ayah, showing who will receive the punishment.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters together

The ا after ذ and the ٌ at the end work together to change this from a verb (punished) into a noun (punishment). The ٌ shows it's indefinite (like 'a/an' in English). This word pairs with شَدِيدٌ (severe) to mean 'a severe punishment'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the middle vowel to kasra and added ي to show this describes a strong quality
Added tanween damma to show this is indefinite and describes the punishment (عَذَابٌ) mentioned in the ayah
This is the unique proper name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning of divinity and worship, but has become a distinct proper noun.
Added shadda on ل to show it is doubled in pronunciation
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence. Allah is being described as عَزِيزٌ (Mighty).

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of عَزِيزٌ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he became strong'
Changed the word pattern to show a strong quality. The tanween (ٌ) shows this is in the nominative case and describes Allah in this ayah.
This is a special word that connects two nouns to show ownership or possession. Here it connects Allah (الله) with vengeance (انتِقَامٍ) to show that Allah possesses/owns this quality.
Added basic past tense vowels
Added Form VIII pattern (ا at start and ت after first root letter) to show doing action for oneself
Changed from a past tense verb (he took revenge) to a noun meaning 'the act of taking revenge' by adding an alif and changing the middle vowel

steps.m: vengeance (possessed by Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show possession relationship with ذو (possessor of) and changed initial alif to wasl form

steps.m: indeed/truly (emphasizes that اللَّهَ is the subject of يَخْفَىٰ)
steps.n: This is a special word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain. Here it emphasizes that Allah is the one who nothing is hidden from.
Added shadda to show emphasis of the ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it connects with previous word
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily)
This is a simple word that makes things negative, like saying 'not' in English
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
The root letters tell us this is about something being hidden
Added vowels to make the past tense form
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start. The final letter changes shape (ي to ى) since it's at the end, but sounds the same
Added alif khanjariya (ٰ) to show the long vowel sound more clearly. This doesn't change the meaning.
The ى at the end changes to يْ when we add something after it. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Adding هِ at the end means 'him/it'. This word connects to يَخْفَىٰ (hidden from) in this ayah - nothing is hidden from Him.
This root developed into meaning 'a thing' in noun form
Connected the root letters and added marks (fatha and sukun) to make the word mean 'a thing'
The hamza (أ) changes to (ء) when it comes at the end of a word after a sukun
Adding tanween (double damma) makes the word indefinite, meaning 'a thing' rather than 'the thing'
Basic form of the preposition that shows something is inside or within something else

steps.m: in (something is inside ٱلْأَرْضِ 'the earth' and ٱلسَّمَآءِ 'the sky')
steps.n: Added ى at the end to complete the word. In this ayah, it shows that nothing can be hidden from Allah, whether it's in the earth or in the sky.
Connected the letters with basic vowel marks to make it pronounceable
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it comes after the word في (in/on)
This is a negative particle that tells us something does not happen. Here it's saying that nothing is hidden from Allah.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this idea with what came before it
This is a basic preposition showing location. In this ayah, it shows where شَىْءٌ (something/anything) is located.
Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high up
Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the sky' instead of just 'sky'
Added ا after م to make the word longer and stronger
Added kasra to show this comes after the word في (in)

steps.m: he (refers to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that points to someone who is being talked about but isn't physically present. In this ayah, it refers to Allah who is being described as the One who shapes us in the wombs.
This is the basic form of the word that connects descriptions to things or people, like saying 'who' or 'which' in English

steps.m: The one who (subject of يُصَوِّرُكُمْ - shapes you)
steps.n: Adding ٱلّ at the beginning makes it point to something specific. In this ayah, it refers back to Allah who is mentioned as هُوَ (He) and is the one who shapes us in the womb.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action stronger or more intensive
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he shapes/forms you all (in the wombs)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who is being shaped. This connects to فِى ٱلْأَرْحَامِ (in the wombs) in the ayah.
This is a preposition that shows location or position
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show it's connecting to the next word. This word connects to 'the wombs' (الْأَرْحَامِ) to tell us where the shaping/forming happens.
Changed the final ي to ى which is just a writing style variation - it doesn't change the meaning
Added vowels to form the singular noun 'womb' - this word comes from the idea of mercy because a mother's womb is a place of protection and care
Forms broken plural from singular رَحِم using pattern أَفْعَال to show multiple wombs
Adds kasra ending because this word follows فِي (in) in the phrase 'in the wombs'
Adds 'the' (ال) to make it specific wombs
This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens or the state of something
Adding vowel marks to make the word pronounceable - fatha on ك and sukoon on ي

steps.m: how (asking about manner of يُصَوِّرُكُمْ - He forms you)
steps.n: Added a small line (fatha) above the last letter to connect it with the next word about how Allah forms us
Added fathas and the weak letter alif. The hamza (ء) moves to the end because it comes after a long alif.

steps.m: he wills/wishes (describing how Allah forms us)
steps.n: Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense, changed final vowel to ُ to show it's happening now, and lengthened the alif to آ for emphasis. This describes Allah's ongoing will in forming humans.
Basic negative particle that makes statements negative
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Lengthened alif adds emphasis. This word negates إِلٰهَ (deity/god) in the phrase لَآ إِلٰهَ (there is no deity).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the first vowel to kasra (إِ) and added alif after second letter to create a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
The word for 'god' gets a special mark above it (ٰ) and an 'a' sound at the end because it comes after لَا (meaning 'no/there is not')
This is a fundamental particle used to make exceptions. Consists of hamza with kasra (إِ) followed by lām with shadda (لّ)

steps.m: except/unless (makes exceptions to هُوَ ٱلَّذِى يُصَوِّرُكُمْ)
steps.n: Adding the alif at the end completes the particle. In this ayah, it's making an exception to the previous statement about who shapes you in the wombs.
This is a basic pronoun used to refer to someone who is male and not present (like saying 'he' in English). In this ayah, it refers to Allah.
Added the vowel marks ُ (damma) and َ (fatha) to show how to pronounce it as 'huwa'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed the word to show someone or something that has this quality strongly

steps.m: The Mighty One (a name of Allah)
steps.n: Adding 'Al' (ال) turns 'mighty' into 'The Mighty One', making it a special name for Allah

steps.m: The Mighty One (a name of Allah, subject of this ayah)
steps.n: Added 'u' sound at end to show this is the subject (doer) of the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Creates an intensive adjective meaning 'one who is consistently wise', used here to describe Allah
Adds ال (the) to make it a specific name/title of Allah, meaning The All-Wise

steps.m: he (referring to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that refers to someone male who was mentioned before or is known from context. In this ayah, it refers to Allah who is about to be described as the One who sent down the Book.
This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence. The definite article ال assimilates with the ذ creating a shadda.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes the meaning 'cause someone/something else to do the action'. Here it shows Allah is causing something to come down.
This is a preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on' - one of the basic Arabic prepositions that shows something is on top of or affecting something else
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for connecting to the next part. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: upon you (connected to أَنزَلَ - sent down upon you)
steps.n: Added ك at the end to show this affects 'you'. In this ayah, it's connected to أَنزَلَ (sent down), showing that something was sent down upon you.
Changed from action to the thing produced by that action. This word is what's being sent down in the phrase 'sent down the book'.
Added ٱلْ to make it specific ('the book' instead of 'a book') and َ at the end to show it's receiving the action of being sent down
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or its source

steps.m: from it/him (referring to الْكِتٰبَ - the Book)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُ to show what is being referred to. Here it refers back to the Book (Quran) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or point to something

steps.n: The ة at the end shows this is a single sign/verse
Added ات to make it plural, showing there are multiple signs or verses
Added tanween dammah to show this is any set of signs/verses (not specific ones)
The middle alif letter was shortened to a small alif (ٰ). The tanween damma was already there in both forms.

steps.m: to make something firm or precise

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at the start to show it's a passive participle - meaning something that was made firm/precise (rather than the act of making something firm)
Added ات to show there are multiple verses that are firm/precise
The doubled م adds extra emphasis to show these are really firmly established
Added two dots on top (tanween) to show this word is complete/definite and is doing something in the sentence
This is the basic form of the pronoun that refers to a single masculine person or thing

steps.m: they (feminine) (referring to آيَاتٌ مُّحْكَمَاتٌ - clear verses)
steps.n: Added نَّ at the end to show this refers to multiple feminine things. In this ayah, it refers back to the clear verses (آيَاتٌ مُّحْكَمَاتٌ) mentioned earlier.

steps.m: to be the source or origin of something

steps.n: The word أُمّ here refers to the fundamental or foundational verses of the Quran, showing they are like a source or origin for understanding the rest of the Book.
Changes the action of writing into the thing that is written
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific book rather than any book

steps.m: of the book (part of أُمُّ ٱلْكِتٰبِ - mother/source of the book)
steps.n: The ِ at the end shows this book belongs to or is connected to something else. Here it's part of the phrase أُمُّ ٱلْكِتٰبِ meaning 'mother/source of the book', referring to the clear verses of the Quran.

steps.m: to come later or be behind

steps.n: Changes to plural form by adding specific vowel marks
Added وَ (and) at the beginning and ُ at the end to show this describes the verses mentioned before

steps.m: to make itself look like something else
steps.n: Added تَ at the start and doubled the middle letter to show something doing the action to itself

steps.m: one who makes himself look like something else
steps.n: Changed from a verb to a noun meaning 'someone who does this action'

steps.m: verses that look similar to each other (feminine plural)
steps.n: Changed from describing one thing to many verses (by adding ات), and made it feminine plural since we're talking about verses (آيات which is feminine). The ٌ shows these are general verses.
Added ا at the end to create the basic particle meaning 'as for'. This particle helps direct attention to what comes next.

steps.m: then as for (introduces what follows ٱلَّذِينَ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this starts a new thought that follows from what came before. This word introduces the people being discussed (ٱلَّذِينَ - those who) in the ayah.
Added ن to make it refer to multiple people instead of just one person

Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific and point to particular people. Also added shadda on ذ to strengthen the connection. Added َ at the end to show this word is the subject of يَتَّبِعُونَ (they follow) in this ayah.

steps.m: in (shows location within قُلُوبِهِمْ - their hearts)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows that something (زَيْغٌ - deviation) is located inside their hearts.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create the basic word for 'heart' - named this way because it can turn emotions and thoughts
Changes the internal vowels and adds و to make it plural. This is a special plural form common for body parts and important objects.
Added ِ to show these hearts belong to someone or something
Added هِمْ to show these are specifically 'their' hearts
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun instead of a verb
The tanwin (ٌ) shows this is indefinite (like saying 'a' in English). This word describes what is in the hearts (قُلُوبِهِمْ) of those who try to misinterpret the Quran.

steps.m: to follow closely or pursue something

steps.n: Basic past tense verb meaning 'he followed'
ي at start shows it's happening now and done by 'he'
Adds the regular present tense ending
The doubled letter shows doing the action intensely or repeatedly

steps.m: so he follows intensively (after those with deviation in their hearts)
steps.n: Adds conjunction prefix to connect with previous statement

steps.m: so they follow intensively (after those with deviation in their hearts)
steps.n: Changes from 'he follows' to 'they follow' (singular to plural)
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to things. In this ayah, it refers to the parts of the Book that are similar/unclear (مُتَشٰبِهٰتٌ) that some people follow.
Basic past tense verb form - added short 'a' vowels (fat-ha) to make 'he looked like'
Added alif to show the action goes both ways - like two things looking like each other
Added تَ at the beginning to show something became similar on its own without someone making it similar

steps.m: it became unclear/ambiguous (referring to آيَاتٌ - verses)
steps.n: Changed the alif to a long alif (ٰ) which is just a writing variation that means the same thing
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it's showing which parts of الْكِتٰبَ (the Book) are being discussed.
The هُ at the end means 'it/him' - it's attached to show this is talking about الْكِتٰبَ (the Book). This prepositional phrase tells us which parts of the Book are being described as clear verses.
This is the basic meaning of wanting or seeking something
Connected the root letters with fathas to make the basic past tense verb
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show doing the action for oneself
Changed from 'he sought' (verb) to 'seeking' (noun) by adding alif after غ and changing middle vowel - like changing 'seek' to 'seeking' in English
Added fatha at end to show this is the object of the verb فَيَتَّبِعُونَ (so they follow). Also changed spelling to standard Uthmani script with ٱ and آ.
This root is about testing or putting someone through trials
Changed to a noun meaning one specific instance of testing or trial
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
The kasra (ِ) shows this word is connected to 'seeking' (ابْتِغَآءَ), indicating what is being sought

steps.m: to seek out or pursue something eagerly
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of wanting or seeking something

steps.m: to actively seek something for oneself
steps.n: Added اِ at beginning and ت after first root letter to create reflexive form VIII, indicating intensive seeking
Changed verb into a noun showing the action of seeking. The long alif (آ) is used instead of regular alif (ا) for emphasis.
Added conjunction prefix وَ meaning 'and'

steps.m: to return something to its original state

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: interpretation (act of returning something to its original meaning)
steps.n: Added تَ at start and changed middle to وِي to make a noun meaning 'the act of returning something to its original meaning'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by ابتغاء (seeking)
Added هِ to show it belongs to something (the Quran/verses being discussed)
Added fatha to show this is the basic particle ما which can mean either 'what' or 'not' depending on context. Here it means 'not' because it's negating the verb يَعْلَمُ (knows).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. The combination وَمَا is negating the verb يَعْلَمُ (knows), so it means 'and not'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, added sukun (ْ) on middle letter, and added ُ at end to show 'he' is doing it

steps.m: to return something to its original state or meaning

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding vowels and doubling the middle letter
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'the act of interpretation'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يَعْلَمُ (knows/understands)
Added pronoun هُ to show whose interpretation it is (referring to the Quran/revelation)
Starting with the basic negation particle لا
Adding إِ at the beginning and shadda on ل changes the meaning from negation to exception. This word connects to يَعْلَمُ (knows) to say 'none knows... except'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'one who is worshipped'
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال at the beginning and the special pronunciation with shadda make it different from the general word for deity (إِلَه).

steps.m: one who is firmly rooted (in knowledge)
steps.n: This pattern shows someone who has this quality deeply embedded in them. In this ayah, it refers specifically to those deeply rooted in knowledge.

steps.m: those who are firmly rooted (in knowledge)
steps.n: The ون ending makes it plural (more than two). In this ayah, it refers to scholars who have deep, well-established knowledge.
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific group - here the scholars who are deeply grounded in religious knowledge

steps.m: and the firmly rooted ones (in knowledge) (subject of يَقُولُونَ)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to the previous phrase about Allah. These people are mentioned as saying يَقُولُونَ (they say) in the next part of the ayah.
This is a preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it shows that something (زَيْغٌ/deviation) is inside their hearts.

Changed the root into a noun meaning 'knowledge' by adding the kasra (ِ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on middle letter. This pattern turns an action into the thing itself, like changing 'know' to 'knowledge'.
Added ال (the) at start to make it specific. The kasra (ِ) at the end shows it follows the word فِي (in/within).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ونَ to show multiple people (they) are doing the action
Adding alif makes it mean 'to actively put trust in something'
Doubling the last letter makes the meaning stronger and more intense
Added نا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This word is what 'they' are saying in the verse.
This is a preposition that shows how something is done or connected to something else
Added هِ to show it refers to 'him/it', specifically referring back to the interpretation (تَأْوِيل) mentioned earlier in the verse
Added small ۦ after هِ which is just a pronunciation mark that doesn't change the meaning
Added damma (ُ) and doubled the ل to make it mean 'all' or 'every'

steps.m: all, every (as the subject of يَقُولُونَ)
steps.n: Added double damma (ٌ) to show this word is the subject doing the action in يَقُولُونَ (they say)
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects to عِندِ (from the presence of)
The shadda (ّ) emphasizes the م to show importance, and the sukun marks the end of the word
Added vowels to make it into a preposition that shows location or possession
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word after it (رَبِّنَا - our Lord)

steps.m: to take care of, nurture, help grow

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter shows this is someone who takes perfect care of others
Adding kasra at the end shows this word comes after مِنْ (from) in the ayah
Added نَا (our) to رَبِّ (Lord) to make it mean 'our Lord'. Comes after مِنْ (from).
Adding 'and' at the beginning
The word 'and' (وَ) joins with مَا to make 'and none/no one' - like adding 'not' to make something negative
Added a shadda (double letter) to make it mean 'making someone else remember' instead of just 'remembering'
Added تَ at start to show remembering/reflecting for oneself (instead of reminding others)
Changed from past to present tense by replacing ت with ي at the start. The ت after ي combined with ذ to make it doubled (ذّ)
Changed from subjunctive to present tense form - shows what is happening now rather than what might happen
This is the basic form of the word used for making exceptions. It's a particle, so it doesn't come from a root.
Added alif at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word (اللَّهُ). This word is restricting who knows the meaning to only Allah.
Changed the regular alif to a long alif (آ). This is just a writing style variation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: owners of, those who have (masculine plural)
steps.n: This is a special word that always comes before another noun to show who has or owns something. It's used for groups of men or mixed groups.

steps.m: owners of, those who have (connected to الْأَلْبٰبِ)
steps.n: Added small alif marks (۟) which help with pronunciation. This word connects to الْأَلْبٰبِ (understanding/intellect) to mean 'people of understanding'.
This word specifically refers to people who have deep understanding and can see the true meaning of things, not just regular minds or intellects
Makes the noun definite
Adding the small diagonal line (kasra) under the last letter shows this word belongs to another word before it

steps.m: to care for, raise, develop to full potential
Lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: The doubled ب shows this is someone who nurtures and cares in a complete way
The suffix نا means 'our', showing we are talking about our Lord. The word is being used to call out to Allah, like saying 'O our Lord!'
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تُزِغْ (cause to deviate) into 'do not cause to deviate'. It's used in a prayer/request to Allah.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation

steps.m: to turn away from the right path

steps.n: Added vowels to make it mean 'he turned away' in the past
Added تُ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show causing something

steps.m: cause to turn away (as a negative command: 'do not cause to turn away')
steps.n: The ي is removed and a sukun (stop mark) is added at the end to make it a 'do not' command - like saying 'Don't make (something) turn away'

steps.m: to turn/change (like how a heart turns emotions)

steps.n: Changed the root meaning from an action (turning/changing) into a physical thing (heart) that does that action
Changed the vowels and added و to make it plural, showing there are many hearts

steps.m: our hearts (being acted upon by تُزِغْ/cause to deviate)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show these hearts belong to us. This word is receiving the action of تُزِغْ (cause to deviate) in the prayer 'do not cause our hearts to deviate'

steps.m: to be far away from something

steps.n: When we connect the letters with these vowels, it changes from meaning 'far' to meaning 'after' (since what comes after is far from the beginning)

steps.m: after (connected to هَدَيْتَنَا - You guided us)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this word is connected to the verb هَدَيْتَنَا (You guided us) in the ayah

steps.m: when (referring to هَدَيْتَنَا - You guided us)
steps.n: This is a particle that shows when something happened in the past. In this ayah, it connects the time they were guided (هَدَيْتَنَا) with their current prayer asking not to go astray.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Adding fatha marks creates the basic past tense verb meaning 'guided'
Added تَ to show 'you' did the action
Added نَا meaning 'us' to show who was guided. This verb comes after إِذْ (when) in the ayah.
Basic root meaning of giving something as a gift without expecting anything in return
Adding vowels to make it a complete past tense verb
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced
Added نَا which means 'us' to show who the action is for. This connects to the verb هَبْ (grant) in the ayah, showing what is being requested to be granted.
This is a helper word that shows something coming from somewhere else. In this ayah, it shows mercy (رَحْمَةً) coming from Your presence (لَّدُنكَ).
The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word is connecting to what comes after it
The vowels change the word into a preposition meaning 'from' or 'near'
The sukun marks the end of the word and helps with pronunciation
Added كَ (meaning 'Your') at the end to show it's from Your presence. The ل got stronger (doubled) because it follows مِن (from) in this ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together. The fatha (َ) and sukun (ْ) pattern makes this into a noun.

steps.m: a specific act of kindness and care
steps.n: Added ة at the end to show we're talking about one specific instance of mercy
Added tanween (ً) to show this is a general/indefinite mercy being asked for in the prayer
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphasis word 'indeed'

steps.m: indeed you (addressing the one being called upon as أَنتَ)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the end makes this address a specific person (you). This word is emphasizing أَنتَ (you) in this ayah.
This is a standalone pronoun meaning 'you' (when speaking to one male). In this ayah, it connects to الْوَهَّابُ (The Bestower) to say 'You are The Bestower'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
The shadda (double letter) and alif show this is someone who gives repeatedly or abundantly. The damma at the end makes it a noun.

steps.m: The Most Generous Giver (referring to Allah)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it definite and specific. In this context, it refers to Allah as the ultimate source of all giving.

steps.m: to care for and help grow/develop

steps.n: The doubled letter ب shows this is someone who really takes care of others completely
Adding نا at the end shows we're talking about our Lord. This word is being used to call out to Allah directly.
The long alif at the end shows we're calling out to Allah - like saying 'O our Lord!'
This is a particle that adds emphasis to what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes 'you' before stating that Allah will gather all people.

steps.m: indeed you (being addressed by رَبَّنَآ - our Lord)
steps.n: Adding كَ (meaning 'you') to إِنَّ (meaning 'indeed') to say 'indeed you'

steps.m: to gather, bring together, or collect

steps.n: Added ا after first letter and vowel marks to show who does the gathering. This word connects to الناس (people) in this ayah, showing Allah is the one who gathers people.
Added ُ at the end to show this is the main subject doing the gathering

steps.m: to be close or familiar with others

steps.n: Root means 'to be social' - changed to mean 'people' by adding ال (the) and doubling ن
Added ٱلْ to show we're talking about specific people

steps.m: of the people (connected to جَامِعُ - gatherer)
steps.n: Added ِ to show this word is connected to جَامِعُ (gatherer), meaning 'gatherer of the people'

steps.m: to pass time, specifically a day

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make the basic noun 'day'
Added tanween (double dots under) to show it's any day, not a specific day

steps.m: for/to a day (related to جَامِعُ - gathering)
steps.n: Added لِ to show this day is what people are being gathered for. This connects to جَامِعُ (gathering) in the ayah.
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it. It has no root as it is a particle (حرف).
Adding shadda makes the negation stronger. In this ayah, it strongly negates يُخْلِفُ (breaks/fails), saying that Allah absolutely does not break His promise.

steps.m: to doubt, suspect, or be uncertain

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to doubt' into the noun 'doubt'
Added fatha at the end because this word is being acted upon by لَا (no) in the phrase لَا رَيْبَ (there is no doubt)
This is a basic preposition that shows location or presence - either physical (like being inside a house) or abstract (like being in a situation)

steps.m: in it (referring to يَوْمٍ - the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Adding هِ shows this refers back to يَوْمٍ (the Day of Judgment) mentioned earlier. The word فِيهِ tells us there is something about or within that day - specifically that there is no doubt in it.
This is a special word that makes what comes after it stronger or more emphasized. In this ayah, it emphasizes the word كَ (you) that comes right after it.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became the name for God in Arabic.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being referred to)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a special mark (shadda) to show double letters, and a mark at the end (fatha) to show something is happening to Allah in this sentence
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb يُخْلِفُ (breaks/violates) into its opposite meaning - 'does not break/violate'. It's one of the most basic negative words in Arabic.
Added fatha mark on ل to show the correct pronunciation

steps.m: to break (a promise) or come after

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowels - shows someone breaking a promise now rather than in the past
Connected the root letters with basic vowels to make a noun
Added مِ at start and يا in middle to show where/when something happens
Added ال to point to a specific appointed time

steps.m: the appointed time/place of promise (object of يُخْلِفُ - breaks/fails to keep)
steps.n: Added َ (fatha) at the end because it's receiving the action of 'breaking/failing to keep' - Allah never fails to keep His promises
This is the basic form of the word that shows a condition or possibility
Adding the shadda changes this from a word meaning 'if' to one that adds emphasis to what follows. It's like adding an exclamation point or saying 'Listen up!' before an important statement.

steps.m: those who - points to specific people being talked about
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun used to describe a group of people. Base form ذِي with definite article ٱلْ and plural suffix ينَ

steps.m: to deny, reject, or cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense. This word describes what those mentioned in إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ (indeed those who) did.
The suffix وا changes the subject from 'he' to 'they (masculine)'. This refers to the disbelievers whose wealth and children won't help them against Allah.
This is a special word that makes something impossible in the future. Here it's saying the disbelievers' wealth and children will definitely not help them with Allah.
Adding vowels to show this is a past tense verb describing a state of being rich/sufficient
Adding ت changes it from 'he was rich' to 'she becomes rich'
Changed to passive voice - someone/something makes her self-sufficient
This is a preposition that shows separation or moving away from something. The sukun (ْ) shows where the word pauses.

steps.m: from them/away from them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in كَفَرُوا)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُم meaning 'them' to show who is being moved away from. This refers back to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the verse.
The root letters م-و-ل become مَال by adding the vowel َ (fatha) and changing و to ا
Changes from singular to plural by adding أ at the start and putting و in the middle - a common way to make plurals in Arabic
Adding tanween (double damma) at the end makes it indefinite - meaning 'some wealth' rather than 'the wealth'
Changed from indefinite (with tanween) to definite form, with damma ending to show it's the subject
Adds هُمْ to show possession. This word is the subject of تُغْنِىَ (will avail) in this ayah.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added alif with madda to show long vowel sound
Added و at beginning to connect this negative statement with the previous phrase about those who disbelieve
Changed from action to the thing itself (from verb to noun)
Changed from single child to plural children by adding أ at start and ا in middle - this is a common plural pattern
Changed alif to alif maqsurah for writing style, no meaning change
Added damma to show this is the subject doing something
Added هُم at the end to show these are their children. This word is one of the subjects of تُغْنِىَ (will not avail) in this ayah.
This is the basic form of the word meaning 'from'
The shadda (ّ) emphasizes the م sound, making it stronger
The fatha (َ) at the end shows this word connects to the next word اللَّهِ (Allah)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Added ال (the) and changed the word to become the proper name of The One True God. The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it in the sentence.
Root letters put together with fatha and sukoon to make the common word for 'thing'
Added tanween fatha (ًا) to show this is the object being acted upon by تُغْنِى (will not avail/benefit). The meaning becomes 'anything' in this context because it's in a negative sentence.

steps.m: those (pointing to the disbelievers mentioned before)
steps.n: This is a demonstrative pronoun that points to people or things that are far away. Here it refers back to the disbelievers (الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: and those (pointing to the disbelievers mentioned before)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
This is a pronoun that refers back to 'those who disbelieved' (الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا) mentioned earlier in the ayah. It shows who is being talked about.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (to burn) into a noun meaning the material that gets burned
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of هُمْ (they are)
Adds alif after first letter to make the word mean 'fire/light'
Adds kasra at end to show it's being used as object of preposition

steps.m: to persist or keep doing something habitually
like the habit/custom (of ءَالِ فِرْعَوْنَ - the people of Pharaoh)
steps.n: Adds كَ meaning 'like' at the start and ِ at the end to show connection to what follows. This describes how the disbelievers' behavior is similar to that of Pharaoh's people.

steps.m: to come first, be at the front

steps.n: Root letters أ-و-ل combined to make a word meaning 'family' - like turning separate puzzle pieces into one picture
The و changes to ا due to a common Arabic spelling pattern
The kasra at the end shows this belongs to what comes after it (Pharaoh)
This is a proper noun borrowed from ancient Egyptian language

steps.m: Pharaoh (object of كَدَأْبِ - like the way of)
steps.n: Added fatḥa at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the phrase 'like the way of'. This connects it to the previous words in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to refer to someone or something
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one person
Adding و at the beginning connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it correctly. This word connects with قَبْلِهِمْ to mean 'from before them'

steps.m: to be in front of or before

steps.n: Changed verb concept into a noun meaning 'before'
Added kasra to connect this word to the next word
Added هِمْ to show this refers to 'them'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning more intense
Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This word is followed by فَأَخَذَهُمُ (then He seized them) in the ayah.
Adding ة at the end turns this into a noun meaning 'a sign'
Added ات to make it plural (more than one sign)
Added نَا to show these are Allah's signs
Added بِ at start to show these signs were the means by which something happened. This connects to the verb كذّبوا (they denied) showing what they denied.

steps.m: to seize or take hold of

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fathas) to make it mean 'he seized' in the past
Added هُمُ to show who was seized

steps.m: so he seized them (connected to كَذَّبُوا before it)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened as a result of what came before - they denied Our signs, so Allah seized them
This is the basic concept the word comes from - something worthy of worship
Root meaning 'worship' became a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, changing it from 'a god' to 'The God'
Added ُ at the end to show Allah is doing the action. The ٱ at the start and doubled ل (shown by ّ ) are always there when writing Allah's name properly.

steps.m: to commit a sin or fault

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning one sin
This is a broken plural form showing multiple sins
Adds هِمْ to show the sins belong to them

steps.m: because of their sins (reason for فَأَخَذَهُمُ)
steps.n: Adds بِ to show this is the reason why Allah seized them (فَأَخَذَهُمُ)
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show Allah is the one doing the action in this sentence
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, and added ٱ (waslah alif) which helps connect the و with the word
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this describes a strong quality
Added ُ at the end to show this is the main subject describing punishment
Root meaning 'follow/consequence' became a noun meaning 'punishment' - showing what follows bad actions. The vowel pattern makes it a noun.
Added ال to show this is the specific punishment being discussed. This word is being described by شَدِيدُ (severe) to form the phrase 'severe in punishment'.
Added ِ at the end to show this word is possessed by شَدِيدُ (severe), forming the phrase 'severe in punishment'.
Connected the letters together with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he said' to a command 'say!' by changing the middle vowel to 'u' and dropping the last vowel

steps.m: say! (command to one person) (speaking to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: The و drops out because it's between a damma and a sukoon, which is a common rule for weak letters
Starting with the basic demonstrative word that points to something
Adding ال and ي changes it from pointing at something to describing someone or something
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one

steps.m: for those who (who will كَفَرُوا - disbelieve)
steps.n: Adding لِ with emphasis (shown by ّ) shows this is directed towards or for these people. This word connects to كَفَرُوا (disbelieve) which comes after it.

steps.m: to reject, to cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past

steps.m: they rejected (truth) (subject of سَتُغْلَبُونَ - they will be overcome)
steps.n: Added وا at the end and changed the last vowel to ُ to show many people did this action. This word is connected to سَتُغْلَبُونَ which tells what will happen to these rejecters.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show the action happened to someone rather than they did it
Changed to present tense by adding تُ
Made it plural (more than one person)

steps.m: you (all) will be overcome (addressed to those who disbelieved in كَفَرُوا)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice (something being done to you) in present tense
Added ونَ to show this applies to a group of people

steps.m: and you all are being gathered (to جَهَنَّمَ)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this with what comes before it. This word shows what will happen to the disbelievers - they will be gathered to Hell.
This is a fixed word that shows direction or destination. It connects with the word جَهَنَّمَ (Hell) to show where something is going.
Added a small alif (ٰ) above the ى. This is just a writing style that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: place of intense fire and punishment
steps.n: This is the base form of the word Jahannam, which comes from non-Arabic origins (likely Hebrew 'Gehinnom'). It refers to the place of punishment in the afterlife.
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters and make them pronounceable
Added shadda on ن and final fatha. In this ayah, it's mentioned as the destination that the disbelievers will be gathered to, connected to the word إِلَىٰ (towards/to).
Adding vowels changes this into a common word used to criticize something
Adds 'and' to connect this criticism with the previous statement. This word is describing المِهَادُ (the destination) that comes after it.

steps.m: to spread out or prepare (like making a bed)
a place that is spread out (like a bed or mattress)
steps.n: The pattern فِعَال is used to create nouns that describe places or things used for the root meaning. This word is the subject of بئس (what an evil...) in a common Arabic expression to criticize something.
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific resting place (in this case, Hell, which was mentioned just before in جَهَنَّمَ)

steps.m: the resting place (subject of بِئْسَ - what an evil...)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is the subject of بِئْسَ, making the full phrase mean 'what an evil resting place!'
This is a helper word that tells us the action definitely happened in the past. In this ayah, it works with كَانَ (was) to say 'there certainly was'.
This is the basic concept of existing or being
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he was (describing something in لَكُمْ ءَايَةٌ)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter between two fathas
Starting with the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added the pronoun كُمْ to show this is directed to multiple people being addressed
The root letters combine with the first letter becoming hamza with fatha (ءَ), and the first ي changing to alif (ا), and the second ي remaining (يَ)
Adds ة to specify one single sign

steps.m: a sign (that points to something in فِئَتَيْنِ ٱلْتَقَتَا - two groups that met)
steps.n: Adds tanween dammah (ٌ) to show this is indefinite (any sign) and is the subject of what's being discussed in the ayah
A basic preposition showing location or path. Here it connects the verb تُقٰتِلُ (fights) with سَبِيلِ (path/way) to show fighting happens 'in the path/way'.

steps.m: to gather or come together as a group
a group or party of people
steps.n: Changes from basic root to noun meaning a group
Changes from singular (one group) to dual (two groups)
Basic meaning of coming together or meeting
Adding vowel marks changes it from root to past tense verb meaning 'he met'
Added Form VIII pattern (ا and ت) to show mutual action between two parties
Added feminine marker ت to show the subject is feminine
Changed ending to show two females meeting each other (added ا)

steps.m: to separate or split into groups
a group (that fights in سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Changes from root meaning 'to split' into a noun meaning 'a group' - like how splitting creates separate groups

steps.m: a group (that fights in سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added double dots (tanween) at the end to show this group is doing the fighting
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Added alif to show mutual action - fighting against each other
تُ at start shows either 'she' or 'you (male)'
This is a preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it connects 'two groups' (فِئَتَيْنِ) with 'path' (سَبِيلِ) to show where the groups were.
Added ي between second and third letters to make the word flow better
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and shadda on ل to emphasize it. The word shows who the path belongs to in the phrase 'path of Allah'.
Added damma and sukun to form the base word meaning 'other'
Added ى at the end to make it mean 'another' for feminine things
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'. The word describes فِئَةٌ (group) from earlier in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Changed to describe someone who rejects truth. This describes the group (فئة) fighting against the believers.
Added ة to make it feminine since it describes فئة (group), which is feminine
Adding tanween makes it indefinite

steps.m: to see with one's eyes or mind
steps.n: Basic meaning of seeing or viewing
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb
Added يَ at start to make it present tense. The middle letter أ is dropped and replaced with ى at the end because it's a special 'seeing' verb
Added ون to show plural subject (they). The ى changes to وْ before plural ending.

steps.m: they see them (referring to the disbelieving army seeing the Muslim army)
steps.n: Added هُم suffix meaning 'them' to show who is being seen. In this ayah, it refers to the disbelieving army seeing the Muslim army.

steps.m: to be like or similar to
steps.n: Basic meaning of similarity or likeness
Changed vowels to create the noun form meaning 'like' or 'similar to'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being used as an object

steps.m: twice of them (refers to the disbelieving group)
steps.n: Added 'twice' (dual form) plus 'them' at the end to show double the number of enemies
Doubles the first letter م to add emphasis to the word
Basic meaning of seeing or viewing
Added fathas to make past tense verb meaning 'he saw'
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
The word 'eye' gets 'the' (ال) added at the start and a kasra ending because it's what is being seen
This is a unique proper name that doesn't come from a root. It's the personal name of God in Arabic.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added damma at the end to show this is the doer of the action (supports/aids) in this sentence

steps.m: and God (as the subject of يُؤَيِّدُ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this part to what came before it, like saying 'and'

steps.m: and God (as the subject of يُؤَيِّدُ)
steps.n: Changed the alif to a special form (ٱ) used with the definite article ال
Basic meaning of giving strength or support
Added shadda to middle letter to show intensity of support
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start, with hamza after it because word starts with alif. The ِ vowel and ُ ending show it's present tense. This is Allah supporting whom He wills.
Basic meaning of helping or supporting
Root letters arranged with fatha and sukun to make noun meaning 'help'
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added ه to show this belongs to Allah
Added ب at beginning to show 'with/by' and small alif after ه to show proper pronunciation

This is a relative pronoun meaning 'who' or 'whoever'. It connects to the verb يَشَآءُ (He wills) that comes after it, showing who is being referred to. Adding fatha vowel mark for proper pronunciation.

steps.m: to want or wish for something
steps.n: Basic meaning of wanting or willing
The root letters were joined together and the middle letter ي changed to ا to make the past tense 'he wanted'
Changed from past tense (شَاءَ) to present tense by adding يَ at the start and changing the ending vowel to ُ
Added long alif to show extended duration of wanting/wishing
Added kasra (ِ) under the alif to show this is the emphatic particle that strengthens statements
Added shadda (ّ) on the ن to make the emphasis even stronger. This particle introduces an important statement and makes what comes after it take a special ending.
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. It connects with سَبِيلِ to show 'in the path/way'.
Added kasra (ِ) vowel mark to show how the word is pronounced.
Changed the final ي to ى which is a common spelling variation at the end of words.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) above the ذ to show elongation
Adding ل with kasra (ِ) changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far

steps.m: that (pointing to something in the previous statement)
steps.n: The ك at the end makes it specifically point to something mentioned before

steps.m: to cross over, draw lessons from
steps.n: Basic meaning of crossing over or interpreting
Root meaning 'cross over' becomes a noun meaning 'lesson' by adding vowels - like turning 'teach' into 'teaching'
Added ة to specify one instance of a lesson
Added tanween fatha to show this is the object being acted upon
Added لَ at beginning to emphasize that this is definitely a lesson
This is a special form used to show ownership or possession. The ى at the end makes it refer to multiple people.
Added لِ at the beginning to show purpose or direction - 'for' or 'to'
Added shadda on ل for emphasis and added the small و mark (۟) which is a pronunciation helper

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes or mind
steps.n: Basic meaning of seeing or insight
Added vowels to make the basic noun form meaning 'sight'
Changes the word to show many types of vision/insight (3-10)
Adding ال makes it specific, like saying 'the' in English
Adding kasra at the end shows this word follows another word it belongs to
Adding the shadda (ّ) on ي shows that this beautification was done with extra effort or intensity

steps.m: was made attractive (to لِلنَّاسِ - for the people)
steps.n: Changed to passive voice to show that something was beautified, rather than someone doing the beautifying. In this ayah, worldly desires were made to appear attractive to people.
Changes from verb 'to be social' to noun meaning 'people'
Added ال to specify we're talking about people in general
Added لِ (li) at the start to mean 'for/to' and changed the ending to match the grammar

steps.m: to feel or show deep affection

steps.n: Adding vowel marks to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: love (subject of زُيِّنَ - was made attractive)
steps.n: Changed vowels to damma and added shadda to make it into an abstract concept. This word is what was made attractive to people in the verse.
Root letters ش-ه-و got vowels (َ) and (ْ) to form the noun 'desire'
Added ة to show one specific instance of desire
Changed to plural form to show multiple desires
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ش with shadda
Changed to show it belongs to 'love' (حُبُّ), with a small change in spelling
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. In this ayah, it's used to specify what types of things people are attracted to.
Added َ (fatha) at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word (الذَّهَبِ). This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: women (related to نسو 'to forget', as women were traditionally seen as forgetful)

steps.n: Changes root letters to make plural form meaning 'group of women'
Adding definite article causes the ن to get a shadda (doubled)
Added kasra at the end because this word follows مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
Adding vowel marks to create basic past tense verb form
Adding ين makes it plural for male children/sons
Makes it specific which sons we're talking about
Adds 'and' to connect this with the previous word in the verse

steps.m: to store up treasure, amass wealth
a great sum (of wealth or money)
steps.n: Forms a noun meaning a specific large quantity or measure, like a talent of gold
Changes from one large sum to many large sums by making it plural - like changing 'fortune' to 'fortunes'
Adds kasra to show genitive case
Makes the noun definite

steps.m: and the large amounts of wealth
steps.n: Adds 'and' (wa) at the start

steps.m: to pile up in large amounts
to pile up in large amounts
steps.n: Adding vowel marks to make it a proper verb
Added مُ at the beginning to show this is happening to something rather than something doing it
Added ة to make it feminine, which matches with other words it's describing in the verse

steps.m: the piled up (feminine, describing القناطير/heaps of wealth)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it's describing القناطير (heaps of wealth) mentioned earlier in the verse
This is the basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to a category
Added َ (fatha) at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word that starts with ال. This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to go, to shine like gold

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the noun for the precious metal
Added 'the' and doubled the first letter to show it's a specific gold being mentioned

steps.m: of the gold (possessed by/made of gold)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this gold is being described as belonging to or being made of something in the sentence
Turns the basic root into the word for silver metal by doubling the middle letter and adding -ah at the end
Adds و to connect this word to الذَّهَبِ (gold) mentioned just before it
Adds definite article after conjunction

steps.m: and the silver (possessed, part of مِنَ - from)
steps.n: Adds kasra to show this word follows مِنَ (from) in the ayah, making it part of what people love
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to the previous items in the list of worldly pleasures

steps.m: and the horses (as part of the list of desires)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word is part of a list following مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah

steps.m: to mark or brand (like putting a special sign on something)

steps.n: Added shadda to show intensive marking/branding
Added مُ at start to show this is happening to something rather than someone doing it
Added ة to show this describes a feminine word

steps.m: the marked/branded one (feminine) (describes ٱلْخَيْلِ - the horses)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to specify these particular marked horses, and ِ to show this word describes the horses mentioned in the verse
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Changed from verb 'to be blessed' into plural noun meaning 'blessed animals/cattle' that help people
Added 'the' to make it specific
Added kasra to show possession or connection
Added 'and' to connect with previous items in list
Changed from action to the result of that action (like changing 'farm' to 'farming')
Added kasra to show this belongs to something (the love/desire mentioned earlier)
Added 'and the' at beginning. This word is part of a list of things that people love/desire, connected to حُبُّ (love) in the ayah.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is replaced with a long alif (ٰ) to show distance - like pointing to something far away instead of nearby
The ل with kasra (لِ) is added as a connector that helps join the next part
The كَ is added at the end to show we're talking about something masculine. In this ayah, it points to مَتٰعُ (enjoyment) which comes right after it.

steps.m: to provide temporary enjoyment or benefit

steps.n: Basic vowel marks (fatha) added to make past tense verb meaning 'he provided enjoyment'
Added alif to make it into a noun meaning the result or object of enjoyment
Added damma to show this word is in the nominative case (subject/topic)
The alif changed to a long alif (ا to ٰ), but the meaning stays the same - it's still 'enjoyment' owned by 'life'

steps.m: to be alive, to live life

steps.n: Root meaning 'life' changed to noun 'living/life' by adding و at the end
Added ة at the end to make it a complete noun meaning 'life'
Added definite article to specify this particular life, referring to worldly life in this context

steps.m: of the life (possessed by الدُّنْيَا - this world)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this life belongs to or is connected to الدُّنْيَا (this world) in the ayah
Changed the root into a feminine noun describing something that is close/near in time or space (compared to the afterlife)
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the د letter because it comes after ال. This word is describing what kind of life/existence is being talked about in the verse.
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. Unlike other Arabic words, it doesn't come from a three-letter root.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added damma at the end to show Allah is the subject doing something in this sentence
Added و at the beginning to connect this part to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Changed the regular alif to a special kind (ٱ) that's used with the word Allah
This is a common preposition in Arabic that comes from the idea of being present with something
Adding vowel marks to the root letters to make a word meaning 'at' or 'with'
Adding fatha at the end to prepare for connection with next part
Adding هُۥ at the end to show this means 'with Him', referring to Allah mentioned in the ayah
Changed into a noun showing the quality itself. This word is connected to عِندَهُ (with Him) in the ayah, showing that true beauty/excellence is with Allah.

steps.m: to return or come back to a place

steps.n: The و and hamza combine into a long آ sound because they're next to each other
Added ٱلْ to show we're talking about a specific place of return

steps.m: the place of return (possessed by/connected to something)
steps.n: Added ِ at the end to show this word is connected to something else in the sentence
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: say! (command to one person) (followed by أَؤُنَبِّئُكُم - shall I inform you)
steps.n: Changed to a command form by dropping the middle و and adding ُ and ْ. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to say something to people.
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he told'
Doubled the middle letter to show intensity of action
Added the first person prefix to show 'I' am doing the action

steps.m: shall I tell important news intensely?
steps.n: Added أَ at the start to make it a yes/no question - like adding 'shall' in English

steps.m: shall I tell you all important news? (followed by بِخَيْرٍ - about something better)
steps.n: Added كُم (kum) at the end to show 'you all' as the ones being told the news
Changes the concept of 'good' into a noun meaning 'something good' or 'something better'
Adds preposition ب meaning 'with/by'. This word is what you are being informed of (object of أُنَبِّئُ - I inform you)

steps.m: with something good/better (indefinite, object of أُنَبِّئُ)
steps.n: The ٍ shows this is indefinite (like 'a/an' in English) and is required after the preposition بِ. This completes the phrase 'I will inform you of something better'
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or that it's part of something. It has no root since it's a helper word.

Added a strong 'm' sound (shown by ّ) because it comes after a vowel sound in the previous word. This helps make the words flow together smoothly when reading. This word connects to تَجْرِى (flows) to tell us what's flowing from underneath.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far

steps.m: that (pointing to something far when talking to multiple people)
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows we're talking to multiple people
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that refers to someone or something
Adding ال (the) turns 'who/which' into 'the one who' - making it point to something specific
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding لِ at the beginning shows this is 'for' or 'to' those people
Connected the root letters و-ق-ي with vowels to make 'he protected' in the past tense
Added اِتَّ at the beginning to make it mean 'protecting oneself' instead of 'protecting others'
Changed to plural form by adding وْا at the end and changing initial alif to ٱ
Adding kasra changes this from an action into a word showing location or position
Adding fatha at the end shows this word connects to the word after it (رَبِّهِمْ - their Lord)
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this an intensive form, emphasizing the complete nature of the nurturing

steps.m: their Lord (the one being referred to in ٱتَّقَوْا۟)
steps.n: Adds the 3rd person plural possessive pronoun هِمْ to show it belongs to them. This refers back to those who have taqwa (fear/consciousness of Allah) mentioned in ٱتَّقَوْا۟.
Added vowels and doubled middle letter to create past tense verb

steps.m: a garden (a place covered with vegetation)
steps.n: Forms a noun of place from the root meaning 'to cover/conceal', referring to a garden as a place covered by vegetation
Adds double dammah to show this is a garden (not a specific one) as the subject
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed to feminine form because it's describing gardens (جَنَّاتٌ) which is feminine in Arabic
This is a helper word that shows something comes from or is compared to something else. Here it helps compare خَيْرٍ (better) with ذٰلِكُمْ (that/those)
Makes the basic direction word. This word describes where the rivers (الْأَنْهٰرُ) flow in relation to the gardens (جَنَّاتٌ).
ها refers back to the gardens (جَنَّاتٌ) which is feminine plural. The whole phrase تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهٰرُ means 'the rivers flow from beneath them (the gardens)'
Changed the root into a noun for the thing that flows
Changed single river to multiple rivers using a broken plural pattern
Added ال to make it specific and changed alif to alif madd for pronunciation
Added damma to show this is the subject of the verb تَجْرِى (flows/flowing) in this ayah
Changes from verb (to stay) to noun (one who stays)

steps.m: those who are staying forever (describing the gardens جَنَّاتٌ)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people who will stay in the gardens forever
This is a helper word that shows something is inside something else. Here it refers to being inside the gardens (جَنَّاتٌ) mentioned earlier.
ها refers back to the gardens (جَنَّاتٌ) mentioned earlier. Even though جَنَّاتٌ is plural, ها can refer to plural non-human things.

steps.m: to join or pair things together

steps.n: Changed the action into a noun for one person in a pair
Changed from one spouse to many spouses by adding 'a' at the start and making it plural
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect it with what came before
Added double damma to show this is a subject in the sentence
The vowels show this is about becoming characterized by purity
Adding shadda makes it about causing something else to become pure, not just being pure itself
Changed from a verb (to purify) to a passive participle (something purified) by adding مُ at start and keeping shadda
The letter م has a shaddah (doubled) which adds emphasis, meaning 'thoroughly/completely purified'
Adding ة makes this word feminine, showing it describes a feminine noun
Adding tanween (double dammah) makes the word indefinite, meaning 'a purified one' rather than 'the purified one'

steps.m: to be content or pleased with something

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a noun by adding the vowel 'i' and sukoon marks to mean the feeling of being pleased
Adding ان at the end makes it mean 'complete/intense contentment'
Changed the alif to a long alif (ٰ) which is just a writing variation
Added double damma to show this is a subject in the sentence
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'
This is a helper word that connects things by showing where something comes from, like saying 'from' in English
The shadda (ّ) makes the م sound stronger because it comes before the word اللَّهِ (Allah)
The fatha (َ) at the end shows this word connects to the next word اللَّهِ (Allah)
Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in pronunciation
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word. This shows that Allah's pleasure is being discussed.
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity/god
Added a double L sound (shadda) to change from 'any god' to 'Allah' - the One True God
Added fatha on the shadda to help with pronunciation
Added damma at the end to show Allah is the subject (doer) of the next word بَصِيرٌۢ (All-Seeing)

steps.m: and Allah (as the subject of بَصِيرٌۢ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed pattern to فَعِيل to show Allah's perfect and complete attribute of seeing

steps.m: All-Seeing (as a divine attribute of اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added the double damma mark (ٌ) at the end to show this word is describing Allah as the main subject
Changes from singular 'servant' to plural form meaning 'servants'
Adds preposition meaning 'with/by'
Makes the word specific by adding 'the'
Shows this word is connected to Allah being 'seeing/watchful' (بَصِيرٌۢ) over His servants in this ayah
Starting with the basic demonstrative pronoun and changing it to the relative pronoun form
Added ن at the end to make it refer to multiple people/things (plural)
Added ال with shadda to make it definite. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَقُولُونَ (they say) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا returns to و.

steps.m: they say/are saying (subject of إِنَّنَآ ءَامَنَّا)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people are speaking. This word is connected to what comes after, where they say 'Indeed, we have believed.'
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
The doubled middle letter shows intensity, indicating complete mastery and lordship
نا means 'our' when added to the end. This word is the subject of the verb يقولون (they say), showing who is being spoken about.
Starting with the basic conditional word إِنْ
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphatic 'indeed'
Adding نا at the end shows this refers to 'we/us'
The alif is elongated for pronunciation emphasis at the end of the word

steps.m: to cause to feel safe/to believe
steps.n: Adding long alif at start changes meaning to 'make someone feel safe/secure'
Added emphasis (shadda) to show strong/definite belief

steps.m: we believed (object of يَقُولُونَ - they say)
steps.n: Added نا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This word is what is being said by 'they' in يَقُولُونَ (they say)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to command form by adding alif at start, changing vowels, and adding sukun at end

steps.m: so forgive! (command to Allah in دُعَاء)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this is a response or result following from the previous statement about belief. The command here is part of a prayer (دُعَاء) to Allah.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added ل at the beginning to show this is being done for us. This word is connected to فَٱغْفِرْ (so forgive) in the ayah, showing what should be forgiven for us.
Connected the root letters with fatha and sukun to form the basic noun for a single sin
