steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects the good deeds mentioned earlier with the evil deeds mentioned here.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: she worked/did (referring to نَفْسٍ - a soul)
steps.n: Added ت to show the subject is feminine (نَفْسٍ - a soul) in the past tense
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects what someone did (عَمِلَتْ) with the good deeds (خَيْرٍ) they did.
Root meaning 'bad' becomes a noun meaning 'badness' by adding the vowel marks ُ and ْ
The long vowel is written with a special mark (ٓ) to show elongation

steps.m: any evil (in مَا عَمِلَتْ مِن سُوٓءٍ - whatever they did of evil)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is any general evil, not a specific evil. This word follows مِن (of/from) in the ayah.
Added vowels and combined letters with shadda to make basic past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning to show present tense feminine subject. This verb describes what each soul (نَفْس) does in the ayah, connecting to تَجِدُ (she finds).
Doubling the middle letter intensifies the meaning
Adding damma as nominative case marker

This is a particle that introduces wishes or hypothetical situations that are unlikely or impossible. In this ayah, it connects to تَوَدُّ (wishes/desires) to show what each soul will wish for on that Day.

steps.m: that there is (connecting تَوَدُّ with what follows)
steps.n: This particle connects what someone wishes (تَوَدُّ - 'she wishes') with what they wish for (that there would be a great distance). It makes the wish more specific.
Forms the basic noun meaning 'between' by adding vowel marks to connect the letters
Adds fatha to show this word describes where something happens

steps.m: between her/it (referring to each soul and what it did)
steps.n: Adds ها to show what comes between - in this case referring back to each soul (نَفْس) and its deeds mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: to separate or be between things

steps.n: Root meaning 'separate' became a word for 'between' - like the space that separates things

steps.m: between it (referring to the evil deeds mentioned)
steps.n: Added pronoun هُ to show what's being separated. In this ayah, it refers back to the evil deeds (سُوٓءٍ) that were mentioned.

steps.m: and between it (connecting to بَيْنَهَا from earlier in the verse)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this phrase with بَيْنَهَا (between her/it) from earlier in the verse, showing the two things being separated
Connected the letters and added vowels to make it a noun referring to a span of time
Added tanween (double vowel mark) to show this is describing something - in this case how long the period is
Added ي between ع and د and vowels to make an adjective showing a strong quality of distance

steps.m: far away (describing how distant something is)
steps.n: Adding ًا at the end changes it from an adjective (far/distant) to an adverb (far away) - it now describes how far something is
The doubled ذ shows this is done with extra emphasis
Adding يُ at the start shows 'he' is doing the warning in the present time
The كُمُ at the end means 'you all'

steps.m: and he warns you all intensively
steps.n: The وَ at the start means 'and'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
The word for 'deity' (إلَه) became the name 'Allah' by adding ال and special merging. The ُ at the end shows it's doing the action in the sentence.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يُحَذِّرُ (warns)
The هُ at the end shows this refers to Allah's self, as in the phrase 'Allah warns you about Himself'
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. Unlike other Arabic words, it doesn't come from a root - it's a unique word.
Added a shadda on the ل to show it's pronounced strongly

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of رَءُوفٌۢ/most kind)
steps.n: Added ُ (damma) at the end to show Allah is the subject doing the action in this sentence - He is the one who is most kind.

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of رَءُوفٌۢ/most kind)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before it. Also added ٱ (wasl form of alif) which helps connect the sounds smoothly.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
The و in the middle and ُ vowel make it an intensive form, showing someone who has this quality to the highest degree. The hamza (ء) replaces the original أ for easier pronunciation.

steps.m: extremely kind and merciful (describes Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: The tanween (double damma ٌ) shows this is indefinite and in the subject position. This word is describing Allah in this ayah.
Changes from singular 'servant' to plural form meaning 'servants'
Adding ال makes it specific/definite, like adding 'the' in English
Adding بِ prefix to indicate 'with/by'
The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to رَءُوفٌ (Most Kind) in the ayah, telling us that Allah is Most Kind with/to His servants.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, 3) adding sukun on last letter. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to the people.
This is a conditional particle that introduces the condition 'if you love Allah'. The word تُحِبُّونَ that follows completes the condition.
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
The middle weak letter و drops out and changes the vowels around it
Added تُمْ to show that multiple people are being addressed. This word comes before تُحِبُّونَ (you love) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
The middle letter is doubled to show intensity of the love, with kasra on first letter
Added تُ at the beginning to show present tense and 'you' as the subject

steps.m: you (all) love deeply (object is اللَّهَ - Allah)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action. This verb's object is Allah (اللَّهَ) in this ayah.
The ال makes it specific and unique, referring to the One True God. The hamza is dropped because this is a special divine name.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being loved in تُحِبُّونَ)
steps.n: The ٱ is a special way of writing ال, the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the َ shows Allah is the object being loved in the phrase 'if you love Allah'
Adding vowel marks to show this is a complete past tense verb meaning 'he followed'
Added ا at start and doubled middle letter to show stronger meaning
Added فَ at beginning to connect with previous statement about loving Allah
Added damma at end to show this is present tense
Added و to make it plural 'you' (masculine)
Added نِى at the end to show who should be followed (me)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the past tense form
Added يُ at start to make it mean 'he loves' (present), middle vowels changed to show it's about loving someone else

steps.m: he loves you all (Allah will love you all)
steps.n: Added كُمُ at the end to show the action affects multiple people being spoken to. This word is part of Allah's promise in the ayah that He will love those who follow the Prophet ﷺ.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed to the proper name of God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of يُحْبِبْ (will love) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed to present/future tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The sukun at the end shows this is in a special grammar form following another verb.

steps.m: and He will forgive (connected to يُحْبِبْكُمُ - He will love you)
steps.n: Added و to connect this with the previous verb يُحْبِبْكُمُ (He will love you), showing these actions happen together
Added fatha to the letter lam to show it's a preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'
Added كُمْ at the end to show this is directed to multiple people. This word is receiving the action of Allah loving (يُحْبِبْ) in this ayah.

steps.m: to commit a fault or sin

steps.n: This is a broken plural form showing multiple sins. This word is what is being forgiven in the phrase 'forgive for you' (يَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ) in this ayah.
Adds كُمْ at the end to mean 'your' (when speaking to multiple people)
This is the base word Allah - the proper name of God in Arabic. It's related to the word إله (god/deity) but is a unique proper noun.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly, emphasizing the uniqueness and importance of this name

steps.m: Allah (as the doer of يَغْفِرْ - forgives)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) at the end to show Allah is the one doing the action (forgiving) in this verse

steps.m: and Allah (as the doer of يَغْفِرْ - forgives)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this part with what came before it in the verse

steps.m: and Allah (as the doer of يَغْفِرْ - forgives)
steps.n: Changed regular alif to wasl alif (ٱ) since it connects with the و before it in pronunciation

steps.m: to cover up or remove faults/sins

steps.n: Added و after second root letter and vowels to show this describes someone who forgives a lot. This is describing Allah in this ayah.
The double damma (ٌ) shows this is one of Allah's names or attributes. In this ayah, it pairs with رَّحِيمٌ (Most Merciful) to show two of Allah's qualities.
The ي makes it mean 'full of mercy' instead of just 'merciful'
The ٌ shows this is being used as a complete noun/adjective
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Adding sukun on last letter, 3) Dropping the middle weak letter و. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'

steps.m: you all obey! (command to اللَّهَ وَٱلرَّسُولَ)
steps.n: Changed from past tense 'made obey' to a direct command 'obey!' for multiple people. The وا ending shows it's addressing a group. Connected to اللَّهَ وَٱلرَّسُولَ (Allah and the Messenger) who should be obeyed.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns

steps.m: Allah (the one true God who is being disobeyed in this verse)
steps.n: Added ال with special spelling to make it the proper name of the One God. The fatha at the end shows Allah is the object of the verb 'obey' in this verse.

steps.m: to send or deliver a message

steps.n: The و makes it mean 'someone who does this action a lot'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ر with shadda
Added وَ meaning 'and' at the beginning. The اَل changes to ٱل after وَ

steps.m: and the messenger (object of أَطِيعُوا - obey)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the command 'obey' (أَطِيعُوا)
This is a conditional particle that introduces the first part of an if-then statement. In this ayah, it introduces what happens if people turn away.

steps.m: and if/then if (connecting to previous statement)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition to the previous command (أَطِيعُوا - obey). It shows what happens next if people don't follow that command.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Adding shadda (doubling) on ل shows the action is done with intensity
Adding تَ at start shows the person is doing the action to themselves

steps.m: they turned away (from obeying الله والرسول)
steps.n: Added وْا to show multiple people did this action. This word relates to the previous command to 'obey Allah and the Messenger' by showing what happens if people do the opposite.
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows. It's like adding 'indeed' or 'surely' in English.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement about obeying Allah and the Messenger.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became a proper name for the One God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object of أَطِيعُوا - obey)
steps.n: Added the fatha at the end to show this is the object of the command 'obey'. Also added the shadda to emphasize the L sound and changed the alif to a wasl form since it connects to the previous word.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يُحِبُّ (loves) into 'does not love' in this ayah. It's one of the most basic Arabic words used to say 'no' or make something negative.
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to love deeply, to show affection

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
When two identical letters come together, they combine with a shadda
Added يُ at the start to show present tense, and changed vowels to show ongoing action. This verb is negated by لَا (not) in the ayah.

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed to describe someone who does this action
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: the ones who reject (truth) (object of لَا يُحِبُّ)
steps.n: Added ين to show multiple people. This word is what's being acted upon in the phrase 'Allah does not love the disbelievers.'
This is a special emphatic particle used at the start of sentences to say 'indeed' or 'truly'
Adding the shadda makes this word stronger - it tells us that what comes next is definitely true. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah really did choose Adam, Noah, and the families mentioned.
Added shadda to show emphasis on the ل
Changed initial alif to wasl form and added fatha at end to show this is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily)
Form VIII (added ت between first two letters) shows someone putting effort into carefully choosing/selecting
Added the small vertical line (ٓ) to show where to pause if stopping at this word
The root letters were turned into the name Adam by adding a long 'aa' sound at the start

steps.m: Adam (as the object of اصْطَفَىٰ - chose/selected)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Adam is the object being chosen by Allah in this ayah
This is the proper name of Prophet Noah (peace be upon him), derived from the root meaning of lamenting/mourning, as he grieved for his people
The ًا at the end shows that Noah is the object being chosen by Allah in this verse

steps.m: and Noah (as the object being chosen by اصْطَفَى)
steps.n: The وَ connects Noah to Adam who was mentioned before, showing Allah chose both of them

steps.m: to gather around or be close to

steps.n: Changed from root meaning 'gather' to mean 'family/followers' - like saying 'the people of Adam and Noah'

steps.m: and the family/followers (connected to ءَادَمَ وَنُوحًا)
steps.n: Added وَ at the start to mean 'and', connecting this word to what came before it
This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew/Aramaic into Arabic, meaning 'father of many'. It doesn't have an Arabic root.
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation and grammatical role. The fatha at the end shows this is the object of the verb اصْطَفَىٰ (chose/selected).

steps.m: to be close to or follow

steps.n: The root letters combine with ءَ at the start and alif after first letter to form a noun meaning 'family' or 'followers' - those who are close to and follow someone
The prefix وَ means 'and', connecting this to the previous words ءَادَمَ وَنُوحًا (Adam and Noah)
This is the basic form of the proper name Imran (عمران), who was the father of Mary (Maryam) in Islamic tradition.
Added vowel marks to show this is Imran's name when it follows آل (family of). The long alif shows it's a special name.

This is a preposition that shows something is on top of, higher than, or has authority over something else. In this ayah, it connects اصْطَفَىٰٓ (chose) with العٰلَمِينَ (the worlds), showing Allah chose these people over all others.
Root ع-ل-م becomes a noun عَالَم meaning 'world' by adding vowels - like how Earth is a world we know and can see
Creates regular masculine plural form to show multiple worlds/realms

steps.m: the worlds, the realms, the universes
steps.n: Adds the definite article to specify these particular worlds/realms

steps.m: the worlds, the realms, the universes (object of عَلَى)
steps.n: Changes alif to alif madd (ٰ) for pronunciation and adds final fatha because it follows the preposition عَلَى

steps.m: to scatter/spread (like seeds or tiny things)
offspring/descendants (those who are spread from a lineage)
steps.n: The word means descendants because they spread out from their ancestors like scattered seeds
The ً shows this word is receiving the action (like saying 'the descendants' vs 'to the descendants')
Added a small mark (tanween) at the end to show this word is receiving the action
Basic noun form showing a piece or portion of something
Added ُ at the end to show this word is the subject (doer) in the sentence

steps.m: some of it (referring to ذُرِّيَّةً - offspring)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end which means 'it/her' and refers back to the word ذُرِّيَّةً (offspring) mentioned earlier
This is a basic preposition that connects two words, showing that one thing comes from another. Here it shows that some offspring come from others.
Added a small ن (called nun ghunna) at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'some' or 'part'
Added two dots under the last letter to show it belongs to something (like when you say 'of some')

This is a unique word that doesn't come from a typical root. It's the proper name of God in Arabic and is related to the word إله (god/deity), but has special characteristics that make it exclusively used for the One True God.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly, which is unique to this word's special status.

steps.m: Allah (as the doer of سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show that Allah is the subject of the sentence, being described as سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (All-Hearing, All-Knowing).

steps.m: And Allah (as the doer of سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it. Also added the connecting hamza (ٱ) which helps pronounce the word smoothly after و.
Adding ي between م and ع with the vowels makes it mean someone who hears in a perfect and complete way
The double damma (tanween) at the end shows this is describing Allah as the All-Hearing one

steps.m: one who knows deeply/completely (as a quality of اللَّهُ)
steps.n: The ي between ل and م shows this is someone who has this quality in a complete and permanent way. Here it describes Allah.
The ٌ (tanween) at the end shows this is a complete word describing Allah's perfect knowledge

steps.m: when (introducing the time when امْرَأَتُ عِمْرٰنَ said)
steps.n: This is a time word that introduces a past event. It helps tell stories by showing exactly when something happened. Here it shows when the wife of Imran made her statement.
Added fathas and changed و to ا to make past tense form

steps.m: she said (subject of this statement is امْرَأَتُ عِمْرٰنَ)
steps.n: Added ت with kasra to show the subject is feminine (wife of Imran)
Basic verb form showing past tense 'he/it was feminine' by adding the vowel marks
This is a special word for 'woman' - it's not actually derived from مَرَأَ. The word ٱمْرَأ is a standalone noun meaning 'woman'
Added ة to specify this is a feminine noun
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action in قَالَتِ (she said)
This is a proper name that doesn't come from a root. It's a name of Hebrew origin used in Arabic.

steps.m: Imran (the husband of the woman speaking in this verse)
steps.n: The name Imran gets a special mark (ٰ) and ending (َ) to show it's being used as part of 'wife of Imran'

steps.m: to care for and help grow

steps.n: Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to show this is someone who perfectly takes care of everything
Adding kasra at the end makes this a call/plea to the Lord
This is a special word that makes what follows more certain or emphasized

steps.m: indeed I (speaking to رَبِّ - my Lord)
steps.n: Added ي (written as ى) at the end to show 'I' am speaking. This word is part of what the wife of Imran is saying to her Lord.
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he vowed'
The تُ at the end shows that 'I' did the action. In this ayah, the wife of Imran is speaking to her Lord about what she has dedicated.
This is a preposition that shows something is being directed towards someone or something. Here it shows who the vow is being made for.

steps.m: to/for you (connected to نَذَرْتُ - I vowed)
steps.n: The كَ shows this is directed to 'you' (when talking to one male person). In this ayah, the wife of Imran is speaking to Allah, saying she has made a vow to/for Him.
This word is used to point to something without naming it specifically
This word connects to 'in my womb' (في بطني) to show she is referring to what she is carrying. She doesn't say 'baby' or 'child' because she doesn't know exactly what it is yet.

steps.m: in (connects بَطْنِى with what comes before it)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something is located inside something else. In this ayah, it connects 'my womb' (بَطْنِى) with what was mentioned before it.

steps.m: in (connects بَطْنِى with what comes before it)
steps.n: The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added vowel marks to make the noun for 'womb' - the place that is inside the body where babies grow

steps.m: my womb (that belongs to امْرَأَتُ عِمْرٰنَ)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show it's 'my womb'. This is being said by the wife of Imran about her own womb.

steps.m: to be free or to set free

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows the action is done thoroughly or with dedication
Adding مُ at the start changes it from 'to dedicate' into 'one who is dedicated' - like changing 'free' to 'freed'

steps.m: one who is fully dedicated (object of نَذَرْتُ)
steps.n: Adding ًا shows this is what was vowed/dedicated in the phrase 'I vowed' (نَذَرْتُ)
This is the basic meaning of accepting something
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added ت at start and doubled middle letter to show doing action thoroughly
Changed from 'he accepted' to 'you accept!' by putting a stop mark (sukun) at the end instead of a fatha

steps.m: so accept thoroughly! (command to Allah in prayer)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before in the prayer
This is a basic word showing where something comes from or its source
The doubled ن (with shadda) adds emphasis to show importance
Adding ي at the end shows this is happening to or coming from me
The alif at the end (written as a small line) shows this is at the end of a phrase or statement. Here it's part of the woman's speech to her Lord.
This is a particle that adds emphasis to what comes after it. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English.
Adding كَ at the end makes this word directly address someone. In this ayah, it's part of عِمْرٰنَ's wife's prayer to Allah.

steps.m: you (the one being addressed in إِنَّكَ)
steps.n: This is a basic pronoun meaning 'you' when talking to one male person. In this ayah, it's part of إِنَّكَ (indeed you), emphasizing who is being addressed.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
This pattern shows someone who does the action extensively or has this quality strongly
Adding ٱل with shadda on س makes it definite and specific, like 'The' in English. The ٱ is a special form of ال used before س
The ُ shows this is a main word in the sentence. When used as a name of Allah, it means He hears everything perfectly.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed pattern to show someone who has this quality in an intensive way

steps.m: The All-Knowing (as a name of Allah)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite and specific, here used as a divine name

steps.m: The All-Knowing (as a name of Allah, describing Him)
steps.n: Added damma to show this is describing Allah in this ayah
This is a time particle that shows something happened right after something else, like saying 'as soon as' or 'when' in English

steps.m: and when/so when (connects to وَضَعَتْهَا - she delivered her)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this event to what was mentioned before, showing this happened next in the sequence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding ت shows a woman did the action
ها means 'her' or 'it' (feminine)
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
The middle letter و changes to ا because it comes after fatha - this is a common Arabic spelling rule
Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine (she)
Doubling the middle letter ب shows this is someone who really takes care of others
Adding kasra at the end shows you're talking to Allah and saying 'my Lord'
This is a basic emphasis word that makes statements stronger, like adding 'definitely' in English

steps.m: indeed I (subject of وَضَعْتُهَآ - I delivered/gave birth to her)
steps.n: Added ي at the end to show 'I' am speaking. This connects to the verb وَضَعْتُهَآ (I delivered/gave birth to her) where the speaker is telling what she did.

steps.m: placed/put down (past tense verb root form)
steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding vowels - 'a' sound on first two letters, silent ending
Adding تُ shows that 'I' did the action
Adding هَا shows that 'her' is what was placed

steps.m: I placed her (referring to the baby mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: The alif is lengthened to show emphasis and pause in recitation
Changes from root meaning into noun for 'female'
Same word with slight spelling variation - both mean 'female'
This is a unique word that refers specifically to the one true God in Islam. The double ل with shadda makes it a proper name.

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of أَعْلَمُ - knows best)
steps.n: The ُ (damma) at the end shows this word is doing the action in the sentence - Allah is the one who knows best.

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of أَعْلَمُ - knows best)
steps.n: The وَ at the beginning connects this part to what came before it in the verse, while ٱ is just a different way of writing the first ا.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to comparative/superlative form. This word describes Allah's complete knowledge compared to others.
This is a relative pronoun that means 'what' - it's used to refer to things rather than people
Added the preposition بِ (with/by) to show how or by what means something was done. Here it connects to the verb وَضَعَتْ (she delivered) to show 'with what she delivered'
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form showing a male did the action

steps.m: she put/placed (referring to her putting her baby in وَضَعَتْهَا)
steps.n: Adding ت at the end changes 'he' to 'she' as the doer. In this ayah, it refers to the mother placing her baby (Maryam).
This is a special verb that only comes in past tense form but gives present meaning. It's used to negate things.
Adding the vowels makes it a complete verb meaning 'is not'
Adding وَ (and) connects this statement to what came before, where she said 'I delivered her as female'
Forms a noun meaning 'male'
The fathas create a noun meaning 'male'
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning
Added the definite article 'al' to specify a particular male
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject being compared to الأنثى (the female) in this ayah
This is the root that represents the concept of being feminine
Added damma (ُ) on first letter and alif maksura (ىٰ) at end to make it mean a female being
Added ال to make it specific - 'the female' instead of just any female
Added كَ at beginning to show comparison - this word is comparing something to 'the female'
This is the basic emphasis word that adds certainty to what follows
Added ي at the end to show this is about me (first person)

steps.m: and indeed I (speaking to رَبِّ - my Lord)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before. This is part of what the mother of Mary is saying to her Lord.

steps.m: to name, call, or make high
steps.n: This root relates to naming things and making them known or elevated
The doubled middle letter makes it mean 'to name' instead of just 'being high'
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this action in the past
Added هَا at the end to show who was named (her)
This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew/Aramaic 'Miriam', meaning 'wished-for child' or 'beloved'. In this ayah, it's the name being given to the baby girl.
The fatha at the end shows that Mary is the object being named in the sentence (after سَمَّيْتُهَا - 'I named her')
This is a basic word that adds emphasis to what comes after it, like saying 'truly' in English
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before it
Added ي at the end to show this is about me ('I')
Added an elongation mark (ٓ) over the ى to show emphasis in pronunciation
Changed the root letters into past tense by adding the vowel 'aa' in the middle and 'a' at the end
Changed from 'he sought refuge' to 'I seek refuge' in present tense. The middle letter changes to ي because it's a hollow verb.
Added هَا which shows the protection is for her. In this ayah, it refers to Maryam who was just mentioned.
This is a preposition showing the means or instrument. It connects to the verb أُعِيذُ (I seek refuge) in this ayah.

steps.m: with you (object of أُعِيذُ - I seek refuge)
steps.n: Added كَ which means 'you' (masculine singular). This shows who the refuge is being sought with.
Changes the root into a concrete noun meaning descendants
Added و meaning 'and' to connect this to the previous word
Added ها meaning 'her' to show possession. This word is what is being protected in the phrase 'I seek protection for her and her offspring'
This is a preposition showing the source or origin
The fatha at the end connects this word to the definite noun الشَّيْطٰنِ (Satan) that follows it
Added letters to turn the meaning from 'being rebellious' into 'a rebellious one' (like Satan)
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: of the rebellious one (possessed by مِنَ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show it's owned by or connected to something, and changed the spelling of the long a
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who is repeatedly cast away/cursed
steps.n: Changed from 'stoned' to mean someone who is always being cursed and pushed away

steps.m: the one who is repeatedly cast away/cursed
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ر because it comes after ال

steps.m: of the one who is repeatedly cast away/cursed (describing الشيطان)
steps.n: Has kasra (i) sound at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from) - describes الشيطان (Satan)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: to accept/receive with care and attention
steps.n: Adding shadda (ّ) shows the action is done thoroughly and with special care
Adding تَ at the start shows someone is accepting something willingly and with care

steps.m: and so willingly accepted/received with care
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this acceptance happened as a result of Maryam being offered to Allah's service

steps.m: and so He (Allah) accepted her (Maryam) with special care
steps.n: Adding هَا (ha) at the end shows that Maryam was the one being accepted - it means 'her'. Without هَا we just have the action of accepting, but with هَا we know who was accepted.

steps.m: to nurture, care for, raise up

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures in a complete and perfect way
The damma shows this is the doer of the action in relation to the verb تَقَبَّلَ (accepted) in this ayah

steps.m: her Lord (the one who nurtured her completely)
steps.n: Adding هَا (meaning 'her') at the end shows this is specifically her Lord/Nurturer (referring to Maryam)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from verb 'accepted' to noun 'acceptance' by adding و

steps.m: with acceptance (describing how رَبُّهَا accepted her)
steps.n: Added بِ to show how the action was done and tanween to show it's indefinite. This connects to the earlier verb فَتَقَبَّلَهَا (so He accepted her) by showing how He accepted her.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Removed the final fatha to change from a verb to an adjective that describes نَبَاتًا (growth/plant)
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this describes قَبُولٍ (acceptance) and matches its grammatical case
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changes from something growing on its own to someone making it grow. The ۢ (small م) shows a necessary sound change when ن meets ب.
Adds feminine singular object pronoun 'her' to show that Mary was the one being made to grow

steps.m: and made her grow (connected to Allah accepting her with good acceptance)
steps.n: The و (and) was added to connect this to Allah's earlier actions, showing He both accepted AND made her grow
Changes verb into noun showing the result of growing. This word describes how Allah made Maryam grow.
Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this describes how Allah made Maryam grow - with excellent growth. This connects to حَسَنًا (excellent) which comes right after it.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make it into a describing word

steps.m: in a good/pleasing way (describes how نَبَاتًا happened)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is describing how Allah made her grow up (نَبَاتًا). It's like saying 'He made her grow up well'.

steps.m: to take responsibility for, to care for

steps.n: Past tense verb meaning 'he took care of/sponsored' - root letters given vowels to make it a complete word

steps.m: he put in charge of caring
steps.n: Adding a shadda (doubled letter) changes 'took responsibility' to 'put in charge of caring' - making the action more intense and involving another person
Adding ها at the end makes it 'her' - the person being put in charge

steps.m: and he put her in charge
steps.n: Adding و at start connects this action to what happened before
This is a proper noun name of Hebrew origin meaning "God has remembered". In this ayah, he is mentioned as entering upon Maryam in her prayer chamber.
The root letters get combined with a ُ (damma) and ّ (shadda) to make a word meaning 'all' or 'every'
Adding ما changes this from just meaning 'every' to meaning 'whenever' - it connects to زَكَرِيَّا's actions in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is a completed action. This word shows who did the finding (وَجَدَ) mentioned later in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is above or on top of something else. It's like saying 'on' or 'upon' in English.
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for adding a pronoun. This is like getting a word ready to connect with something else.
Added هَا which means 'her' to show who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers to Maryam.

This is a proper noun (name) of Hebrew origin meaning "God has remembered". As a foreign proper noun, it doesn't follow standard Arabic root patterns. In this verse, he is mentioned as the guardian who was entrusted with taking care of Mary.

steps.m: Zakariya (the subject of كَفَّلَهَا - 'he entrusted her to')
steps.n: In this ayah, Zakariya is the object of the verb كَفَّلَهَا (he entrusted her to), showing that Allah assigned him as Mary's guardian.
This root carries the basic meaning of setting something apart or dedicating it
Added مِ at beginning to show place, and ا after second root letter to strengthen the meaning. This creates a noun meaning 'a dedicated place for worship'
Added ٱلْ to specify this particular prayer chamber, and َ at the end because it's the object of دَخَلَ (entered)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is connected to زَكَرِيَّا (Zakariya) who found something.
This word is used to show location or possession, like saying something is 'at' or 'with' someone
The root letters ع-ن-د become عِندَ (near/at/with) by adding the vowels kasra and fatha. The fatha at the end shows it's connected to the verb وَجَدَ (he found)
Added هَا (meaning 'her') to show it was near/with her. Connected to the word وَجَدَ (found) to show what was found.

steps.m: to provide with what is needed
steps.n: This is the basic root meaning showing the concept of providing necessities
Root meaning 'provide' becomes a noun meaning 'what is provided' by adding i-vowel
Added -an ending to show this is the thing that was found (like saying 'a provision' instead of just 'provision')
This is the basic concept of speaking or saying something
Added fathas to make it past tense, meaning someone did this action in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in hollow verbs (verbs with و in the middle) but doesn't change the meaning
This is a proper name, likely derived from ancient Hebrew. The vowel pattern helps make it pronounceable.
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject being addressed
Added يٰ at the beginning to show someone is calling out to Maryam. In this ayah, Zakariya is calling to her when he finds provision with her.
This is the basic form of the word meaning 'that'

steps.m: how/from where (asking about the source of رِزْقًا)
steps.n: Adding ىٰ changes this from 'that' to a question word. Here Zakariya is asking Mary about where she got the provisions (رِزْقًا) he found with her.
Basic preposition showing direction or purpose
Added كِ to show this is addressed to a female (in this case, Maryam who is being asked about the provision she received)
Basic pointing word that shows something is near
The alif is replaced with a small alif above the ه to show it's a demonstrative
Added ذَ to make it specifically mean 'this' instead of just 'here'

steps.m: this (referring to the رِزْقًا - provision - that Zakariya found)
steps.n: Added ا at the end to show this is masculine singular. In this ayah, it's pointing to the رِزْقًا (provision) that Zakariya found with Maryam.
The و (waw) changes to ا (alif) because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic words with و (waw) in the middle.
Added تْ at the end to show a female (Maryam) is speaking. This تْ is silent (has sukun) because it's at the end of the word.
This is a standalone pronoun used to refer back to something just mentioned. Here it refers to the رِزْقًا (provision) that Zakariya found with Maryam.

steps.m: from (connects عِندِ to what comes before it)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it connects عِندِ (presence/with) to the previous part, showing that something is 'from the presence of Allah'.
Used as a noun meaning 'presence' or 'near' in the genitive case
Changed into a noun meaning presence or nearness
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to اللَّهِ (Allah). Together they mean 'from Allah's presence'
Added shadda to emphasize the ل, which is a special feature of this divine name
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word. This shows that this belongs to or comes from Allah in the phrase 'from Allah'
This is a basic emphasis word that introduces and strengthens what comes after it. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English.
The shadda (ّ) is added to make the emphasis even stronger. This word connects to and emphasizes the next part that talks about Allah providing.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.
The word 'Allah' got a stronger L sound (shadda), a special beginning A (hamzat wasl), and an -a ending because it comes after 'inna' (indeed)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on first root letter, and damma on middle root letter

This is a special word that introduces a person or people we're talking about. In this ayah, it's used to say Allah provides for whoever He chooses. It's like saying 'the one who' or 'anyone who' in English.
The root letters were connected and vowels added. The middle letter ي changed to ا to make 'he wanted' in past tense
Changed from past tense (he wanted) to present tense (he wants) by adding يَ at the start
Added long alif to show extended/ongoing wanting
This is a location word (ظرف) that shows position above something
Root meaning 'change' becomes a simple word meaning 'other than' - like turning 'change' into 'different'

steps.m: without, by other than (connected to حِسَابٍ)
steps.n: Adding بِ changes meaning to 'without' or 'by other than'. This preposition connects this word to حِسَابٍ (reckoning/account) in the ayah.

steps.m: without, by other than (connected to حِسَابٍ)
steps.n: The ِ ending shows this word is connected to حِسَابٍ (reckoning/account), forming the phrase 'without reckoning'
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and change it from a verb to a noun
Added alif to make it into a noun meaning the result of counting
Added tanween kasra to show this word comes after بِغَيْرِ (without) in the phrase بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ (without calculation)
This is a demonstrative word that points to a nearby place or situation

steps.m: there (where زَكَرِيَّا called upon his Lord)
steps.n: Adding لِكَ changes the meaning from 'here' (close) to 'there' (far away). In this ayah, it refers to the place or moment where Zakariya made his prayer.

steps.m: to call upon, pray to, or invite

steps.n: Adding the three fathas shows this is a complete past tense verb
The و at the end changes to ا because it has a fatha before it. This is a common change in Arabic that doesn't affect the meaning.
This is a proper name of Hebrew/Aramaic origin referring to the prophet Zakariya. No morphological derivation applies as it's a borrowed name.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to form the proper name. This is the subject (doer) of the verb دَعَا (called upon) in this ayah.

steps.m: to take care of, raise, look after

steps.n: Adding fatha marks creates the basic past tense verb meaning 'he did'
The root رَبَبَ (to raise/care for) becomes رَبّ (Lord) with a shadda, then هُ (his) is added to show it's his Lord
Connected the root letters together
Added fatha marks to show past tense and who did the action
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)

steps.m: to care for and help grow

steps.n: Doubling the ب shows intensity of caring/nurturing
Adding kasra at the end makes it a call/address to Allah

steps.m: to give or grant as a gift

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a complete past tense verb
Changed to command form. The initial و is dropped because it's a weak letter. In this ayah, Prophet Zakariya is making dua to Allah asking Him to grant him something.
Basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'for'
Added kasra to show it connects to what comes after it
Added ى at the end to show this means 'to me'. This word connects to هَبْ (grant), showing who should receive what is being granted.
Basic preposition showing that the offspring (ذُرِّيَّةً) would come from His presence (لَّدُنكَ)
The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word connects to what comes after it
Becomes a noun meaning presence or nearness
Adds vowels to make it a proper noun meaning 'presence'
Adds a stop mark at the end to complete the word
Added كَ to show it's Your presence. This word follows مِن (from) in the ayah.

steps.m: to spread or scatter (like seeds or descendants)

steps.n: Added damma and doubled the middle letter to show lots of scattering
Added ي with shadda to turn 'scattered' into 'offspring/descendants' - like how children spread out from parents
Added ة to make it refer to one specific offspring or group of descendants

steps.m: offspring (as the object being asked for)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is what Zakariya was asking for in his prayer
Added vowels to make it an adjective describing a quality
Doubled the middle letter to show stronger quality
Added feminine ending because ذُرِّيَّةً (offspring) is feminine

steps.m: very pleasant, pure, good offspring (as the object being asked for)
steps.n: Added -an ending to show this is describing what the person is asking Allah for in their prayer
This is an emphasis particle that strengthens the meaning of what follows

steps.m: indeed you, verily you (subject of سَمِيعُ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is addressing 'you'. This word connects to سَمِيعُ (All-Hearing) in this ayah, showing that Allah is being addressed.
Adding vowels makes this a past tense verb meaning 'to hear'

steps.m: one who hears intensely (attribute of Allah)
steps.n: Changed from past tense verb 'heard' to a special form with ي that means 'one who hears always/perfectly' - used as one of Allah's names
Added ُ to show this describes Allah in the phrase 'Indeed You are' (إِنَّكَ)
Changed the word from an action into a noun that means the act itself
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the د letter because it comes after ال

steps.m: of the prayer (possessed by سَمِيعُ - Hearer)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to سَمِيعُ (Hearer) in the phrase 'Hearer of prayer'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added ى at the end to complete the past tense verb
Added ت to show a female did the action
Added فَ at beginning to show this happened after something else
Added هُ at the end to show who was called. The angels (ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةُ) are doing the calling in this verse.
Root 'rule/authority' becomes 'angel' by adding the vowels a-a, showing angels have authority from Allah
The word for one angel changes its middle letters to make it mean many angels - like how we add 's' in English but Arabic changes the inside instead
Added feminine ending to complete the plural form
Added the definite article ال to specify these particular angels
Added final damma to show this is the subject doing the calling in فَنَادَتْهُ (and she/it called him)
This is a pronoun that means 'he'. In this ayah, it refers to Zakariya who was standing in prayer when the angels called him.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This shows that Zakariya was in this state (standing in prayer) when the angels called him.
Added vowels to make it 'he stood' in the past tense
Changed from verb 'he stood' to noun meaning 'one who stands/standing'
Changed the ا to آ for proper writing, but doesn't change the meaning
Added ٌ to show this is the main subject doing the standing
Root ص-ل-و became 'he prayed' by adding shadda (doubled middle letter) to show strong/intense action

steps.m: he is praying (standing in prayer in المِحْرَابِ)
steps.n: Added يُ prefix to show present tense and changed vowels to match Form II present tense pattern
Changed from active (he prays) to passive (being prayed for) with a slight spelling change at the end
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it shows that the angels called to Zakariya while he was inside the prayer area (محراب).
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This is a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to fight or struggle against something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added مِ at the start to show it's a place, and changed the middle to make it a special room/chamber. The word came to mean a prayer room since it's a place set apart for worship.
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ِ to show it follows the word فِى (in)
This is the basic form of the word that connects two parts of a sentence, like saying 'I know that you are here' in English

steps.m: that/indeed (introduces اللَّهَ يُبَشِّرُكَ - Allah gives you good news)
steps.n: The shadda (ّ) on ن and the fathas (َ) make the word stronger. It's like saying 'Listen carefully, because what comes next is important!' In this ayah, it introduces the important news that Allah is giving to Zakariya.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
Added ال and a shadda to make it a proper noun referring specifically to Allah. The hamza is dropped when ال is added.

steps.m: Allah (the one true God) (object of أَنَّ)
steps.n: Changed alif to wasl-alif and added fatha at the end because it's the object of أَنَّ (that/indeed) in this ayah
The shadda (ّ) on ش makes the action stronger and shows it's done to someone else
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start. Still shows intense/repeated action through the ّ (shaddah).

steps.m: he gives you good news (object of أَنَّ)
steps.n: Added كَ to show the news is being given to 'you'. This word follows أَنَّ (that) in the ayah.
Alif maqsura is used at end instead of ي

steps.m: with Yahya (object of يُبَشِّرُكَ - gives you glad tidings)
steps.n: Adds the preposition بِ (with/of) to show this is the object of the glad tidings. The small alif (ٰ) is added at the end to show proper pronunciation.

steps.m: to speak truth or confirm something

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger
Adding مُ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ shows who does the action
Adding ًا shows this word describes Yahya (John) in the ayah

steps.m: to speak or communicate with words

steps.n: Added vowels to make it into a noun meaning 'word' and added ة to make it feminine
Added بِ at the beginning to show this word is connected to the verb يُبَشِّرُكَ (gives you good news)
Added the two dots ( ٍ ) under the last letter to show this word connects with 'from Allah' that comes right after it
This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something. In this ayah, it connects الصَّالِحِينَ (the righteous) to يَحْيَىٰ (Yahya), showing he is from among them.
The shadda appears when مِن comes before a word starting with ل or ن, making the pronunciation blend together smoothly
The fatha is added at the end to connect this word with the next word الصَّالِحِينَ (the righteous ones)
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One True God. The hamza is dropped and the lam gets a shadda.
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of the word مِنَ (from). Also changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters together
The و changes to ي and gets doubled (shadda) to show this is someone who leads a lot or has authority

steps.m: a leader (receiving the action of يُبَشِّرُكَ - gives good news)
steps.n: Added ًا (tanween) to show this word is receiving the action - Allah is giving good news about a leader

steps.m: and a leader (receiving the action of يُبَشِّرُكَ - gives good news)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this quality with the other good news being given
Basic past tense verb form - added short vowels (fatha) to make 'he restrained'

steps.m: one who restrains himself a lot
steps.n: Changed the middle vowel to ُ and added و to show this describes someone who does this action frequently
Added ًا to show this is describing Yahya (John) in the ayah
Added وَ (and) to connect this description with the other qualities of Yahya mentioned in the ayah
The doubled ي (yaa) shows this is their special role or job

steps.m: a prophet (object of يُبَشِّرُكَ - gives good news)
steps.n: The ًا shows this word is receiving the action of Allah giving good news to Zakariya

steps.m: and a prophet (object of يُبَشِّرُكَ - gives good news)
steps.n: Adding conjunction prefix to connect this to previous phrase
This is a basic preposition showing something comes from or belongs to something else. Here it connects to ٱللَّهِ (Allah) to show the origin.
The shadda (doubled م) makes the meaning stronger, and the َ (fatha) at the end helps it flow into the next word ٱللَّهِ

steps.m: to make things right, to be good
steps.n: This root is about being morally good and doing the right thing
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: one who makes things right/is good
steps.n: This pattern shows someone who has this quality as part of their character

steps.m: one who makes things right/is good
steps.n: When ال is added before this word, the first letter gets doubled (shown by the shadda mark)

steps.m: the one who makes things right/is good
steps.n: Adding ال makes it specific rather than general
Adding ين makes it plural and shows it comes after the word مِنَ (from/of) in this ayah
Final fatha mark shows this is the object of مِنَ (from/of)
Added fathas to make it past tense and show who did the action
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop to completion

steps.n: Doubling the ب makes it mean 'one who does a lot of nurturing'
Adding kasra at the end makes it into a call/address to the Lord
This is a question word that doesn't come from a root. It's used to ask about manner or means.
Adding ى with alif maksura (ىٰ) changes this from a word meaning 'that' to a question word meaning 'how'. In this ayah, Zakariya is asking how he could have a son given his old age.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he becomes/will become (describing how غُلٰمٌ/son can exist)
steps.n: Changed to present/future tense. In this ayah, it's part of the question 'How can I have a son?' showing disbelief about a future possibility.
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'

steps.m: for me, to me (connected to يَكُونُ - will be)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show this means 'me'. This word connects to يَكُونُ (will be) in the phrase 'how will there be for me...'

steps.m: to be youthful or grow up

steps.n: Changes from root meaning into a noun for a young person
Adding tanween makes it indefinite - meaning 'a' boy rather than 'the' boy
Added tanween dammah (ٌ) to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a boy' instead of 'the boy'). This word is the subject of يَكُونُ (to be) in this ayah.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation

Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what follows (بَلَغَنِىَ meaning 'reached me'). This shows the connection between stating that old age has reached him after expressing wonder at having a son.

steps.m: to reach or arrive at something

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added نِ at the end to show the action was done to 'me'
Added ىَ at the end to clearly show 'me' (the person being reached). The word comes after وَقَدْ which tells us this definitely happened in the past.
Changes from verb 'to grow old' to noun meaning 'old age'

steps.m: the old age (that has reached me)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to the specific old age mentioned in context - the old age that has reached the speaker

steps.m: the old age (subject of بَلَغَنِىَ - has reached me)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is the subject doing the reaching in بَلَغَنِىَ (has reached me)
Adding اِ turns the root into a noun meaning 'person'
Adding ة makes the word feminine, changing meaning from 'person' to 'woman'
Changed the ending ة to ت and added a kasra (i sound) to show it belongs to someone - like saying 'someone's wife'
Added ي at the end to show first person possession (my)
Added و at beginning to mean 'and', and changed initial hamza to connecting form

steps.m: to be unable to have children
someone who cannot have children (describes امرأتي my wife)
steps.n: Added ا after first letter and ِ after second letter to show this describes someone with this quality. This word tells us about امرأتي (my wife) in the sentence.
Adding the two dots on top (tanween) makes it mean 'a barren person' instead of 'the barren person'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. This is the main verb at the start of what Zakariya said.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to say.
Starting with the comparison particle ك that shows similarity
Added ذا which is a demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this/that'
Added ل with kasra to make it point to something far away instead of close by

steps.m: like that (referring to what was just mentioned)
steps.n: Added final ك to make it point to something specific that was just mentioned. In this ayah, it refers to how Allah can make the seemingly impossible happen, as mentioned in the previous statement.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added the U mark at the end to show it's the subject
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb

steps.m: he does/will do (subject of كَذٰلِكَ ٱللَّهُ - thus Allah)
steps.n: Changed to present/future tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on middle letter, and ُ at end. This word describes what Allah does, following the phrase 'thus Allah'
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'whatever' here. It's the object of the verb يَفْعَلُ (He does), showing that Allah does whatever He wills.
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding the vowel marks 'aa' and 'a'
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing the ending to ُ
Added long alif to show the action takes time or happens over time
Added short vowels (fatha) to make the past tense verb meaning 'he said'
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs

steps.m: to care for, nurture, raise up

steps.n: Root letters combined with a fatha and doubled middle letter to make 'Lord' - someone who nurtures and takes care of others

steps.m: O my Lord (addressing Allah in قَالَ / he said)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show both calling out to Lord (O Lord!) and possession (my Lord). This word appears in speech (قَالَ / he said) as Zakariya addresses Allah directly.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from 'he made' to 'make!' by removing vowels and adding a stop mark (sukun) on first letter - this makes it a command
Added helper alif at start to make it easier to pronounce the word starting with a sukun
This is a basic word that shows something is being done for a purpose or given to someone
Added ي at the end to show this is happening to or for me specifically
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning, showing this is definitely or surely for me
Changed the writing style of ي to ى with an alif mark (ٓ) on top. This doesn't change the meaning, just the way it looks
The root letters combine with special spelling because of the weak letter ي. The hamza (ء) is written separately with fatha.
Adding ة makes this refer to one specific sign or miracle

steps.m: a sign/miracle (that Zechariah asked Allah for)
steps.n: The ً (tanween fatha) shows this is the object of اجْعَل (make/appoint) in the phrase 'make for me a sign'
Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding vowel marks (fathas). This shows Zakariya did the action of speaking.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it has a fatha and comes between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic words with و as their middle root letter.

steps.m: to show up, appear, or become visible

steps.n: Adding ة at the end turns this into a noun meaning 'sign'
The words are the same - just written in two different but valid ways. Both mean 'a sign'
Added ُ to show this is the subject (doer) in the sentence
Added كَ to show the sign belongs to the person being spoken to. This word is the subject in the sentence after قَالَ (he said).
Combined the two separate particles أَنْ (that) and لَا (not) together
The ن merges with the ل creating a doubled ل (shown by the ّ). This is a common pronunciation rule when these letters come together.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action more intense or direct
Adding تُ changes 'he speaks' to 'you speak'
Root أنس became نَّاس (people) with shadda for emphasis. Like saying 'all the people'
Added ال to make it specific (like 'the' in English)

steps.m: the people (object of تُكَلِّمَ - speak to)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being spoken to in the phrase 'speak to the people'
Adding vowel marks to make the word readable
Adding ا makes it a special word for counting
Added ة to make it feminine form of three (used when counting feminine nouns)
Added fatha at end to show this is the object of the verb اجْعَل (make/set)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun form
Adding أ at the start is the first step in making this type of plural
Adding ا in the middle makes this a broken plural form meaning 'days'
Added tanween kasra to show this word belongs to the number 'three' before it
Starting with the two original particles that combined to form this word: إِن (if) + لَا (not)
The word إِلَّا is actually not derived from إِنْلَا. It's a separate word that means 'except'. The connection to تُكَلِّمَ is correct - it shows what is being excepted from the speaking.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it into a noun meaning a single gesture or sign

steps.m: by gesture only (excepting تُكَلِّمَ - you speak)
steps.n: The ًا ending turns the noun 'sign/gesture' into 'by gesture', showing how something is done - like saying 'I talked by gesture'
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he remembered' to 'you remember!' - becomes a command to one person
Added 'and' (و) at the start to connect this command with the previous one
Doubling the middle letter makes it stronger - shows complete mastery/lordship
Adding كَ shows this belongs to 'you'. This word is what is being remembered in the command 'remember'.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and show this is a past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters to make it into an adjective meaning 'much' or 'many'
Added ًا at the end to show this describes how the action (remembering Allah) should be done - frequently or abundantly

steps.m: to praise, glorify, or declare perfection

steps.n: Adding a shadda (double letter) makes it a strong command to praise Allah

steps.m: and praise/glorify intensively! (connected to وَٱذْكُر - and remember)
steps.n: Adding و connects this command with the previous command to remember/mention Allah
Adding shadda at end makes it mean 'time of evening'
ٱلْ at the start means 'the'

steps.m: in the evening (time when Zakariya should remember Allah)
steps.n: بِ adds meaning of 'in/at/by'. This word pairs with وَٱلْإِبْكٰرِ (and in the early morning) to show the times when Zakariya should remember Allah.
Changes from verb to noun meaning 'the act of doing something early'
Adds 'and' to connect with previous word
Added ٱلْ to make it specific

steps.m: and the early morning (after بِ meaning 'in/during')
steps.n: Added kasra at end because it comes after بِ in the phrase بِٱلْعَشِىِّ وَٱلْإِبْكٰرِ meaning 'in the evening and early morning'

steps.m: when (referring to past events in this ayah about when the angels spoke to Maryam)
steps.n: This is a time particle that refers to past events. In this ayah, it introduces the story of when the angels spoke to Maryam.
The و at the beginning connects this phrase to what came before it
Adding the basic vowel pattern for past tense verbs
Changed vowels and added ت at the end to make it mean 'she said' instead of 'he said'
Added kasra at the end to connect with the next word (المَلٰٓئِكَةُ - the angels)

steps.m: angel (one who has authority from Allah)
steps.n: Changed the root 'to own/rule' into 'angel' by adding the vowels fatha (َ) - someone given authority by Allah
The word for one angel changes its middle letters to make it plural (many angels)
Added ة to show this is a complete group of angels
Added ال to show these are specific angels. These angels are the ones doing the action of saying (قَالَتِ) in this verse.
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action of saying. The alif is written as ٰٓ which is just a writing style.
This is a proper name that comes from Hebrew/Aramaic meaning 'the exalted one'
The ُ shows this is the main subject being called out to

steps.m: O Mary! (being addressed by ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةُ - the angels)
steps.n: The يٰ is added at the beginning to call out to someone directly. In this ayah, the angels are calling out to Mary.

This is an emphasis particle that makes the next word (ٱللَّهَ) more important. It's like adding an exclamation mark or saying 'Listen!' before an important statement. It also makes ٱللَّهَ end in َ (fatha) instead of ُ (damma).
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' as a proper name. The first letter changed to ل and doubled to show this is THE one God.

Added shadda on ل to emphasize it's THE one God. Added fatha at end because Allah is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily) in this ayah. Changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.

steps.m: to select or choose something special

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Added اِ at start and ط after ص to show doing something for a special purpose. The و at the end changed to ى because it's at the end of the word.
Changed hamza to alif and added elongation mark (ٰ) to show long vowel sound
Added كِ at the end to show the action was done to 'you' (feminine singular, referring to Maryam)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda creates Form II intensive meaning
Added ك (ki) at the end to mean 'you' as the one being purified. Like saying 'purified' vs 'purified you'

steps.m: and he purified you intensely (connected to ٱصْطَفَىٰكِ)
steps.n: Adding conjunction 'and' to connect this with the previous verb ٱصْطَفَىٰكِ (he chose you)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added اِ at start and ط between first two letters to show doing something with effort. The ت changed to ط after ص for easier pronunciation. The final ا changed to ى because it's at the end.
Added وَ (and) at the beginning. The اِ changes to ٱ after وَ.
Added كِ at the end to show the action was done to 'you' (feminine). The ٰ mark shows the ى is pronounced long.
This is a preposition that shows something is higher than or above something else. In this ayah, it connects to نِسَآءِ (women) to show Mary was chosen above/over the women of all worlds.
Added an alif that shows the word ends with a long 'aa' sound. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.
Changes root word for 'woman' into plural 'women' by adding special pattern of vowels
Makes the middle alif longer to emphasize it's a large group
Added kasra at the end to show this word follows عَلَىٰ (upon) in the sentence
The root meaning 'to know' becomes a physical 'sign/mark' by adding fathas - like how a flag marks something
Adding alif between second and third root letters changes it from a 'sign' to mean the entire 'world'
The ين ending makes this plural and shows it's being affected by words before it
Adding ال makes it specific/definite. The alif is written as ٰ (dagger alif)
This is a proper name, likely of Hebrew origin
Added يٰ at the beginning to show someone is calling Mary. This is like saying 'Hey Mary!' in English.
Added ُ at the end to show Mary is being directly spoken to in this sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changes to command form by adding alif at start and changing vowels

steps.m: be devoted! (command to a female) (connected to يٰمَرْيَمُ)
steps.n: The ى (ya) at the end makes this a command for a female (Mary) instead of a male

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop gradually
lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to show complete mastery and care
Added لِ at the beginning to show direction or purpose
Added كِ at the end to show feminine possession ('your' for a female)

steps.m: to bow down with forehead touching ground

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from 'he bowed' to 'bow!' by adding u- at start and changing the middle vowel - this makes it a command
Added ي at the end to show we're speaking to a woman
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this command with the previous one in the verse. The command is specifically directed to Mary (مريم).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he bowed' to 'bow!' by adding alif at start and making it a command
Added ي at the end to make it a command for a female (like speaking to Maryam in this verse)

steps.m: and bow down! (command to a female)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to mean 'and', and changed initial alif to alif wasl (ٱ)
This is a preposition that shows doing something together with others. In this ayah, it shows Mary is told to bow down with those who bow down.
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he bowed'

steps.m: one who bows down in prayer
steps.n: Changed to active participle form showing who does the action
Added tanween dammah to make it indefinite

steps.m: the one who bows down in prayer
steps.n: Added definite article to specify a particular person/group

steps.m: those who bow down in prayer
steps.n: Added masculine plural ending to show multiple people

steps.m: those who bow down in prayer (with مَعَ)
steps.n: Added fatha to show this word comes after مَعَ (with)
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show extended distance
Adding لِ changes it to point to something far away

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (refers to the story being told)
steps.n: The كَ at the end makes it more specific and emphatic. In this ayah, it points to the story that will be told about Maryam.
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects 'that' (ذٰلِكَ) with 'news' (أَنۢبَآءِ) to mean 'that is from the news'
Root meaning 'news' becomes a single piece of news by adding the vowels
Changed from singular to plural by following the pattern أفعال (af'aal), a common way to make plurals in Arabic
Added kasra to show this word belongs to something after it

Changes from an action (being hidden) to a noun (hidden thing). This is part of the phrase 'news of the unseen' (أَنۢبَآءِ ٱلْغَيْبِ) referring to information about things that cannot be seen or known normally.
The ِ ending shows this word belongs to or describes أَنۢبَآءِ (news/information), making the phrase mean 'news of unseen things'

steps.m: of the unseen (things that cannot normally be known)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to all unseen things in general, not just some unseen things. In this ayah, it refers to information that could only be known through divine revelation.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The word changes from 'he revealed' to 'we reveal' by adding نُ at the start to mean 'we' and changing the vowels

steps.m: we reveal it (referring to أَنۢبَآءِ ٱلْغَيْبِ - news of the unseen)
steps.n: The هِ refers back to the news of the unseen mentioned earlier in the ayah
Basic preposition meaning direction or movement towards something
The ى changes to ي before adding a suffix
Added ك at the end to show this is directed to 'you' (Muhammad ﷺ in this verse)
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation. This word negates the verb كُنتَ (you were) in this ayah.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects the two statements about 'you were not there'.
Added vowels to connect the letters together and make it past tense
The middle letter و drops out and the first vowel changes to ُ. This is a common change in words with و in the middle.
Added تَ at the end to change from 'he' to 'you', and added a sukun (ْ) before it. In this ayah, Allah is addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ about something he wasn't present for.
This is a location word that shows being near or with something
Added sukun before attaching pronoun

steps.m: with them, at their place (when they were casting lots)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show this is happening with or near them. In this ayah, it refers to being present when they were casting lots to see who would take care of Maryam.
This is a time word that shows when something happened in the past. In this ayah, it tells us when 'they cast their pens' happened.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at the beginning to show this happens in the present/future
Changed vowels to make it passive - something is being thrown rather than throwing

steps.m: they throw (the priests throw their pens)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show multiple people are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to the priests throwing their pens to decide who would take care of Maryam.
Adding vowels changes this into a noun meaning the tool used for writing
The word changes to show many pens
The word gets a fatHa (َ) at the end to show it's being acted on
Adds attached pronoun هُمْ meaning 'their' (masculine plural). This word is the object of يُلْقُونَ (they cast/throw) in the ayah.
Connected the letters and doubled the ي with shadda to make the question word stronger
Added damma to show this is the subject of يَكْفُلُ (takes care of)
Added هُمْ to show we're asking which one from their group. This word asks which of them will take care of Maryam.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he takes care of (referring to who would take care of مَرْيَمَ)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, damma on second root letter, and damma at end. This word is about who would be responsible for taking care of Maryam.

steps.m: Mary (the one they were competing to take care of)
steps.n: This is a proper name that comes from ancient Hebrew/Aramaic. In this ayah, it refers to Mary, the mother of Jesus, when the priests were drawing lots to see who would be her guardian.

steps.m: Mary (the object of يَكْفُلُ - who would take care of)
steps.n: The َ (fatha) at the end shows Mary is the one being taken care of in يَكْفُلُ (who would take care of)

This is a particle word that doesn't come from a root. It can mean 'what' when asking questions, or 'that which' when connecting ideas. In this ayah, it's used with كنت (you were) to say 'you were not'.

steps.m: and what/that which (connects to كنت - you were)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. Together with كنت it forms the phrase 'and you were not'.
Added fathas (َ) after each letter to make the basic past tense form
Changed to command form by putting 'u' sound on first letter and quiet mark on second letter, which makes the middle letter drop out

steps.m: you were (when they cast their pens)
steps.n: Added تَ at the end to show 'you' (masculine singular) did this action in the past. This word refers to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ not being present when the priests cast lots with their pens to see who would take care of Maryam.
This is a basic word showing location or being close to something
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for adding 'them' at the end

steps.m: with them (referring to the people who were casting lots for Mary)
steps.n: Adding هِمْ shows this is happening with or near them. In this ayah, it refers to the people who were casting lots to see who would take care of Mary.
This is the basic form of the word without any vowel marks. It's a particle that points to a specific time in the past.
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced: kasra (ِ) under the إ and sukun (ْ) on the ذ
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he disputes with others/argues back and forth
steps.n: Adding ت shows the arguing is going back and forth between people
The suffix ونَ shows multiple people are involved in the arguing
This is a time word that connects to the past event of the angels speaking. It helps tell the story by showing when something happened in the past.
Added short vowels 'a' to make it mean 'he said' in the past
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ by replacing the weak letter و with ا
Added ت to show the subject is feminine (the angels)
Added kasra at the end to help connect with the next word
Added vowel marks (fatha) between the root letters to make the word mean 'angel' instead of 'to rule'
Changed internal word structure to show multiple angels using a special plural pattern
Added ة to show this is a collective group of angels
Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it's doing the action of saying in this ayah
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the name correctly

steps.m: O Mary! (being called by the angels)
steps.n: Added يٰ at the beginning to show someone is calling Mary's name. The angels are calling to her to give her news.

steps.m: O Mary! (being called by the angels)
steps.n: Added ُ at the end to show Mary is the main person being addressed in this part of the sentence

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizes that اللَّهَ is coming next)
steps.n: This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain. It also causes the next word (اللَّهَ) to end in َ instead of ُ
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, making this the unique name for The One True God
Added fatha at the end because Allah is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily). Changed alif to wasl-alif for pronunciation. The shadda shows emphasis on the L sound.
Added shadda to make the meaning stronger and more intensive
Changes from past to present tense by adding يُ at the start - both mean 'give good news' but one is past and one is ongoing/present

steps.m: he gives you (feminine) good news (referring to what the angels said to Mary)
steps.n: Adding feminine singular object pronoun كِ to show the good news is being given to Mary. This connects to يٰمَرْيَمُ (O Mary) earlier in the verse.
Adding vowels to make a basic past tense verb meaning 'he spoke'
Added ة to make it into a noun representing one instance of speech

steps.m: with/by a word (object of يُبَشِّرُكِ - gives you good news)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to mean 'with/by' and tanween kasra at end to show indefiniteness. This word explains how Allah gives the good news to Mary.
This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or someone
Adding هُ shows we're talking about a male person
The letter ن merged into م with a shadda, making it easier to say. This happens when these letters come together.
Root س-م-و becomes the noun 'name' (ٱسْمُ). The ٱ is part of the word, not for making it definite.
Added هُ to show possession. This word is the subject in the sentence 'His name is the Messiah'
Forms the word for 'Messiah' by showing someone who has been anointed (wiped with oil in a special ceremony)
Adding ال makes this a specific title for Jesus (عِيسَى), who is mentioned right after this word in the ayah
