Added 'u' sound at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of the sentence

steps.m: Jesus (the subject of what follows in the ayah)
steps.n: This is a proper name that doesn't come from an Arabic root. It's the name of the prophet Jesus, peace be upon him. In this ayah, he is being mentioned as the one who will be honored in this world and the hereafter.

steps.m: to construct or build (especially family relationships)

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning someone who continues the family line
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject (doer) in the sentence
Added ٱ at start to help with pronunciation
This is a proper name that doesn't come from an Arabic root. In this ayah, Mary is being directly addressed by the angels.
The fatha at the end shows this is the object of the angels' address
Added ي between ج and ه with vowels to create an adjective meaning someone with strong qualities. This describes Jesus (عِيسَى) in the ayah.

steps.m: noble, eminent (as a description of عِيسَى in the sentence)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is describing Jesus in the sentence structure
This is a preposition that shows Jesus has high status in/within the worldly life. It connects عِيسَى (Jesus) to الدُّنْيَا (the worldly life) in this ayah.
The root letters form a noun meaning 'nearest/closest' to describe this life. The last letter changes to make it easier to say.
Makes the noun definite. The ل in ال causes the د to double (called a sun letter)
Adding vowels changes it from a root to an adjective meaning 'late'
Adding ة makes this word feminine, like changing 'late' to refer to a feminine noun
Added 'the' (ال) to turn 'last/final' into 'the Hereafter' - the special name for life after death
Added و to connect this with الدُّنْيَا (this world) mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: and the Hereafter (in this world and the Hereafter)
steps.n: Added kasra because this word follows فِى (in) from earlier in the verse, showing Jesus's honor exists in both realms
This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to a group
Changed the ending to make it flow better with the next word (المقربين)
Adding وَ (and) at the start connects this word to what came before, joining two descriptions together
Changes from verb 'to come near' to 'one who brings near'
Makes the word plural - more than one person who brings near
Makes it specific - 'the' ones who bring near

steps.m: the ones who bring near (in accusative case)
steps.n: Shows this word is the object in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Shadda doubles middle root letter to intensify meaning

steps.m: and he speaks intensively/repeatedly (to ٱلنَّاسَ - the people)
to be social or friendly with others

steps.n: Root meaning 'to be social' becomes a noun for 'people' by dropping the first letter and adding a long 'aa' sound
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning of 'people' as a collective group
Added ال to specify these particular people being talked about

steps.m: the people (as object of يُكَلِّمُ - he speaks to)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of speaking in the phrase 'he speaks to the people'
This is a helper word that shows where something happens. Here it shows Jesus speaking while in the cradle.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it's at the end of a word. This doesn't change the meaning, it's just how we write it.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and form the noun 'cradle' - a place prepared for comfort
Added the definite article 'the'

steps.m: in the cradle (location where يُكَلِّمُ/he speaks)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it comes after فِى (in). This shows where Jesus speaks to people.
Changed verb into noun for a person with this quality

steps.m: a middle-aged person (object of يُكَلِّمُ - speaks to)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon in the phrase 'speaks to people as a middle-aged person'

steps.m: and a middle-aged person (object of يُكَلِّمُ - speaks to)
steps.n: Added و to connect this to the previous descriptions of Jesus speaking to people
This is a preposition that shows the starting point or source. It doesn't come from a root.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Added fatha at the end because this word connects to الصَّالِحِينَ (the righteous ones) that comes after it

steps.m: to make something good or right

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed the word to describe someone who has this quality
Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the first letter
Changed to plural form to show we're talking about multiple righteous people. This comes after the word مِنَ (from/among) in the ayah.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is in the accusative case, which often happens after certain words like مِنَ
Added fathas and the weak letter و changed to ا, making it past tense
Added ت with sukun to show the subject is feminine (Maryam in this case)

steps.m: to be a lord/master, to nurture

steps.n: Doubling of middle letter ب intensifies meaning
Adding kasra at end makes it into a call/address to Lord
Added fatha and shadda to strengthen the questioning meaning
These are actually two different words - أَنَّى is a question word meaning 'how', while أَنَّ means 'that/indeed'
Added alif khanjariya (ٰ) which is a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning. This word is questioning the verb يَكُونُ (it will be) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb

steps.m: he is/becomes (part of كُن فَيَكُونُ - 'Be! And it becomes')
steps.n: Changes to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This word appears in the phrase 'Be! And it becomes' (كُن فَيَكُونُ) showing how Allah's command instantly causes things to exist.
Added kasra (ِ) to show this means 'for' or 'to'
Added ى at the end to show this means 'me'. This word connects to يَكُونُ (it will be) in Maryam's question about how she will have a child.
Added fathas to make the root into a noun meaning 'child'
Added tanween damma to show this is any child in general. This word is the subject of يَكُونُ (to be) in Maryam's question about how she could have a child.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it past tense. It's like adding 'did not' in English.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement about having a child.

steps.m: to touch, feel, or make physical contact

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense and changed vowels to show ongoing action

steps.m: he touches me (in response to وَلَمْ - and not)
steps.n: Added نِى at the end to show the action is happening to 'me'. This is part of Maryam saying that no man has touched her.
Adding fatha vowels creates the basic noun form meaning 'human being'
Adding tanween (double damma) shows this is indefinite - meaning 'a human being' rather than 'the human being'
Added fathas to make it past tense. This word is followed by كَذٰلِكِ (like that) as Allah's response to Maryam.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in middle)
Combines كَ (like) with ذَا (this) to form the basic demonstrative phrase
The alif is written as a small alif (ٰ) above the ذ when more letters are added after it
Adds لِ to extend the demonstrative meaning from 'this' to 'that'
Adds كِ at the end to show this is being said to a female (Maryam in this case)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This shows Allah's ongoing power to create. The word is part of the phrase 'Allah creates what He wills.'
This is a special word that connects to the verb after it (يَشَآءُ) to show that Allah creates whatever He wills. It's like saying 'what' or 'whatever' in English.
Added short vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he wanted'
The weak letter ي changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs with ي in the middle
Changed from past tense (he willed) to present tense (he wills) by adding يَ at the start
Adding long alif makes the word stronger/more emphatic
This is the basic form of the word that refers to a point in time
Adding ا at the end makes it refer to something that could happen multiple times or in the future. In this ayah, it refers to whenever Allah decrees something.

steps.m: to decide, decree, or settle a matter

steps.n: Added fatha vowels to make it past tense. This word is connected to يَقُولُ (he says) later in the ayah, showing what happened before he speaks.
The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end of the word - this is a common pattern for verbs ending in ي
Added alif khanjariyah (small alif) and a pause mark to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'command' or 'matter'. This word is what is being decreed in the ayah.
Added tanween alif to show this is receiving the action of decreeing. In the ayah, Allah decrees a command/matter and it happens.
This is the base emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before

steps.m: then only/so surely (connects to يَقُولُ - He says)
steps.n: Added مَا at the end which restricts the meaning to 'only'. This word connects to يَقُولُ (He says) in the next part of the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense, and changed vowels to show ongoing action. The و stays because it's part of the root.
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced
Added هُ which means 'him' to show who the action is for

steps.m: to/for him (subject of يَقُولُ - he says)
steps.n: Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a pronunciation helper. This word shows who the saying is directed to in the phrase 'He says to him'.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels

steps.m: be! (command from Allah in response to قَالَ)
steps.n: Changed to command form by adding damma on first letter. The middle weak letter و drops out in command form, which is common with hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle). This is Allah's direct command that creates existence, showing His absolute power.
Past tense form meaning 'he was' - basic 3 letter past tense pattern
Changed to present tense form. This word is the result of Allah's command كُن (be!) mentioned earlier in the verse.
The فَ at the start means 'and so' or 'then', showing what happens right after something else
Doubling the middle letter makes it mean 'cause someone to know' = 'teach'
Adding يُ at the start changes it from 'taught' to 'being taught' (passive voice)
The kasra (ِ) vowel changes it from passive to active voice - now means 'to teach' rather than 'being taught'
The damma (ُ) at the end shows this is in the normal indicative mood

steps.m: he teaches him (what is taught: الكتاب/the book)
steps.n: Adding هُ shows who is being taught

steps.m: and he teaches him (what is taught: الكتاب/the book)
steps.n: Adding conjunction prefix
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and show this is a past-tense verb
Changed the vowel pattern and added alif to transform the verb into a noun for the thing that results from writing
Added the definite article ال with proper pronunciation marks

steps.m: the book (object being taught in يُعَلِّمُهُ - he teaches him)
steps.n:
1. Changed اَلْ to ٱلْ (a common writing variation)
2. Changed اَ to ٰ (another writing variation)
3. Added َ (fatha) at the end to show this is the object being taught
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and form a noun
Adding ة makes it refer to a specific wisdom or piece of wisdom
Added definite article to specify this particular wisdom
Added conjunction prefix and final fatha to show this is the object being taught in the phrase 'and He teaches him'

steps.m: Torah (the holy book given to Moses)
steps.n: This is a borrowed word from Hebrew, so it doesn't have an Arabic root

steps.m: Torah (the holy book given to Moses)
steps.n: Added vowel marks and elongation sign to show correct pronunciation
Added the definite article to specify this particular holy book
Added conjunction و to connect with the previous words (الكتاب والحكمة) in the ayah

steps.m: and the Torah (object of يُعَلِّمُهُ - teaches him)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object (thing being taught) in the phrase 'teaches him the Torah'

steps.m: Gospel (the holy book revealed to Prophet Jesus)
steps.n: This is a borrowed word from Greek 'evangelion', so it doesn't have an Arabic root
Added ال to show we're talking about the specific holy book
Added و to connect this with the previous holy books mentioned (the Book, the Wisdom, and the Torah)

steps.m: and the Gospel (object of يُعَلِّمُهُ - he teaches him)
steps.n: Added marks to show how to pronounce it, with a fatha (a-sound) at the end because it's receiving the action of teaching
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changes verb into noun for someone who does this action regularly

steps.m: a messenger (object of جِئْتُكُم - I came to you)
steps.n: Shows this word is receiving the action of the verb 'I came to you' in the verse

steps.m: and a messenger (object of جِئْتُكُم - I came to you)
steps.n: Adds conjunction 'and' to connect to previous phrase
This is a preposition that shows Jesus being sent to the Children of Israel. It connects the sender with the destination.
Added an alif symbol (ٰ) to show the long vowel sound at the end. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.
This root developed to mean establishing a family through sons/children
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
These are two different words - 'banaa' means 'he built' while 'bani' means 'children/descendants of'. They just look similar but aren't related.
The vowel pattern بَنِي is fixed for this word meaning 'children/descendants of'

steps.m: Israel (a name meaning 'one who struggles with God')
steps.n: This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew (ישראל/Yisrael). It combines two Hebrew words: ישרה (struggled/persevered) and אל (God). In the Quran, it refers to Prophet Yaqub (Jacob) who was given this name, and also to his descendants (Children of Israel/Bani Israel).
This is the basic emphasis particle that makes statements stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'verily' in English
The word changes from إِنَّ to أَنَّ when it comes after another word, like when saying 'I know that...' instead of starting a new sentence
Added ي at the end to show this is about 'I' (referring to عِيسَى speaking). The ي gets a kasra (ِ ) because it comes before another word.

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizing جِئْتُكُم - I have come to you)
steps.n: This is a special word that emphasizes that something has definitely happened or is definitely true. Here it emphasizes that Jesus definitely came to the Children of Israel with signs.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding تُ at the end makes it mean 'I' did the action
Adding كُم shows the action was done to 'you all' (plural)
Adding sukun at the end helps with pronunciation when stopping

steps.m: to show, to be clear, to be evident

steps.n: Adding ة at the end turns this into a noun meaning 'sign'
The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this is indefinite ('a' instead of 'the') and follows the verb جِئْتُكُم (I have come to you)

steps.m: with a sign (that proves my message)
steps.n: The بِ shows this sign is the means by which Jesus proves his message to the Children of Israel
Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what something is made of
Added shadda (ّ) to show strong connection with the next word. Here it connects 'from' with 'your Lord' to mean 'from your Lord'
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning more intense and turns it into a noun
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes before it (مِّن)

steps.m: your (plural) lord (of عِيسَى speaking to بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ)
steps.n: Added plural 'your' ending كُمْ because Jesus is speaking to all the Children of Israel
This is a basic connecting particle that introduces a new statement or clause
Added ي with elongation (ىٓ) at the end to show first person ('I'). The kasra (ِ) before ي is added for pronunciation. This connects to what Jesus says next in the ayah.

steps.m: to create or form something new

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed vowels to show present tense - sukun on first letter, damma on second and last letters
Added أَ at the beginning to show 'I' am doing the action
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for you all (object of أَخْلُقُ - I create)
steps.n: Added كم at the end to show this is directed to multiple people. This word is connected to the verb أَخْلُقُ (I create) showing who the creation is for.
Basic preposition showing something comes from or originates from something else. Here it connects to الطِّينِ (clay) to show what Jesus makes the bird figure from.
Added shadda on م for easier pronunciation when connecting with previous word
Adding the kasra vowel mark turns this into a proper noun meaning clay
Added ال to make it specific. The ل combines with ط to make it doubled (shown by ّ)
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to the word before it (مِّنَ meaning "from")
Root letters connected with a fat-ha (a) and sukoon (stop mark) to make the command 'shape!'
Added ة to show it's one specific instance, and the hamza changes form
Added ك with fatha to mean 'like/similar to'
Added kasra at the end to show connection to the next word (الطَّيْرِ/the bird)
Adding vowels changes verb 'to fly' into noun 'bird'
Adding the definite article ال makes the noun definite, changing it from 'a bird' to 'the bird'
Adding kasra shows this word is possessed by كَهَيْـَٔةِ (shape/form of)

steps.m: to blow air or breathe into something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
Changed to present tense first person (I) form by adding أَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: then I blow (connected to أَخْلُقُ - I create)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this happens after the creating mentioned before it in the verse
This is a preposition that doesn't come from a root
Added the kasra vowel mark to show proper pronunciation

steps.m: in it (referring to the clay bird shape that Jesus blows into)
steps.n: Added هِ to show what is being blown into. This connects to the clay bird shape mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added basic vowels to connect letters and show basic noun form

steps.m: it becomes (result of فَأَنفُخُ - I blow)
steps.n: Present tense prefix يَ shows ongoing/future action. This describes what happens after Jesus blows into the clay bird.

steps.m: and then it becomes (result of فَأَنفُخُ - I blow)
steps.n: فَ shows this is result/consequence of previous action

steps.m: to fly or move through air

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create the noun for a flying creature

steps.m: a bird (that will become/transform into something real by God's permission)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is the thing being transformed through يَكُونُ (becomes). This connects to the story of Jesus making birds from clay with Allah's permission.
Changed from a verb meaning 'to allow' into a noun meaning 'permission' by adding vowel marks
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is done 'by means of' permission. This word explains how the bird comes to life in يَكُونُ (it becomes)
The word gets a kasra (small diagonal line) at the end because it comes after the word بِ (meaning 'by/with')
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' or 'something worshipped'
Added ال and merged it with the word, changing the first letter. The shadda shows emphasis. This makes it a proper name for The One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word before it (بِإِذْنِ meaning 'by permission'). Together they mean 'by permission of Allah'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: I cause to be cured/I heal
steps.n: Changed from 'he cured' to 'I cause to cure/heal' by adding letters that make it more active

steps.m: and I cure (connected to أَخْلُقُ - I create)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this action with the previous one (creating) in the verse
Added vowels (fatha-kasra-fatha) to make it mean 'he was blind from birth'
Adding أ at start turns the verb into an adjective meaning 'one who is born blind'

steps.m: the one who is born blind (object of وَأُبْرِئُ - and I cure)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and fatha to show it's the object being cured

steps.m: to have a skin condition with white patches
and person with white patches on skin (leper)
steps.n: Adds 'wa' (and) at start + changes the root into a noun meaning 'person with this condition', connecting it to 'I heal the blind' before it
Makes it refer to a specific person with the condition (the one being healed)

steps.m: and the person with white patches (object of أُبْرِئُ - I heal)
steps.n: The و (and) was removed, and the word is now the object (the one being healed)
Changes to Form IV pattern to mean 'cause to live/make alive'
Added و at beginning to connect this action with previous statements about Jesus's miracles
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a noun from the basic meaning
Added ى at the end to make it refer to multiple dead things/people

steps.m: the dead ones (object of أُحْىِ - I give life to)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and added the small alif (ٰ) at the end. This word is what receives the action of 'giving life' in the verse.
The root letters أذن became the noun 'permission' by adding the vowel marks
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'by/with' and ِ at the end because it's connected to the word اللَّهِ (of Allah) that comes after it
Added shadda to emphasize the L sound, showing the specialness of this name
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word
Basic past tense verb pattern - added vowels to root letters to make 'he told'
Adding a shadda (doubled middle letter) makes the action more intense - from simply telling news to thoroughly informing
Adding أُ at the start makes it 'I' do the action
Added 'kum' at the end to mean 'you all' as the object (who is being informed)
This is a basic interrogative particle meaning 'what'
Added the preposition بِ (with/by) to show how something is done. In this ayah, it connects to تَأْكُلُونَ (you eat) to show 'with what you eat'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense with ت for 'you' and adjusted vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are eating
Starting with the basic interrogative particle that means 'what'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with the previous statement about Jesus telling people what they eat

steps.m: to store up or save for later use

steps.n: Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added form VIII pattern which shows doing something for oneself
The ذ and ت combine to become دّ (with shadda) due to a phonetic rule
Changed to present tense with 'you' as subject
Added plural 'you all' ending
This is a basic preposition that shows something is located inside or within something else. Here it shows that something is located inside 'your houses' (بُيُوتِكُمْ).
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the singular noun
Changes from one house to many houses by adding و and changing vowels to make it plural
Added kasra to prepare for possession
Added second person plural possessive pronoun suffix
Adding the shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional word meaning 'if' to an emphasis word meaning 'indeed'. The shadda makes the ن sound stronger to show the stronger meaning.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it's showing that something is happening inside the houses.
Starting with the basic demonstrative pronoun ذا which points to something nearby. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) called an overbar.
Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far away

steps.m: that (pointing to all the signs and miracles mentioned before)
steps.n: Adding كَ makes it specifically refer to something being discussed or mentioned. Here it points back to the miracles Jesus performed that were just mentioned.
Basic preposition meaning 'for' or 'belonging to'
Adding ة makes this a feminine noun meaning 'a sign'
Adding ل at start adds emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'indeed'
Adds tanween (double fatha) to show this is the object of إِنَّ in this ayah
Basic preposition showing direction or purpose
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic
Added كُمْ to show this is directed at multiple people being spoken to

steps.m: if (introduces condition for كُنتُم - you were)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that connects two ideas - here it connects 'if you were believers' with the signs mentioned before. It's like saying 'these are signs for you, if you are believers'
Added fathas to connect the letters and make the past tense form
Changed from past tense 'he was' to a simpler command form meaning 'be!'
Added تُم at the end to show this is talking about multiple people being addressed ('you all')
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling secure
Changed from a past action (trusted) to describe a person who believes/trusts
Added ين to show many believers. The ّ (shadda) on م shows it connects to the previous word when read. This word is the subject of كُنتُم (you were) in 'if you were believers'.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Added مُ prefix to show who does the action and doubled the middle letter (د) to show intensity/emphasis
Added ًا to make it describe what something is doing (like 'as a confirmer' or 'confirming')

steps.m: and verifying/confirming (what came before in the Torah)
steps.n: Added و meaning 'and' to connect this to previous statements. This word describes how Jesus confirms the previous scripture.
Added ل at the beginning to show purpose or direction, like adding 'for' or 'to' in English
Added shadda on the ل to add emphasis to the meaning
Adding vowels changes this into the common word meaning 'between'

steps.m: to extend/reach out with the hand

steps.n: The root letters form the word 'hand' - not a transformation from verb to noun
Added a fatha (small line above) at the end to show it's being used as an object
Added ى and shadda at the end to show it means 'my two hands' instead of just 'hand'
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or is part of something
Added fatha (َ) at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word التَّوْرَىٰةِ (the Torah)

steps.m: Torah (the holy book given to Prophet Moses)
steps.n: This is the base form of the word Torah, a proper noun for the holy book given to Prophet Moses. Since it's a borrowed word, it doesn't come from an Arabic root.

steps.m: Torah (the holy book given to Prophet Moses)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: fatha on ت, sukun on و
Added ٱل (the) at the beginning, shadda on ت for emphasis, and kasra at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from) in the ayah
Adding vowel marks to show it's a complete past tense verb
Changed to passive voice - something was made permissible by someone else
Added لِ (li-) at the start to mean 'so that' or 'in order to'

steps.m: and so that I make lawful
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this clause with previous statement
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for you all (receiving what is being made permissible in أُحِلَّ)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show this is directed to multiple people. This word connects to أُحِلَّ (I make permissible) to show who receives the benefit of the action.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of لِأُحِلَّ (to make lawful). It means 'to make lawful for you some of that which...'
Base demonstrative word that points to something
Adding ٱلْ changes this from pointing to something to connecting two parts of a sentence. The ل merges with ذ to make ّذ. This word connects to حُرِّمَ (was forbidden) in the next part of the verse.

steps.m: to prevent or make something off-limits

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that something received the action instead of doing it
Added shadda to make the meaning stronger and more specific - changing from general prevention to complete forbiddance
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
When adding a suffix, the ى changes to يْ to help with pronunciation
Added كُمْ at the end to show this affects multiple people being spoken to
Added basic past tense form with fatha vowels

steps.m: and I came to you all
to show, make clear, or be evident

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a noun meaning 'sign' by adding vowels and the feminine ending ة
Added a long alif at the start to strengthen the word's meaning
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'with'

steps.m: with a sign/miracle (object of جِئْتُكُم - I have come to you with)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is indefinite and follows the preposition بِ. The word is the object of جِئْتُكُم (I have come to you)
Basic form of the word that shows something's source or origin
The shadda (ّ) on م adds emphasis to show this is definitely or specifically from something (in this case, from رَّبِّكُمْ - your Lord)
Doubling the middle letter makes it stronger - shows complete/intense nurturing
The double letter and kasra (small line below) shows this is 'my Lord' or 'Lord of' something
The كُمْ at the end shows this Lord belongs to all the people being spoken to

steps.m: to be mindful and protect oneself
steps.n: The و at the start changes to ت and combines with another ت to make a stronger ت sound (with ّ). This shows doing something carefully for your own protection.

steps.m: be mindful and protect yourselves! (command to a group)
steps.n: Adding وا at the end makes it a command to many people, like saying "all of you do this!"

steps.m: so be mindful and protect yourselves! (command to a group)
steps.n: Adding فَ at the start shows this is a result or response to what came before
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that refers specifically to the One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object of فَٱتَّقُوا۟ - so fear/be mindful of)
steps.n: Added the fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being acted upon in this sentence. The word comes after فَٱتَّقُوا۟ (so fear/be mindful of) which requires its object to take fatha.
Changed from root letters to a command form by adding kasra (short i sound) between first two letters
Added hamza with fatha at beginning to make it a command
Added و to make it plural command form
Added نِ to show the command is to obey 'me'
Added وَ (and) to connect this command with previous statement
This is the basic form of the emphasis particle that tells us to pay special attention to what comes next

steps.m: indeed/truly (emphasizing what comes after: اللَّهَ رَبِّى - Allah is my Lord)
steps.n: The shadda makes the word stronger and shows it will affect the next word's ending
Changed the root letters into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel marks

steps.m: Allah (the one true God who is being worshipped)
steps.n: Added ال (the) at the start and doubled the ل with a shadda to make it mean THE one true God. The َ (fatha) at the end shows this is the object being acted upon in the sentence.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: one who cares for others completely
steps.n: The doubled letter shows this is someone's main quality or role

steps.m: lord (ready to be connected to something)
steps.n: The small i-sound (kasra) at the end shows this word will be connected to something after it, like saying 'Lord of...'
Adding ى at the end shows this is 'my Lord'. This word appears in the phrase 'Indeed, Allah is my Lord' (إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ رَبِّى)

steps.m: to care for, raise, and help develop/grow
one who cares for and develops completely
steps.n: The doubled ب shows this person does a lot of caring and developing

steps.m: one who cares for and develops completely (subject of إِنَّ)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is connected to إِنَّ (indeed/truly) in this ayah

steps.m: your (all of you) complete caretaker and developer (subject of إِنَّ)
steps.n: The word رَبُّ (Lord/caretaker) gets كُمْ added to show it's your Lord - like adding 'your' in English

steps.m: and your (all of you) complete caretaker and developer (subject of إِنَّ)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement

steps.m: to worship, serve, or obey completely

steps.n: Changes root into a command telling one person to worship (no alif prefix needed)
Adds plural masculine marker to make command apply to multiple people

steps.m: worship Him! (command to all of you)
steps.n: Adds هُ to show who should be worshipped (referring to Allah mentioned in previous part of verse)

steps.m: so worship Him! (because He is your Lord, as mentioned before)
steps.n: Added فَ (meaning 'so/therefore') and ٱ (helping letter) to connect this command with the previous statement about Allah being 'your Lord'
This is the basic attention-getting part that starts many pointing words
The alif is written above the ه as a small mark (ٰ) in this special word
The ذَ shows we're pointing to something specific
The ا at the end shows we're pointing to something masculine and singular
Adding the basic vowels to make the word readable
The alif with a line over it (ٰ) shows a long 'aa' sound
The double-damma (ٌ) makes this indefinite (like 'a' in English) and shows it's the subject being described by the word مُّسْتَقِيمٌ (straight)
Added vowels to connect the root letters

steps.m: one who seeks to be upright
steps.n: Added 'must-' pattern to show someone trying to be/do something + 'one who' ending
The middle letter و changes to ي, making it easier to say. This is a common change in Arabic words to make them flow better when speaking.
Added emphasis with shadda to show completeness of the quality. This word describes the 'path' (صِرٰطٌ) mentioned in the ayah.
Starting with the basic particle that shows something coming together or happening
Adding ا turns this into a different word entirely - they are not related. لَمَّا means 'when' and is used for timing, while لَمَّ means to gather/join
Adding فَ at the beginning shows this follows something else that happened. The آ at the end makes the word pause dramatically.
Adding vowel marks makes this a past tense verb meaning 'to sense'
Added أَ at the beginning to show Jesus actively noticed something. This form often makes actions more direct or intentional.
This is a proper noun (name) that comes from the Aramaic name Yeshua/Eesa. In this ayah, Jesus is the one who perceived/felt something from the disbelievers.
Basic preposition meaning 'from' - shows where something starts or comes from

steps.m: from them (the disbelievers mentioned in الْكُفْرَ)
steps.n: Added the pronoun 'them' (هُمُ) to show who the action is happening to. In this ayah, it refers back to the disbelievers mentioned in الْكُفْرَ.
Changed from a verb (doing something) into a noun (thing/concept) by adding the vowel marks ُ and ْ
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to point to a specific rejection, like saying "the" in English
Added َ at the end because this word is being acted upon by the verb أَحَسَّ (he perceived/felt)
Added fathas to make it past tense and show who did the action
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to say, but doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: who (asking about who will be عِيسَىٰ's helpers)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask about people. In this ayah, عِيسَىٰ (Jesus) is asking who will be his helpers in calling to Allah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (helped) to a noun meaning someone who helps
Changed to plural form to show multiple helpers
Added kasra to show this word belongs to or is connected to something
Added ى at the end to show these are 'my' helpers
This is a preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to اللَّهِ (Allah) to show 'to Allah'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to the One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show possession - this word is possessed by أَنصَارُ (helpers) in the phrase 'helpers of Allah'
Added short vowels (fatha) to make it mean 'he said' in the past tense

steps.m: he said (introducing what عِيسَىٰ says next)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb. This is a common pattern in Arabic that makes the word easier to pronounce. In this ayah, this word introduces what عِيسَىٰ (Jesus) says next.

steps.m: to be pure white, to return

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic word
Changed to plural form by adding ا after second root letter and ِ at end
Added ي (ya) with shadda at the end to mean 'one who is devoted to' - like changing 'follower' to 'devoted follower'
Added plural ending to show multiple disciples

steps.m: the devoted disciples (who responded to عِيسَىٰ)
steps.n: Added the definite article to specify these particular disciples of Jesus
This is the base form of the personal pronoun meaning 'we'. It doesn't come from a root since it's a fundamental pronoun.
Added نُ at the end to complete the pronoun. This word is the subject (doer) of قَالَ (said) in this ayah, showing who is speaking.
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it 'he helped' in the past
Active participle form meaning 'one who does the action'
Changed to broken plural form by adding أَ at start and modifying internal vowels
Added damma at end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Changed to the proper name for the One God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at end to show this is the object of the word إِلَى (to). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since this is how Allah is always written.
Adding alif makes it mean 'to actively put trust in something'

steps.m: we believed (said by ٱلْحَوَارِيُّونَ - the disciples)
steps.n: Added نَّا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. In this ayah, 'we' refers to the disciples (ٱلْحَوَارِيُّونَ) speaking to Jesus
This is the base word for Allah, referring to the concept of divinity
Adding the shadda on ل makes this word specifically refer to Allah, the one true God
The kasra at the end shows this word is being acted upon by something
The بِ at the beginning shows how something is done or the means by which it happens
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he witnessed' to 'witness!' by adding ا at start and changing vowels to make it a command
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Adding ا shows this is about 'we/us'

steps.m: in that we (connected to ءَامَنَّا - we believed)
steps.n: Adding بِ connects this to the previous word ءَامَنَّا (we believed), showing what we believed in

steps.m: to submit, surrender, be at peace

Added أ at the beginning changes it from 'being peaceful' to 'actively making peace/submitting' - like the difference between 'being safe' and 'making yourself safe'
Changed to show the person who does the action rather than the action itself
Added ون to show this refers to multiple people
Doubling the middle letter ب with shadda makes the meaning more intense, showing complete mastery and lordship
Added نَا at the end to show we are talking about our Lord. This is part of a prayer where believers are speaking to Allah.
The extra alif (آ) shows we are calling out to Allah in prayer
Adding alif makes it mean 'to actively put trust in something'
Added shadda to emphasize the strength of belief
Added نا at the end to show we are the ones who believed. This is the main verb that comes after رَبَّنَآ (Our Lord) in the ayah.

steps.m: with what (connected to ءَامَنَّا - we believed)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this is about believing 'with/in' something. This connects to the verb ءَامَنَّا (we believed) earlier in the verse.

steps.m: with what (connected to ءَامَنَّا - we believed)
steps.n: Added an alif at the end to show emphasis and elongation in pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The أَ prefix changes the meaning to make someone/something else do the action

steps.m: you sent down (referring to what was revealed by Allah)
steps.n: The تَ suffix shows that 'you' did this action in the past. In this ayah, the believers are speaking to Allah about what He revealed.

steps.m: to follow closely or walk behind

Added ا at start and merged two ت letters together with shadda to show doing something with extra effort or purpose
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action, and changed the last vowel to sukun

steps.m: and we followed intensely/deliberately (connected to ءَامَنَّا - we believed)
