Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يُ with تُ
Added plural marker to show multiple people
This is a basic word that means 'except'. In this ayah, it connects to the previous phrase وَلَا تُؤْمِنُوٓا۟ (and do not believe) to say who should be excepted from that command.
This is a relative pronoun that refers to people. In this ayah, it's the subject of the verb تَبِعَ (followed)

Added لِ at the beginning to show direction or purpose. This connects to the previous words وَلَا تُؤْمِنُوٓا۟ إِلَّا (and do not believe except), completing the meaning as 'and do not believe except in/for whoever...'

steps.m: to follow or go after something/someone

steps.n: Adding vowels to make a basic past tense verb meaning 'to follow'

steps.m: to follow a way of life
steps.n: This root represents the basic concept of following a path or way of life
Added vowel marks to create the noun meaning 'religion' or 'way of life'
Added fatha to show this word is being acted upon
Added كُمْ to show this belongs to multiple people being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, 3) adding sukun on last letter. This is telling one person to speak.

This is an emphasis particle that makes the statement stronger. The shadda (ّ) doubles the ن sound to show extra emphasis. In this ayah, it emphasizes that 'the bounty' (ٱلْفَضْلَ) truly belongs to Allah.
Changed the root into a noun by adding damma (ُ) on first letter and changing final ي to ى. This makes it mean the act or result of guiding.

steps.m: the guidance (that is being discussed in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific. The small alif (ٰ) at the end is just a pronunciation mark.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to guide' into the noun 'guidance'
Small alif helps show correct pronunciation of the ending
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at end to show possession, and shadda on ل. This word shows who owns/possesses بِيَدِ (in the hand of) in this ayah.

steps.m: that (introduces what is being said about يُؤْتَىٰٓ)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that introduces what comes next in the sentence. Here it connects to يُؤْتَىٰٓ (is given) to show what is being given.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added يُ at the beginning to make it passive - showing something is being brought rather than someone bringing it
The أ changes to ؤ after يُ, and ى is written with an alif above it (ىٰ) at the end
Added fatha to connect the letters and make it into a noun
Adding tanween (double damma) makes the word indefinite, changing it from a specific 'one' to 'anyone' or 'someone'
Added kasra and sukun to make it into a noun meaning 'likeness'
Added fatha to make it usable in a sentence
This is a question word meaning 'what' that connects to the word أُوتِيتُمْ (you were given) in this ayah. Together they mean 'what you were given.'
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer, but this doesn't change the meaning
Changed to 'was given' form. The middle letter ت changed to و because it's a special type of verb. Like saying 'I give' → 'I was given'
Added suffix تُمْ to show the subject is 'you all' in the past tense
This is a connecting word that joins two possibilities: 'being given' and 'arguing with you'. It shows these are alternative scenarios the speakers are worried about.

steps.m: to argue or debate with evidence

steps.n: Added alif to show mutual action between two parties arguing with each other
Added يُ at start to show present tense, and combined the double ج into جّ with damma
Added و to show multiple people doing the action
Added كُمْ to show who they are arguing with - you all
Adding kasra on first letter changes this from an action into a word showing location or presence
Adding fatha at the end shows this word connects to what comes after it (your Lord)

steps.m: to nurture, care for, raise up
one who nurtures and cares completely
steps.n: The doubled ب with shadda shows this is someone who does this action perfectly and completely
Adding kasra at the end makes this a call/address to the Lord, like saying 'O my Lord!'
Added كُمْ to show this refers to multiple people being spoken to. This word is connected to قُلْ (say) in the ayah, showing who is being addressed.
This is the basic concept of speaking or saying something
Connected the letters together with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Adding sukun on second letter, 3) Dropping the middle weak letter و. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.
Added kasra (ِ) under the إ to show this is an emphasis word that introduces what comes next. This word emphasizes that 'guidance is Allah's guidance' in this ayah.
Added shadda (ّ) on the ن to make the emphasis stronger, like saying 'most certainly' instead of just 'certainly'

steps.m: to give extra, to do more than required

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning the gift or blessing that comes from doing more than required. This word is what إنّ is acting on in this ayah.
Adding definite article makes the noun definite
The fatha shows this word is affected by إنّ (indeed/truly) at the start of the phrase
Connected the root letters to form the basic noun. This is a special two-letter word that comes from a three-letter root.

steps.m: in/by the hand of (connected to اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show 'in/by' and kasra at the end to show possession. This word connects to اللَّهِ (Allah) in the ayah to mean 'in Allah's hand/control'.
Added emphasis to show this is a unique and special name
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection, and changed alif to wasl form
Connected the root letters together to form the basic past tense verb
The ي at the beginning shows it's happening now or in the future. This is a verb, not a noun, so it doesn't have case endings.
The هِ refers back to الفضل (favor) mentioned earlier in the ayah
This is a special word that refers to people. In this ayah, it refers to whoever Allah wishes to give His favor to, as it's the subject of يَشَآءُ (He wills/wishes).
The root letters were joined together and vowels were added to make the past tense 'he' form
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and ُ at end to show he is doing the action
Added long ا to show the extended nature of wanting/wishing
This is the base form of Allah's name, which is unique and doesn't come from a root like other Arabic words
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show Allah is the subject doing something in this sentence. He is the one who is وٰسِعٌ (vast/all-encompassing)

steps.m: and Allah (as the subject of وٰسِعٌ)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this part to what came before it in the verse

steps.m: to be vast or have room/capacity

steps.n: Adding vowels to show this is about being spacious
Added alif after first letter to show who/what has this quality
Changed the alif to an alif with overbar (this is just a writing variation, doesn't change meaning)
Added two dots on top (tanween) to show this is a describing word that stands alone
Added ي between first and second root letters with vowels to show this describes someone who has complete knowledge
Added ٌ (tanween) at the end to show this word stands alone as one of Allah's names
Adding vowel marks makes this a complete past tense verb
Adding اِ at start and تَ after first letter shows doing the action for oneself

steps.m: he chooses specially for himself (subject of يَشَآءُ)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by replacing اِ with يَ and adding ُ at the end. This word is connected to يَشَآءُ (he wills) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the quality or instance of mercy
Added ة (ta) and هِ (hi) at the end to make it 'His mercy'
Added بِ at beginning to show 'with/by'. This word shows how Allah chooses/specifies (يَخْتَصُّ) - He does it with His mercy.
This is a standalone relative pronoun used to refer to people, not derived from a root. In this ayah, it refers to whichever person Allah chooses for His mercy.
Added fatha marks and the weak letter alif replaced the root letter ي
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing the ending vowel to ُ
Adds a long vowel to extend the sound, common spelling variation
This is Allah's proper name. It's unique and doesn't come from a typical root.

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يَخْتَصُّ - chooses/selects)
steps.n: The ُ (damma) shows that Allah is the one doing the action in this sentence - specifically choosing/selecting who receives His mercy.

steps.m: and Allah (as the subject of يَخْتَصُّ - chooses/selects)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement, and ٱ (waslah) which helps with pronunciation when connecting words.
This is one of the six special nouns in Arabic that change their ending based on their role in the sentence. The basic form means owner or possessor.

steps.m: owner of, possessor of (describes اللَّهُ - Allah)
steps.n: The damma (ُ) shows this word is the subject of the sentence, describing Allah. It connects to the words after it (الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِيمِ) to mean 'Owner of Great Bounty'.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it a noun meaning the result of being generous
Adding ٱلْ at the beginning makes it refer to a specific favor/bounty that was mentioned or understood from context
Adding kasra shows this word belongs to or is possessed by ذُو (meaning 'possessor of')

steps.m: to be large/great in size, rank, or status

steps.n: Changed the verb into an adjective showing permanent quality
Adding genitive case because it describes 'the bounty' in the phrase 'possessor of the great bounty'

steps.m: the Great One (describing ٱلْفَضْلِ - the bounty)
steps.n: Adding 'the' (ال) to describe something specific and great

steps.m: from/among (connects to أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتٰبِ - People of the Book)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to. In this ayah, it introduces which group is being discussed (the People of the Book).

steps.m: and from/among (connects to أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتٰبِ - People of the Book)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. This و shows that we're adding new information about another group.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'people' or 'family'
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it. Here it connects to الكتاب (the scripture), making the phrase 'people of the scripture'
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changes the verb into a noun for the thing that results from writing
Adds the definite article to specify a particular book
The ِ at the end shows this word belongs to أَهْلِ (people) in the phrase 'people of the book'

steps.m: whoever (subject of يُؤَدِّهِ - will return it)
steps.n: This is a word that refers to people in a general way. Here it introduces the person who might or might not return what was entrusted to them. It connects to the verb يُؤَدِّهِ (will return it) that comes later.

steps.m: if (introduces condition for تَأْمَنْهُ - you entrust him)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'if you entrust him' before stating what would happen in that case.

steps.m: to trust or feel secure with someone/something

steps.n: Added present tense prefix for 'you'. This verb follows إِن (if) in the ayah, making it conditional.
A small mark (sukun) was added at the end to show this is part of an 'if' statement

steps.m: you entrust him (with بِدِينَارٍ - with a dinar)
steps.n: Added object pronoun 'him' to show who is being trusted. This connects to بِدِينَارٍ (with a dinar) showing what is being entrusted.
Added vowel marks to connect the first part of the word

steps.m: a large sum (usually of money or gold)
steps.n: The base قِنْطَ becomes قِنْطَار by adding ار at the end to make a noun that means 'a large amount' (like a million or more)
Adds preposition meaning 'with/by'

steps.m: with a large sum (that is entrusted)
steps.n: The word gets dots under the last letter to show it's connected to the word 'entrust' - like saying 'with a large sum' instead of just 'large sum'

steps.m: to pay back or return something

steps.n: Root letters got fatha marks and middle letter doubled (shadda) to make 'he paid back'
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he pays it back (referring to the trust/amount mentioned)
steps.n: Added هِ to show what is being paid back, with ۦٓ as pronunciation guide
This is the basic preposition meaning 'towards' or 'to'
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to a suffix
Added كَ at the end to show the direction is towards 'you'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Added هم at the end to show who is being talked about (them)
This is the basic form of the word that refers to people in questions or statements. It's like saying 'who' or 'whoever' in English.

steps.m: whoever (subject of يُؤَدِّهِ - will return it)
steps.n: The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to show this is the start of a new phrase. The sukun (ْ) shows the word stops here. In this ayah, this word refers to the person who will or won't return what was entrusted to them.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition - in this case, if you trust someone with wealth, then they will return it to you

steps.m: to trust or feel safe with

steps.n: Added vowels to make the basic past tense form showing a state of trust
Added ت at start to show 'you' and changed vowels for present tense. The sukun (ْ) on م shows it's affected by إِن (if) earlier in the verse.

steps.m: you trust him (object of إِن if)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who is being trusted. This word comes after إِن (if) in the verse, showing a conditional meaning - 'if you trust him'
The ه at the end means 'him' - the person being trusted. Like saying 'trust him' instead of just 'trust'
The root letters get arranged into the pattern 'dinar' which makes it mean a specific type of coin
Adding ب means 'with' or 'for'

steps.m: with/by a dinar (in context: if you entrust someone with a dinar)
steps.n: The tanween (double kasra) shows this is indefinite (any dinar). This word relates to قِنطَارٍ (huge sum) mentioned earlier, contrasting a small amount with a large one.
Basic negative word with fatha added for pronunciation
Added shadda to make the negative stronger. This word negates يُؤَدِّهِ (he will return it) in this ayah.

steps.m: to return or pay back what is due

steps.n: Connected root letters with vowels and doubled middle letter to show past tense. The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end.
Added يُ prefix to show present tense. The initial أ changes to ؤ after يُ. Changed middle vowel to kasra for present tense form.
Added هِ suffix to show the object (the thing being returned)

steps.m: he returns/pays it back to him
steps.n: Added ۦ to show the final vowel sound more clearly
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination. It's one of the most common prepositions in Arabic.
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to you (object of يُؤَدِّهِ - he returns it)
steps.n: Added ك at the end to show this is happening to 'you'. In this ayah, it's connected to يُؤَدِّهِ (he returns it), showing who receives the return.
This is a fixed particle (حرف) used for making exceptions. It does not have a root or derivational pattern.

steps.m: except (what follows is excluded from يُؤَدِّهِ - he will return it)
steps.n: Added the long alif at the end to complete the particle. In this ayah, it introduces an exception to when someone will return what was entrusted to them - they will return it except in certain conditions.
This is a particle that means 'as long as' when used with دُمْتَ (you remained). Together they form the meaning 'as long as you remain'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he remained' to command form 'you remain!'
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did the action
This is a preposition that comes from the concept of being high or elevated. The final ى shows it's a preposition.
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Adding هِ at the end shows this means 'upon him'
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Changed the middle و to ا and added ِ to show this describes someone or something that is standing
Changed the writing of the long aa sound to use آ instead of ا for clarity

steps.m: continuously standing (describing how long دُمْتَ/you remained)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes the state of 'remaining' (دُمْتَ) in the ayah
Basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
Added alif khanjariyah (small alif) above the ذ to show proper pronunciation
Added ل with kasra (لِ) to change the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)
Added ك with fatha (كَ) at the end to show we're pointing to something specific that was just mentioned
This is a basic particle that emphasizes what comes after it, like saying 'indeed' or 'that' in English
Adding بِ at the beginning changes the meaning from just emphasizing to showing a reason or cause

steps.m: because they (referring to those who don't return what is entrusted to them)
steps.n: Adding هُمْ shows who the word is talking about - in this case, it refers to those people mentioned earlier who don't return what is entrusted to them
Basic meaning of speaking or saying something
Added fatha marks to make the past tense form. The middle letter و changes to ا, which is common in words with و in the middle
Adding وا at the end changes 'he said' to 'they said'
This is a special verb used for negation in Arabic. It's irregular and only appears in past tense form but gives present meaning.
This is a special verb that always stays in past tense form but gives present meaning. In this ayah, it negates that there is a way/obligation (سَبِيلٌ) regarding the matter being discussed.
This is a special word that means 'not' or 'is not'. The only change is removing the small mark (fatHa) at the end, which doesn't change the meaning.
This is a preposition showing something is on top of, against, or directed at something else. In this ayah, it's used to show what is being claimed about someone.
The ى changes to ي with sukun to prepare for attaching the next part. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: against us (object of قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: Added نَا which means 'us', showing who is being affected. In this ayah, this is part of what some People of the Book said - they claimed something against themselves.
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects to الْأُمِّيِّۦنَ (the unlettered ones) to show what the previous statement is about.
Basic meaning relates to directing or leading
Adding vowels to make it a plural noun form
Creates an adjective meaning 'belonging to the common people'
Makes it plural - referring to multiple people who cannot read or write
Makes the word definite

steps.m: the unlettered ones (object of قَالُوا - what they said about)
steps.n: Changed ending to show this word is the object (what/who is being talked about) after 'they said'
Connected the root letters and added vowels to form a noun meaning 'path' or 'way'
Added tanween dammah to make it 'a path' instead of 'the path'
Basic meaning of speaking or saying something
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and vowels. The و from root becomes silent.

steps.m: they say (referring to the People of the Book)
steps.n: The ونَ at the end shows many people are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to some of the People of the Book (أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتٰبِ) who are making false claims.

steps.m: and they say (connecting to their previous claim)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at start to connect this with their earlier statement that 'there is no blame upon us concerning the unlearned'
This is a preposition showing something is on top of, against, or directed at something else. Here it connects to Allah's name to show something being attributed to or against Him.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.
The word Allah gets a kasra (i) sound at the end because it comes after عَلَى (against/about) in this ayah
Basic meaning of not telling the truth
Adding vowel marks turns the root into a complete past tense verb meaning 'he lied'

steps.m: the lie (that they tell about Allah)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ (the) changes it from a verb (he lied) to a noun (the lie) and makes it specific to this case
This is a pronoun for a group of people (all male or mixed). It's the subject of the verb يَعْلَمُونَ (they know) in this ayah.
Added و at the beginning to connect this to what came before, showing these people are doing something while also doing something else mentioned earlier
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he knew) to present tense (he knows) by adding يَ at the start
Changes from singular (he knows) to plural (they know) by adding ون

steps.m: they know (referring to those who falsely claim)
steps.n: Adds the plural ending ون to show multiple people are doing the action. In this verse, it refers to those who falsely claim there is no obligation towards the unlettered people.

This is a special word that strongly affirms something after it has been denied. It's like saying 'Actually, that's not true - it IS that way!' in English. In this ayah, it's responding to previous denials about fulfilling promises.
This is a special word that introduces a conditional statement about people. Here it connects to the verb أَوْفَىٰ (fulfills) to mean 'whoever fulfills...'
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes the meaning stronger - 'fulfilled completely/thoroughly' instead of just 'fulfilled'. Like adding 'very' or 'completely' in English.
The alif khanjariyah is added above the final ى to indicate proper pronunciation

steps.m: to promise, make a covenant, commit

steps.n: Root letters combined with vowels to make the noun meaning 'promise/covenant'
Adding بِ means 'with' or 'by'
The word gets a kasra (i) ending because it comes after the preposition bi (with/by) and connects to the verb 'fulfill' (أوفى)
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Added اِتَّ at the beginning to show being extra careful to protect oneself from harm
Added و (and) at beginning and changed اِ to ٱ after و. The ى at the end shows this action is directed towards Allah.
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English.
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before, showing that one thing leads to another. This word connects to يُحِبُّ (loves) in the ayah.
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, making this the unique name for The One God

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يُحِبُّ - He loves)
steps.n: Changed the first letter to hamzat wasl (ٱ) for pronunciation, added shadda on ل, and fatha at end to show Allah is the object being loved in this sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Combined the two ب letters with a shadda to show stronger meaning
Changed from past tense 'loved' to present tense 'loves' by adding يُ at the start. The word connects to Allah (فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ) who is doing the loving.

steps.m: to protect oneself from harm or wrong
one who protects themselves from wrong
steps.n: Added مُ at the start to show who does the action, and تّ between first two root letters to show doing it to oneself

steps.m: people who protect themselves from wrong
steps.n: Regular sound masculine plural ending
Added ال to show these are specific people. In this ayah, they are the ones who Allah loves.
Added kasra (ِ) to show this is a special word that emphasizes what comes next
Added shadda (ّ) to make the emphasis stronger. This word introduces and emphasizes the phrase about those who exchange Allah's covenant for a small price.
This is the basic form of the word used to connect descriptions to people or things
Adding ال makes it point to specific people or things, and the shadda emphasizes the connection
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people. This word is describing the people who are the subject of يَشْتَرُونَ (they purchase/exchange)
This is the basic meaning of buying or trading
Basic past tense pattern added vowels (َ) to make 'he sold/traded'
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ت between first and second root letters to show the action is done for oneself
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: to make a promise or agreement
steps.n: This is about making promises or agreements
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning the promise itself
Added بِ at the beginning to show this promise is being used as a tool or means. This word connects to اللَّهِ (Allah) to form 'by/with Allah's promise'
Added ِ at the end because it's followed by اللَّهِ to show they're connected, like saying 'promise of Allah'
Added ال and merged it with the word, then doubled the ل with a shadda to show this is the proper name for The One True God
Added kasra at the end to show possession. This word is possessed by عَهْدِ (covenant) in the phrase 'covenant of Allah'
Added vowels to turn the root 'swear' into the noun 'oath'
Changed from single oath to plural oaths by adding أ at the start and ا in the middle - this is a common way to make plurals in Arabic
Added conjunction وَ (and) to connect this word to what came before it
Added هِمْ at the end to show these are their oaths, with ِ before it because the word is possessed

steps.m: to set a price or value for something

steps.n: Connected the letters and added vowels to make a noun meaning 'price'

steps.m: a price (object of يَشْتَرُونَ - they purchase/exchange)
steps.n: Added ًا (tanween) to show this is the object - the thing being purchased/exchanged
Adds vowels to show this describes a temporary quality of being small in amount
Added ي to make it an adjective meaning 'little' or 'small' as a permanent quality
Added tanween alif to show this is describing the price they are paying, which is mentioned in the ayah as ثَمَنًا (a price)
This is the base form used to point to things that are far away
Added the plural marker ائِ to show we're talking about multiple people or things
Added كَ at the end which makes it specifically for pointing to things that are far away

steps.m: those (referring to the people who exchange Allah's covenant for a small price)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٓ) and elongation mark (ٰ) to show proper pronunciation. This word points back to the people mentioned before who 'exchange Allah's covenant for a small price'
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يُكَلِّمُهُمُ (speak to them) in this ayah, showing that Allah will not speak to them.
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to create or shape something new

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
These are two different words - خَلَقَ means 'he created' while خَلٰقَ means 'share/portion'. They look similar but have different meanings.
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows direction or purpose
Added the pronoun هم (them) to show who the preposition is directed towards
Basic preposition showing location or being inside something
The ى at the end extends the meaning to show being fully within something. Here it shows being completely in the afterlife.
Changed into a word describing something that comes after other things
Adding ة changes the meaning to refer specifically to the next life after this one
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific, well-known Hereafter. The word comes after في (in) in this ayah.
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word comes after the word في (in)
This is the basic negation word that makes something negative, like adding 'not' in English
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation with what came before. This word negates يُكَلِّمُهُمُ (speak to them) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Added shadda to show direct, intensive communication
Changed to present tense with prefix يُ and appropriate vowel changes

steps.m: he speaks to them (object of لا in لا يُكَلِّمُهُمُ)
steps.n: Added pronoun 'them' as object of the verb. This word follows لا (not) in the ayah, showing Allah will not speak to them.
Root meaning 'worship' became a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يُكَلِّمُ - speaks to)

steps.m: and not (connects to previous negation لَا خَلٰقَ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this negation with the previous statement 'they have no share'. The و shows these two negative things happen together.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this is a past-tense verb

steps.m: he looks/is looking (at إِلَيْهِمْ - at them)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. In this ayah, this verb connects to إِلَيْهِمْ (at them) to show who is being looked at.
Basic preposition showing direction or movement towards something
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to pronouns

steps.m: to them (referring to those who exchange Allah's covenant for a small price)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show this means 'to them' (masculine plural)
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'day'
Added fatha at the end because this word is connected to القيامة, making it 'Day of Resurrection' - like saying 'my book' instead of just 'book'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he stood' to a special noun meaning 'one time of standing/rising' - used for the Day of Rising

Added kasra at the end to show this word follows يَوْمَ (day of) in the ayah
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يُكَلِّمُهُمُ (speak to them) negative, like adding 'not' in English. In this ayah, it's saying Allah will not speak to them.
The و at the start means 'and', connecting this negative statement with the previous one. In this ayah, it's connecting multiple things that Allah will not do for these people.

steps.m: to purify or cause to grow
steps.n: This root is about making things pure or helping them grow better
Added shadda (ّ) to make the meaning stronger, and the و changed to ى at the end because it's a weak letter
Added يُ at the start to show it's happening in the present/future. Changed the middle vowel to ِ (kasra).
Added هِمْ at the end to show who receives the action (them)
This is a basic preposition that shows possession or purpose. In this ayah, it connects to عَذَابٌ (punishment) to show who the punishment is for.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next part
The و connects this phrase to the previous statements about Allah not speaking to them or looking at them on the Day of Judgment
Added هُمْ which means 'them' (for a group of males or mixed group). This shows who will receive the punishment (عَذَابٌ) mentioned next.
Changed from an action (punish) to a thing (punishment) by adding vowels and alif
Added double dammah to make it 'a punishment' instead of 'the punishment'
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Forms intensive adjective pattern فَعِيل showing something that causes strong pain. This word describes عَذَابٌ (punishment) in this ayah.

steps.m: causing intense pain (describes عَذَابٌ/punishment, in a general sense)
steps.n: Adding tanween dammah makes the noun indefinite
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it more certain. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English.
Added و at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Basic preposition showing something comes from somewhere or is part of something

steps.m: from them (referring to a group mentioned earlier in وَإِنَّ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being referred to. This connects back to the group mentioned at the start of the verse.
This is the basic root meaning

steps.m: a distinct group (that has split from others)
steps.n: Creates a noun meaning 'a group that has separated from others'. The ي shows this is a lasting characteristic.
The ًا shows this word is being acted upon by إِنَّ (indeed/verily) which comes earlier in the verse

steps.m: indeed/truly a distinct group (object of إِنَّ)
steps.n: The لَ adds emphasis, working together with إِنَّ to strongly emphasize that this group definitely exists
This is the basic root meaning
Added vowels (fatha) to make it mean 'he twisted' in the past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This shows an ongoing action of twisting/distorting that happens alongside their saying (يَقُولُونَ) in the verse.

steps.m: they twist/distort (their tongues with the Book)
steps.n: Changed to plural form to show multiple people doing the action. In this verse, it refers to those who twist their tongues (distort their speech) when reciting the Book.
This is the basic root meaning
Forms the singular noun for 'tongue'. This word is what's being twisted in the phrase 'they twist their tongues.'
Changes the singular 'tongue' into a plural meaning 'a few tongues'
Adds 'their' to show these tongues belong to them
This is the basic root meaning
Changes the action of writing into the thing that is written - a book
Adding ال makes it specific - 'the book' instead of just any book
Adding بِ shows how the book is being used
The letter 'bi' at the start needs the word to end with kasra, and the regular alif was made longer
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Adding ت at start changes it from 'he' to 'you/she', and the pattern change makes it happening now instead of in the past
Adding و at the end makes it plural 'you' (for a group of people)
Added هُ (hu) at the end to show what is being considered - like saying 'it' in English

steps.m: so that you all consider it (as part of يَلْوُۥنَ/they twist)
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why they twist their tongues with the Book
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of
Added fatha at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word الْكِتٰبِ (the book). This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
This pattern is often used for things that result from an action - a book is the result of writing. The word is in the genitive case because it comes after the preposition بِ (with).
Adding ال makes it specific - we're talking about a particular book
The word gets a kasra (small diagonal line) at the end because it comes after مِنَ (meaning 'from/of'). This shows it belongs to or comes from something.
This is a negation particle that makes the statement 'it is from Allah' into 'it is not from Allah'

steps.m: and not (negates هُوَ مِنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: The و connects this negation to the previous statement

steps.m: it/he (referring to what they falsely claim is from the Book)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that refers back to what they are falsely claiming is from the Book (الْكِتَابِ). In this ayah, it's used to emphasize that what they're claiming is NOT actually from Allah.
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it shows that something is claimed to be from (part of) الْكِتٰبِ (the Book).
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word الْكِتٰبِ (the Book). This connection shows what thing something is from.
The pattern changes this from an action to the thing produced by that action
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
The kasra at the end shows this word is owned by or connected to something else mentioned later
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا returns to و.
Makes the verb plural - now means 'they' instead of 'he'
Adding وَ connects this word to what came before it. In this ayah, they are saying something about Allah.

steps.m: it (referring to what they twisted from the Book)
steps.n: This is a basic pronoun that points back to something already mentioned. In this verse, it refers back to what they twisted from the Book. The هُ shows it's talking about something, and the وَ makes it stronger and clearer.

steps.m: from (shows this part of الْكِتَابِ comes from somewhere)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or belongs to. In this ayah, it's used to talk about where الْكِتَابِ (the book/scripture) comes from.
Added vowels to make the noun meaning 'presence' or 'nearness'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it, forming the phrase 'from the presence of Allah'
Added shadda to show emphasis on the ل
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed alif to wasl-alif at start. This word is possessed by عِندِ (from) in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes the statement 'it is from the Book' into 'it is not from the Book'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it

steps.m: it (referring to what they twisted from الْكِتَابِ)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that doesn't come from a root. In this ayah, it refers back to what they twisted from the Book (الْكِتَابِ) that they falsely claim is from Allah.

steps.m: from (shows this is from عِندِ اللَّهِ - Allah's presence)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or its source. In this ayah, it's used to falsely claim something is from Allah's presence.
Added kasra and sukun to form the basic preposition meaning 'near' or 'at'

steps.m: at, with, in possession of (of اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that follows it, forming the phrase 'from Allah's presence'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became the proper name for God in Arabic.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and shadda on ل. This word is possessed by عِندِ (from) in the phrase 'from Allah'.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make past tense. The middle و becomes ا because it's a weak letter.
Changes from past tense 'said' to present tense 'says' by adding يَ at the start
Adds plural masculine subject marker to indicate 'they' as subject
Adds conjunction 'and' to connect with previous phrase
This is a preposition that shows something is being done against or upon something else. In this ayah, it shows people are speaking lies against Allah.
Changed the root into the basic noun form meaning 'a deity'
Changed from the general word for 'a god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال and joining letters together
Added kasra at the end to show possession, since this word shows the source in the phrase 'from Allah'
Changes from verb 'to lie' into the noun 'a lie'
Adding ال makes it specific - we're talking about a particular instance of lying
Adding fatha at the end shows this is the object of the verb يَقُولُونَ (they say) in this ayah
This is a pronoun used to talk about a group of people (all male or mixed). In this ayah, it's the subject of the verb يَعْلَمُونَ (they know).
The و at the beginning connects this to the previous statement, showing these actions happen together
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from singular to plural by adding ون, showing that a group of people (all male or mixed) are doing the action
This is a negative particle that makes the verb كَانَ (was) into 'was not'. In this ayah, it helps express that 'it was not for any human...'
Added short vowels (fatha) to make it past tense meaning 'he was'

steps.m: he was (describes what happened before لِبَشَرٍ)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in past tense. This is a common change in Arabic words with و in the middle.

steps.m: to be human, have skin/outer appearance

steps.m: for a human being (object of كان)

This is a connecting word that joins two parts of the sentence. Here it connects 'it was not' (كَانَ) with 'he would say' (يَقُولَ), showing what was not possible.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form
Changed to Form IV pattern to show causing someone else to do the action
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: that He (Allah) gives it (the Book) to him
steps.n: Added object pronoun هُ (him/it) to show who receives the Book. This connects to الكتاب (the Book) mentioned later in the ayah.

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun form that means 'one who is worshipped'
The word for 'a god' (إِلَه) becomes the special name 'Allah' (ٱللَّهُ) by adding ال, doubling the ل, and dropping the first vowel. This makes it mean 'The One True God' instead of just any deity.

This pattern is often used for things that result from an action - a book is the result of writing. In this ayah, 'book' is the object receiving the action of 'giving' (يُؤْتِيَهُ).

steps.m: prophethood (the role of delivering divine news)

Connected the root letters with fatha. The middle و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.

Added يَ at beginning to show it's happening now and the doer is 'he'

Changed the root into the basic noun form for 'a deity'

steps.m: of Allah (possessed by/connected to what comes before)

This root represents the basic concept of being or existing

The long vowel و gets removed to make it a command form (be!)
Added تُ at the end to change from a command ('be!') to past tense 'I was'

steps.m: you all were (referring to those who teach the Book)

This is a negative particle that negates the verb يَأْمُرَكُمْ (he commands you) that comes after it

Added 'ال' (the) at the start, which made the ن double with a shadda mark
Added 'and' at the beginning and the final vowel mark

steps.m: to nurture, raise, take care of

Added damma to show this is a present tense verb
Added يَ at the beginning to make it 'he' doing the action in present tense
Added كُم at the end to show the command is directed at multiple people

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth
steps.n: Basic Form I verb meaning 'to disbelieve'

steps.n: Changed into a word showing when something happens. In this ayah, it works with إِذْ (when) to show timing - 'after when you were Muslims'

These are actually two different words - أَنْ means 'that' while أَنتُم means 'you all'. They're not related by transformation. أَنتُم is a separate pronoun meaning 'you all' that acts as the subject of مُّسْلِمُونَ (Muslims) in this ayah.
This form makes it mean 'one who submits (to God)'
Added مُ at start and ِ in middle to show who does the action

Added 'the' to make it specific/definite

Added 'the' to make it specific
Added ين at the end to make it plural (many prophets)

steps.m: when/at the time that (connects to ءَاتَيْتُكُم)
steps.n: Adding لَ at the beginning changes this from a general 'what' particle to one specifically about time. It connects to the verb ءَاتَيْتُكُم (I gave you) in this ayah.
Changed the first letter to hamza with madd (ءَا) to show it means making something happen to someone else
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action
Added كُم to show the action was done to 'you all'
