Changed from 'to eat' into 'food' by adding alif after first letter
Added 'the' and genitive case ending because this word is the object of كُلُّ (all)
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters. This is a theoretical form that changes in the next step due to the weak middle letter و.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter between two fathas
Added kasra to first letter and combined the repeated ل with shadda to create a noun meaning 'something lawful'
Added tanween alif to show this word is the object of كَانَ (was)
Adding vowels creates the basic past tense verb form
Added لِ (li) meaning 'for/to' at the start of the word
Added ى (alif maqsurah) at the end to show possession
This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew meaning 'one who struggles with God'. It refers to the prophet Jacob (يعقوب) who was given this name.
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object (thing being acted upon) of the verb حَرَّمَ (he forbade)
This is a basic exception word that shows something is different from what came before it. In this ayah, it shows that some food was different from the general rule mentioned.

steps.m: except (what follows is different from كُلُّ ٱلطَّعَامِ - all food)
steps.n: The ا at the end helps connect this word to what comes after it. Here it connects to what إِسْرٰٓءِيلُ made forbidden, showing this was an exception to all food being allowed.
This is a connecting word that joins 'except' (إِلَّا) with 'he forbade' (حَرَّمَ) to mean 'except that which he forbade'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he made completely forbidden (object of كَانَ)
steps.n: Adding shadda (doubling mark) on the middle letter makes the forbidding stronger and more complete. This word describes what was done to the food (طَّعَامِ) mentioned in the ayah.

This is a proper name that comes from Hebrew. It was given to Prophet Jacob (يعقوب) after he struggled with an angel, and means 'one who struggles with God'. His descendants became known as the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل).
Basic preposition showing something is on top of or against something else
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show this is the end of the word. In this ayah, it connects to نَفْسِهِۦ (himself) to show what something is upon.
Root means 'breath/soul' - kasra ending shows something owns/possesses this soul
Adds هِ to show it belongs to him. The word is in genitive case because it is possessed by Israel (إِسْرٰٓءِيلُ) in the phrase 'upon himself.'
This is a preposition that connects two words, showing that something starts from a particular point. Here it connects 'himself' (نَفْسِهِۦ) to 'before' (قَبْلِ), showing when something started.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the noun form
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِن (from) in the ayah

This is a particle that introduces what comes next. Here it connects to the verb تُنَزَّلَ (to be sent down), showing what happened before the Torah. It's like saying 'before that the Torah was sent down'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action more intense or repeated

steps.m: it is sent down (passive of نَزَّلَ)
steps.n: Adding تُ at the start and changing vowels makes it passive, showing the action is being done to something rather than by something
This is the Hebrew word for the holy book given to Moses, adapted into Arabic. The vowel marks help with pronunciation.
Added emphasis (shadda) on first letter for stronger pronunciation
Added the feminine ending ة and elongated alif (ىٰ) which is common in Arabic for foreign words
Added the definite article 'the' at the beginning

steps.m: the Torah (subject of تُنَزَّلَ - to be sent down)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the verb 'to be sent down' in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, 3) adding sukun on last letter. This is telling one person to speak.
The وا ending shows this is a command for 2 or more people

steps.m: so bring! (command to a group) (connected to قُلْ - say)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this command with the previous word قُلْ (say), showing this is what should be said

steps.m: Torah (the book of divine law revealed to Moses)
steps.n: Base noun referring to the Torah
Added ٱل (the) at the beginning. The ت gets a shadda (double mark) because it combines with the ل
Added بِ at the start to mean 'with' and ِ at the end to show it follows the word 'with'

steps.m: to follow or recite something in sequence

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense verb form
Changed to command form by adding initial alif with damma, putting sukun on middle letter, and damma on final letter
Added هَا (meaning 'it') at the end to show what is being recited
Added فَ at beginning to show this is a response or result, and lengthened final alif for emphasis

steps.m: if (introduces condition for كُنتُمْ صٰدِقِينَ - 'you are truthful')
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces an 'if' statement. In this ayah, it connects the condition 'if you are truthful' to what came before. The kasra (إِ) is added to show it's the conditional form.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he was' to 'you were' by adding تَ at the end. The middle و gets dropped to make it easier to say.
Changed from singular 'you' to plural 'you all' by adding مْ and changing the vowel
Added ا after first letter to show who does the action, like changing 'tell truth' to 'truth teller'
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people. This word describes what 'you all' were in the phrase 'if you were truthful'
This is a question word used to ask about people. It's one of the special words in Arabic that doesn't come from a three-letter root.
Added kasra at the end because this word connects to the verb that follows it (ٱفْتَرَىٰ meaning 'invents/fabricates')
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this to what came before. It shows this is a result or consequence, like saying 'so' or 'then'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added ٱ and ت to the root word to show making up something that is not real or true
Added ى at the end to show the long 'aa' sound at the end of the word
This is a helper word that shows something is on top of or against something else. Here it connects to the word ٱللَّهِ (Allah) to show who the lie is against.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
This is the unique name of God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the doubled ل shows emphasis, and the ِ shows it's being acted upon by the word before it (عَلَى meaning 'against')
Adds vowel marks to show this describes the act of lying
Added ال to make it specific and َ at the end to show it's the object being fabricated against Allah in this verse
This is a preposition that shows where something starts from. Here it connects 'the lie' (ٱلْكَذِبَ) with 'after' (بَعْدِ) to show the sequence of events.
Added a small ن (called tanwin) after the main ن to help with smooth pronunciation when connecting to the next word

steps.m: to be distant or come after

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fatha and sukun) to the root letters to make it mean 'after'
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to ذٰلِكَ (that) in the phrase 'after that'
This is the basic pointing word used to point to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) to show distance

steps.m: that (referring to what was mentioned before about lying against Allah)
steps.n: ذٰ becomes ذٰلِكَ by adding لِكَ to make it a complete word meaning 'that'. Like pointing to something far away with your finger.
This is the basic pointing word used for multiple people or things that are far away
Added ئِكَ to make the full word meaning 'those over there' - like pointing to something far away
The فَ at the beginning shows this is a result of what was mentioned before - in this case, whoever invents lies about Allah after receiving clear proof
Basic pronoun form meaning 'they' or 'them'
Added damma marks to show this is the subject form of the pronoun. This pronoun is connected to ٱلظَّالِمُونَ (the wrongdoers) - it tells us who the wrongdoers are.
Past tense verb form of the root meaning 'to harm/wrong' - shows that 'he' did this action in the past
Changes from a verb (to wrong) to a noun meaning 'wrongdoer'
Makes the word plural to mean multiple wrongdoers
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Adding sukun on last letter, 3) Dropping the middle weak letter و. This is telling one person to do something.

steps.m: to tell truth, to be honest
he spoke the truth (subject of قُلْ)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is something that happened in the past. This word follows قُلْ (say) in the ayah, showing what Allah commanded to be said.
Changed the concept of worship into a word meaning 'a deity' or 'god' in general

steps.m: Allah (the one true God) (subject of صَدَقَ - spoke truth)
steps.n: Changed from 'a god' to 'THE God' by adding ال and merging letters. The ُ shows Allah is the one who spoke truth in this ayah.
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb
Added alif at start and doubled the ت to make it a command telling someone to follow
Added plural command ending وا with damma before it

steps.m: so all of you follow! (plural command)
steps.n: Added فَ at start to mean 'so/then' and changed hamza to wasl form
Added kasra under first letter and shadda on middle letter to transform from action to noun meaning path/way

steps.m: religion/way of life (object of اتَّبِعُوا - follow)
steps.n: Added fatha on middle letter and ta marbuta with fatha at end to make it a specific instance of a path/way. This word is the object (thing being followed) after the command اتَّبِعُوا (follow) in this ayah.
This is a proper name borrowed from Hebrew/Aramaic, not derived from an Arabic root. It refers to the prophet Abraham, who is mentioned frequently in the Quran.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: kasra (ِ) under إ, sukun (ْ) on ب, and elongated alif (ٰ) after ر
Added fatha (َ) at the end to show this is the object of the verb اتَّبِعُوا (follow) in the phrase 'follow the way of Abraham'

steps.m: to turn away from falsehood towards truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: one who naturally turns away from falsehood
steps.n: Added ي and changed vowels to show this describes someone who has this quality as part of their nature

steps.m: as one who naturally turns away from falsehood (describing إِبْرٰهِيمَ)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this describes how Ibrahim was, like saying 'as a truth-seeker'
This is a negative particle that makes something not true
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous command 'follow'. The full meaning is 'follow... and he was not'

steps.m: to be or exist in a state

steps.n: Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make it mean 'he was' in the past tense
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak middle letter in the past tense. This word tells us about Ibrahim's state.
This is the basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
Added fatha at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (the polytheists)

steps.m: one who joins partners with Allah
steps.n: The مُ at the beginning and ِ in the middle work together to make this word mean someone who does this action regularly

steps.m: the one who joins partners with Allah
steps.n: Makes the noun definite ('the')

steps.m: the ones who join partners with Allah (after مِنَ)
steps.n: Changes to plural form and shows it comes after the word مِنَ (from/of) in this ayah

This is a special word that comes at the start of sentences to emphasize that what follows is definitely true, like saying 'I'm telling you that...' in English. In this ayah, it emphasizes the importance of what's being said about the first house built for people.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together
Doubled the middle letter to make it stronger

steps.m: to stay, dwell, or spend the night

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a noun by adding the vowel marks َ (fatha) and ْ (sukun). This turns it from 'to stay/dwell' into 'a place where people stay/dwell' (house).
Added tanwin (double kasra) to show this is indefinite (like 'a house') and is possessed by the word أَوَّلَ (first), making it mean 'first house'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that this house was the thing being placed/established, rather than it placing something else. This relates to 'أَوَّلَ بَيْتٍ' (first house) which is what was placed.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'people' (those who are social with each other)
Added 'the' to make it specific and added kasra at the end because it's being acted upon by the لِ that comes next

steps.m: for the people (connected to بَيْتٍ - house)
steps.n: Added لِ at the start to show this word is connected to بَيْتٍ (house), telling us the house was placed for the people
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence
Adding ال and doubling the first letter makes it more specific, like saying 'the one which' instead of just 'which'
The لَ at the start adds emphasis because this is an important statement about the first house built for people
This is a special name for the city of Makkah
Added ة to make it a proper noun for the place. Bakkah is an ancient name for Makkah, referring to how pilgrims crowd together there.
Added بِ at the start to mean 'in' and ة with fatha at the end to show it follows the preposition. This word tells us where the first house (بَيْتٍ) was placed.

steps.m: to bless, to increase, to make grow

steps.n: Added alif after first root letter to show continuous or abundant blessing
Added مُ prefix to make it mean 'blessed' (something receiving blessings) instead of 'blessing' (giving blessings)
The ًا ending shows this describes how the House (بَيْتٍ) is - it is in a blessed state

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Root letters h-d-y became a noun meaning 'guidance', with alif maqsurah (ى) at end and tanween marks
Added وَ to connect this word with the previous word مُبَارَكًا (blessed)
Root meaning 'know' becomes a noun meaning 'everything that exists' - like how all the things we can know make up our world
Makes the word plural to show multiple worlds/realms. This special ending ين shows it's being acted upon by something else in the sentence.
Added لِ at the beginning to show these worlds are receiving something (in this case, they're receiving guidance)
Added a shadda on ل and lengthened the ا to ٰ for proper pronunciation. Also added the final vowel َ to show this word connects to what comes after it.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.

steps.m: in it (referring to the Sacred House/Ka'bah)
steps.n: The هِ at the end refers back to البيت (the House/Ka'bah) mentioned in this ayah. This connects the preposition to what it's talking about.
Adds the basic vowel mark to create the singular noun form
Adds double dammah to make the word indefinite
Small meem shows this is the end of a Quranic verse
Connected the root letters with vowels to make basic past tense verb
Shadda makes the meaning stronger and changes it to an adjective
Added ات to make feminine plural, showing multiple clear signs or proofs

steps.m: very clear signs/proofs (as subject of the sentence)
steps.n: Added tanween damma to show this word is the subject, and alif is written as a long alif
Added fatha marks and combined letters. The و changes to ا because it's between two fathas.
Added مَ at the beginning to create a noun meaning 'place where something happens'
Added ُ to show this word is in the subject/nominative case
Doubles the م to add emphasis to the meaning
This is a proper name that comes from Hebrew, referring to the prophet Abraham (Ibrahim in Arabic)

steps.m: Abraham (object of مَّقَامُ - the station of)
steps.n: The fatha (َ) at the end shows this is the object being acted upon - here it shows whose station/place is being referred to
This combines 'and' (و) with 'whoever' (من) to make one word
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he went inside it (referring to البيت - the House/Ka'bah)
steps.n: Added هُۥ at the end to show what was entered. Here it refers back to البيت (the House/Ka'bah) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added fathas to make it past tense. This is the theoretical form before the weak letter change.

The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic to make words easier to pronounce. This word is describing مَقَامُ إِبْرٰهِيمَ (the Station of Ibrahim) in this verse.
Changed the root into an active participle form that describes a state of being safe. This word tells us how something is, following the verb كَانَ (was).
Added ًا at the end to show this word describes a state of being safe/secure. This ending is needed because it follows كَانَ (was).
This is the base word referring to God in Arabic. It's a unique word that doesn't come from a typical root.
Added ل at the beginning which shows something belongs to or is for Allah. The first ل combines with ال to become لل with a shadda.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it in the sentence
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it in the verse
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or elevated

steps.m: upon, on (connects to ٱلنَّاسِ - the people)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'. In this ayah, it shows that something (pilgrimage) is required upon/of the people.

steps.m: people (those who are social/friendly with each other)
steps.n: Added alif after first letter to make a noun meaning 'people' - this makes sense because people are social beings who live together
Added ال at beginning to make it specific people, and doubled the ن with shadda to connect the sounds smoothly

steps.m: of the people (connected to عَلَى before it)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word عَلَى (upon) which needs the thing after it to end in kasra

steps.m: to repeatedly visit or aim for an important destination

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it into 'he performed Hajj' in the past tense
Combined the repeated ج into one letter with shadda to make it a noun for the action itself
Changed the vowels to show this is part of a connected phrase حِجُّ البَيْتِ (pilgrimage to the House)
Changes from root meaning into a noun meaning the place where someone dwells

steps.m: the House (referring to Ka'bah, owned by الله)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ makes it specific and ِ shows it belongs to Allah. In this context, 'the House' specifically refers to the Ka'bah in Mecca.
Basic form of this word that means 'who' or 'whoever' - used to start a condition
Added kasra at the end because this word connects to the verb after it (اسْتَطَاعَ meaning 'is able'). Together they mean 'whoever is able'.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters

steps.m: he was able (to do حِجُّ ٱلْبَيْتِ)
steps.n: Added است at the beginning to show seeking or trying to do something. The و changed to ا because it came between two vowels. This word relates to being able to perform the Hajj mentioned in the ayah.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'
The ى changes to ي with sukun when something is attached to it

steps.m: to him/it (referring to البَيْت - the House)
steps.n: Added هِ which means 'him/it' - here referring to the House (the Ka'bah)

steps.m: to travel or follow a path

steps.n: Adding ي and vowels changes it from a basic root into a noun meaning 'path'
Added ًا to show this is the object of the verb اسْتَطَاعَ (was able to). The ending shows it's indefinite (like 'a/an' in English) and is being acted upon.
This is a word that introduces a condition - it means 'whoever' or 'anyone who' does something. In this ayah, it starts a new part that talks about what happens to whoever does something specific.

steps.m: and whoever (connected to دَخَلَهُۥ - entered it)
steps.n: The و connects this with the previous statement. It's like saying 'and also, whoever...' to add another important point. Here it connects to the word دَخَلَهُۥ (entered it) to tell us what happens to whoever enters.

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth/blessings

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word is connected to فَإِنَّ (then indeed) which comes after it in the ayah.
This is an emphasis particle that makes what follows more certain and adds importance to it. The shadda (ّ) shows extra strength in the meaning.

steps.m: then indeed (connects to previous statement about those who disbelieve)
steps.n: The فَ shows this is connected to and follows from the previous statement about those who disbelieve. It's like saying 'if someone disbelieves, then as a result...'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters with shadda. This is a unique word that refers specifically to the One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being described as غَنِىٌّ/self-sufficient)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is being described. Changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes in middle of speech. In this ayah, Allah is being described as self-sufficient/free of need.
Added vowels to make the past tense verb
The shadda shows this is a permanent quality

steps.m: one who is free from need
steps.n: The tanween shows this is 'a' rich one (indefinite)
Basic preposition showing separation or moving away from something
Added kasra at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱلْعٰلَمِينَ (the worlds)
Changes the word from 'knowing' to mean 'everything that can be known' - like how a world contains everything we can learn about
Makes it plural to show many worlds. This word follows عَنِ (meaning 'from') in the ayah.
Adding ال makes it specific - like saying 'the worlds' instead of just 'worlds'. The long alif (ٰ) is just a different way of writing the same sound.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed from 'he said' to 'say!' by dropping first vowel and changing middle vowel - makes it a command
Weak middle letter is dropped in command form to make it shorter and easier to say

steps.m: to dwell/live together as a family

steps.n: Changed from a verb about living together into a noun meaning the people who live together. This word is being used to address the People of the Book (followers of previous scriptures).
Added يا with a long vowel (ٰ) and a small alif (ٓ) at the start to call out to someone, like saying 'hey!' The َ (fatha) at the end shows this is direct speech.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (wrote) to a noun (book/writing) by adding alif after second root letter
Added ٱلْ to make it definite ('the book') and ِ at the end because it's possessed by أَهْلَ (people) in أَهْلَ ٱلْكِتٰبِ (people of the book)
Starting with the question word ما which means 'what'
Added لِ at the beginning to mean 'for'
The ما is shortened to مَ when used in questions. This word is asking about why they are doing the action of disbelieving (تَكْفُرُونَ)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added تَ at the start to change from 'he' to 'you', and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ون to show multiple people are being addressed (in this ayah, referring to the People of the Book mentioned earlier as يٰٓأَهْلَ ٱلْكِتٰبِ)
Changed the root into a noun meaning one clear sign or miracle
Made the word plural to show multiple signs
Added بِ at start to mean 'with/by' and ِ at end because it's possessed by Allah (اللَّهِ) in this phrase
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Changed to the unique name for The One God by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows emphasis.
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word before it (بِـَٔايٰتِ - signs of)
This is the unique proper name of God in Islam. While it comes from the root meaning 'divine/deity', it is always used as a proper noun.
Added shadda on ل to show it is doubled in pronunciation
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of the word شَهِيدٌ (witness) in this ayah

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of شَهِيدٌ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of شَهِيدٌ)
steps.n: Changed regular alif to wasl alif which connects in pronunciation with the previous word
Added ي between second and third root letters and vowel marks to create the pattern for someone who does the action
The double damma (ٌ) at the end shows this is a noun describing Allah as a witness
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of, over, or upon something else

steps.m: upon, on, over (what تَعْمَلُونَ you do)
steps.n: Added the alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning. This word connects to تَعْمَلُونَ (you do) in the next part of the ayah.
This is a question word that asks about actions or things. Here it's asking about what the People of the Book do, connecting to the verb تَعْمَلُونَ (you do/practice).
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action, and added vowel marks to connect the letters and show it's happening in the present

steps.m: you (all) act/work/do deeds (addressing the People of the Book)
steps.n: Added ون to show it's addressing multiple people. This word is questioning what the People of the Book are doing, connected to تَكْفُرُونَ (you all disbelieve) earlier in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, 3) adding sukun at the end. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.

steps.m: to live somewhere, be part of a family

steps.n: Added fatha and sukun to make it mean 'people/family'. This word belongs to 'the Book' (الكتاب) making it mean 'People of the Book'
Added يٰٓ at the start to call out to someone (like saying 'hey!') and َ at the end to show it's being called out to
Changed the root into a noun representing the result of writing. This word is in the genitive case because it comes after أهل (people) in the phrase 'People of the Book'.
Adding the definite article ال makes the noun definite
The small diagonal line under the last letter shows this word belongs to or is connected to 'people' (أهل)
Combined the preposition لِ (for) with ما (what) to start forming the question word
The ا is dropped from ما when used in questions, changing 'for what' to mean 'why'. This word is asking about the action تَصُدُّونَ (you prevent/hinder) that follows it.
The doubled middle letter shows this action is done with extra force
Adding تَ at start changes it from 'he blocked' to 'you block' in present tense

steps.m: you all block/prevent (those who believe from اللَّهِ's path)
steps.n: Adding ون shows this is done by multiple people. The word relates to من ءامن (those who believe) who are being prevented from سبيل الله (Allah's path)

This is a preposition that shows movement away from something or preventing something. In this ayah, it connects the verb تَصُدُّونَ (you prevent/turn away) with سَبِيلِ (path), showing what people are being turned away from.
Added ي between second and third letters to emphasize the meaning
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it
Added emphasis to middle letter to show uniqueness and importance
Added -i sound at the end to show 'of' (possession), and changed the first A to connect smoothly with previous word
This is a relative pronoun referring to people. In this ayah, it refers to the believers who are being prevented from Allah's path. It is the object of the verb تَصُدُّونَ (you prevent/hinder).
Basic meaning of being in a state of safety or security
Adding hamza and alif at start changes meaning from 'being safe' to 'entering into safety through faith/belief'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added ت prefix for 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense. The ى changes to ي in present tense due to weak letter rules.
Changed to plural 'you all' by adding ون and dropping the final ي

steps.m: you all seek it (crookedness) wrongfully (referring to سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ - the path of Allah)
steps.n: Added ها ('it') at the end to show they are seeking crookedness in Allah's path - ها points back to the word عِوَجًا (crookedness) mentioned earlier

steps.m: to be crooked, deviate from straightness

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun describing the state of being crooked or deviated

steps.m: crookedness (object of تَبْغُونَهَا - you seek it)
steps.n: Added tanween (ًا) to show this word is the object being sought. It answers 'what?' in relation to the verb 'you seek it'
Added م with sukun at the end to show this means multiple people
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it

steps.m: to witness or testify to something

steps.n: Adding vowels makes this a past tense verb meaning 'to witness'
Changed from past tense verb 'witnessed' to noun 'witness' by adding ي in the middle
Makes the word plural by changing vowels and adding اء at the end
Added ُ to show this word is doing something in the sentence
Basic negative particle ما with fatha added for pronunciation

steps.m: and not (referring to Allah not being unaware of what they do)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. In this ayah, it's connecting to the previous statement, leading into the fact that Allah is not unaware of their actions.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel patterns
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Added alif after first letter and vowels to show who does the action

steps.m: with one who is unaware (connected to اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this describes how Allah is not unaware of what they do

steps.m: with one who is unaware (of عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ - what you do)
steps.n: The alif is written differently (ٰ) but sounds the same. Added two dots under (ٍ) to show it connects to 'what you do'
This is a basic preposition that shows moving away from something or talking about something
Added the question word مَا to ask about something
When عَنْ combines with مَا, the ن changes to م and gets a shadda. This is just a pronunciation change that happens when these words come together.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ
Added plural marker to show multiple people are doing the action
The root letters أ-ي-ي form a different word - these notes don't explain how 'أَيّ' (which/who) is formed. Better notes would be: 'A common question word made by combining أ with a doubled ي'
Adding damma to show this is the main word being used
Adding ها changes this into a way to address people

steps.m: O you all! (calling out to a group)
steps.n: Adding يٰٓ (yaa) at the start turns it into a way to get attention, like saying 'Hey everyone!' or 'O people!'
Starting with the basic pointing word ذا which means 'this'
Changed the ending to make it mean 'who' or 'which' instead of just 'this'
Added ال at the beginning to make it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Added vowel marks and shadda to show how to pronounce it correctly

steps.m: those who (referring to a group being talked about)
steps.n: Changed the ending to ين to show we're talking about many people, not just one person. This word refers to a group that was mentioned before.

steps.m: to cause to feel safe/to believe
steps.n: Adding long alif at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Added وا with the elongation mark (ـٓ) and small high rounded zero (۟) to show multiple people did this action. This word is the subject of the conditional phrase 'if you obey' in this ayah.

This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition - it shows that what follows (obeying) might lead to a result (them turning you into disbelievers). It's connected to the verb تُطِيعُوا (you obey) in this ayah.
Added kasra mark under the alif to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added تُ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense. The و changed to ي because it's between two vowels
Added وا to show that multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: to split or divide into separate parts

steps.n: The ي makes it mean 'a distinct group' rather than just the action of separating
Added ًا at the end to show this group is the object (the ones being acted upon) in the sentence
Basic preposition form indicating something comes from somewhere/someone
Added emphasis by doubling the م sound
Added fatha at the end to connect with the next word ٱلَّذِينَ (those who)
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to refer to someone or something. It's a particle word that doesn't come from a root.
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Adding shadda makes the ل sound stronger
Adding ن at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one person
Changed to passive voice to show receiving something
Added و because أ-ت-ي is a weak root
Added وا at the end to show multiple people received something. This word connects to كِتٰبَ (the book/scripture) which is what was given to them.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (wrote) to a noun (book/writing) by adding an alif
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb أُوتُوا (were given). Also changed alif to alif madd for proper spelling.

steps.m: to send back or return something

steps.n: Adding the vowel marks makes this a complete past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show 'he' in present tense, and merged the double د into دّ with a shadda
Added و to show multiple people doing the action
Added كُم to show who is being sent back (you all)

steps.m: to be distant or come after something

steps.n: Root letters got vowel marks (fatHa and sukoon) to make a word meaning 'after'
Added fatha at the end to show this word connects to إِيمٰنِكُمْ (your faith) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Added an alif at the start to make it mean 'to cause someone/oneself to trust/believe' rather than just 'to trust'
Changed the word from an action (believing) to a concept (faith)
Changed the alif to a long alif (ٰ) which is just a writing variation
Added kasra to show this connects to something after it
Added كُمْ to show this belongs to multiple people being spoken to
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed the word to describe someone who does this action
Added two damma marks to show this is any rejecter (not a specific one)
Added ين to show this describes multiple people and is being acted upon by the verb يَرُدُّوكُم (they return you)
This is a question word that always asks about the way something happens
Adding vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly

steps.m: and how (asking about manner/way of تَكْفُرُونَ)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'. This word is questioning the manner of the action تَكْفُرُونَ (you all disbelieve) that follows it in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added ت at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ون to show multiple people are being addressed. This matches with وَأَنتُمْ (and you all) in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the pronoun meaning 'you'
Adding م with sukun at the end makes it refer to multiple people
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'

steps.m: to recite, to read out loud

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense form
Changed to passive present tense form. The تُ at start shows it's present tense, and the vowel pattern shows it's passive.
Added superscript alif (ٰ) as a pronunciation guide
This is a basic preposition showing something is above or over something else
The ى changes to ي before adding a suffix - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning

steps.m: upon you all (being addressed in وَكَيْفَ تَكْفُرُونَ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being talked to directly. This connects to the previous phrase 'How could you all disbelieve?'

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: The root letters were turned into a noun by adding alif at the start and changing the middle letter to alif. The ة at the end makes it mean 'one sign/miracle'
Changed to plural by replacing ة with ات

steps.m: signs, verses, or miracles (subject of تُتْلَىٰ)
steps.n: Added damma to show this is the subject of the verb تُتْلَىٰ (being recited). The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this form.
Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in this divine name
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of the preposition بِ (by/with) in the phrase يَعْتَصِم بِٱللَّهِ (holds fast to Allah)
This is a basic word that shows something is inside or among something else
Added kasra under ف to show how it's pronounced
Added كُمْ to show this affects multiple people being spoken to
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Root ر-س-ل becomes رَسُول meaning 'messenger' by adding vowels. Like how 'send' becomes 'sender' in English. This messenger is doing something 'among you' (فِيكُمْ)
Adds possessive pronoun 'his' as suffix. This word is the subject in the phrase 'His messenger is among you' (وَفِيكُمْ رَسُولُهُ).
This is a conditional word meaning 'whoever' - it introduces a condition about anyone who does something. Here it refers to whoever holds firmly to Allah.
Adding وَ at the start connects this condition with what came before it in the ayah

steps.m: to protect or prevent from harm

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb meaning 'he protected' by adding vowel marks
Added اِعْتَ to show someone protecting themselves rather than protecting others
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Removed final vowel because word appears in middle of sentence
This is the proper name of God in Islam. It's a unique word that doesn't come from a root.
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is being done with or by Allah's help

steps.m: by/with Allah (connected to يَعْتَصِم - holds fast to)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a special connecting alif (ٱ) and a kasra ending (-ِ) because it's being held onto (connected to يَعْتَصِم)
This is a particle that adds emphasis to what comes after it

steps.m: and indeed, then certainly (connects to يَعْتَصِم - holds fast)
steps.n: The فَ (fa) at the start means 'then/so', connecting this word to what happened before - like saying 'if you do this, then this happens'

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added fathas to make the basic past tense verb. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changed the vowels to show that the action happened to someone rather than someone doing it. The ُ on first letter and ِ on middle letter make it passive (something done to someone).
Basic preposition showing direction or movement towards something. Here it connects to the word صِرٰطٍ (path)
Added an alif khanjariyah (small alif) above the ى to show proper pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added the basic vowel pattern to connect the letters
The alif is written as a long vowel mark (ٰ) in this word
The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this is indefinite ('a path') and is in the genitive case because it follows the preposition إلى (to)
Basic root letters connected with vowels to make past tense 'he' verb
Added مُسْتَ at the start to mean 'seeking to be' straight/upright - like asking for guidance to the right path
Changed vowel to ِ to show this is describing the path itself, not the action of making something straight
Added shadda on م and tanween at end to show this describes something general

steps.m: to point to or indicate something
which (used to point to something specific)
steps.n: The root letters combine and the middle ي doubles to make a word that asks 'which one?'

steps.m: O which (calling attention to something specific)
steps.n: Added يا with long alif to call attention, like saying 'hey!' to get someone's attention
Added short vowels (fatha and damma) to show this is calling out to someone - like saying 'Hey you!'
Added ها to make it a complete calling phrase - like saying 'O you there!'
This is the basic form of the word used to point to someone or something specific
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people or things instead of just one
Adding ال makes it point to specific people or things that were mentioned or will be described
The word gets special marks to show it's connected to the word after it (ءَامَنُوا۟) which describes who these people are - the believers
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding alif after first letter makes it stronger/more intense

steps.m: they believed (referring to the believers being addressed)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show that many people did this action. This word is describing the believers being addressed with يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ (O you who...)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added اِتَّ at the beginning to show doing the action to yourself. The ت combines with ق using shadda (ّ) for easier pronunciation
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action
Added ٱ to help pronounce the word - it's not written in most Qurans
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed to the unique name of The One True God by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows emphasis.
Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the one being feared in this sentence
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
The doubled letter shows this is the complete/intense form of being right. This word describes تُقَاتِهِۦ (His fear/consciousness) in the ayah, showing the complete way Allah should be feared.

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the word into a noun showing the action itself
Changed from basic 'protection' to a stronger form meaning 'careful guarding' by adding ت at start and end
Added the possessive pronoun هِ (his) with kasra before it to connect the words. This word is the object of حَقَّ (right of) in this ayah.
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this negative statement with what came before. This word connects to تَمُوتُنَّ (you all die) to say 'and do not die'.
Added vowel marks to form the basic noun
Changed the 'a' sound to 'u' sound to make it mean 'to die' instead of 'death'
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action

steps.m: you shall surely die (command in this ayah)
steps.n: Added نَّ at the end to show strong emphasis, making it a strong command or prohibition when combined with لا before it
This is a special word that means 'except'. In this ayah, it connects to the word مُّسْلِمُونَ (Muslims) to say 'except while being Muslims', showing the only acceptable state for dying.
This is a word that means 'you' when talking to many people
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before. This word shows who is being described as مُّسْلِمُونَ (Muslims) in this ayah.
Adding مُ at the start and ِ in the middle makes this describe someone who does the action of submitting
The doubled م adds emphasis, showing complete/total submission

steps.m: those who submit (to Allah) - masculine plural
steps.n: Adding ون makes it refer to multiple people
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Adding اِ and ت to the start makes it mean 'to hold onto something yourself', like grabbing and not letting go
Adding وا changes it from 'he held' to 'you all hold' - it's now a command for a group

steps.m: and hold firmly to (something) (command to بِحَبْلِ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Command to hold firmly, with 'wa' (and) added at start, telling people to grasp Allah's rope
Changed the word from an action (tying) to a thing (rope) that can tie or connect things
Added بِ at start to show how to use the rope. This word is connected to وَٱعْتَصِمُوا۟ (hold fast) in this ayah.
Added ِ at the end because this word is connected to اللَّهِ (Allah) that comes right after it, showing it's Allah's rope
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special word used so often
Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to something else (the rope/bond mentioned before it). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it's in the middle of speech.
Added ي between second and third root letters to show completeness, with vowels to connect letters. This word describes how to 'hold fast' - doing it all together as one group.

steps.m: all together as one (describes how to hold fast to Allah's rope)
steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this describes how the action should be done - emphasizing unity and togetherness in holding fast to Allah's rope
This is the basic negative particle that tells us not to do something. Here it's telling us not to divide.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this command with the previous one, showing these are related instructions.

steps.m: to split apart or separate from each other

steps.n: Added vowels to make it 'he split' in the past tense
Doubling the middle root letter intensifies the meaning of separation
Adding تَ at the beginning shows the action happens to the doer themselves, like saying 'split themselves apart'
Changes to 'they' form by adding وا at the end
Adds helping alif at start to make proper command form
Adds plural suffix to make it a command for a group
Adds 'and' at beginning to connect with previous phrase
Changed the root letters into a simple noun meaning 'blessing' by adding the vowel marks kasra (ِ) and sukoon (ْ)
Added ة to make it refer to one specific blessing
Changed the ending to show this is what's being remembered/mentioned in the phrase 'remember Allah's blessing'
Changed the concept of worship into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'

Added ال to specify the one true deity, doubled the ل with shadda, and added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the word بِحَبْلِ (rope/connection) that comes before it in the ayah
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else. Here it connects to Allah's favor (نِعْمَتَ) mentioned in the ayah.
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for attaching the next part. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Adds كُمْ at the end to show this affects multiple people being spoken to. This connects Allah's favor to the group being addressed.

steps.m: when (referring to past events in كُنتُمْ)
steps.n: This word connects to كُنتُمْ (you were) to show when something happened in the past. It's like saying 'Remember the time when you were...'
This root represents the basic concept of existing or being in a state
Changed the root letters to make 'he was' by adding fathas (the small diagonal lines above)
Changed from 'he was' to the command 'be!' - a shorter form used to give orders
Added تُمْ at the end to show this means 'you all' in the past

steps.m: to be hostile or exceed proper bounds

steps.n: The root letters get vowels added, and the last و becomes doubled (ّ) to make it mean 'one who is hostile' (enemy)
Changes from single enemy to plural by adding أ at start and ا after د, making it 'enemies'
Added ً (tanween) at the end to show this word is receiving the action in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter ل makes the meaning stronger - from just bringing together to uniting firmly

steps.m: and then He united (referring to Allah uniting the hearts)
steps.n: The letter فَ (fa) was added at the start to mean 'and then/so', showing this uniting happened after something else
Changed from a verb meaning to a preposition showing location or relationship between things. In this ayah, it refers to the space/relationship between hearts that were brought together.
Added final fatha to show this word's connection to what follows it

steps.m: to turn over, to change direction

steps.n: Root meaning 'to turn/change' becomes the noun 'heart' - like how a heart turns blood around the body
Changes the word from one heart to many hearts by adding و between letters and changing vowels to ُ
Adding genitive case marker
Adding possessive pronoun suffix for 'your (plural masculine)'

steps.m: to enter into a state or time

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Added أ at the beginning to mean 'becoming' or 'entering into' something
Added تُم to show multiple people (you all) did this in the past
Added فَ to show this happened as a result of what came before
Changed root letters into a noun pattern and added ة at the end to mean 'one blessing'
Added بِ to show how the blessing is used or experienced
Added i-sound at the end to connect it to the next word, showing 'blessing of...'
Added هِ to show it belongs to Him (Allah)
Changes from verb root to plural noun meaning brotherhood/companionship

steps.m: brothers (as the object being acted upon)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb فَأَصْبَحْتُم (then you became)

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: Changed from basic root to past tense verb by adding vowels - the u sound (ُ) shows something happened to someone/something
Changed from 'he was' to 'you were' by adding تُ at the end. The و in the middle drops out because it's between two vowels.
Adding مْ makes it refer to multiple people (you all)
Adding وَ connects this word to what came before it in the sentence
This is a primitive preposition, not derived from a root
Added fatha marks to show correct pronunciation
Added superscript alif (ٰ) after the alif maqsurah (ى) to show it's a long vowel sound
Adding vowel marks to make the word readable
The weak letter و at the end changes to ا. This word is connected to حُفْرَةٍ (pit) in the ayah, showing being on the edge of a pit.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he dug' (verb) to 'hole' (noun) by changing the vowels inside the word
Added ة to show this refers to one specific pit

steps.m: a pit/hole (being described by مِّنَ ٱلنَّارِ - of the Fire)
steps.n: Added two dots under the last letter to show it connects with 'of the Fire' that comes after it
This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
The shadda emphasizes the م to show a stronger connection
The fatha at the end connects this word to the next word النَّارِ (the Fire), showing what we're coming from
Changes from basic root to word meaning 'that which gives light'
Adding ٱل makes it specific and doubles the ن with shadda
The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to something else in the sentence (in this case, it follows مِّنَ meaning 'from')

steps.m: he caused to be saved/he rescued
steps.n: Adding أ at the beginning makes it mean 'caused something to happen' - in this case, causing someone to be saved means rescuing them

steps.m: he rescued you all (after being enemies)
steps.n: كم shows who was rescued - in this ayah, it refers to the people who were previously enemies before Allah brought their hearts together

steps.m: then he rescued you all (after being enemies)
steps.n: فَ shows this rescue came after the previous action of Allah bringing their hearts together (فَأَلَّفَ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِكُمْ)
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
The م has a shadda because it comes after a word ending in ن or م (called assimilation)

steps.m: from it/her (referring to حُفْرَةٍ - the pit)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to the pit (حُفْرَةٍ) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Starting with the basic demonstrative word ذا and writing it with an alif khanjariya (ٰ) which is a common spelling variation
Adding لِ changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)
Adding كَ makes it mean 'that specific thing' instead of just 'that'

steps.m: like that (points to how Allah saved you from the fire mentioned in previous ayah)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the beginning changes the meaning to 'like that' or 'similarly', referring back to how Allah saved them from the fire mentioned in the previous part of the ayah

steps.m: to make something clear or distinct

steps.n: Added vowels and doubled the middle letter (shadda) to make it mean 'he made clear' instead of just the basic idea of clarity
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at the start. The ُ ending shows Allah is doing the action of making things clear.
Changed the concept of worship into a word for the one who is worshipped
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال makes it specific and the doubled ل shows the uniqueness and completeness of Allah's divinity.
Added fatha to show the basic meaning of direction or purpose

steps.m: for you all (connected to يُبَيِّنُ - He makes clear)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this is directed to multiple people. This word connects to يُبَيِّنُ (He makes clear) to show who He makes things clear for.

steps.m: to show a sign or mark

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a noun by combining them with special marks and adding ة at the end to make it feminine
Changed from one sign to many signs by removing ة and adding ات
Changed spelling to match Quranic writing style - same meaning, just written differently

steps.m: His signs or marks (referring to Allah's signs)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show these are Allah's signs
This is the base word that expresses hope or purpose

steps.m: perhaps you all/so that you all (subject of تَهْتَدُونَ)
steps.n: Added the plural 'you' pronoun كُمْ to show who the hope/purpose applies to. This word connects to تَهْتَدُونَ (you are guided) in the next ayah.

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Shows someone receiving or seeking guidance themselves rather than guiding others
Changed to present tense and second person (you)
Changed to plural form to show many people are being addressed
Basic command form telling someone to be/exist
Changes from command to present tense, meaning 'you' do something
Adding لْ makes it into a polite command or suggestion
Adding وَ connects this command to the previous phrase
This is a preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or being part of something
Added sukun to show where the word pauses
The letter م gets a shadda because it comes after a word ending in ن - this is a common pronunciation rule

steps.m: from you all (connected to وَلْتَكُن - let there be)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this applies to all the people being addressed. This connects to وَلْتَكُن (let there be) to say 'let there be from among you all'

steps.m: to lead or be a source/reference point

steps.n: Root letters أمم became أُمّ (mother) by adding damma and combining the double م into one letter with shaddah
Added ة to make it refer to a unified group that comes from the same source

steps.n: The word changed to show it belongs to/of something (shown by the -i ending)
The root letters ك-و-ن become كَانَ (he was) in past tense - the و in the middle changes to ا, which happens a lot with similar words

steps.m: it would have been (in response to لَوْ/if)
steps.n: The لَ here is used with لَوْ (if) earlier in the verse to show what would have happened in a hypothetical situation. Together they mean 'if...then it would have been'
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to form the basic noun

steps.m: good (as the object being described in لَكَانَ خَيْرًا - it would have been good)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word is describing 'how something would be' in the phrase 'it would have been good'
Basic preposition showing direction or purpose
Added emphasis by doubling the ل with a shadda
Added هُم at the end to show who the action is for (them)
Basic preposition showing source or origin
Added emphasis on the م with a shadda for pronunciation

steps.m: from them (referring to أَهْلُ ٱلْكِتٰبِ - People of the Book)
steps.n: Added هُمُ at the end to show this refers to 'them' (the People of the Book mentioned earlier in the verse)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed middle vowels to make it mean 'someone who believes' (active participle)
Added ون to show multiple believers
Added ٱلْ to point to specific believers
The vowels َُ show this is about becoming more numerous
Added أ at beginning and changed vowels to make comparative form
Added pronoun هُمُ to show who is being referred to

steps.m: and most of them (of أَهْلُ ٱلْكِتٰبِ)
steps.n: Added conjunction وَ to connect with previous phrase. This refers to most of the People of the Book mentioned earlier.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb

steps.m: one who breaks away from obedience (describes أكثر)
steps.n: Changed to active participle form to describe someone who does this action. This word describes أكثر (most) in the ayah.

steps.m: those who break away from obedience
steps.n: Changed to plural form to show multiple people
Added 'the' at the beginning to make it specific and a final vowel mark. The long alif (ٰ) is just a writing variation.

steps.m: never (with يَضُرُّوكُمْ - they will harm you)
steps.n: This is a negative particle that makes future actions impossible

steps.m: never (with يَضُرُّوكُمْ - they will harm you)
steps.n: Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation. This word comes before يَضُرُّوكُمْ (they will harm you) to say they will never harm you.
Connected the letters and doubled the middle letter to show strong or intense harm
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added و to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: they harm you all (after لَن meaning 'never')
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who is being harmed - it's you all. This word comes after لَن which means it will never happen.
Connected the basic letters with vowels to form the exception particle
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning

steps.m: except for (in relation to يَضُرُّوكُمْ - they will not harm you)
steps.n: Changed the final alif to a long alif to extend the sound. This word creates an exception to the previous statement 'they will not harm you', saying that they will only cause minor harm.
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a noun meaning the result of harming
Added tanween (ً) to show this is any kind of harm. This word is what comes after إِلَّا (except) in this ayah.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition - it shows that something will happen if something else happens first
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement 'they will not harm you except for slight hurt'
Added vowels (a-a) to make it 'he killed' in the past tense
Added alif to show mutual fighting action between two parties
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Changed from singular 'he' to plural 'they' by adding و

steps.m: they fight against you all (after لَن)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who they're fighting against. This word comes after لَن (will not) in the ayah.
Changed from basic root to past tense verb, doubled middle letter to show complete/strong turning away
Added يُ at the start to change from past (he turned) to present/future (he turns)
Added و and ُ to show plural (they)

steps.m: they turn away from you all (object of يَضُرُّوكُمْ)
steps.n: Added كُمُ to show who they turn away from. This word connects to يَضُرُّوكُمْ (they harm you) earlier in the verse, referring to the same group being addressed.
Forms broken plural pattern أَفْعَال. This word is what they turn towards when retreating, as the object of يُوَلُّوكُمُ (they will turn to you).
ٱلْ makes it specific 'the backs' and َ shows it's the object being turned towards when retreating
Basic particle that connects events in sequence

Added damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) to show emphasis and indicate a pause between events. In this ayah, it connects two statements about the disbelievers: first they will turn their backs (يُوَلُّوكُمُ), then after that they will not be helped (لَا يُنصَرُونَ).
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb يُنصَرُونَ (they are helped) into 'they are not helped'
Added fatha mark to show the correct pronunciation

steps.m: to help, support, or give victory

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Adding يُ at the start and changing the vowels shows this is happening to someone now or in the future, rather than them doing it
Adding ونَ shows this is happening to multiple people (masculine or mixed group)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that something received the action instead of doing it
Added feminine marker ت because the subject المَسْكَنَةُ (humiliation/poverty) is feminine. In this ayah, it means humiliation was imposed upon them.
This is a preposition that shows something is placed or imposed on something else. In this ayah, it connects with ضُرِبَتْ (was struck) to show that something was imposed upon someone.
Added ي with sukun (ْ) to prepare the word for connecting with the next part
Added هِمُ which means 'them' when referring to a group. This shows who the action is affecting.
Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Changes from verb (being humble) to noun (state of humiliation)
Added ال to make it specific and shadda on ذ because it comes after ال
Added damma to show this is the subject of the verb ضُرِبَتْ (was struck upon them)

This is a question word asking about location, but in this context it's used with ما to mean 'wherever' rather than just 'where'. It's connected to the verb ثُقِفُوٓا۟ (they are found/encountered) that comes after it.
In this ayah, مَا connects with the verb ثُقِفُوٓا۟ (they are found/encountered) to mean 'wherever they are found'. It's showing location or circumstance rather than asking a question.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it
Added وا to show this happened to a group of people
Added shadda to strengthen the ل sound. This is a particle that shows something is being excluded from what came before.

steps.m: except (excluding what follows from ضُرِبَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلذِّلَّةُ)
steps.n: Added alif at the end to complete the particle. In this ayah, it shows that what follows (being connected to Allah and people) is excluded from the humiliation mentioned before.

steps.m: to tie or bind something with rope

steps.n: The root letters combine to make a noun meaning the thing used for tying
Added بِ at the start to mean 'by/with' and added ٍ at the end to show we mean 'a rope' rather than just 'rope'
This is the basic form of the word meaning 'from' - it shows where something starts or comes from
The shadda (ّ) makes the م sound stronger, which happens because this word comes after a pause in speech
The fatha (َ) at the end shows this word connects to the next word (ٱللَّهِ) meaning 'from Allah'
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the hamza was dropped due to frequent use. The doubled ل shows emphasis.
Added kasra at the end to show possession/connection, and added shadda for emphasis. The initial alif became a wasl form (ٱ) because it connects with the previous word.

steps.m: to bind, connect, or make covenant

steps.n: Changed from action to thing by adding fatha and sukun. In this ayah, it refers specifically to a covenant or connection with Allah.

steps.m: and a rope/covenant (connected to مِنَ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added 'wa' (وَ) meaning 'and' at the start, plus a kasra (ٍ) at the end to show it's 'a rope' (not 'the rope') that connects to 'from Allah' later in the sentence
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
The shadda emphasizes the م, which happens because this word follows a word ending in a similar sound
The fatha is added to connect this word smoothly to the next word (ٱللَّهِ - Allah)

steps.m: to be social, friendly with others

steps.n: Changes from basic concept to describing those who do it. Like changing 'social' to 'social beings' or 'people'
Adds 'the' (ال) at the start, which causes the ن to be doubled when pronounced

steps.m: of the people (connected to مِّنَ meaning 'from')
steps.n: Adding kasra shows this word follows مِّنَ (from) in the ayah, showing where something comes from

steps.m: to return or come back with something

steps.n: The root letters changed to make 'he returned' - the middle letter و turned into ا, which is common for hollow verbs
Added و at the end to show multiple people did the action. Changed the last vowel to ُ to connect with و. The alif gets a special mark (آ) when followed by و.

steps.m: and they returned (with غَضَبٍ/anger from Allah)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'. This word connects to what follows - they returned with Allah's anger.
Root meaning 'anger' changed to a noun with tanwin (double dots under) to show it's receiving the preposition 'with'
Adds preposition ب meaning 'with'
Added fatha at the end to connect this word to what follows it, showing it's connected to the word Allah (ٱللَّهِ)
Added shadda because this word comes after a word ending in ن or tanween, which makes the م sound stronger. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Added ال to make it specific and unique. The hamza is dropped and the lam gets a shadda because this is a special word for God.

steps.m: of Allah (possessed by/connected to وَحَبْلٍ - rope/covenant)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show possession, and added the special beginning alif (ٱ). This word is connected to وَحَبْلٍ (rope/covenant) in this ayah, showing Allah's covenant with people.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice to show the action happened to something

steps.m: it (fem) was struck/set forth (referring to الذِّلَّةُ - humiliation)
steps.n: Added ت at the end to show it's talking about something feminine (humiliation)
Added و to connect this clause with what came before, showing sequence of events
Starting with the basic preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun. This is a spelling change that happens when adding something to the end of على

steps.m: upon them (affected by ضُرِبَتْ - was struck)
steps.n: Added هِمُ at the end to show who is being affected. This word connects to ضُرِبَتْ (was struck) to show who had something struck upon them.

steps.m: to be still, settle down, or rest in a place
steps.n: Basic meaning relates to stillness or settling

steps.m: a place where someone lives or settles
steps.n: Changes verb (to settle) into noun meaning the place where settling happens
Adding ة changes a physical place (house/dwelling) into a state or feeling (being poor/humiliated)
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific state of poverty/humiliation (the one being discussed)

steps.m: the poverty/humiliation (subject of ضُرِبَتْ - was struck upon them)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is the subject of ضُرِبَتْ (was struck/imposed). In English we might say 'poverty was imposed upon them'
Basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show distance
Added لِ to emphasize distance - like saying 'over there' instead of just 'there'

steps.m: that (pointing to what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show we're referring back to something already mentioned - in this case, referring back to the humiliation and anger mentioned in the previous verse
This is the base word that shows confirmation or emphasis
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being talked about (they/them)
Added بِ at the beginning to show reason or cause, changing 'that they' to 'because they'
Basic meaning showing existence or being in a state
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). This is a common change in Arabic.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action. This word connects to يَكْفُرُونَ (they disbelieve) in the ayah, showing who was doing the disbelieving.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense form
Changes كَفَرَ (past) to يَكْفُرُ (present) by adding يَ at start and changing vowels - like changing 'rejected' to 'rejects'

steps.m: they reject/disbelieve (referring to those who rejected Allah's signs)
steps.n: Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action. This connects to the previous part of the ayah talking about those who earned Allah's anger.
Basic meaning relates to signs or symbols
Root letters combine and get stretched into a long 'aa' sound (آ) to make the word for 'sign'
Makes the word plural by adding ات at the end
Adds بِ at start to mean 'by/with' and ِ at end to show it connects to the next word (اللَّهِ)
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that always refers to the One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection, and shadda on ل to emphasize it. The hamza at the start becomes a special connecting hamza (ٱ).
Basic meaning of taking life
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added و at beginning to connect with previous statement
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This refers to those who rejected Allah's signs, as mentioned in the previous ayah.
Changed from action to person who does the action
Changed from single prophet to plural form (like how 'cat' becomes 'cats' but with Arabic pattern)
Adding ال makes it refer to specific prophets being discussed

steps.m: the prophets (object of يَقْتُلُونَ - they kill)
steps.n: Added ending to show this word is receiving the action of killing in the phrase 'they kill the prophets'
Connected letters and added vowel marks to create noun meaning 'other than'
Added kasra at end to show this connects to the word حَقٍّ (right/truth) that comes after it
Added بِ at beginning to mean 'with/by/without' depending on context. Here it combines with غَيْرِ to mean 'without'
Basic meaning of truth or rightness
Root letters combined with a shadda (double letter) to make the noun 'truth'
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this is the object of بِغَيْرِ (without). Together they mean 'without any right/justification'
This is the basic demonstrative word used to point to something nearby, like saying 'this' in English
The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) above the ذ to show it's a long sound
Adding لِ changes 'this' to 'that', like pointing with your finger to something far instead of close

steps.m: that (pointing to the previous statements about their punishment and humiliation)
steps.n: Adding كَ completes the word and makes it point specifically to something mentioned before. In this ayah, it points back to the previous statements about their punishment and humiliation.
This combines the preposition بِ (with/by) with ما (what) to mean 'because of what' or 'due to what'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: they disobeyed (connected to يَكْفُرُونَ - they disbelieve)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. The ي changes to و because it's a weak letter at the end when we add وا. This word is connected to يَكْفُرُونَ (they disbelieve) in the ayah, showing multiple actions they did.
Changed from basic root to past tense by adding vowel marks (fatha) and pronouncing as 'kaana', meaning 'he/it was'
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they)
Added و with shadda at beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: to go beyond limits, to transgress

steps.n: The combination of ا and ت shows doing something intentionally beyond proper bounds
Adding يَ at the start changes it from command form to 'he is doing it now' form
The ون ending shows multiple people doing the action. This verb is connected to كَانُوا (they were) in the ayah, showing continuous past action.
Added vowels to make it mean 'is not' - this is a special word that always means 'not' in the past tense
Changed the final vowel to damma to prepare for plural ending
Added وا at the end to show that 'they' (a group) are doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form a basic past tense verb
Changed the final ي to ا to form a noun describing the state of being equal
Added long alif to emphasize the quality of being equal
Added tanween to show this describes how they are not equal (relates to لَيْسُوا۟ - they are not)
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to. Here it connects to the word أَهْلِ (people) to show which group is being talked about.
Added a shadda (doubling mark) on the م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel (سَوَآءً). This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to dwell, to be settled, to be familiar with

steps.n: Basic noun pattern with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter. This forms the word for 'people/family' - those who dwell together.
Adding kasra at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to what comes after it (الكتاب - the Book)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show the result of writing - a book
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific book
The kasra at the end shows this book belongs to or is connected to أَهْلِ (people), forming 'people of the book'

steps.m: to lead or be a source/origin

steps.n: The root letters combine with a damma (u sound) and doubled middle letter to make the word for 'mother'

steps.m: a community (as a unified group with shared origin)
steps.n: Adding ة changes it from the concept of origin/source to a group unified by that source

steps.m: a community (that stands upright in prayer - subject of قَآئِمَةٌ)
steps.n: Adding tanween shows this is an undefined community. This community is described as قَآئِمَةٌ (standing upright) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the middle letter و to ئ and added alif and kasra to show who does the action. This describes the word أُمَّةٌ (community).
Added ة to make it feminine since it describes أُمَّةٌ (which is feminine)
Added tanween (double damma) to make it indefinite
The alif is written longer (آ) but pronounced the same way - this is just a writing style change. The word describes أُمَّةٌ (community) as standing
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Changes to present tense by adding يَ at start. The middle letter gets sukun (ْ) and the last vowel changes to damma (ُ). The final و shows it's ongoing action.
Adding نَ shows multiple people are doing the action. This refers to أُمَّةٌ قَآئِمَةٌ (an upright community) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Changed the root into a singular noun for one sign/verse
Made the word plural by adding ات. This shows there are multiple signs/verses being recited.
The word gets a kasra (small diagonal line) at the end to show it belongs to Allah. This word means signs/verses and is being read out loud by good people.
Added shadda to emphasize this is the one and only God

steps.m: of Allah (possessed by/connected to what comes before it)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word before it (آيٰتِ - verses), showing these are Allah's verses. The alif at the start changed to a wasla because it comes after another word.
Letters أ-ن-ي were joined together with fatha marks (َ) to make the word أَنَى meaning 'when/how'
Changed the word to show multiple times or periods
Added a long vowel mark to show emphasis and added a final fatha because this word connects to 'the night' after it
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to make the basic word for 'night'
Added 'the' at the beginning and the possession marker at the end because this word belongs to ءَانَآءَ (parts/hours) in the phrase 'parts of the night'
Added diacritics to show this is a pronoun meaning 'they'
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before. This pronoun 'they' refers to the people who are doing the prostrating (يَسْجُدُونَ) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
The ي at the start shows it's happening now or in the future

steps.m: they prostrate (referring to the righteous People of the Book mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Adding ون shows that many people (all male or mixed group) are doing the action
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. The hamza (ء) changes to ؤ when it has a damma.
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This word is connected to يَأْمُرُونَ (they command) in the ayah, showing the same group doing multiple actions.
This is the unique proper name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning of divinity but is used exclusively as God's name.

steps.m: in/by/with Allah (object of يُؤْمِنُونَ - they believe)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show connection or means, and ِ at the end because it's being acted upon by the verb 'believe'. This word is what they believe in.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a noun meaning 'day'
Adding ال makes it specific - 'the day' instead of just any day. The ِ (kasra) at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it.
Adding و connects this word to what came before it

steps.m: to come after or be delayed
