steps.n: Forms active participle pattern فَاعِل showing 'one who/which comes after'. This word describes 'Day' (يَوْم) in the phrase 'the Last Day'.
Adds the definite article to match with يَوْم (Day) which it describes
Adds kasra at the end because this word follows بِ (in/with) in the phrase 'in the Last Day'
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding ي at the start shows this is happening now or in the future
Adding ون makes this plural 'they' form

steps.m: and they command (connected to يُؤْمِنُونَ - they believe)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to the previous verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe), showing these are things the same people do
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added مَ at start and changed vowels to show something that people know and accept as good
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: with/by what is good and right (object of يَأْمُرُونَ - they command)
steps.n: Added بِ at start to show 'with/by' and ِ at end because it follows the word يَأْمُرُونَ (they command)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show ongoing action
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. The ى changes to و because it comes before ون

steps.m: and they stop/prevent (from الْمُنكَرِ - what is wrong)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the start to connect this word to the previous actions in the verse. This word is followed by عَنِ الْمُنكَرِ which tells us what they prevent - wrong things.

steps.m: away from (relates to يَنْهَوْنَ - they forbid)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something is being moved away from or prevented. In this ayah, it connects to the verb يَنْهَوْنَ (they forbid) to show what they are forbidding people from doing.

steps.m: away from (relates to يَنْهَوْنَ - they forbid)
steps.n: Added kasra (ِ) at the end because the next word starts with ال. This makes it easier to pronounce.

steps.m: to reject or disapprove of something

steps.n: Adding مُ at the start and changing vowels turns this into a word describing something that is rejected or disapproved of
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to a specific wrong thing rather than any wrong thing. The hamza at the start (ٱ) is used because this is how it appears in the Quran.

steps.m: of the wrong thing (object of عَنِ)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word follows عَنِ (meaning 'from' or 'away from'). Together they mean 'away from the wrong thing'.

steps.m: to move quickly or rush forward

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding ا changes it from just 'being quick' to 'racing/competing with others'
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels - shows ongoing rushing/hurrying action
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: and they rush/hurry (connected to يُؤْمِنُونَ - they believe)
steps.n: Added و to connect this action with the previous actions in the verse
This is a primitive preposition that is not derived from a root. It exists in this form as a basic building block of Arabic grammar.
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show how it's pronounced
Changed the final ي to ى which is a common spelling variation at the end of words
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form a noun meaning 'good thing'
Added the feminine plural ending ات to show multiple good things
Added ال to make it specific 'the good things'
Added kasra (i sound) at the end because this word is being pulled by في (in/towards) in the ayah, showing movement towards good things
This is a demonstrative pronoun used to point to people or things that are far away, like saying 'those people over there' in English

steps.m: and those (far away) (subject of مِنَ ٱلصَّالِحِينَ)
steps.n: Adding وَ at the start connects this word to what came before it. This word is connected to the next phrase 'among the righteous'
Basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
Added fatha at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word الصَّالِحِينَ (the righteous ones). This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
Added alif after first letter and vowels to make it mean 'someone who is righteous'
Added ين to make it plural, showing it refers to multiple righteous people
Added ال (the) at the beginning to make it 'the righteous ones' instead of just 'righteous ones'
Added fatha (َ) at the end to show it is in the accusative case
This is a question word that can also refer to something unknown or general
Added و at the beginning to connect this to what comes before it. Here it introduces a new clause about whatever good deeds they do.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense base
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on middle letter, and ُ at end

steps.m: they do/will do (subject of فَلَن يُكْفَرُوهُ)
steps.n: Added plural masculine ending وا to show multiple people doing the action. This verb is connected to فَلَن يُكْفَرُوهُ (it will never be denied) in the ayah.

steps.m: from (connects خَيْرٍ to show what they do)
steps.n: This is a helper word that shows where something starts or what it's made of. Here it connects to the word خَيْرٍ (good) to show what type of actions they do.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun. This word follows مِنْ (from) in the ayah.
Added two dots under the word (tanween) to show it's 'any good thing' - this happens after مِنْ (from)
This is a word that shows something will never happen in the future. Here it's saying the good deeds will never be denied.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before - because they do good deeds, then as a result those deeds will never be denied.

steps.m: to deny, to reject, to be ungrateful

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels to show something is being acted upon
Added و and ا at the end to make it plural 'they' instead of 'it'

steps.m: it will be denied to them (object of فَلَن)
steps.n: Added هُ to show what is being denied. This word comes after فَلَن (so/then never) in the ayah, showing what will never happen.

steps.m: Allah (the proper name of God in Islam)
steps.n: This is the base form of Allah's name. It comes from combining ال (the) with إله (deity), but is now treated as a complete proper name.
The ُ (damma) at the end shows that Allah is the subject doing the action in this sentence - specifically being the one who is عَلِيمٌۢ (knowing/All-Knowing).

steps.m: and Allah (as the subject of عَلِيمٌۢ/knowing)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this part to what came before it, like saying 'and'. The ٱ is just a special way of writing the first letter when it comes after وَ.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Pattern فَعِيل adds intensive meaning to base concept of knowledge
The two dots on top show this is describing something without saying 'the' before it. This word is describing Allah, telling us that He is very knowledgeable. It matches Allah in the nominative case.

steps.m: to guard against harm or evil

steps.n: Added مُ at start and doubled ت to make it mean 'someone who protects themselves from wrong things'
Added ين to show we're talking about many people who guard against evil

steps.m: the ones who guard against evil
steps.n: Added ال to point to specific God-fearing people

steps.m: with/by the ones who guard against evil
steps.n: Added بِ at start to show relationship and َ at end to show this word is being acted upon

This is an emphasis word that makes the statement after it stronger and more certain. In this ayah, it emphasizes that those who disbelieve (كَفَرُوا۟) will definitely not be helped by their wealth or children.
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ذ letter to connect them smoothly
Added ين at the end to make it plural. This word points to the disbelievers who are mentioned right after it in كَفَرُوا (they disbelieved)

steps.m: to reject, deny, or be ungrateful

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb. This word is the subject of لَن تُغْنِىَ (will never avail) in this ayah.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. The small mark after وا (۟) is just to help with pronunciation.

This is a particle that makes a strong statement that something definitely won't happen in the future. Here it negates the verb يُغْنِيَ (will suffice/avail), saying their wealth and children will definitely not help them.
Changed root letters into past tense verb meaning 'he helped' by adding the vowel marks a-a
Changed to present tense with prefix يُ and vowel pattern changes
Changed from 'he' to 'she' by replacing يُ with تُ at start
Added fatha at end to show this is in the subjunctive mood (after أَن, لَن, etc.)
This is a preposition that shows something moving away from or separating from something else

steps.m: from them/away from them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in كَفَرُوا)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this applies to 'them' (the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the verse)
The root letters م-و-ل combine to make مال (māl), where the و changes to ا in the middle. This is a common pattern for nouns meaning 'things you can have or own'.
Changed from singular to plural by adding أ at start and bringing back و. The م gets a sukun (little circle) to make it easier to say.
Added two damma marks (ٌ) at the end to show this is any wealth, not a specific wealth
Added هُمْ at the end to show possession, with ُ connecting it to the word
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Extended the alif to emphasize the negation. This is a common spelling variation in the Quran.
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation with the previous statement about those who disbelieve (كَفَرُوا)
Changed from root meaning 'to give birth' into the simple noun meaning 'child' by adding vowel marks
Changed to broken plural form by adding hamza at start and alif after second root letter
The 'a' sound (alif) in the middle changed shape and a 'u' sound was added at the end
Added attached pronoun هُم meaning 'their'
Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to. Here it connects with ٱللَّهِ to show something coming from Allah.
Added emphasis on the م with a shadda mark to strengthen pronunciation when connecting with the next word ٱللَّهِ
Changed إِلَه to اللَه, which is a unique name specifically referring to The One True God in Islam
Added a kasra (small diagonal line) at the end to show it belongs to something, and added a shadda (small w) on the ل. This word is connected to مِنَ (meaning 'from') in this ayah.
Adding vowels to make the word pronounceable

Added the accusative case ending to show this is receiving the action. In this context, it means 'anything at all' - emphasizing that neither wealth nor children will help the disbelievers in any way against Allah.

steps.m: those (pointing to specific people far away)
steps.n: Added vowel marks and elongation sign to show this is a demonstrative pronoun that points to something far away. In this verse, it points back to 'those who disbelieved' mentioned earlier.

steps.m: and those (pointing to specific people far away)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. In this verse, it connects to the previous statement about the disbelievers' wealth and children not helping them.

steps.m: to accompany closely or be a close companion

steps.n: Basic past tense verb showing someone accompanied or befriended another
Changes the verb into a noun meaning 'one who does the action'
Changes the singular noun into its plural form using a special pattern for groups
Added the ُ ending to show this is the subject of هُمْ (they) in the phrase 'they are the companions of the fire'
Root meaning 'light' changed to make the noun 'fire' by putting a fatha on both letters
Added -i sound at the end because it comes after the word 'from' (min)
Added shadda to emphasize the intensity of the hellfire being described
Added ال to specify this refers to hellfire specifically
This is a pronoun that refers to a group of people (all male or mixed gender). In this ayah, it refers back to الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (those who disbelieved).
Basic preposition meaning 'in' or 'within'

steps.m: in it (feminine) (referring to النَّارِ - the Fire)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show this means 'in it'. The 'it' refers to النَّارِ (the Fire) which is feminine in Arabic, so we use the feminine form.

steps.m: to stay forever, to be eternal

steps.n: This word tells us what the people of the fire (هم) are like - they stay in it forever. The alif (ا) and kasra (ِ) work together to show who does the action.
The ون ending shows we're talking about many people, not just one person
Basic root meaning showing the concept of similarity or comparison
Connected the letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (meaning 'he resembled') to a noun (meaning 'example/likeness'). The damma ending shows it's the subject that connects to what they spend in the ayah.

steps.m: what/that which (connects to يُنفِقُونَ - they spend)
steps.n: This is a special connecting word that introduces what comes after it. Here it connects to يُنفِقُونَ (they spend), making the meaning 'what they spend' or 'that which they spend'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he spent) to present tense (he spends) by adding يُ at the start. The middle letter gets a different vowel mark too.

steps.m: they spend/are spending (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in the surrounding verses)
steps.n: Adding ون shows that many people (all male or mixed group) are doing the action

steps.m: in/within (connects هٰذِهِ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ to what comes before)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something is inside, within, or part of something else. Here it connects 'this worldly life' to what they spend.
Basic demonstrative word meaning 'this'
Added ذا to specify something nearby
Changed ending to هِ and added ِ to make it feminine. This word describes 'life' (الْحَيَوٰةِ) which is feminine in Arabic.
Root meaning 'live' becomes a noun 'life' by adding و and ة
Added ال to make it specific ('the life') and ِ to show it's connected to الدُّنْيَا (worldly). The و is written as وٰ which is a common spelling variation.
Changed the root letters to make a word for things that are closer to us (like this life compared to the afterlife)
Added ال to make it specific. In this ayah, it describes 'the life' (ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ) to show we're talking about life in this world versus the afterlife.
Adding fatha vowels creates a basic noun meaning 'example'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it

steps.m: like an example (of رِيحٍ - a wind)
steps.n: Added كَ at the beginning to mean 'like' or 'similar to'. This word is comparing what was mentioned before to رِيحٍ (a wind) that comes after it.
Changed و to ي and added kasra to create a noun meaning the thing that blows/moves through air

steps.m: a wind (that belongs to something else)
steps.n: The ٍ shows this is 'a wind' (not specific) and it belongs to or relates to something else in the sentence
Basic preposition that shows location or containment. This word has no root since it's a basic building block of Arabic.
Added the kasra vowel mark to show how the word is pronounced

steps.m: in it (referring to الْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا - this worldly life)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to 'this worldly life' mentioned earlier in the verse. The ها is feminine because الْحَيَوٰةِ (life) is feminine in Arabic.

steps.m: to be cold, make a harsh sound

steps.n: The doubled ر with kasra (ِ) under first letter creates a noun describing an intense cold, especially used for freezing wind

steps.m: an intense cold (that destroys the crops in this ayah)
steps.n: Adding the two dots (tanween) at the end shows this is a noun describing the cold wind that hits the crops
Connected the root letters with vowels. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Adding أَ at the beginning makes the verb mean 'to reach or afflict something' rather than just 'to strike'
Added ت at the end to show the subject is feminine (the wind) that did the action
Changed the verb into a noun by adding vowel marks. This shows the result of cultivation - like the crops or farmland.

steps.m: cultivation, crops (being affected by أَصَابَتْ - struck)
steps.n: Added a fatha at the end to show this is what was affected by the wind. In the ayah, this word is what the harmful wind (رِيحٍ فِيهَا صِرٌّ) struck and destroyed.

steps.m: to stand/rise up (especially as a group)

steps.n: Added vowels to make a noun meaning a group of people who stand together. The و is part of the root and stays as a long vowel.
Added the tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show this word belongs to something else (the حَرْث/cultivation mentioned before it)
Added fatha marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added وا at the end to show a group of people (they) did the action. This word is connected to أَصَابَتْ (it struck) in this ayah, describing what a group did.
Changes from root to noun meaning 'soul/self'
Changes the singular 'self' into plural 'selves' using the أَفْعُل pattern
Adds هُمْ to show possession ('their') and َ to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يَظْلِمُونَ (they wrong)

steps.m: to destroy or cause to perish

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start changes meaning from 'perish' to 'cause to perish'
Adding تْ at the end shows a female did the action
فَ connects this to the previous thought, showing what happened next
هُ means 'him' - shows who received the action
This is a particle used for negation. In this ayah, it negates the verb ظَلَمَهُمُ (wronged them), showing that Allah did not wrong them.
The و connects this phrase to the previous statement, showing contrast between what Allah did and didn't do

steps.m: to do wrong or act unjustly to someone

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is something that happened in the past. This word describes what the people did in the story before the wind struck their crops.
Added هُمُ at the end to show who was wronged (them). The ُ at the end of هُمُ shows this is connected to Allah (اللَّهُ) who comes next in the ayah.
Changed from a verb root meaning 'to worship' into a noun meaning 'god' by adding special vowel marks
Added ال to make it specific and changed the first letter, making it a unique name for The One True God
Added shadda to emphasize the uniqueness and greatness

steps.m: Allah (as the doer of ظَلَمَهُمُ - wronged them)
steps.n: Changed alif to wasl-alif and added damma to show Allah is the subject (doer) of the verb ظَلَمَهُمُ (wronged them) in this ayah

steps.m: but, however (contrasts with وَمَا ظَلَمَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ)
steps.n: This is a conjunction particle that introduces a contrast with the previous statement. Here it contrasts with 'Allah did not wrong them'

steps.m: and but, but rather (connects to وَمَا ظَلَمَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ)
steps.n: وَ adds 'and' to connect this contrasting statement with the previous one, showing the complete thought: 'Allah did not wrong them, but rather they wrong themselves'
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Changed to broken plural pattern by adding hamza at start and changing middle vowel
Added هُمْ at the end to show possession and fatha before it. This word is being acted upon by the verb ظَلَمُوا (they wronged)

steps.m: to wrong, to be unfair, to harm

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fathas to make a past-tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people (3 or more) are doing the action. This verb connects to أَنفُسَهُمْ (themselves) in the ayah, showing who they wrong.
Adding damma to show it's the subject
Vocative particle added to call attention
Adds ها to make it a complete vocative phrase
Starting with the basic word that points to someone or something. Added kasra and ي to make it work with singular things or people.
Added ن to make it refer to many people or things instead of just one
Added ال at the beginning to point to specific people - in this case, it's pointing to the believers who are being addressed
Changed the writing style slightly - the ل gets a shadda and the first alif becomes a wasl alif. This doesn't change the meaning, just how it's written and pronounced.

steps.m: to cause to feel safe/to believe
steps.n: Adding long alif at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Added وا۟ at the end to show many people did this action. This word is the subject of the command 'do not take' (لَا تَتَّخِذُوا۟) in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that tells us something should not happen. Here it's telling the believers not to take (تَتَّخِذُوا۟) certain people as close friends.
Added fatha mark on the ل to show how it's pronounced
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added اِت at the start to show someone is taking something for their own use/benefit
Changed to present tense with تَ prefix for 'you' and changed middle vowel to kasra
Added وا to show plural 'you all'
This root relates to things being inside or hidden from view
Added kasra and alif to create a noun meaning a group of people who are close or internal. This word is being acted on by the verb تَتَّخِذُوا (do not take).

steps.m: a close friend/confidant (acted on by تَتَّخِذُوا)
steps.n: Added ة to specify a single close friend or confidant rather than the whole group

steps.m: a close friend/confidant (as object of تَتَّخِذُوا)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this word is the direct object of the verb تَتَّخِذُوا (do not take)
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from outside or beyond something else. Here it connects to دُونِكُمْ (other than you/besides you)
The letter م gets doubled (with shadda ّ) because it comes after a word ending in ن - this is a common pronunciation rule

steps.m: to be below or less than something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'below/lesser than'
Gets an 'i' sound at the end because it's connected to another word after it
Adds كُمْ to show this refers to multiple people being addressed, changing the meaning to 'other than you all'
This is a basic negative particle that makes the verb after it negative. Here it makes تَتَّخِذُوا۟ (take) into 'do not take'.
The root letters show the basic idea of holding back or not giving full effort
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and adjusting vowels
Added ون at the end to change from 'he' to 'they' (plural)

steps.m: they spare no effort against you
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who is affected by their action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he ruined' (a verb) to 'ruin' (the concept/noun)
The ًا ending shows this is a specific instance of ruin/damage that is happening, not just the general concept of ruin/damage
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Doubling of middle root letter intensifies the meaning
Changes from singular (he wished) to plural (they wished)
This is a particle word that connects to the word عَنِتُّمْ (you suffered hardship) to show what they wanted
Added fatha mark on م to show correct pronunciation
Added vowels (fatha-kasra-fatha) to make it mean 'he faced hardship' in the past
Changed ending to show 'you all' instead of 'he' did the action
Added م with sukun to complete the 'you all' ending
This is a particle that adds emphasis to the verb بَيَّنَّا (we have made clear) after it. It shows the action has definitely happened.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end of the word.
Added ت to show the subject is feminine (البَغْضَآءُ meaning 'hatred'). The final ا is dropped when ت is added.
Added kasra to help connect with the next word in speech
Forms an abstract noun indicating the concept or state of intense hatred
The word gets a 'u' ending (ُ) because it's doing the action of 'becoming apparent'
Added definite article ٱلْ to specify this particular hatred that has become apparent from their mouths, as mentioned in the ayah
This is a basic preposition particle that shows where something starts or comes from
Added vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce it - ِ (kasra) under م and ْ (sukun) on ن
Added vowel marks to connect the letters into a basic singular noun
The word for 'mouth' changes to 'mouths' by adding 'a' at the start and making it longer in the middle - this is a special way to make plurals for body parts
Adding the kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word belongs to something else, like saying 'of mouths'
Adding هِمْ at the end shows the mouths belong to them
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation of the question word ما

steps.m: and what (that which is hidden in صُدُورُهُمْ)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This وَمَا refers to what is hidden in their hearts (صُدُورُهُمْ) in this ayah.
This is the basic concept of hiding or concealing
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding تُ at start
Adds ي to show it's either 'you' (masculine) or 'she' doing the action
Changes the singular word 'chest' into multiple 'chests' by adding ُ (damma) after first and second root letters and و between them

steps.m: chests (subject of تُخْفِى - what they hide)
steps.n: Shows this word is doing the action in the sentence - these are the chests that are hiding something
Adds هُمْ at the end to show these chests belong to them (referring to the hypocrites mentioned earlier in the ayah)

steps.m: to be large/great in size, amount, or status

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb showing something becoming great
Added أ at beginning and changed vowels to make it mean 'more/greater than'
Added ُ to show this word is doing the action in the sentence - it is what 'has appeared' (بَدَتِ)
This word adds emphasis to the verb that follows it (بَدَتِ). It's like adding 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English to show something definitely happened.
Added shadda (ّ) on ي to show intensive meaning - doing something thoroughly or to many things

steps.m: we made (something) very clear (connected to الْءَايٰتِ - the signs/verses)
steps.n: Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word connects to الْءَايٰتِ (the signs/verses) which comes right after it in the ayah.
Added fatha to show the basic preposition meaning
Added كُمُ to show this is directed at multiple people being addressed

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident

steps.n: Root letters combine with long 'aa' sound and 'ah' ending to make the word for 'sign'
Makes the word plural to show multiple signs
Makes it specific by adding 'the'
Changes to show this word is being acted upon by the verb بَيَّنَّا (we have made clear)
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects with تَعْقِلُونَ (you use reason/understand) to mean 'if you use reason/understand'
Added basic past tense voweling to connect the letters
Changed from past tense 'he was' to command form 'be!' by dropping letters and changing vowels
Added ت at the end to change from a command ('be!') to past tense ('was')
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this is the subject doing the action

steps.m: to understand, to reason, to comprehend

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to start with تَ (ta) to mean 'you' instead of 'he', and vowels changed to match present tense
Added ون to show multiple people are being addressed
The basic attention-drawing particle with alif extension mark (similar to saying 'hey' to get attention)
Added elongation mark (ٓ) to emphasize the call for attention
Added أَ as a connecting particle between the attention-drawer and the pronoun
Added the plural pronoun 'you all' (أَنتُمْ) to specify who is being addressed
This is the basic form of the demonstrative pronoun that points to things. The damma (ُ) shows it's being used as a subject.
The long alif (آ) shows that what we're pointing to is far away

steps.m: those (far away) (subject of تُحِبُّونَهُمْ - you love them)
steps.n: The ءِ ending shows we're pointing to multiple things or people. In this ayah, it refers to the people being discussed who the believers love but who don't love them back.

steps.m: to feel deep affection or care for something/someone

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Double middle letter shows stronger meaning
Added تُ at start and changed vowels to show present tense and second person
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Added هُمْ to show who is being loved
This is a negative particle that means 'not' or 'no'. It negates what comes after it in the ayah.

steps.m: and not (they do not love you)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This connects to يُحِبُّونَكُمْ (they love you) to show 'and they do not love you'.

steps.m: to love deeply or have strong affection

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Combined the double ب into one letter with shadda for stronger meaning
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added plural masculine ending to show multiple people doing the action
Added object pronoun to show who is being loved
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by adding تُ at start, and made it present tense by adding hamza
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Added و to connect this with the previous statement. This word connects to الْكِتٰبِ (the Book) in the ayah.

steps.m: book, writing, scripture (object of تُؤْمِنُونَ - you believe)
steps.n: Changes the action of writing into the thing that is written. This word is what you believe in (تُؤْمِنُونَ).
ٱلْ means 'the' - it makes the book specific. The alif has a special mark (waṣla) showing it connects with the previous word.
بِ adds meaning of 'with' or 'by' to the noun
The kasra ending shows this word is affected by the بِ before it. The alif is written as a long ā (ٰ) in this word.
Added damma and doubled the middle letter to create the word meaning 'all'
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added pronoun 'hi' to show this refers to 'the Book' (الكتاب) mentioned earlier
This is a time word that means 'when' or 'whenever'. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when the hypocrites meet the believers.
Adding وَ connects this to the previous statement about how the hypocrites don't love the believers, showing another aspect of their behavior
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
The weak letter ي changes to و because it's at the end of a plural past tense verb

steps.m: they met you all (when they meet you all)
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows who was met. The meaning in parentheses shows this word's connection to إِذَا (when) in the verse.
Changed the root letters to past tense by adding vowels and turning و into ا in the middle
Added وا at the end to show many people (they) did the action
Added optional marks (small و above alif and elongation mark) that don't change meaning

steps.m: to cause to feel secure, to believe
steps.n: Adding long alif at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'

steps.m: we believed (what was said by قَالُوا)
steps.n: Added نَّا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This is what they said when they met the believers, as shown by قَالُوا (they said).

steps.m: when (introduces what they do when meeting the believers)
steps.n: This is a word that introduces a time condition - it shows when something happens and implies that what follows is connected to that time. In this ayah, it introduces what the hypocrites do when they meet the believers.

steps.m: and when (connects to their previous actions mentioned)
steps.n: Added و to connect this with their previous actions mentioned in the ayah (that they don't love the believers even though the believers love them)
Added fatha marks to connect the letters and show past tense. The weak letter و changes to ا at the end.
Added وْا۟ at the end to show multiple people did the action. The و returns because it's followed by another letter.
The middle letter gets doubled (with shadda) to show intensity of the action
Converting singular past tense to plural past tense by adding وا suffix
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of, over, or affecting something else. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning

steps.m: upon you all (affecting the fingers/أَنَامِلَ in this ayah)
steps.n: Added كُمُ to show this affects multiple people being spoken to. In this ayah, it's describing how the fingers (أَنَامِلَ) are being bitten in anger.
Plural form of fingertip (أُنْمُلَة) - like changing 'finger' to 'fingers' but in a special Arabic way
Added ال which became ٱلْ due to connecting rules, making it refer to specific fingertips

steps.m: the fingertips (object of عَضُّوا - they bit)
steps.n: Added َ (fatha) to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb عَضُّوا (they bit)
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or originates
Changed ending to connect smoothly with the next word الْغَيْظِ (rage/anger). This combination means 'from rage/anger'

steps.m: to rage, to be filled with anger

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun showing the intense feeling or state of anger. This word follows مِنَ (from) in the ayah.
The kasra ending shows this word is connected to or possessed by something else, or comes after certain words like مِنَ (from)

steps.m: the rage, the intense anger (after مِنَ)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to a specific rage - in this context, it refers to the rage felt by those who bite their fingertips in anger when they are alone, as mentioned in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, 3) adding sukun on last letter. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak.
Added damma to create basic command form

steps.m: die, all of you! (command to those who bite their fingertips in rage)
steps.n: Added plural masculine ending to make command apply to group. This is directed at those mentioned earlier who bite their fingertips in rage.

steps.m: to be intensely angry, to rage with fury

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun. This word describes the intense anger mentioned in the ayah that makes them bite their fingertips.
Added preposition بِ which here shows they should die with/in their rage. This بِ connects to the command مُوتُوا (die) in the ayah.

steps.m: with your rage (referring to the hypocrites' anger)
steps.n: Added كُمْ meaning 'your (plural)' to show this rage belongs to the hypocrites being addressed. The ِ (kasra) before كُمْ is needed because it follows the preposition بِ.

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizes اللَّهَ عَلِيمٌۢ - Allah is knowing)
steps.n: This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain and definite. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah is definitely knowing what's in the hearts.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the ل is doubled.
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily). Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it connects with previous word.

steps.m: one who knows deeply (attribute of Allah)
steps.n: Adding ي between second and third letters with vowels makes this describe someone who has this quality very strongly. When used for Allah, it means He has perfect and complete knowledge of everything.
The tanween (double damma) shows this is describing Allah's general and complete attribute of knowledge, not limited to any specific instance of knowing

steps.m: that which (is inside الصُّدُورِ - the chests/hearts)
steps.n: This is a special word used to refer to the essential nature or contents of something

steps.m: with what (is inside الصُّدُورِ - the chests/hearts)
steps.n: Added بِ meaning 'with' and ِ because it's after a preposition
Added vowels to the root letters to make the word for chest/breast
Changed from single chest (صَدْر) to plural chests (صُدُور) by changing the middle vowels
Added ٱل to make it definite and doubled the first letter with shadda
Added kasra at the end because this word is possessed by ذَاتِ in the phrase ذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ (what is in the chests)

This is a conditional particle that introduces the first part of an if-then statement. Here it connects two ideas: IF something good touches you (تَمْسَسْكُمْ حَسَنَةٌ), THEN it grieves them (تَسُؤْهُمْ)
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Added تَ at start to show present tense, and sukun marks to show this is conditional (because it comes after إِن meaning 'if')

steps.m: it touches/affects you all (after إِن meaning 'if')
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this affects 'you all'. This word follows إِن (if), showing this is a conditional statement - 'if it touches/affects you all'

steps.m: to be good, beautiful, or excellent

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Added ة to make it a feminine noun referring to one specific good thing or deed

steps.m: a good deed/thing (that affects تَمْسَسْكُمْ - touches you all)
steps.n: Added two small dashes on top (double damma) to show this word is doing something in the sentence, like a good deed affecting you
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding the vowel 'aa' in the middle
Changed vowels to make it present tense

steps.m: it makes them sad (referring to good fortune making the disbelievers sad)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being made sad. In this ayah, this refers to how any good fortune that comes to the believers makes the disbelievers sad.
This is a combination of two particles: و (and) + إن (if)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding تُ at start and changing vowels. The weak letter و becomes ي in present tense.
Changed to a shorter form by removing the vowel at the end and dropping the middle letter ي. This happens in 'if' statements.

steps.m: it befalls you all (in conditional after إِن)
steps.n: Added pronoun suffix كُمْ meaning 'you all'. This word is conditional because it comes after إِن (if).
The و changes to ي and gets doubled with shadda to show intensity
Adding ة makes it a specific instance of badness - a bad deed
The tanween shows this is indefinite (any bad deed) and is the subject doing the action in تُصِبْكُمْ (befalls you)
Basic past tense form indicating temporary emotional state
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from singular to plural by adding وا at the end
Basic preposition showing how something is done or used

steps.m: with it/her (referring to سَيِّئَةٌ - bad thing)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to the bad thing (سَيِّئَةٌ) mentioned earlier in the verse. This shows what they are happy with.
This is a compound particle made of 'wa' (and) plus 'in' (if)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense with 'you' as the doer by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people are being addressed
These three letters together represent the basic idea of protection or guarding against something harmful
Connected the letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added pattern that shows doing something to yourself, with the ت merging with the following letter
Changed to present tense and second person (you)
Changed to plural 'you all' form
Added 'and' at beginning to connect with previous phrase
This is a negative particle that makes something 'not' happen. In this ayah, it negates يَضُرُّكُمْ (harm you), making the meaning 'will not harm you'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he harms you all (object of لَا)
steps.n: The middle letter ر is doubled (with shaddah), and كُمْ is added at the end to mean 'you all' as the object being harmed. The word is negated by لَا (not) in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to turn the root into a noun meaning the plot itself rather than the action of plotting
Added damma to show this is the subject (doer) of لَا يَضُرُّ (will not harm) in this ayah
Added pronoun to show whose plot it is
Root meaning related to willing or wishing
The root letters were given vowels (َ and ْ) to make the basic word for 'thing'. The hamza (ء) at the end is part of the original root.
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon. In this ayah, it means 'anything' that their plotting won't harm you.
Added kasra vowel mark to help with pronunciation

steps.m: indeed/verily (emphasizing what follows: اللَّهَ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطٌ)
steps.n: Added shadda to make the meaning stronger. This particle emphasizes that the statement that follows it (Allah encompasses what they do) is definitely true.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for the one true God. The hamza is dropped and the lam gets a shadda.
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily). Also changed alif to wasl alif for pronunciation.
This is a relative pronoun that can mean 'what' or 'whatever' depending on context. In this ayah, it refers to what they (the disbelievers) are doing.
Added the preposition بِ (bi) which shows how something is done. Here it connects to يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/are doing) in the next part of the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create basic past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show ongoing action in present time

steps.m: they do/act/work (referring to what they do in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at start and ِ after second root letter to show this describes someone/something that does the action
The weak letter و changes to ي due to being between two kasras

steps.m: All-Encompassing (as an attribute of Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: The tanween shows this is describing Allah's complete and perfect encompassing of all things
This is a time word that points to a specific moment in the past. Here it introduces the time when the Prophet departed.

steps.m: and when (connecting to غَدَوْتَ - you departed)
steps.n: The و at the beginning connects this phrase to the previous story, showing this is another important event being mentioned

steps.m: to leave or go out early in the morning
he left early in the morning
steps.n: Root letters got fatha vowels (َ) to make 'he' past tense verb

steps.m: you left early in the morning
steps.n: Adding تَ at the end changes it from 'he' to 'you' (masculine)

steps.m: you left early in the morning
steps.n: Word is already in correct form as second person masculine past tense
This is a basic preposition showing where something starts from. In this ayah, it connects 'your family' (أَهْلِكَ) to 'you left early' (غَدَوْتَ), showing you left from your family.
Root letters أ-ه-ل form the basic noun 'family/household' with vowel marks added. The word is possessed by 'your' (ك) in the phrase 'from your family'.
The كَ at the end shows it belongs to 'you' (if speaking to one man). The word comes after the preposition مِنْ which puts it in the genitive case.

steps.m: to settle or prepare a place

steps.n: The doubled و makes the meaning more intensive - to really establish someone somewhere
Added تُ at start to mean 'you' and changed vowels to show ongoing action (present tense) instead of completed action
Added ئ to show the original root letter that was hidden

steps.m: to trust, feel safe, be faithful

steps.n: Changes the word to mean someone who does the action of believing/having faith
Makes the word refer to more than one male believer
Shows we're talking about a specific group of believers, not just any believers

steps.m: the believers (object of تُبَوِّئُ - you were assigning)
steps.n: Shows these believers are receiving the action of being assigned positions by the Prophet
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added مَ at start to make it mean 'place where sitting happens'
Added ا after ق to start making it plural
Added fatha at end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb تُبَوِّئُ (you were assigning)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes to show mutual fighting action between two or more parties
The لِ prefix is added to indicate purpose or destination
The word 'the' (ال) is added to make it specific fighting, and the two ل letters combine into one
The genitive case ending is added due to the preceding preposition لِ
This is the base form of Allah's name. It's a proper noun that doesn't come from a root.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added damma at the end to show Allah is the subject doing something in this sentence

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to mean 'and'. The word connects to سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (All-Hearing, All-Knowing) which describe Allah.

steps.m: One who hears intensively (attribute of اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added vowels and ي to show this describes someone who hears in an intense or complete way. This word describes Allah in this ayah.
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is a complete description. When used for Allah, this means He hears everything completely.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who knows deeply and completely (attribute of اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Changed to show this is a permanent, intense quality of Allah
The tanween shows this is one of Allah's attributes being used as a description
This is a time particle that connects two events in the past. Here it introduces the time when the two groups attempted something, connecting it to what was mentioned before.
Connected the root letters with fathas to make a basic past tense verb
Combined the double م into one letter with shadda for emphasis
Added feminine marker ت to show the subject is طَّآئِفَتَانِ (two groups)
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters

steps.m: one who goes around (doer of همَّت)
steps.n: The word changed from a past tense verb 'went around' to a noun meaning 'one who goes around' by adding an ا after the first letter and an ِ after the second letter. This person/thing is doing the action in the sentence.
Adding ة makes this a feminine noun referring to a group/party
Added dual marker انِ to make it refer to two groups in nominative case
Added emphasis mark (shadda) on first letter and stretched the alif to show importance of these two groups
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or originates

steps.m: from you all (referring to two groups mentioned as طَّآئِفَتَانِ)
steps.n: كُمْ means 'you all' when added to the end. In this ayah, it refers back to the two groups (طَّآئِفَتَانِ) who were thinking about giving up.

steps.m: that (introduces تَفْشَلَا/they both would lose heart)
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces what might happen - in this case, it connects to the next word تَفْشَلَا (they both would lose heart). It shows what two groups almost did.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
The تَ at the start changes it from 'he' to 'you/she', and the sukun appears because أَن makes it focus on the possibility of something happening

steps.m: both of them lose heart (after أَن)
steps.n: The alif at the end shows this refers to both groups mentioned earlier (طَّآئِفَتَانِ - two parties)
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. Unlike other Arabic words, it doesn't come from a three-letter root.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of this sentence)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) at the end to show Allah is the subject doing the action in this sentence

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of this sentence)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to mean 'and', connecting this part to what came before it
Added basic vowels to connect the letters
Added shadda to show someone who does the protection
Added damma to show this is the subject doing the protecting

steps.m: protector of them both (referring to الطَّائِفَتَانِ - the two groups)
steps.n: Added هُمَا to show this is the protector of both groups mentioned in the ayah
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or dependent on something else
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the ل is doubled.
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection, and changed initial alif to wasl form since it connects to previous word

steps.m: to depend on or entrust something to someone

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger
Adding تَ at the beginning shows doing the action to/for oneself
Changed from past tense to present tense by adding يَ at the start
Adds 'so/then' to connect this action with what came before

steps.m: so let him put complete trust
steps.n: Adds 'let/should' to make it a command

steps.m: to trust, feel secure, or believe

steps.n: Forms the active participle showing who does the action
Adds 'u' sound at the end to show this word is doing the action (like adding -er in English)
Adds ون at the end to make it mean 'many believers' instead of just one
Makes the word definite by adding ال. This word refers to the faithful believers mentioned in this ayah.
This is a particle of emphasis that strengthens the meaning of what follows. Here it emphasizes Allah's help (نَصَرَكُمُ) that is mentioned next.

steps.m: certainly/indeed with extra emphasis (emphasizes نَصَرَكُمُ that follows)
steps.n: Adding لَ makes the emphasis even stronger, like saying 'most certainly' instead of just 'certainly'

steps.m: and certainly/and indeed (emphasizes نَصَرَكُمُ that follows)
steps.n: وَ connects this statement to what came before it, adding the meaning of 'and'
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This refers to Allah's help at Badr.
The word changed from 'he helped' to 'He helped you all' by adding كُمُ (kum) at the end to show who received the help
Changed the word from an action (to worship) into a thing (one who is worshipped)
This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it specific, and the doubled ل shows the word's special importance.

steps.m: Badr (the place of the famous battle)
steps.n: This is the name of a location where an important battle took place during the time of Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). The battle is mentioned in the Quran because Allah helped the Muslims achieve victory there.

steps.m: at Badr (location where Allah helped you)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'at' and tanween (ٍ) at the end. This shows where Allah gave victory to the Muslims, connecting to نَصَرَكُمُ (helped you) in the ayah.
This is a pronoun that means 'you' when talking to multiple people. In this ayah, it's describing who was in a state of being few/weak.
The وَ connects this phrase to what came before it in the ayah, showing that Allah helped them at Badr while they were in a state of being few/weak.
Changed root letters into a past tense verb by adding vowels and doubling the last letter
Changed from an action (becoming humble) to describing someone as humble by adding ي
Changed to plural by adding أَ at start and ة at end, also doubled the ل (laam) letter
Added tanween to show this is a general description

steps.m: to protect oneself or be careful

steps.n: Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Added reflexive meaning (doing action to oneself)
Added فَ to show this follows from previous statement
Changed from 'he protected himself' to 'you all protect yourselves!' - making it a command for many people
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' - this is a unique word that became the proper name for the One True God in Islam
Added the fatha at the end to show Allah is being talked about as the object in this sentence. Also added the shadda to emphasize the L sound and changed the alif to a special connecting form.
This word shows hope or expectation for the following verb تَشْكُرُونَ (you give thanks)

steps.m: so that you all might (give thanks)
steps.n: لَعَلَّ + كُمْ (you all) = perhaps/maybe you all - shows hope for multiple people
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' (singular) are doing the action of thanking
Added ُ to complete the present tense form
Added ونَ to show this refers to a group of people

steps.m: when (referring to a past time when you said to the believers)
steps.n: This is a time particle that connects to تَقُولُ (you say) to show when something happened in the past. It helps set up the scene by taking us back to that specific moment.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to common past tense form by adding alif after ق
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا returns to و between two ُ vowels.
Changed يَ to تَ to show 'you' instead of 'he'. This word is addressing the believers (المؤمنين) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (to believe) into a noun meaning 'one who believes'. The ؤ comes from the original أ.
Added ين to show multiple believers (more than two). The ِ vowel comes because it follows لِ later.
Added لِ (li) meaning 'for' and ال (al) meaning 'the' to specify these particular believers
This particle turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English

steps.m: is it not going to? (questioning future negation)
steps.n: Added لَن which shows something won't happen in the future. Together with أَ, it questions whether something will not happen.
Changes from base meaning to present tense 'is sufficient'

steps.m: is sufficient for you all (object of تَقُولُ - you say)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this is happening to 'you all'. This word is receiving the action of تَقُولُ (you say) in this ayah.

steps.m: that (connects to يَكْفِيَكُمْ - 'be sufficient for you')
steps.n: This is a connecting particle that introduces what is being said. In this ayah, it connects to the verb يَكْفِيَكُمْ to show what will be sufficient.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Added يُ at start to show present tense, changed vowels to ُ and ِ, and added shadda to show emphasis

steps.m: he reinforces you all (object of تَقُولُ - when you said)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show the action is happening to 'you all'. This word appears in a context where the Prophet is speaking to the believers about Allah's support.

steps.m: to care for and help grow
one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: The doubled letter (shown by ّ) makes it mean the one who does this action
The ُ shows this word is doing something in the sentence - it's the one doing the helping (يَكْفِيَكُمْ) in this ayah
The كُم at the end shows this is talking to many people, like saying 'your' when speaking to a group
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) between the letters to make the basic word for 'three'
Added the feminine marker ة to make it refer to feminine things
Added ا in the middle to make it a number word
Added بِ meaning 'with' at start and kasra at end because it's connected to the word آلافٍ (thousands) that comes after it

steps.m: to join or unite things together

steps.n: The root letters were given vowel marks (fatha and sukun) to form the common number word meaning 'thousand'
The word 'thousand' becomes plural by adding a long 'aa' sound after the first letter
Added tanwin kasra to show this is being counted by the word ثَلٰثَةِ (three) in this ayah. Also replaced alif with alif maqsura.
Basic form of the word meaning 'from'
The shadda (ّ) shows that this word is connected to the word before it in pronunciation. It's followed by 'the angels' (ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةِ) to say 'from the angels'
Adding fathas creates the basic Form I past tense verb
Adding alif changes the verb 'to rule/possess' into a noun meaning 'messenger/angel'
Adding hamza and kasra creates broken plural pattern for supernatural beings
Adding feminine marker to complete plural form for non-human beings
Adding definite article. This word is connected to مُنزَلِينَ (descending) which describes these angels.
Adding kasra because this word comes after ءَالٰفٍ (thousands) in the phrase 'thousands of angels'
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb

steps.m: sent down (describing something that was sent)
steps.n: Changes from 'he sent down' to 'something that was sent down' by adding مُ at start

steps.m: ones who are sent down (describes الْمَلٰٓئِكَةِ - the angels)
steps.n: The ين ending shows this is plural and possessed/affected by something. It describes the angels mentioned in the ayah.

steps.m: Yes indeed! (responding to 'Didn't Allah help you?')
steps.n: This special word is used to strongly say 'Actually, yes!' when someone says something negative. In this ayah, it's responding to a previous question about Allah's help. The long alif (ىٰ) at the end makes the word stronger, like saying 'Absolutely yes!'
This is a conditional word that introduces a 'what if' statement. Here it starts a condition about 'if you are patient' (تَصْبِرُوا۟)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added 'ت' at the start to show it's happening now and you're doing it

steps.m: you all are patient (connected to وَتَتَّقُوا۟ - and you all fear)
steps.n: Added plural masculine suffix وا to indicate multiple people being patient

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added 'wa' (and) at start, 'ta' shows 'you', doubled middle letter shows protecting yourself

steps.m: and you all protect yourselves (if you do this in يَأْتُوكُم - they come to you)
steps.n: Changed to plural 'you all' form. This is part of a conditional statement with يَأْتُوكُم (they come to you)

steps.m: and they come to you all (as a condition with تَصْبِرُوا and تَتَّقُوا)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the start and كُم (to you all) at the end. Shows they are coming towards multiple people, connecting with earlier actions.
