steps.m: to rush immediately, to boil over

steps.n: Changed to show 'immediate rush of' something - like saying 'rush of water' instead of just 'rush'
Added هِمْ to show it belongs to them. In this ayah, it refers to the immediate response of the believers.
This is the basic pointing word that shows something is near to you
Added ذا to show we're pointing to something masculine that's close by
Added the alif mark (ٰ) over ه and fatha on ذ to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he will reinforce (in response to تَصْبِرُوا - if you are patient)
steps.n: Added 'يُ' at the start to show future tense, changed middle letter to kasra (i sound), and added sukun at the end because it's responding to a condition (if you are patient, then this will happen)

steps.m: he will reinforce you all (in response to تَصْبِرُوا - if you are patient)
steps.n: Added 'كُمْ' to show Allah is reinforcing you (plural). This is Allah's promise of help to the believers if they remain patient.
Basic vowels (fatha) added between letters to make past tense verb 'he cared for'
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this an intensive form, showing complete and perfect nurturing
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added كُم to show this lord belongs to all of you
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a basic number word
Adding ة at the end makes it a standalone number five (like when counting 1,2,3,4,5)
Added 'بِ' (with) at the start and changed the ending to connect with 'thousands'
Added vowel marks to make the basic number 'thousand'
Changed to broken plural form to mean multiple thousands
Added tanwin kasra to show this word is possessed by 'five' (خَمْسَةِ) in the phrase 'five thousand'
This is the basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or is part of something
The noon gets a shadda because it comes before the letter ل in the next word (ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةِ). This is just a pronunciation change.
The fatha is added at the end to connect this word with the next word (ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةِ). The meaning also includes 'the' because it connects to the definite noun after it.
Changed the root 'rule/authority' into a noun by adding vowels to mean 'angel' - someone with heavenly power
The singular 'angel' becomes plural 'angels' by adding letters in the middle (broken plural pattern)
Added feminine ending to complete the plural form
Adding 'al' makes it specific/definite angels
Adding kasra shows this word is being described by another word
The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to another word
Added shadda to intensify the meaning and show doing something thoroughly

steps.m: one who marks distinctly (describing the angels)
steps.n: Forms active participle showing who does the action. In this ayah, it describes the angels (ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةِ) who are sent to help.

steps.m: ones who mark distinctly (plural, describing the angels)
steps.n: Changes to masculine plural form by adding ين at the end to show multiple angels
This is a particle that can mean either 'what' (for questions) or 'not' (for negation). Here it means 'not' because it's negating the verb جَعَلَهُ (He made it).
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. وَ means 'and' and connects different parts of the sentence together.

steps.m: to make something into something else, to put, to set

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is Allah's action in the past

steps.m: He made it (referring to help/victory mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هُ to show what was made/set. Here it refers back to the help/victory Allah sent.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added ال to change 'a god' to 'The God', doubled the ل to make it special for Allah's name, and added ُ to show Allah is doing the action

steps.m: except (connects بُشْرَىٰ with what comes before)
steps.n: This is a fundamental particle used for exception/exclusion. In this ayah, it shows that Allah made it only as good news, excluding other purposes.
This is the basic concept of delivering positive news or glad tidings
Changed from root meaning 'bring good news' to simple noun meaning 'good news' by adding vowel marks
Added ى with alif above it at the end to show this is a special form of good news, often used for divine glad tidings in the Quran
This is a preposition that shows something is being given or directed to someone
Added the pronoun كُمْ which means 'you all'. This word connects to بُشْرَىٰ (glad tidings) to show who the glad tidings are for.
Adding vowel marks to make it a complete verb
Added ت at start to make it 'you/she', and changed vowels to show it's happening now (present tense)

steps.m: you/she definitely becomes peaceful and calm
steps.n: Added emphasis with shadda and changed final vowel

steps.m: so that you/she becomes peaceful and calm
steps.n: Adds purpose/intent meaning ('in order to')

steps.m: and so that you become tranquil (stressed)
steps.n: Adds 'and' (و) at the beginning to connect it to what came before
The root meaning of turning/changing becomes 'heart' since the heart is always turning and changing emotions. In this ayah, the hearts are what become tranquil.
Changes the inside of the word to show many hearts instead of just one
Added كُم at the end to show these are your (all of you) hearts
Added kasra (ِ) to show this is a preposition meaning with/by
Added هِ (hi) at the end to show this means 'it/him'

steps.m: with/by it/him (referring to the help/victory mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates النَّصْرُ (victory), saying 'victory is not except from Allah'
The و connects this phrase to what came before it, joining two related ideas in the ayah
Changes the verb into a noun meaning the act itself
Changed to noun form meaning the act of helping
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added ال at the beginning and doubled the ن with shadda to show this is a specific victory being talked about
This is an exception particle that means 'except' or 'only'. In this ayah, it's used to show that Allah made it ONLY as good news for you, not for any other reason.

steps.m: from (connects عِندِ with what comes before it)
steps.n: This is a preposition that connects words by showing where something comes from or what it belongs to. In this ayah, it connects 'victory' with 'presence/side' (عِندِ) of Allah.
Connected the root letters together to form the basic noun
Added kasra vowels to show this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it, forming the phrase 'from the presence of Allah'
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show possession, shadda on ل for emphasis, and changed initial alif to wasl form. This shows Allah belongs to or is connected to عِندِ (presence/with) in this ayah.

steps.m: to be mighty, to be powerful

steps.n: Added ي between the second and third root letters to show this is a permanent quality. When used for Allah, it means He has absolute might and power.

steps.m: The Mighty, The Powerful (a name of Allah)
steps.n: Adding ال makes this one of Allah's names, showing these are His unique attributes

steps.m: of The Mighty, The Powerful (describing Allah)
steps.n: The kasra ending shows this describes Allah in the ayah and connects to عِندِ (from) before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show someone who has a lot of wisdom as their main quality
Added ال to make it definite, referring specifically to Allah's attribute of perfect wisdom
Added kasra at the end to show this is describing Allah in the ayah
Added vowel marks to make it 'he cut' in the past tense
Adding ي at the start and changing the vowels makes it present tense instead of past tense
Adding ل prefix indicates purpose ('in order to')
Adding fatha at end shows this is subjunctive mood - something that might happen
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form a basic noun

steps.m: a part (object of لِيَقْطَعَ - that He might cut off)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being cut off in the phrase 'that He might cut off a part'
Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to
Added emphasis by doubling the م sound
Added fatha at the end to connect this word with the next word الَّذِينَ (those who)
This is the basic demonstrative word meaning 'this'
Adding ال at the beginning and ي at the end changes it from pointing to something to describing someone or something
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Added proper pronunciation marks and connected this word to كَفَرُوٓا۟ (they disbelieved) which comes right after it in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added وا with a long vowel (ـوٓ) and small و (۟) to show multiple people did this action

This is a connecting word that shows there are two possible outcomes in this ayah - either cutting off a portion or humiliating them. It connects two verbs: يَقْطَعَ (to cut off) and يَكْبِتَهُمْ (to humiliate them).
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added يَ at start to show it's happening now, and changed vowels to match present tense pattern
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being suppressed
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding يَ at the start changes it from past to present tense
The ُ (damma) at the end shows this is a normal present tense verb
The وا ending shows multiple people (men) are doing the action
فَ means 'and so' or 'then' - it connects this action to what happened before
Basic vowel marks (fatha and kasra) added to make the root letters readable as 'kha-yi-ba', meaning disappointed
Added alif after first root letter to show who does the action
Changed alif to madda for pronunciation

steps.m: those who are failing/disappointed (in this ayah, describing those who disbelieve)
steps.n: Added plural ending to show it's talking about many people

steps.m: is not (negates لَكَ مِنَ ٱلْأَمْرِ شَىْءٌ - for you from the matter anything)
steps.n: This special word is used to negate things in the present tense. In this ayah, it's saying that something is not the case - specifically that nothing of the matter belongs to the person being addressed.
This is a basic particle that shows something is being directed towards something else

steps.m: for you (when speaking to one male)
steps.n: Added ك at the end to show this is directed to one male person. This word appears after لَيْسَ (is not) and before مِنَ الْأَمْرِ (from the matter), forming the phrase 'not for you from the matter'
Basic preposition showing where something starts from
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word ٱلْأَمْرِ (the matter). This helps make the words flow together smoothly when reading.
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'matter' or 'affair'. The sukun (ْ) on م shows it's part of one unit with the first letter.

steps.m: the matter/affair (possessed by something mentioned in مِنَ)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from/of)
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make it into a noun. This word is the subject of ليس (is not) in this ayah.
Added tanween damma at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence. The ي is written as ى when followed by hamza.
This is a connecting word that shows a choice between two possibilities: Allah either accepting their repentance (يَتُوبَ) or punishing them (يُعَذِّبَهُمْ). It's like saying 'either this OR that'.

steps.m: he returned back (to what is right)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense and adds subjunctive mood (indicated by the final fatha) because it comes after أَوْ (or) in this context
This is a preposition that comes from the concept of being high/elevated, which evolved to mean 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to pronouns
Added هِمْ at the end to show this applies to 'them'

steps.m: or (connecting يَتُوبَ 'He may forgive' with يُعَذِّبَهُمْ 'He may punish them')
steps.n: This is a basic conjunction particle that connects two possible outcomes in this ayah: Allah may either forgive them or punish them
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda intensifies the meaning of punishment
Changes from past to present tense by adding يُ at the start. The word comes after أَوْ (or) which affects how it works

steps.m: that he punishes them severely (after أَوْ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who receives the punishment. This word comes after أَوْ (or) in the ayah.
This is a basic emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English
Adding فَ shows this is connected to what came before, like saying 'and so' or 'therefore'
Adding هُمْ shows we're talking about a group of people (they/them). This connects to the word ظٰلِمُونَ (wrongdoers) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who acts unjustly (predicate of إِنَّ)
steps.n: Changes from 'he wronged' to 'wrongdoer' - like changing from the action to the person who does it. Works like English '-er' (e.g. teach → teacher)

steps.m: they are unjust ones (predicate of إِنَّ)
steps.n: Adding ون at the end makes this refer to three or more males who do the action
Starting with the base word Allah, which is a proper noun that doesn't come from a root
Added ل at the beginning to show something belongs to or is for Allah. The first ل combines with ال of Allah to become لل with a shadda.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it in the sentence

steps.m: and for/to Allah (connected to what comes after in the ayah)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what comes after it in the ayah

steps.m: what/whatever (connects to فِى ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ - in the heavens)
steps.n: This word introduces what belongs to Allah in this verse. It's used to refer to everything in the heavens and earth.
This is one of the most common prepositions. It shows where something is located. In this ayah, it shows what exists within the heavens.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning or pronunciation.
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Verb 'to rise up' became a noun meaning 'sky' - named because it rises above us
Made the word plural by adding وات at the end
Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the heavens' instead of just 'heavens'
Changed to 'in the heavens' because it comes after the word 'in' (في). The alif was written differently but pronounced the same
This is a special word that refers back to everything mentioned in السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens). It's like saying 'everything that exists there'.

steps.m: and what/whatever (refers to everything in السَّمٰوٰتِ)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
This is a basic preposition that shows location. In this ayah, it connects 'what' (ما) with 'the heavens' (السَّمٰوٰتِ) to say 'what is in the heavens'
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced
Adding 'ال' makes it refer to the specific earth we know
The kasra (small diagonal line) at the end shows this word is connected to 'Allah' - meaning it belongs to Him
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he forgives (someone who يَشَآءُ - He wills)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end. This word relates to يَشَآءُ (He wills) in the ayah, showing Allah forgives whom He wills.
This is a special word (relative pronoun) that refers to people in a general way, like saying 'whoever' or 'anyone who' in English
Added لِ at the beginning to show this is being done for or to someone. This word connects to يَغْفِرُ (He forgives) showing who receives the forgiveness.

steps.m: to want or wish for something
steps.n: The root expresses wanting or wishing something
Added fatha vowels and combined letters. The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's between two fathas.

steps.m: he wants/wishes (subject of يُعَذِّبُ - he punishes)
steps.n: Added يَ at start to show present tense, and changed final َ to ُ to show it's doing the action. This word is connected to يُعَذِّبُ (he punishes) in the ayah.
Changed the alif to a long alif (آ) to show the extended sound. This doesn't change the meaning.
The root refers to causing pain or punishment
The doubled ذ shows this is intense or repeated punishment
Changed from past tense to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels - shows ongoing punishment
Added وَ at start to connect this word to the previous one
This is a relative pronoun that means 'whoever'. In this ayah, it refers to the people that Allah forgives (يَغْفِرُ) or punishes (يُعَذِّبُ). It's like saying 'He forgives whoever He wills.'
The root letters ش-ي-أ became شَاءَ (he wanted) by adding vowel marks and changing ي to ا in the middle

steps.m: he wants/wishes (subject of يَغْفِرُ - he forgives)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end. This word shows who does the forgiving mentioned earlier in يَغْفِرُ.
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's a long vowel
This is the unique proper name of God in Arabic. It's related to the root meaning of divinity but is a distinct proper noun.
Added shadda to show emphasis on the ل. This is a unique feature of this word.

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يَغْفِرُ - forgives)
steps.n: The ُ (damma) at the end shows this is the subject doing the action in the sentence - Allah is the one who forgives.

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of يَغْفِرُ - forgives)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to mean 'and'. The ٱ is just a helper mark to show how to pronounce the word smoothly.

steps.m: to cover up faults or mistakes

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show someone who does this action intensely or repeatedly

steps.m: repeatedly forgiving (as a quality of اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added two dots (tanween) to show this word describes Allah in this ayah
Changes from basic 'mercy' root to pattern that means 'one who shows lots of mercy'

steps.m: one who shows abundant mercy (describing اللَّهُ)
steps.n: The double damma (ٌ) at the end shows this is a describing word in its basic form
Shadda adds emphasis to the meaning

steps.m: to point to or draw attention to something

steps.n: Takes the root letters and combines them with a shadda to make a word that asks 'which' or 'who'
Adds the calling particle يٰٓ and changes the ending vowel to show it's addressing someone
Adds ها to make it a complete calling phrase
This is the complete vocative form used to address a group
This is the basic form of the word used to point to someone or something. It's like saying 'who' or 'which' in English.
Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one person
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the ones who' instead of just 'who'
Final spelling adjustments: the first alif becomes ٱ, we add a shadda on ل, and add a fatha at the end. This word points to the believers who are being addressed in this verse.
Added alif after first letter and fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added 'وا' at the end to make it 'they' instead of 'he' believed
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it negative. Here it makes تَأْكُلُوا۟ (you eat) into 'do not eat'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed يَ to تَ to show this is talking to someone (you) instead of about someone (he)

steps.m: you (all) eat/are eating (negative command in لَا تَأْكُلُوا۟ ٱلرِّبَوٰٓا۟)
steps.n: Added وا to show this is speaking to multiple people. The word appears after لا which makes it a command not to do something (don't eat)

steps.m: to grow excessively or increase beyond proper bounds

steps.n: The root letters form a past tense verb. The last letter و becomes ا to make it easier to say, like many Arabic words ending in و.
Changes from a verb (to increase) to a noun meaning forbidden interest/usury by changing the first vowel
Adds emphasis through doubling of first letter to show severity of the forbidden nature

steps.m: the usury/interest (referring to the specific forbidden practice)
steps.n: Adds 'the' (ال) at the start and stretches the ending sound to emphasize how bad interest/usury is
Root meaning 'double' became a noun for 'one multiple/double' of something
Added أَ at the beginning to make it a broken plural - means 'many multiples' instead of just one double/multiple
Added tanween to show this is the object of the verb تَأْكُلُوا (you eat)
Added alif after first letter to show intensive or repeated action
Added مُ at start to show this is happening to something rather than someone doing it
Added ة at the end to make it feminine
Added shadda on م and tanween at end to show this describes how the interest (الرِّبَوٰٓا۟) is being multiplied
Connected the root letters و-ق-ي with vowels (َ) to make 'he protected' in the past tense
Added اِتَّ at the beginning to make it mean 'protect oneself' instead of 'protect others'
Added وَ at beginning to mean 'and', with helping vowel ٱ

steps.m: and protect yourselves (command to a group)
steps.n: Changed from 'he protected himself' to 'protect yourselves!' by adding وا to make it a command for a group
Adding conjunction prefix وَ to connect with previous phrase
Changed إِلَه to اللَه to make it a proper name for The One True God. The ال here is part of the name itself, not just 'the'.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being feared/revered in وَٱتَّقُوا۟)
steps.n: Allah gets a line over ل and a small 'a' sound at the end because it's receiving the action of 'fear/revere'
This is a special word that shows hope that something might happen. The shadda (double letter) makes it stronger.
Added كُمْ at the end to show this is addressing multiple people. This word connects to تُفْلِحُونَ (you will succeed) in the next ayah, showing hope for their success.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he succeeded' to 'you succeed' by adding تُ at start and changing vowels to show present tense

steps.m: you all achieve success (addressed to the believers)
steps.n: Added ون to show this applies to multiple people. This is addressing the believers (الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا) mentioned at the start of the verse.

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding اِتَّ at the beginning changes 'protect' to 'protect oneself' - like adding 'self' in English
Changed from 'he protected himself' to a command meaning 'you all protect yourselves' by adding وا at the end
Added و meaning 'and' at the beginning. The ٱ is just a different way to write the same sound as اِ
Changed the root letters to make a noun meaning 'fire' - something that gives off light

steps.m: the fire (that is mentioned in وَٱتَّقُوا۟)
steps.n: Added ال to show this is the specific fire being warned about in the command 'fear/guard against'

steps.m: the fire (object of وَٱتَّقُوا۟ - guard against)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show this is what we're being told to guard against in the command وَٱتَّقُوا۟ (guard against)
Added kasra to show this is for feminine things
Added ال to make it specific, like saying 'the one that' instead of just 'that'
The 'al' at the start gets a special mark (ٱ) and doubles the next letter (ّ) to show it's connected
Changed spelling: ي becomes ى with ٓ to show it's connecting to describe 'the fire' (النَّارَ) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Combined the repeated letters with a shadda mark to show emphasis
Changed from 'he prepared' to 'it was prepared' by adding أُ at start - this makes it passive (something was done to it)
Added ت at the end to show this refers to something feminine (the Fire/النار mentioned before)

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed to describe someone who does the action
Made the word plural to refer to multiple people
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: for the ones who reject truth (prepared for them is النَّارَ/the fire)
steps.n: Added لِ meaning 'for/to' to show this describes who the fire is prepared for
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he obeyed' to 'I obey' by adding أ at start and changing middle letter to ي
Changed from 'I obey' to a command for a group by adding وا at the end
Adding conjunction و to connect with previous phrase
Changed to the special form used only for Allah, with ال merged into the word

steps.m: Allah (as the object of أَطِيعُوا۟ - obey)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a mark at the end (fatha) to show it receives the action 'obey'

steps.m: to send messages or deliver information

steps.n: The و makes it mean 'someone who does this action a lot'
Added ال to make it specific and the ل combines with ر to make a stronger r sound (shown by ّ)
The َ at the end shows this is the object - the one being obeyed in أَطِيعُوا (obey)
Added و at the beginning to connect it with the previous word (Allah) - we should obey both Allah and the messenger
This is a particle that expresses hope or expectation

steps.m: perhaps you all (so that you all may be shown mercy)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this applies to a group of people being addressed. In this ayah, it connects to تُرْحَمُونَ (you are shown mercy) to explain the purpose - perhaps you'll receive mercy if you obey Allah and the Messenger.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he showed mercy' to 'mercy is shown to you' by adding تُ at start and changing middle vowels to make it passive

steps.m: you all are shown mercy (لَعَلَّكُمْ - so that you may)
steps.n: Adds plural marker to show the action applies to multiple people. This verb comes after لَعَلَّكُمْ (so that you may) in the ayah.

steps.m: to move quickly, rush, or hasten

steps.n: Changes from verb (to rush) to noun (one who rushes)
Adds nominative case ending to show this is the subject
Adds وا to make it a command for multiple men

steps.m: and hurry/rush! (all of you) (connected to إِلَىٰ مَغْفِرَةٍ - towards forgiveness)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this command to what follows - the direction/goal of hurrying towards forgiveness
This is a basic preposition that shows movement or direction towards something. It connects the verb سَارِعُوٓا۟ (hurry) to its destination مَغْفِرَةٍ (forgiveness).
The alif at the end is written with a small mark above it (called an alif maqsurah). This is just a writing variation and doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added مَ prefix and changed vowels to make a noun meaning the act itself
Added ة to show one specific instance of forgiveness
Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite and is connected to what comes after it in the ayah
Basic form of the word with kasra added to show it's a preposition
The shadda shows that when reading continuously, the previous word's ن or م sound combines with this م. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, or develop something to completion

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes it stronger and shows mastery/authority

steps.m: O my Lord! (calling upon one's Lord)
steps.n: Adding kasra at the end makes it into a form of calling/addressing your Lord

steps.m: your Lord (referring to all those being addressed)
steps.n: The word changed from 'my Lord' to 'your Lord' by adding كُمْ at the end to mean 'your' for many people
Added fatha to first letter and shadda to double the middle letter, making it into a past tense verb
Adding ة changes it from a verb into a noun meaning a covered/protected place (garden)
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this to the previous word مَغْفِرَةٍ (forgiveness), showing both are things to race towards
The tanwin kasra (ٍ) shows this word follows the word إِلَىٰ (to) in the ayah, making it part of what we should race towards

steps.m: to extend sideways or present something

steps.n: Changes verb into noun showing the basic concept of width/presentation

Adding ُهَا shows two things: 1) The ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence (what we're talking about), 2) The هَا shows we're talking about the width of something feminine (paradise). The full meaning in context is 'its width is (as vast as) the heavens and earth'.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters
Changed from root word meaning 'rise up' to plural noun 'heavens' by adding 'ات' at the end and changing middle letter to 'ا'
Added ال with shadda on س to make it specific
Added damma to show this is the subject of the verb أُعِدَّتْ (was prepared). The alifs are written as long marks in this style.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
Added 'u' sound at the end to show this word is doing the action (subject)
Added conjunction to connect this word with what came before it in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from active 'he prepared' to passive 'it was prepared' by adding أُ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ. The two د letters combine into one دّ with a shadda
Added feminine marker ت because it refers to جَنَّة (Paradise) which is feminine in Arabic

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he protected'
Changed to start with مُ and added ت with shadda to make it mean 'someone who protects themselves'
Added ين to make plural form
Added ال to make it definite

steps.m: for the ones who guard against (evil/harm)
steps.n: Added لِ to show 'for/to' meaning
This is a demonstrative relative pronoun formed by adding the definite article ال and the masculine plural suffix ين

steps.m: to spend, use up, or deplete resources

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changes to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. This describes what 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) do.
The suffix ونَ shows that multiple people are doing the action. This refers back to 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) in the ayah.
This is a preposition that shows where or when something happens. Here it connects to the verb يُنفِقُونَ (they spend) to show where the spending happens.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word in the Quran. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with basic vowels
Added shadda to make the meaning stronger
Added اء to turn the word for 'happiness' into a longer word meaning 'good times/prosperity' - like turning 'happy' into 'happiness'
Added ال at beginning to make it specific and ِ at end to show it's connected to what comes after it in the sentence

steps.m: to harm, hurt, or cause hardship

steps.n: Adding shadda doubles the middle letter to show the harm is more intense
Adding ال makes it specific/definite, like 'the' in English
Adding و connects this word to what came before it, like adding 'and'
Doubling the ر makes the harm feel stronger/more intense
The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to a preposition
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed to show who does the action
Made the word plural to show multiple people who hold back
Added 'the' to make it specific

steps.m: and the ones who hold back
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect it with previous descriptions
The spelling is updated to show the proper Arabic writing, with a final mark (fatHa) added to show this is part of a longer description about good people mentioned earlier

steps.m: to be filled with intense anger/rage
rage/intense anger (object of كٰظِمِينَ - those who suppress)
steps.n: Converting to noun form showing the intense emotion being suppressed. This word is what is being controlled by كٰظِمِينَ (those who suppress) in this ayah.

steps.m: the rage (object of كٰظِمِينَ - those who suppress)
steps.n: ٱلْ makes it definite, َ shows accusative case
Added alif after first letter and vowels to make it mean 'someone who forgives'
Changed to plural form to show multiple people forgiving
Adding ال makes the word definite

steps.m: and the ones who forgive (connected to وَٱلْكٰظِمِينَ)
steps.n: Adding و connects this to the previous phrase about those who suppress anger
This is a basic preposition that shows moving away from something or being separate from it
Added kasra at the end to help connect it smoothly to the next word ٱلنَّاسِ (the people)

steps.m: to be social, familiar with others

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'people' by adding vowels and dropping the initial hamza
Added 'ال' (the) at the start to make it specific, which causes the ن to be doubled when pronounced
Added kasra at the end because this word follows عَنِ (from/about) in the ayah
Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in pronunciation
Added damma at the end to show Allah is the one doing the action (loving) in this ayah

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of يُحِبُّ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this part to what came before, and added ٱ (alif with wasl) which helps in pronunciation when connecting words
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he loved'
The middle letter gets doubled to show stronger meaning
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ. This shows Allah's ongoing love for those who do good.

steps.m: to do good, to be excellent

steps.n: Added short vowels (fatha-damma-fatha) to make it mean 'he was excellent' in the past tense
Added مُ prefix and changed middle vowel to make it mean 'someone who does good deeds' (like changing 'was good' to 'doer of good')
Added sound masculine plural ending to show multiple people doing good
Added definite article ال and final fatḥa to show this is the object being loved in the phrase 'Allah loves the good-doers'
This is a special word used to point to someone or something specific. It's one of the most common words in Arabic.
Added ال with a shadda to make it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people
Added و at the beginning to connect this to what came before it
This is the basic form of the word that refers to a specific point in time
Adding ا at the end makes it refer to future events or conditional situations. In this ayah, it introduces what these people do when they commit sins.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: they did (referring to those who remember Allah in 3:135)
steps.n: Added وا to show many people did this action. The small mark after وا (۟) helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to go beyond proper limits/be shamefully bad
one who/that which is shamefully bad
steps.n: Changed the root into the active participle pattern to show who/what has this quality
Added ة to make it refer to one specific instance of the action or quality

steps.m: a shamefully bad deed (object of فَعَلُوا - they did)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is the object being acted upon. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this word.
This word gives a choice between two things. Here it connects 'they did' with 'they wronged', showing these are two different situations where the same response applies.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action together
Word is now in its final form showing multiple people doing the action in the past
Added vowels to make the basic noun meaning one self or soul
Changes the word to mean a small group of souls (between 3-10)

steps.m: their selves/souls (being acted upon by ظَلَمُوا - they wronged)
steps.n: Added 'their' (هُمْ) at the end to show who owns/possesses the souls
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb. This word comes after إِذَا (when) in the ayah, showing when something happened.
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people did the action. The small circle (۟) is just a pronunciation mark showing where to stop if needed.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God who is being remembered)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل with shadda to show this is the one true God. The َ at the end shows Allah is being acted upon in this sentence (people are remembering Allah).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or requesting forgiveness
Added فَ at beginning to show this action follows what was mentioned before
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action

steps.m: to commit a wrong or mistake

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun by adding vowel marks. This makes it refer to one specific wrong action instead of the general idea of doing wrong.
Changed the vowels and added و to make it plural. This special pattern (فُعُول) is often used for serious mistakes or violations.
Added لِ at the beginning to show these sins are the reason for something (in this case, asking Allah for forgiveness)
Added هِمْ to show these sins belong to them. This word is what they're asking forgiveness for (connected to فَٱسْتَغْفَرُوا۟ meaning 'so they asked for forgiveness')
This is a relative pronoun that means 'whoever' when followed by a verb. Here it introduces the question 'who forgives sins?' in the next part of the ayah.
وَ means 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Adds ُ at the end to show this is a present tense verb doing the action
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the singular noun
Changes from single sin to multiple sins by changing middle vowels
Adding ٱل makes it specific sins. The ذ gets a shadda because it assimilates with the ل
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object being forgiven in the phrase 'who forgives the sins'

steps.m: except (excluding اللَّهُ from those who can forgive sins)
steps.n: This is a particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before. In this ayah, it's saying that no one can forgive sins except Allah.
Changed the root letters into a noun form that means 'one who is worshipped'

This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال at the beginning and the shadda on ل make it specific and definite. The ُ at the end shows this word is the subject doing something in the sentence.
This is a special word that makes verbs negative and changes their meaning to the past. It's used with يُصِرُّوا (persist/continue) in this ayah to say they did not persist.
Added وَ (and) to connect this with the previous actions mentioned in the ayah. This connects to يُصِرُّوا (persist/continue) to say 'and they did not persist.'
Added vowels and doubled middle letter (shadda) to show strong/intense persistence
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they persist/continue (referring to those who committed sins in the previous part of the ayah)
steps.n: Added plural masculine ending وا to show multiple people doing the action
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of, upon, or about something else. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
Added an alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) at the end. This is just a writing style that doesn't change the meaning - it shows the word ends with a long 'aa' sound.
This word refers to the actions mentioned before (فَعَلُوا - they did). In this context, it's connecting to explain what they didn't persist in doing.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense. This word describes what they did, with فٰحِشَةً (immorality) being what was done.
The suffix وا indicates masculine plural subject (they)
This is a pronoun meaning 'they' for multiple people (masculine). It's the subject of the verb يَعْلَمُونَ (they know) in this ayah.
Added وَ at the beginning which means 'and'. This connects this phrase to what came before it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is the basic form of the word used to point to multiple things that are far away
Added damma and alif maksura to show this is a complete word
Added hamza with kasra to emphasize distance
Added كَ at the end to show we're pointing to something specific. This word is the subject of جَزَآؤُهُم (their reward) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun meaning the result of the action
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's a long vowel
Adds ُ to show this is the subject of the sentence
Adds هُم to show the reward belongs to them. This word is the subject of مَّغْفِرَةٌ (forgiveness) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'forgiveness' by adding مَ at the start, changing middle vowel to kasra, and adding ة at the end
Added emphasis by doubling the م at the beginning
Added tanween (double damma) to make the word indefinite
Added kasra to make the basic preposition that shows where something comes from
Added shadda because it comes before a word starting with ر. This is just a pronunciation change that happens when مِن comes before certain letters.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, or develop something to its full potential

steps.n: The doubled ب (shadda) shows this is someone who perfectly embodies the quality of nurturing and sustaining
The kasra shows this word is connected to the word مِن (from) that comes before it
Added هِمْ to show this is their Lord
The kasra shows this word is affected by مِن before it

steps.m: to cover, protect, or hide from view

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: a garden (a place that provides cover/protection)
steps.n: The ة makes this a noun for a single garden - a place that provides cover and protection through its shade and vegetation
The double damma (ٌ) shows this is a single garden without 'the' in front, used as a subject
Added 'and' (wa) to connect this word to what came before it
Changed to mean multiple gardens by adding the feminine plural ending
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding تَ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra

This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it connects 'their Lord' (رَّبِّهِمْ) to 'forgiveness' (مَّغْفِرَةٌ), showing the forgiveness comes from their Lord.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a word showing location
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it
Added هَا at the end to mean 'it', referring to the rivers (الْأَنْهٰرُ) mentioned right after this word
Added vowels to make the basic noun form for a single river
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple rivers
Added ال to show these are specific rivers being talked about
Added ُ to show this is the subject of the sentence, and changed ا to ٰ which is just a different way of writing the same sound

steps.m: one who lasts forever (moved by تَجْرِى)
steps.n: Added alif after first letter and vowels to show who does the action. This describes someone in Paradise who is affected by the verb تَجْرِى (flows/runs).

steps.m: those who last forever (moved by تَجْرِى)
steps.n: Added ين to show this refers to multiple people

steps.m: those who last forever (moved by تَجْرِى)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by تَجْرِى (flows/runs). Also changed alif to alif with bar above for proper spelling.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment

Changes to a special verb used to praise something
The و connects this praise of the reward to the previous description of Paradise and forgiveness

steps.m: to pay back, compensate, or reward for work

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning the thing given as compensation. This word is owned by 'the workers' (العاملين) in this ayah.
The ُ (damma) at the end shows this is the subject doing the action in the sentence
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changes verb into noun for person who does the action
Makes the word plural to show multiple workers
Makes the word definite and shows these are specific workers. This word is connected to أَجْرُ (reward) in the phrase 'reward of the workers'.
Adds the fatha mark at the end to show this word is in the accusative case
This is a special word that comes before past tense verbs to emphasize that something definitely happened. Here it emphasizes خَلَتْ (passed away/gone by).

steps.m: to pass away or be finished

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end of a past tense verb.

steps.n: Added feminine past tense marker ت with sukun. This shows the subject is feminine (سُنَنٌ - ways/patterns that occurred in the past).

steps.m: from (connects قَبْلِكُمْ to what came before)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts from or comes from. In this ayah, it connects to قَبْلِكُمْ (before you) to show 'from before you'

steps.m: from (connects قَبْلِكُمْ to what came before)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word is connecting to what comes after it. It's like saying 'from' before listing where something is from.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the noun form meaning 'before'
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it with 'of'

steps.m: before you all (مِن قَبْلِكُمْ - from before you all)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this refers to 'you all'. This word is part of the phrase مِن قَبْلِكُمْ meaning 'from before you all'

Added فَ at beginning to mean 'so' or 'then', connecting it to the previous statement

The ي changes to ى because it's written in the Uthmani script style of the Quran
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
Added kasra at the end because it follows the word في (in/on/at)
The word 'earth' gets 'the' (ال) added to its beginning to make it specific
Added vowels (fatha) to make it 'he looked' in past tense
Changed from 'he looked' to 'look!' by adding اُ at start and putting a sukoon at end - this makes it a command to one person
Added وا to make it a command to many people

This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens or the state of something
Adding vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly

steps.m: how (asking about manner or state of عٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُكَذِّبِينَ)

steps.m: he was (describing عٰقِبَةُ - the end result)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter) in the past tense
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb

steps.m: a specific outcome or end result
steps.n: Changed middle letter to ا (alif) and added ة at end to make it mean 'one specific end result' instead of the action of following
Added U ending to show this is the subject of كَانَ (was). The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this word.
Doubling the middle letter shows doing something strongly or repeatedly
Adding مُ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ makes it describe someone who does the action
Adding ين makes it refer to many people who do this action
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to specific deniers rather than just any deniers
Adding َ shows this word is being acted upon by the verb فَٱنظُرُوا۟ (look at/observe)
This is a demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby. In this ayah, it points to بَيَانٌ (clarification/explanation) that follows it.
Added the alif with overbar (ٰ) and fatha marks to show the correct pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added alif after second letter to turn the action into a noun meaning the result of explaining
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is any explanation, not a specific one

steps.m: to be close or familiar with others
people (those who are social/familiar with each other)
