steps.m: of Allah (possessed by/connected to مَغْفِرَةٌ - forgiveness)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word 'forgiveness' in the ayah, meaning 'forgiveness of Allah'. Also changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word.

steps.m: to be merciful, to show kindness and care

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun that means one specific instance of mercy or kindness
Added 'and' to connect this mercy with the forgiveness mentioned before it in the ayah

steps.m: and a mercy (that comes from Allah)
steps.n: The ٌ shows this is 'a mercy' rather than 'the mercy', emphasizing it's one of many mercies from Allah
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the comparative noun pattern

steps.m: better (than مِّمَّا يَجْمَعُونَ - what they gather)
steps.n: Added tanween damma to show this is the subject of the sentence, comparing Allah's forgiveness and mercy to what people gather
Starting with the two separate words: مِن (from) and ما (what)
When مِن comes before a word starting with م, its ن changes into another م (shown by ّ). The ا at the end gets a fatha mark.
Added fatha after each letter to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
ونَ shows multiple people are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to the gathering of worldly wealth and possessions.
This is a conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations
The لَ adds emphasis, like adding 'surely' or 'definitely' in English

steps.m: and surely if (connects to مُّتُّمْ أَوْ قُتِلْتُمْ - you die or are killed)
steps.n: The وَ connects this condition to what comes next in the ayah about dying or being killed
The root letters م-و-ت (death) change to form مُتّ (I died) - the و changes to ت and doubles with shadda
Added م at the end to show 'you all' (plural) did the action
Added sukun mark to show end of word
This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two options: 'if you die' or 'if you are killed'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone instead of someone doing it
Added تُمْ to show this happened to multiple people being spoken to
Dropped the last vowel mark but kept the same plural 'you' meaning
Basic preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it shows where people will be gathered to.

The لَ adds emphasis, showing that this gathering to Allah will definitely happen. This emphasis connects to the previous part of the ayah about death, showing that no matter how you die, you will surely return to Allah.
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One God
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of the word إِلَى (to) in the phrase 'to Allah you will be gathered'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed vowels to show the action happening to someone instead of someone doing it
Changed from past tense 'was gathered' to present tense 'are being gathered' by adding تُ at start and changing vowels. Also changes from 'he' to 'you'
Added ونَ at the end to show it's happening to multiple people
Added fatha to show this is a connecting word that links this statement to what came before
Added بِ to show this action was done using something or in some way

steps.m: So by what/that which (referring to رَحْمَةٍ - mercy)
steps.n: Added ما which refers to the mercy (رَحْمَةٍ) mentioned right after this word in the ayah. The full phrase means 'So by the mercy [of Allah]'

steps.m: mercy (connected to مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ - from Allah)
steps.n: Changed the word from an action (showing mercy) to a thing (mercy itself) by adding vowels and ة at the end

steps.m: by mercy (connected to مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ - from Allah)
steps.n: Added the tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is part of a longer phrase 'by mercy from Allah'
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
The 'n' sound gets stronger (doubled) because it comes right after another word ending in a vowel
Added fatha at the end to connect with the word اللَّهِ (Allah) that follows it, showing this is 'from Allah'
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One True God. The hamza is dropped and the lam gets a shadda.
Added kasra at the end to show possession. The alif at the start becomes a wasl form (ٱ). This word is possessed by the word مِنَ (from) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: you were gentle (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Changed the verb to show 'you' did the action in the past. The middle letter ا is dropped because it's a weak letter when adding تَ.
Added fatha to show the basic preposition meaning

steps.m: for them (referring to the people around the Prophet ﷺ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ which means 'them' (referring to multiple males or mixed group). In this ayah, it refers to the companions around the Prophet ﷺ.
This is a conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations that didn't actually happen, like saying 'even if' or 'if it were that' in English
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it in the ayah
Added vowel marks (fathas) to make it mean 'he was' in the past
The weak letter و drops out and vowels adjust in past tense
Added تَ to show this is addressing 'you' (masculine singular). In this ayah, Allah is addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.
Root letters combined with fatha and shadda to make an adjective meaning 'harsh person'

steps.m: harsh/rude (describing how you would have been to them)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes how the Prophet (peace be upon him) would have been if not for Allah's mercy, connecting to كُنتَ (you were) earlier in the verse
Added vowels and ي to make an adjective showing a strong quality. This word describes القَلْبِ (the heart) to mean 'hard-hearted'.
Added fatha at the end because this word is the second part of a compound phrase with القَلْبِ (the heart)

steps.m: to turn over, flip, transform (especially in one's mind/heart)
heart (the organ that turns/processes emotions and thoughts)
steps.n: The noun form shows the physical place where turning/processing happens, like how the heart turns blood and processes emotions
Shows this heart belongs to or is connected to the word غَلِيظَ (harsh) in the phrase 'harsh of heart'
Makes it refer to the specific heart being discussed in 'harsh of heart', not just any heart

steps.m: to break apart or separate from

steps.n: Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Adding ان shows the action is happening to the people themselves - they scattered themselves
Changed to show many people scattered away (added 'they' ending وا), with emphasis on complete separation (doubled ضّ)
Added لَ to emphasize that they definitely scattered/separated
Added ٱن to show the action happened to themselves (Form VII verb pattern)
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from or belongs to something else. In this ayah, it connects 'mercy' (رَحْمَةٍ) with 'Allah' (اللَّهِ) to show the mercy comes from Allah.
Added a sukun (ْ) on the final letter to show where to stop when pronouncing the word
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'around' or 'surrounding area'
Added كَ (you/your) to show who is being surrounded, and ِ to connect it
Basic root meaning focuses on the act of pardoning
Added sukun and damma to make basic command form. The final و drops because it's a weak letter at the end of a command.
Added helping alif at start since Arabic words can't begin with a sukun

steps.m: so pardon! (connected to previous statement about Allah's mercy)
steps.n: Added فَ to connect this command with the previous statement about Allah's mercy, showing this pardoning is a result of that mercy

steps.m: away from (preposition showing separation or distance)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows moving away, separating from, or talking about something. In this ayah, it's used with فَٱعْفُ (so pardon) to show pardoning them or overlooking their faults.

steps.m: from them (object of فَٱعْفُ - so pardon)
steps.n: عَنْ (away from) + هُمْ (them) combine to mean 'from them'. The هُمْ is attached to show who is being pardoned.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or requesting forgiveness
Changed from 'he sought' to 'you seek!' by changing the vowels to make it a command to one person
Added و at beginning to connect with previous phrase

steps.m: to/for (connects to لِنتَ - you became gentle)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows direction or purpose. Here it connects to the verb لِنتَ (you became gentle) to show who the gentleness was for.

steps.m: to/for them (referring to the Prophet's companions)
steps.n: Added هُمْ which means 'them' when referring to a group of men or a mixed group. In this ayah, it refers to the Prophet's companions who are being shown gentleness.
Basic root meaning showing the concept of consultation
Added vowels and alif to form past tense verb pattern III which shows interaction between two parties
Changed to command form by changing vowels and adding sukun at end
Added conjunction و meaning 'and' at the beginning
Added pronoun هُمْ meaning 'them' to show who should be consulted
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or part of something else. Here it connects with ٱلْأَمْرِ (the matter/affair) to mean 'in the matter'.

steps.m: to command/order, or to deal with matters

steps.n: This pattern turns the verb into a noun meaning the matter or issue being dealt with (used with في/in to mean 'concerning the matter')

steps.m: the matter, the affair (object of في/in)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to a specific matter. This word follows the word في (in/concerning) in this ayah
The ِ ending shows this word follows في (in/concerning), telling us what is being consulted about
This is a conditional word that introduces something that will happen in the future. It's like saying 'when' in English, but specifically for things that haven't happened yet.

steps.m: and/then when (connecting to عَزَمْتَ - you have decided)
steps.n: The فَ here connects this condition to what came before it and shows that what follows (deciding) leads to what comes next in the ayah (putting trust in Allah)
Adding vowel marks creates basic past tense verb meaning 'he...'
Changes to past tense second person masculine singular form. This word connects to فَتَوَكَّلْ (so rely) that comes after it, showing what happened before relying on Allah.

steps.m: to depend on or trust someone to handle something
he trusted someone completely with something
steps.n: Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show doing the action strongly
Added تَ at the beginning to show doing the action to yourself - you're the one choosing to rely on someone
Changed the ending to make it a command

steps.m: so rely completely! (command on عَلَى ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this command comes as a result of what was mentioned before. The command is to rely specifically on Allah (عَلَى ٱللَّهِ)
This is a preposition that shows complete reliance and trust being placed upon Allah in this context. It connects فَتَوَكَّلْ (so rely) with اللَّهِ (Allah).
Changed إِلَه to اللَه, which is a unique name specifically referring to The One True God in Islam
Added the kasra at the end to show possession, and doubled the ل with shadda. This word is possessed by رَحْمَةٍ (mercy) in this ayah.

This is a special word that emphasizes what comes after it. Here it emphasizes that Allah (ٱللَّهَ) is the one who loves those who rely on Him. The shadda (ّ) makes the 'n' sound stronger to show this emphasis.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the ل is doubled.
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of إِنَّ (indeed). Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it connects to previous word.
Adding fatha vowels creates the basic past tense verb
When two identical letters come together, they combine with a shadda
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. This shows Allah's ongoing love, not just in the past.

steps.m: to trust, depend on, or hand over responsibility
to make someone responsible for something
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more direct

steps.m: to put one's trust in someone/something
steps.n: Adding تَ shows doing the action for yourself - here it means choosing to trust

steps.m: one who puts their complete trust (in Allah)
steps.n: Changed from action to describing someone who does the action

steps.m: those who put their complete trust (in Allah)
steps.n: Changes from singular (one person who trusts) to plural (many people who trust) by adding ين at the end

steps.m: the ones who put their complete trust (in Allah)
steps.n: Makes the plural noun definite

steps.m: the ones who put their complete trust (in Allah) (object of يُحِبُّ - loves)
steps.n: This word is what Allah loves in this ayah - He loves those who put their trust in Him
This is a conditional particle that introduces the first part of an if-then statement. Here it connects 'if Allah helps you' with 'then none can overcome you'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he helps you all (subject of إِن conditional)
steps.n: Added كُمُ to show who is being helped. This word comes after إِن (if) in the ayah, showing it's part of a conditional statement.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God who helps you)
steps.n: Changed from general word for 'deity' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال in a unique way, doubling the ل, and adding ُ to show Allah is doing the action
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation of the negation particle
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before. Here it connects to the previous phrase about Allah helping you.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed the word to show who does the action. This word comes after لَا (no) in the ayah to say 'no one can overcome'.
Added a fatḥa mark at the end to show this word is affected by لَا
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for you all/to you all (object of غَالِبَ - overcomer)
steps.n: Added كُمْ (you all) to show who the action is for. This word connects to غَالِبَ (overcomer) in the ayah, showing who cannot be overcome.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a possible situation. Here it introduces what might happen if Allah withdraws His help (يَخْذُلْكُمْ).
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement about Allah's help.
Present tense verb starting with يَ (ya) meaning 'he', with sukun at end showing it depends on something else happening first
Added كُم at the end to show who is being abandoned - 'you all'. The مْ has a sukun to show end of word.
This is a question word that asks about people (like 'who' in English)

steps.m: then who (connects to يَنصُرْكُمُ - will help you)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this question follows from the previous statement. In this ayah, it's asking 'then who will help you?' after mentioning that if Allah withdraws His help.

steps.m: this (pointing to what comes next in the ayah)
steps.n: A demonstrative word that points to something. Here it points forward to يَنصُرُكُم (helps/supports you) to ask 'who is this that will help you?'

steps.m: the one who (that will help you)
steps.n: Added ال with shadda at the beginning to make it definite and ى at the end to make it a relative pronoun. This word connects the description 'will help you' to the question 'who is the one?'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he supports/gives victory to you all (conditional clause with اللَّهُ as subject)
steps.n: Added كُم to show who receives the support. This word is part of a conditional statement where Allah is the subject (if Allah helps you...)
Basic preposition that shows where something starts from or comes from
Added shadda on م because it comes after a word ending in a similar sound, making the م sound stronger
Added a small ن mark (called tanwin) to help connect smoothly with the next word بَعْدِهِۦ
Changes from root letters to a basic noun meaning 'after'
Added a kasra (i sound) at the end because another word owns or possesses it
Added هِۦ (him) to show who comes after. The ۦ is just a pronunciation mark for هِ.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. The alif at the end is pronounced like 'aa'.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects this phrase to the previous part about Allah helping you.
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special word. The middle ل gets a shadda to show emphasis.
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word عَلَى (upon) before it. The alif at the start changed to a special connecting alif (ٱ).

steps.m: to trust or rely on someone/something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make basic past tense verb
Added ت at beginning and doubled ك to show doing the action of trusting upon oneself or another - like saying 'I put my trust in'
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end - shows ongoing reliance/trust
Added لْ at start to make it a command and changed ending to sukun for jussive mood
Added فَ at start to mean 'then/so' and kasra at end because it connects to Allah in the ayah
Added an alif at the start to make it mean 'he made himself trust/believe' instead of just 'trust'
Changed to active participle form to show who does the action
Added ون to make it plural, showing multiple believers

steps.m: the believers (who should put their trust in Allah)
steps.n: Added ال to specify these particular believers mentioned in the ayah who should put their trust in Allah
This is a negative particle that negates the verb كَانَ (was) in this ayah, meaning 'it was not'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Added fathas to make it past tense before the weak letter change
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This word is negated by وَمَا before it, meaning 'and not/never'
Root letters got vowels (fat-ha) to form the noun meaning 'news'
Changed the word to mean someone who delivers important news from Allah
Added لِ to mean 'for' and ٍ to show we're talking about any prophet, not a specific one
This is a particle that connects words and introduces what comes next. Here it introduces the verb يَغُلَّ (he would deceive/betray), showing what was not possible for a prophet to do.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
The shadda doubles the middle letter to show something was done to someone
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense action

steps.m: that he would act dishonestly (subjunctive after أَن)
steps.n: Changed the ending to fatha because it comes after أَن (that) in the ayah
This is a basic interrogative/conditional particle meaning 'whoever' or 'who'

Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before. This word starts a new condition - whoever does this action will face its consequences, as shown by the verb يَغْلُلْ (he takes dishonestly) that follows it.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form
Changed from past to present tense by adding ي and changing vowels
Connected the root letters together to form the base word
Added يَ at the beginning to show who is doing the action (he) and when (present/future)
The final ي is dropped because this verb comes after a word that makes it conditional (like 'if' or 'whoever')
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation of the relative pronoun ما

steps.m: with what (connects to غَلَّ - took dishonestly)
steps.n: Added the preposition ب with kasra (بِ) at the beginning to show the meaning 'with'. This combination is used to connect to the verb غَلَّ (took dishonestly) to show what someone took.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
The middle letter is doubled (shown by the ّ mark) to show the action was done with intensity
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make the basic noun form

Added fatha at the end to show this word tells us when something happens. It's connected to the word ٱلْقِيٰمَةِ (the resurrection) that comes after it, forming the phrase 'on the Day of Resurrection'.
Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'the act of standing'
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of rising
Added ال to specify this is THE resurrection (Day of Judgment)
Added kasra at end because it comes after يَوْمَ (Day of) in the ayah. Also changed alif to alif waslah since it's connected to the previous word.

This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. Here it connects what happens on the Day of Judgment - first the person comes with what they took unfairly, then each soul is paid in full.

steps.m: to complete or pay in full

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger, showing complete fulfillment
Adding تُ at the start changes it from active to passive voice - showing the action is being done to someone/something rather than them doing it
The alif at the end is written in a special way (with a small line on top) because it comes at the end of the word
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added damma at the end because this word is doing the action in the sentence - it's the subject of the verb تُوَفَّىٰ (is given in full)
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the noun form meaning 'soul'
Added two dots under (tanwin kasra) to show it's 'a soul' - not a specific soul
Basic relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence, like saying 'what' or 'that which' in English
Added shadda for emphasis. In this ayah, it connects to كَسَبَتْ (earned/gained) to mean 'what they earned'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine, referring to نَفْسٍ (soul) in this ayah
Added diacritics to show proper pronunciation - damma (ُ) shows it's the subject form of 'they', and sukun (ْ) shows the end of the word
Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word is the subject of لَا يُظْلَمُونَ (they will not be wronged) in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it (يُظْلَمُونَ) mean the opposite. It changes 'they are wronged' to 'they are not wronged'.
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this is happening to someone rather than someone doing it
Added يُ at the beginning to show this is happening now, and adjusted other vowels
Added ون at the end to show this is happening to multiple people
Starting with the basic question word 'who' and adding ِ to connect it to what follows
Adding فَ at the beginning to show this follows from previous statement

steps.m: Then is the one who (subject of ٱتَّبَعَ)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question, asking about what follows
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added اِ at beginning and doubled the ت with shadda to show doing something thoroughly
No change from previous step - word is already in Form VIII pattern
Added kasra and sukun to change from verb to noun meaning
Added ان at the end to create a more specific noun form. This word is what is being followed in the phrase 'whoever follows Allah's pleasure'
Changed first letter and added shadda to make it a proper noun for Allah
Added kasra at the end to show possession/connection. This word is connected to سَخَطٍ (anger) in the phrase 'anger of Allah'.
This is the base word meaning 'one who' or 'whoever'. The small ن (nun) at the end helps pronunciation when connecting to the next word.

steps.m: like one who (connects to بَآءَ - returned with)
steps.n: Added كَ at the beginning to show comparison. This word compares someone to another person who returned with Allah's anger.

steps.m: to return or settle in a place

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he settled/returned' in the past tense
The middle letter و changed to ا with hamza because it's between two fathas. This word shows what happened to the person who didn't follow Allah's pleasure.

steps.m: to be angry or displeased with something

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make a noun meaning 'anger' instead of the action 'to be angry'
Added tanwin kasra to show this is receiving the action of 'with'
Added بِ to show this is describing how someone returned - with Allah's anger. This connects to the word اللَّهِ that comes after it.
Basic preposition showing source, origin, or separation from something
Added emphasis by doubling the م sound with a shadda
Added fatha at the end to connect it with the next word اللَّهِ (Allah)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters with shadda. This is a unique word that doesn't follow normal patterns because it refers to the one true God.
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word رِضْوٰنَ (pleasure/satisfaction) before it, showing it's Allah's pleasure/satisfaction
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'place where something happens' by adding مَ at the start and ى at the end
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it in the sentence

steps.m: and his place of shelter (referring to the person who earned Allah's anger)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show this is his shelter. This connects to the person mentioned earlier who earned Allah's anger.
This is the base form of the word, which comes from ancient Semitic languages. In the Quran, it refers specifically to the place of punishment in the afterlife.

steps.m: Hell (as the subject of the sentence)
steps.n: The ُ (damma) at the end shows this word is the subject of the sentence, meaning it's doing something or being described
Changes to a word used to criticize something as being very bad
Adds 'and' to connect this criticism to the mention of Hell (جَهَنَّمُ) in the verse
Adding مَ at the beginning turns the action into a place where that action happens
The ُ shows this word is the subject or completes the sentence
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to a specific destination
ال makes it specific ('the destination'), and the ُ shows it's completing a sentence
This is a basic pronoun that refers to a group being discussed. In this ayah, it connects to the word دَرَجٰتٌ (ranks) to show 'they have ranks with Allah'

steps.m: to go up step by step

steps.n: Changed from a verb meaning 'climbing' to a plural noun meaning 'steps' by adding vowels
Added ة to show one single step or level
Changed to plural form by replacing ة with ٰت to show multiple levels
Added ٌ to show this word is the subject of the sentence
The root letters ع-ن-د become عِند (near/with) by adding the kasra vowel mark, showing where something is located
Adding fatha at the end helps connect this word to what follows it (اللَّهِ)
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
The word for 'god' (ilah) becomes the special name 'Allah' - the one true God. The ِ (kasra) at the end shows it connects to another word.
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning of divinity and worship, but has become a unique proper noun.
Added shadda on ل to show it is doubled in pronunciation
Added damma at the end to show Allah is the subject doing the action in this sentence
Added و at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement, meaning 'and'

steps.m: to see or perceive (with eyes or understanding)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the middle vowel to kasra and added ي to show this is a permanent quality
Added the nominative case ending to show this describes Allah in the sentence
This is a basic preposition that shows how something is done or connected
Added kasra to show how the preposition connects to what follows it
Added مَا which means 'what' to show this connects to the things they do (mentioned in يَعْمَلُونَ - they do)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This verb describes what 'they' (هُمْ) are doing in the ayah.
Added sukun at the end to show this is a complete word
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis. This combination introduces the main verb مَنَّ (bestowed favor) in this ayah

steps.m: to give a gift or favor

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The doubled ن shows this was a special, important favor given to the believers (ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ)
Changed the root into a noun form that means 'one who receives worship'
Added ال (the) to show this is THE one true God, not just any deity. The ل is doubled to make it special for Allah's name. The ُ at the end shows Allah is doing the action.
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of or affecting something else. In this ayah, it shows Allah's favor being upon the believers.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling secure
Changed to show who does the action by adding مُ at start and changing vowels
Added ين at the end to make it plural - means more than one believer
Added ال to specify these particular believers. This word comes after عَلَى (upon) in the ayah.
This is a time particle that connects two events in the past. Here it connects Allah's favor (مَنَّ) with Him sending a messenger (بَعَثَ).

steps.m: to send forth or appoint (someone for a purpose)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word describes what Allah did as part of His favor (مَنَّ) to the believers.
This is the basic preposition في which shows something is inside or among something else
Added هِ to show this refers to a single male person or thing

steps.m: among them/in them (referring to المؤمنين/the believers mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added م with sukun to change from singular (him) to plural (them)
Added و and vowels to show this describes someone who does this job regularly

steps.m: a messenger (object of بَعَثَ - He sent)
steps.n: The ًا shows this word is receiving the action of بَعَثَ (sent) - Allah sent a messenger
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or is part of something. In this ayah, it shows the messenger is from (مِنْ) among themselves.
The n sound (ن) blends into the next letter when speaking, shown by the shadda mark (ّ)

steps.m: to breathe, be precious, be valuable

steps.n: Root meaning 'breathe' becomes a noun meaning 'soul/self' by adding fatha and sukun marks
Makes the word plural by adding hamza at start and changing vowels

steps.m: their selves/souls (referring to the believers mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Adding هِمْ shows it belongs to them (the believers mentioned as ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ earlier in the ayah). The ِ before هِمْ connects it smoothly to the word.
Connected the root letters with fatha. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, and damma on second root letter. The final letter returns to و in present tense.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to pronouns
Added هِمْ at the end to show this means 'them'

steps.m: to show, make clear, be apparent

steps.n: Forms a noun meaning something that shows or makes clear a message
Makes the word plural to show multiple signs/verses (this is what is being recited in the verse)

steps.m: His signs/verses (being recited by the messenger)
steps.n: Adds 'his' to show these are Allah's signs/verses being recited, and changes the middle alif to a long alif
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
The doubled middle letter (ّ) makes it mean 'causes purification' instead of just 'purifies'. The يُ at the start shows it's happening in the present. The weak letter و changes to ي at the end.
Added وَ (and) to connect this action with the previous actions of the Prophet mentioned in the verse
Added هِمْ (them) to show who is being purified - refers back to the believers (المؤمنين) mentioned earlier in the verse
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to make it mean 'to teach' instead of 'to know'
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added conjunction 'and' at start
Added pronoun 'them' at end
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes verb into noun for the result of writing

steps.m: the book (object being taught in يُعَلِّمُهُمُ)
steps.n: Adds 'the' and shows this is what is being taught. The َ at the end shows it's the object receiving the teaching action.

steps.m: to judge wisely, make wise decisions

steps.n: Changes the action into the quality or result of that action - from judging wisely to wisdom itself
Makes the abstract concept into something more specific and concrete that can be taught or shared
Adding 'al' (ال) makes it 'the wisdom' instead of just 'wisdom'
Shows this is what is being taught (object of يُعَلِّمُهُمُ)

steps.m: and the wisdom (as object being taught with الْكِتٰبَ)
steps.n: Connects this with الْكِتٰبَ as things being taught
This is a basic conditional or emphasis particle that can mean either 'if' or 'indeed' depending on context. In this ayah, it's used for emphasis.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it in the ayah

steps.m: to be or exist (in a state or condition)

steps.n: Added fatha (َ) after first letter to show it happened in the past. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a special type of word.
Added ُوا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or originates. In this ayah, it shows that the messenger comes from among the believers themselves.

steps.m: to come before or face towards

steps.n: Changes from root meaning into noun meaning 'before'
Adds damma to show this word is in nominative case
This is a basic preposition meaning 'in'
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, showing this is definitely or surely true

steps.m: to wander off the right path

steps.n: Changes the action into a noun showing the state of being lost or wandering
Adds genitive case ending to show possession or connection

steps.m: in a state of wandering away (in ضَلٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ - clear wandering)
steps.n: The alif (ا) became a long alif (ٰ), with the same meaning of 'wandering'. The kasra marks show it follows 'in' (في)
Added vowels and doubled middle letter to make basic past tense verb
Changed to active participle form showing who/what does the action
The م is doubled (has a shaddah) to blend with the previous word when reading, not for emphasis
Added genitive case ending because it describes the word ضَلٰلٍ (misguidance) in the ayah
This letter turns the statement into a question, showing surprise or disbelief
Adds 'and' to connect this question with what came before
Adds لَمَّا which means 'not yet' or 'when' depending on context. Here it shows something hasn't happened yet

steps.m: And when not yet...? (questioning with emphasis)
steps.n: The long alif at the end adds emphasis to the questioning tone
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb. The middle letter و becomes ا when used in a verb.
Added أَ at the beginning to make it mean 'to cause/make something happen to someone' instead of just 'to strike'
Added تْ to show this is about something (feminine) or she doing the action
Added كُم to show the action is happening to 'you all' (plural)
The كُم was already there in both words - this is just a spelling variation of the same word with same meaning. The alif changed form but pronunciation stays similar.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Added مُ at start to make it a present participle (doer of action) - like changing 'hit' to 'hitting'
Added ة to make it a specific instance of something that strikes/affects
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is any calamity, not a specific one
This is a particle that adds emphasis or certainty to the past action أَصَبْتُم (you struck/achieved). When قَدْ comes before a past tense verb, it confirms that the action definitely happened.

steps.m: to strike or hit something directly

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning to show causing something to happen
The weak letter و drops out and is replaced with a sukun when adding endings
Added تُم to show that multiple people (you all) did this action in the past

steps.m: to be like or similar to something

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun that means 'like' or 'similar to'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being used as an object
Adding ي changes the meaning from 'like' to 'twice the amount'

steps.m: two like it (referring to the previous loss/misfortune)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show this refers back to something mentioned before (the loss/misfortune)
The root letters change to make a command - just say 'say!'
Added تُ to show past tense first person (I)

steps.m: you (all) said (questioning what happened in the Battle of Uhud)
steps.n: Added م with sukun to show plural 'you all'. In this ayah, Allah is questioning what the Muslims said after their setback in the Battle of Uhud.
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning
Added alif maqsura (ى) at the end to change the word into a question word. This transforms it from a conjunction into an interrogative word asking about manner or source.
This is a demonstrative pronoun that points to something near. The ا at the end makes it specifically masculine singular.
Added the small alif (ٰ) above ه and fatha on ذ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The word changes from 'he said' to 'say!' by dropping the middle و and changing the vowels to make it a command to one person
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'he' or 'it' when talking about one male person or thing. In this ayah, it's connected to قُلْ (say), showing who is speaking.
Added the vowel marks ُ (damma) on ه and َ (fatha) on و to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'huwa'

steps.m: from (connects عِندِ with what comes before it)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it connects عِندِ (from) to the previous part, showing the source or origin.

steps.m: from (connects عِندِ with what comes before it)
steps.n: Added kasra (ِ) under م and sukun (ْ) on ن to show how to pronounce it correctly
Added kasra and sukun to create the preposition form meaning 'at/with/by'
Added kasra at the end because it's connected to the word مِنْ in the phrase مِنْ عِندِ (from/at)
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and form a singular noun
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple souls/selves
Added kasra to show possession will follow
Added plural 'your' ending to show who the souls/selves belong to
Added kasra under إ to show proper pronunciation
Added shadda to strengthen the ن, which makes the word emphasize what comes after it. This word introduces an important statement about Allah's power.
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters

steps.m: Allah (as the object being referred to)
steps.n: Added shadda to show emphasis of laam, changed alif to wasl-alif since it connects to previous word, and added fatha at end to show this is the object being referred to
This is a basic preposition that shows something is above or on top of something else, either physically or in authority/power
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation
Added alif khanjariyah (small alif) to show the long vowel sound at the end
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is connected to the word after it (شَىْءٍ meaning 'thing')
Connected the letters and added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it a noun
Added tanween kasra at the end to show this thing is owned by كُلِّ (every). The ي is written as ى which is just a spelling variation.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
This form shows someone who has a lot of this quality
This word describes Allah's complete and perfect power over all things, as mentioned in the ayah
This is a basic question word that can also mean 'that which' or 'whatever'
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'
The alif is written long (آ) to show it's pronounced clearly at the end. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added 'أ' at the start makes it mean 'to cause something to hit/affect' - like making something happen to someone
Adding كُم at the end makes it mean 'you all' as the object - the ones being affected

steps.m: he afflicted you all (object of وَمَآ meaning 'and what')

steps.n: Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make it into a noun meaning 'day'
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is in the accusative case
Adding vowels turns the root into a proper past tense verb meaning 'to meet'
The ت shows this is about two parties meeting each other

steps.m: to gather or join things together

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make the root into a noun meaning 'group'
Added ان to show we're talking about exactly two groups. From context, these are the two armies that met in battle.

steps.m: the two groups (referring to the two armies that met)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ (the) at the beginning to point to these specific two groups, and ِ at the end because it follows يَوْمَ (the day) in the ayah
Transforms into noun meaning 'permission'
Adds 'by/with' meaning to the word
Added فَ (meaning 'so/then') at the start to connect with previous statement. The word means 'permission' and shows it belongs to Allah
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the initial hamza merged with the lam creating a doubled letter

Added kasra at the end to show possession, and shadda on lam to show doubled letter. The alif at start changed to a waṣla because it connects with the previous word. This word is possessed by فَبِإِذْنِ (by permission) in this ayah.
Changed to present tense form showing ongoing knowledge or understanding
Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why something is happening

steps.m: and so that he knows (connected to فَبِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ - by Allah's permission)
steps.n: Added و to connect this purpose with the previous phrase about Allah's permission

steps.m: and so that he knows (object: ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ - the believers)
steps.n: Added fatha because it connects to 'the believers' who are being known/identified
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb showing a feeling of security
Changed from a past action (trusted) to describe someone who believes/trusts as part of their character
Added ending to show multiple believers
Added 'the' at beginning and mark at end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb 'to know' (يَعْلَمَ)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding ي at the start shows this is happening now or in the future
Adding ل shows this is happening for a reason or purpose

steps.m: and so that he knows (connected to previous statement)
steps.n: Adding و connects this clause with what came before
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people or things
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Changed spelling to standard Quranic form with wasl alif (ٱ), shadda on ل, and fatha at end. This word introduces the people who acted hypocritically (نَافَقُوا۟) in this verse.

steps.m: to spend or give out completely

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: he acted hypocritically (pretended to give/spend faith)
steps.n: Adding alif after first letter shows doing something back and forth or pretending - here it means pretending to give faith while keeping disbelief
Adding وا shows a group of people (all male or mixed) did this action in the past
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to passive voice by changing vowels. This shows the action happened but doesn't specify who did it.
Added وَ (and) to connect this with the previous statement
Added fatha to show the basic vowel of the preposition ل

steps.m: for them/to them (who were told تَعَالَوْا۟)
steps.n: Added هُمْ which means 'them' (referring to the hypocrites who were told to 'come' (تَعَالَوْا۟) in this verse)
Basic past tense form where و changes to ا at the end
Adding تَ at start changes it from 'he rose' to 'you come up/rise' - like telling someone to come over
Adding وا at the end makes it address multiple people
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Adding alif shows mutual combat or intensive fighting
Changed from past tense 'he fought' to a command 'fight!' by changing the middle vowel and removing the last vowel
Added وا to make it a command to a group, and changed alif to alif with overbar (قٰ) which is a common spelling variation
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it shows that something is not present inside their hearts.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Adds vowel marks to show this is describing a path or way
Adding ي makes this a permanent path/way rather than temporary condition
Added kasra at the end because it's connected to 'Allah' - like saying 'path of Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added a small alif (ٰ) to make the 'aa' sound longer
Added 'the' to make it specific and definite
The word Allah (ٱللَّه) is a unique name that comes from the word for 'god' (ٱلْإِلَٰه). The kasra ending (-ِ) shows it belongs to or connects with another word, here connecting to 'path' (سَبِيلِ)
This is a basic connecting word that gives a choice between two options. In this ayah, it connects between two commands: قٰتِلُوا۟ (fight) and ٱدْفَعُوا۟ (defend)
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word

steps.m: to push away or defend against

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he pushed' to 'you push!' by adding i- at start and changing the middle vowel - this makes it a command
Changed the beginning alif to a special connecting alif (ٱ) and added a 'u' sound at the end to make the command stronger
Added وا to make it a command to multiple people
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb pattern
Changed middle letter و to ا and added وا at the end to show many people did the action

steps.m: if (hypothetical, introducing the condition 'if we knew of a battle' in this ayah)
steps.n: Basic conditional particle used for unlikely or impossible conditions. Here it introduces a hypothetical situation the hypocrites are using as an excuse.

steps.m: if (hypothetical, introducing the condition 'if we knew of a battle' in this ayah)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: fatha on ل and sukun on و
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adds first person plural prefix
Adds present tense nominative ending
Changes from a basic verb root into a noun meaning 'the act of fighting'
The word 'fighting' gets an extra ending (-an) because it's the object (thing being known) in the phrase 'if we knew fighting'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Added ا at start and doubled ت to show doing something with extra effort and intention
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
Added كُمْ to show who was followed

steps.m: we would have followed you all
steps.n: Added لَ to show this is a hypothetical situation (would have)

steps.m: surely we would have followed you all
steps.n: Added emphasis by doubling the ل
Basic pronoun form showing multiple people (masculine)

steps.m: they/them (masculine, object of قِيلَ - it was said)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) on ه and sukun (ْ) on م to show this is being used as an object pronoun in the sentence. This pronoun refers to the hypocrites (الَّذِينَ نَافَقُوا) mentioned earlier.

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Adding fathas creates the basic past tense verb meaning 'he rejected/covered up truth'
Changes the verb into a noun that names the action itself
Adds لِ to show movement or leaning towards something (like saying 'towards' or 'to')

steps.m: towards the rejection/denial (closer to this than لِلْإِيمٰنِ/to the faith)
steps.n:
1) Adds ال to make it specific ('the rejection')
2) Adds كسرة (ِ) at the end because it follows لِ
3) This word is being compared to لِلْإِيمٰنِ (to the faith) in the ayah
Connected the root letters together to make the basic noun for 'day'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being used as an object or time expression

steps.m: on that day (when they were called to fight)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ to specify 'that day' referring to the specific time when they were called to fight
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'was near' to 'nearer/closer' by adding أ at start - makes it a comparative form
Basic preposition meaning 'from' - shows where something starts or comes from
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being referred to (them). This refers back to the hypocrites (منافقون) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Changed the word into a noun meaning the act of believing
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific concept of faith

steps.m: to/for the faith (contrasted with لِلْكُفْرِ - to/for disbelief)
steps.n: Added لِ to show direction/purpose and ِ at the end because it follows لِ. This word is contrasted with لِلْكُفْرِ (to/for disbelief) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle letter returns to و.
Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: to speak or use the mouth

steps.n: Root letters combined with vowels to make the word for 'mouth'
Changed from singular 'mouth' to plural 'mouths' by using the pattern أفعال (af'aal)
Added بِ at beginning to show how something is done

steps.m: with their mouths (what they say with يَقُولُونَ)
steps.n: Added هِم at end to show possession. This word describes how they say (يَقُولُونَ) something.
This is a relative pronoun that can mean 'what' or 'that which'. No transformation needed as this is the base form.
Added shadda for emphasis and to connect with previous word. This word is the object of يَقُولُونَ (they say) in this ayah, showing what they are saying.
This is a special verb that only comes in past tense form but gives present meaning. It's used to negate things.

steps.m: is not (negates ما in their mouths)
steps.n: This special verb always comes in past tense form but gives present meaning. Here it's negating what the hypocrites say with their mouths (ما), showing that what they claim isn't really in their hearts.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. Here it connects to قُلُوبِهِمْ (their hearts) to show what is inside them.
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under the ف to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'fee'
Changed the regular ي to ى (alif maqsurah) at the end because it's at the end of the word
Connected letters with fatha-sukun pattern to create noun for the thing that changes/turns (heart)
Changed vowel pattern and added و to make plural form

steps.m: their hearts (which is not in يَقُولُونَ)
steps.n: Added هِمْ ('their') at the end to show the hearts belong to them. The ِ (kasra) connects the words smoothly. This word appears in a phrase showing what is not in their hearts.
This is the proper name of God in Islam. It comes from the root meaning of divinity but is a unique word.
Added shadda to show the doubled ل sound

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of أَعْلَمُ - knows best)
steps.n: The ُ (damma) at the end shows that Allah is the subject doing the action in the sentence - specifically the one who 'knows best' what they hide.

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of أَعْلَمُ - knows best)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before it

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of أَعْلَمُ - knows best)
steps.n: Changed the alif to a waṣla form since it connects in pronunciation
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: more knowing (than the hypocrites who claim knowledge)
steps.n: Changed from 'he knew' to 'more knowing' by adding أ at the start to make it comparative, like saying 'I know better than they do'
Starting with the relative pronoun ما which refers to something unknown or general

steps.m: with what/with that which (object of يَكْتُمُونَ - they conceal)
steps.n: Added the preposition ب (with) to show how this connects to the verb يَكْتُمُونَ (they conceal). The phrase means 'with what they conceal' in the ayah.

steps.m: to hide or conceal something deliberately

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word is connected to يَقُولُونَ (they say) in the ayah, showing these are the same people speaking with their mouths what isn't in their hearts.
Starting with the basic word ذا (this) and changing it to ذي which can mean 'who'
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين at the end makes it plural, changing from 'the one who' to 'those who'. This word is the subject of قَالُوا (they said) in this ayah.
Adding the proper pronunciation marks (ٱ with wasl, shadda on ل, kasra on ذ) to show exactly how to say it
Added vowel marks to make the basic past tense form
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ by replacing the weak letter و with ا

steps.m: they said (subject of الَّذِينَ - those who)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people did the action. This word describes what 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) did in this verse.
Forms the basic noun 'brother' from the root concept
Changes to broken plural form meaning multiple brothers
Adds preposition لِ meaning 'to/for'
Changes ending to show it belongs to someone (like 'my brothers' or 'his brothers')
Adds هِمْ to show possession by them

steps.m: to sit, remain behind, stay inactive
he sat/remained behind (opposite of those who went out to fight)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create past tense. In this context, it means physically staying behind from battle, showing inaction or reluctance.

steps.m: they sat/remained behind (referring to the hypocrites who avoided battle)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did this action. This refers to the hypocrites mentioned in the verse who stayed behind from battle and then criticized those who went to fight.

steps.m: and they remained behind (connected to قَالُوا۟ - what they said)
steps.n: Added و to connect this action to what these people said about their brothers. The small mark at the end (۟) is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: if (hypothetical/impossible, used with قَالُوا۟ - they said)
steps.n: This word introduces an impossible or hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it's used with قَالُوا۟ (they said) to show what some people were saying about a situation that can't be changed.
Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding the 'aa' sound between first two letters and 'a' after last letter
Added أَ at the beginning to make it mean 'he obeyed' instead of 'he was naturally obedient'
Added وا to show many people did the action
Added نَا to show that 'us' is who they obeyed
This is a basic particle that can mean different things based on context. Here we start with its most basic form.
Adding the fatha mark shows this is being used as a negative particle. In this ayah, it negates the verb قُتِلُوا۟ (they were killed), making the meaning 'they were not killed'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice form

steps.m: they were killed (referring to those who stayed behind)
steps.n: Added plural masculine suffix وا to indicate 'they'
Added vowels to make the past tense form, which is the base for other forms
Changed to command form by: 1) adding damma at start, 2) removing middle weak letter و, 3) adding sukun at end. This tells one person to do something.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed to a command by adding 'u' at the start and making the word shorter - like saying 'push!' instead of 'he pushed'
Changed to plural command by adding وا at the end
Added فَ at start to show this is a response or result
Shows movement or action away from something

Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the noun form meaning 'death'

Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Command form - shortened by dropping middle letter and changing vowels to say 'be!'
Added تُمْ to show past tense for multiple people being addressed

Added alif after first root letter and kasra after second letter to show who does the action

This is a negative particle used to say 'no' or negate what comes after it

Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' and changed vowels for present tense

steps.n: Added نَّ at the end to add emphasis, like saying 'definitely' or 'surely'. This word is telling Prophet Muhammad ﷺ not to think that the martyrs are dead.

steps.n: Added ال ('the') at the start to point to specific people. This word connects to 'were killed' to tell us about those specific people who died.
This is the basic meaning showing the action of killing or slaying
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that someone received the action instead of doing it
Added وا at the end to show that many people were killed, not just one person

steps.m: in (connects سَبِيلِ with what comes before)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something is inside, within, or in the midst of something else. Here it connects 'path/way' (سَبِيلِ) with what comes before it.

steps.m: in (connects سَبِيلِ with what comes before)
steps.n: The ي at the end is written as ى when it's at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.
This pattern makes the word mean 'a path' instead of the action of making a path
Adding ي in the middle makes it mean a path/way
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it, meaning 'path of Allah'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and shadda on ل to emphasize it. This word shows who the people were killed for the sake of.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun for death
Added أ at the start and changed the middle of the word to make it mean multiple dead things or people

steps.m: dead ones (as the object being described)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word is being described in the sentence. It's being contrasted with أَحْيَآءٌ (alive ones) in the next part of the verse.

steps.n: This word corrects the wrong idea that those killed in Allah's path are dead. It introduces the truth that they are alive. It's like saying 'No, actually...' or 'On the contrary...'
Basic meaning of being in a state of life
Adding the fatha vowel mark helps show this is a past tense verb
Added أ at start and changed the middle to make it plural, showing multiple living things
Added ٌ at the end to show this is a complete description, meaning 'they are alive' rather than just 'living ones'

steps.m: to be present or located at
steps.n: This word is used to show location or possession, like saying something is 'at' or 'with' someone

Added fatha at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it (رَبِّهِمْ - their Lord)
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to create the noun meaning 'lord' - someone who takes care of others
Added kasra to show this word connects to what comes after it
Added هِمْ at the end to show this means 'their Lord'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from active (he provided) to passive (being provided) by adding يُ at start and changing internal vowels

steps.m: they are being provided (refers to those killed in Allah's cause)
steps.n: Added ون to show this is happening to multiple people. This refers back to الَّذِينَ قُتِلُوا (those who were killed) in the ayah.

steps.m: rejoicing ones (describing the martyrs in Paradise)
steps.n: The ين ending makes it a plural active participle, describing the state of multiple people. In this ayah, it describes the martyrs' state of joy in Paradise.
This is the basic relative pronoun meaning 'what' or 'whatever'
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is done 'with' something

steps.m: with what/with whatever (describing what Allah gave them)
steps.n: The alif is written long (آ) to show emphasis in writing, but the meaning stays the same. This word connects to ءَاتَىٰهُمُ (gave them) in the ayah.
Changed to Form IV to show causative meaning - making something happen
Added pronoun 'them' as object of the verb
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this is in the nominative case
Changed the root into the basic noun form for 'a deity'

This is a basic preposition that connects فَضْلِهِۦ (His bounty) to what comes before it, showing the source or origin. In this ayah, it shows the bounty comes from Allah.

steps.m: to give extra, to favor, to be generous

steps.n: Changed from a verb root (to favor) into a noun by adding vowels - now means the actual favor/blessing given

Added هِۦ at the end to show this favor belongs to Him (Allah)

steps.m: to bring good news, to make happy

steps.n: Added vowels to make it mean 'he brought good news' in the past tense
Added يَسْتَ at the start to show 'seeking/wanting' good news for oneself, making it mean 'he rejoices'
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: and they seek good news/rejoice (about الَّذِينَ)
steps.n: Added connecting particle to link with previous phrase. This word connects to الَّذِينَ (those who) in the ayah.

steps.m: with/about those who (connected to يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ - they rejoice)

steps.n: This word changes the meaning of the verb after it (يَلْحَقُوا۟) from present to past tense and makes it negative. It's like adding 'did not' in English.

steps.n: Added the preposition بِ (with/by) to show relationship. This word connects to يَلْحَقُوا۟ (join) in the ayah, referring to those who haven't yet joined the martyrs.
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts
The م gets doubled (gets a shadda) because it comes right after a word ending in ن, making the two sounds blend together. The sukun shows where to stop.
Changes from verb 'to follow' into noun meaning 'behind'
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by what comes after it
Added هِمْ to show this refers to 'them'
Starting with the first part of the compound: أَنْ which means 'that'

This is a basic pronoun for a group of males or mixed group. In this ayah, it's the subject of the verb يَحْزَنُونَ (they grieve).

steps.n: Changes from verb (to be social) to noun (people who are social)

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.n: Added هُمْ (them) to show who should be feared

Changed the word pattern to make it into a noun meaning the concept or state of believing

steps.m: and they said (connected to فَزَادَهُمْ إِيمٰنًا - so it increased them in faith)

Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific

steps.m: to be good, excellent, or pleasing

steps.n: Changes to a special verb used to praise something

steps.n: The prefix وَ (and) connects this praise with حَسْبُنَا اللَّهُ (Allah is sufficient for us) that came before, showing these are related statements of trust in Allah.

steps.m: to entrust or depend upon completely
one who can be fully trusted and depended upon

Changes from نَعْمَ to نِعْمَة by adding ة at the end to make it mean 'a single blessing'
Adds two dots under the end (-in sound) to show it's receiving the action and is not specific

steps.m: with a blessing (connected to فَٱنقَلَبُوا۟ - so they returned)

This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or what something is made of
The shadda (doubled letter) makes the word stronger and connects better with what follows
The fatha at the end helps connect this word to the next word (Allah) to show what something is from
Added shadda to show emphasis on the ل

Adds conjunction 'and' to connect with previous phrase
The ٍ ending shows this is 'a' favor (not specific), like saying 'some favor'

steps.m: not (changes يَمْسَسْهُمْ from present to past)

steps.m: not (changes يَمْسَسْهُمْ from present to past)

steps.m: touch/affect them (after لَمْ: did not touch/affect them)

Connected the root letters ت-ب-ع with fat-ha vowels to make 'he followed' in the past tense
Added Form VIII pattern which shows doing something with extra effort or purpose
Added وا to show multiple people did the action
Added و at beginning to connect this action to what came before it

Added ان at the end to make it a noun meaning 'pleasure/satisfaction'. Connected to اللَّهِ to show it is Allah's pleasure.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.
Added ِ at the end to show this belongs to something else. In this ayah, it shows Allah's bounty (فَضْلٍ).
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning of divinity but is a unique word.
Added shadda on ل to show it is doubled in pronunciation
The ُ (damma) at the end shows that Allah is the subject doing the action in this sentence
Added وَ at the beginning to mean 'and'. The ٱ is just a different way of writing the first letter ا
This is a special word that doesn't come from a typical root. It's used to show that something has or owns a quality.
Added damma (ُ) to show this word is doing something in the sentence. Here it's describing Allah's favor (فَضْلٍ).

steps.m: to give extra, to give more than needed

This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain

Starting with the basic pointing word 'this'. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this style.
Adding لِ changes 'this' (near) to 'that' (far), like pointing to something farther away

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) to all of you (subject of يُخَوِّفُ)
steps.n: Adding كُمُ shows this word is speaking to multiple people. This word points to الشَّيْطٰنُ (Satan) who is mentioned right after it.

steps.m: to be far away from good/truth
one who is far from good/truth
steps.n: Root letters ش-ط-ن got extra letters to make it mean 'the one who is far from good'. The ان ending makes it a noun meaning 'the doer'

steps.m: the Satan (subject of يُخَوِّفُ - he frightens)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific (the Satan), and ُ to show it's doing the action of frightening in this verse
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger
Adding يُ at the start and changing vowels shows this is happening now. This word describes what الشيطان (Satan) is doing.

steps.m: to be close to and protect
one who is close and protects
steps.n: Changed from a verb (to protect) into a noun (protector) by adding vowels and doubling the last letter
Changed to plural form using pattern أفعلاء for important roles
Added هُ to show possession by Satan, who was mentioned earlier in the ayah. The ۥ is just a pronunciation mark.
Added fatha to show clear pronunciation

Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what came before. This word connects to يُخَوِّفُ (he frightens) in the previous part, showing what should happen as a result of Satan's frightening.
Connected the root letters and added vowels. The middle و changes to ا in past tense.
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and changed last vowel to ُ to show present tense
Added و at the end to show multiple people doing the action
Added هُمْ (hum) at the end to mean 'them' - the people being feared
Connected the root letters with vowels. The middle weak letter و changes to ا in past tense.
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this is in the nominative case
Changes from 'he fears' to 'fear me' by adding -ni (meaning 'me') at the end
Adds conjunction meaning 'and'
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition - in this case, it connects 'if you are' (كُنتُم) with what came before it. The complete meaning is 'if you are believers'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed to 'you' form by: 1) adding ت at the end, 2) changing first vowel to ُ, and 3) dropping middle weak letter و
Changed from singular 'you' to plural 'you all' by: 1) adding م at the end, and 2) changing the vowel on ت to ُ. This word connects to مُّؤْمِنِينَ (believers) in the ayah.
Changes the root into 'someone who believes' by adding مُ at the start (like adding '-er' in English to make 'believe' into 'believer')
Adds extra strength to the meaning by doubling the م sound
Makes it plural and shows it's connected to كُنتُم (you were) in the sentence
Plural form of believer (mu'min) with special ending -īna showing it's the object
This is a negative particle that tells us something is not happening. Here it's saying something should not make someone sad.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Added a small line (fatha) above the middle letter to show this means 'not'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding ي at the start shows this happens in the present/future
Changed the middle vowel to show ongoing state of sadness

steps.m: he makes you sad (after لا meaning 'do not let')
steps.n: Added -ka suffix to show who is being made sad. This word comes after لا (do not), so the full phrase means 'do not let them make you sad'
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that refers to someone or something
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to multiple people
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'those who' instead of just 'who'. This word describes the people who are rushing (يُسٰرِعُونَ) in the verse.
Adding the shadda (ّ) emphasizes the sound, and the final fatha (َ) helps connect it to the next word. The hamza at the start (ٱ) is a spelling variation.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding alif shows this is done with/against others, like racing or competing
Added يُ at start to show 'he' is doing this action now (present tense), instead of in the past

steps.m: they rush eagerly/hasten (those who يَحْزُنكَ refers to)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. The long alif (ٰ) is a spelling variation of ا. This refers to the same group mentioned in يَحْزُنكَ (those who grieve you).
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it shows rushing/hastening into disbelief.
Added kasra (ِ) under the ف to show how to pronounce it as 'fee'
Changed the ي to ى which is just a different way of writing the same sound at the end of words

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Changed from action (to reject) into thing/concept (rejection) by adding vowel marks
Added ال to make it specific
Added kasra at the end because it follows فِى (in) in the ayah
This is a basic emphasis particle that makes statements stronger and more certain

steps.m: indeed they (connects to يَضُرُّوا۟ - they will harm)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who the word is talking about. This connects to the next word يَضُرُّوا۟ (they will harm) in the ayah.
This is a fixed particle that negates future actions. In this ayah, it negates يَضُرُّوا۟ (they will harm), making it mean 'they will never harm'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added ي at start to show present tense, changed vowels to damma, and combined the repeated ر with shadda
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action. This verb comes after لَن which means 'will not' in the future.
Changed إِلَه to اللَه, which is a unique name specifically referring to The One True God in Islam

steps.m: Allah (as the object being harmed in يَضُرُّوا)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل and fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being harmed in the phrase 'they will never harm Allah'
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun form
Added ـًا at the end to show this is what's being affected by the action. In this ayah, it shows what they will not harm - they will not harm Allah at all (not even a thing).
The middle letter و changes to ي because it's between a kasra and a fatha
Added يُ at the beginning to show present tense and ُ at the end to show this is the subject doing the action. This word refers to what Allah wants in this ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Starting with أَنْ which means 'that' and is used to introduce a new clause
Added لا (not) after أَنْ to negate what follows (يَجْعَلَ - He makes)
The نْ combines with ل forming لّ with a shadda, a common pronunciation change when these letters meet. This word introduces what Allah does not want to give them.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense verb

steps.m: he makes/sets/puts/places (that Allah will not make)
steps.n: Added يَ at beginning and changed vowels to show present tense. The sukun on ج shows it's connected to the next letter. This verb comes after أَلَّا (that not) in the ayah, showing Allah will not do this action.
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added هُ (him) to show who it's for
Added م with sukun to change 'him' to 'them'
Added basic vowels to connect the letters
Shadda combines the doubled ظ into one emphasized letter
Adding -an ending to show this word receives the action (someone gets a portion)
This is a basic preposition that shows location or being inside something. In this ayah, it shows people rushing into disbelief.
The ي changes to ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to come after or be delayed
steps.n: The root letters show the basic concept of something coming later
Adding آ at start makes it mean 'more/most late' or 'last'
Added ة at the end to make it refer to a specific instance - in this case, the final life (afterlife)
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Added kasra (i) sound at the end because it shows this word is connected to 'share/portion' before it
Added fatha to the basic preposition ل to connect it with what follows
Added هُمْ to show who the preposition is referring to (them)

steps.m: and for them (they have عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This phrase connects to عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ (great punishment) which comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past-tense verb
Changed verb into a noun by adding alif after second root letter. This word is described by عَظِيمٌ (great) in this ayah.
Adding double dammah makes it indefinite, meaning 'a punishment' instead of 'the punishment'
Base form I verb with damma on middle radical indicating a quality/state
Added vowels to make a past tense verb showing something becoming great
Changed the pattern to make an adjective describing something as having this quality
Added the ٌ ending to show this is describing the punishment mentioned in the ayah
This is a basic particle that has no root. It shows conditions or possibilities.
Adding the shadda changes this from a conditional word to one that adds emphasis to what follows. In this ayah, it emphasizes the statement about those who exchange faith for disbelief.
Starting with the basic demonstrative word that points to things
Changed from pointing to something ('this/that') to connecting descriptions ('who/which') by changing the ending
Added ال at the beginning to make it more specific, and the letter gets a shadda (double mark)

steps.m: those who (describes what the people in this ayah did)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word introduces what these people did - they exchanged faith for disbelief.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Added letters to show this is about actively choosing to make an exchange for oneself
Changed to plural form by adding وا and replacing final ى with و. The hamza at the start changed to ٱ due to connection with previous word.

steps.m: to reject or deny truth, to be ungrateful

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun by adding ُ on first letter and ْ on second letter. This makes it describe the act of rejecting truth itself, rather than the action of rejecting.
Added ال to point to specific disbelief mentioned in context, and َ to show it's being exchanged in this verse. This word is paired with بِٱلْإِيمٰنِ (faith) to show what is being exchanged.
Basic vowels (fatha) added to make the root pronounceable
Root verb أَمَن becomes the noun إِيمَان (faith) by adding ا and ي at the start
Adding definite article. This word is what was exchanged/purchased in the phrase 'those who purchased disbelief (in exchange for) faith'
Adding kasra because it comes after the preposition بِ
Adds preposition بِ meaning 'with/by'. Shows what was given up in the exchange mentioned by اشْتَرَوُا (they purchased)
This is a negative particle that makes a strong denial about future actions. In this ayah, it negates the verb يَضُرُّوا۟ (they will harm), showing that they absolutely will not harm Allah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing vowels, and combining repeated ر with shadda

steps.m: they harm (لَن makes it mean 'will never harm' in this ayah)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people doing the action. The word لَن before it makes it mean 'will never harm' in this context.
Changed to the specific name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters

steps.m: Allah (as the object being harmed in لَن يَضُرُّوا۟)
