steps.n: Added shadda to show emphasis, fatha at end to show Allah is the object being harmed in the phrase 'they will never harm Allah', and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word
Added vowels to the root letters to make the common word for 'thing'
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon. In this ayah, it means they won't harm Allah in any way.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it

steps.m: and for them (connected to عَذَابٌ - punishment)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who the 'for' refers to. This phrase connects to عَذَابٌ (punishment) in the ayah, showing who will receive the punishment.

Adding an alif between the second and third root letters and fathas on the first two letters turns this into a noun meaning the punishment itself. This word comes after ولهم (and for them) in the ayah.
Adding the double-damma mark (tanween) at the end shows this is talking about a general punishment, not a specific one. It's like saying 'a punishment' instead of 'the punishment'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed to intensive adjective pattern showing something that causes strong pain. This word describes the punishment (عَذَابٌ) mentioned in the ayah.
Added tanween dammah to make the word indefinite
This is a negative particle that tells us the following verb يَحْسَبَنَّ (think/consider) is not happening
The و at the start connects this with what came before it, while لا negates the following verb يَحْسَبَنَّ (think/consider)
The basic meaning of counting or considering something
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Adds strong emphasis to the verb meaning 'definitely/surely'
This is a special demonstrative word composed of the definite article ال + relative pronoun ذي + masculine plural suffix ن

steps.m: to reject or deny truth, to cover up or hide something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb showing one person did the action
Added وا with an alif after it (وٓا۟) to show multiple people did the action. This refers to the disbelievers (ٱلَّذِينَ) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
This is an emphatic particle that introduces what someone thinks or believes. It makes the meaning stronger and more certain.

steps.m: that/surely that (introducing what the disbelievers think in يَحْسَبَنَّ)
steps.n: Adding مَا makes the meaning more emphatic and certain. This combination is used to introduce what the disbelievers wrongly think in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks and changed و to ا at the end because it's a weak letter

steps.m: we give time (to كَفَرُوا - those who disbelieved)
steps.n: Added نُ at start to mean 'we', changed vowels to show present tense, and changed ا to ي at end to connect with كَفَرُوا (those who disbelieved)
This is a basic particle that shows something is being given or directed to someone

steps.m: for them/to them (receiving نُمْلِى - We give respite)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who is receiving the action. In this ayah, it shows who is being given respite or time by Allah.
Changes from verb meaning 'to be good' into noun meaning 'good'
Adding double dammah makes it an indefinite noun in the nominative case
The root letters are given vowels to make a noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'. When combined with 'their', it becomes 'their selves'.
Changes نَفْس (self) to its plural form by adding أ at the start and changing the middle vowels - like how 'foot' becomes 'feet' in English
Added لِّ with shadda for emphasis, meaning 'for/to' with extra strength
Added pronoun هِمْ meaning 'their' to show possession
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain
Adding مَا changes the meaning from simple emphasis to restriction, showing that what follows (giving them more time) is the only thing happening, not anything else
Added the basic vowels and changed و to ي because it comes at the end of the word
Added نُ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. This word refers to Allah giving time to those who disbelieved (كَفَرُوٓا۟).
Basic directional particle showing something is 'for' or 'to' something else

steps.m: for them (object of نُمْلِى - we give time)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who the action is for. This word connects to نُمْلِى (we give time) to show who is being given time.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make past tense verb
The ت changed to د after ز due to a sound rule, but the meaning is the same as Form VIII
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end
Added لِ to show purpose - why something is happening
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: to do something harmful or wrong

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun by adding إِ at the start and putting a sukun (ْ) in the middle

steps.m: sin (object of يَزْدَادُوا - what they increase in)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is what they are increasing in. This connects to the verb يَزْدَادُوا (to increase) in the ayah.
Added fatha to the basic preposition ل to show its basic meaning of 'for' or 'to'
Added هُمْ at the end to show this is 'for them' (referring to those who disbelieve, mentioned earlier in the ayah)

steps.m: and for them (connected to عَذَابٌ مُّهِينٌ - a humiliating punishment)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to 'a humiliating punishment' that follows in the ayah, showing they will have this punishment
Adding fatha vowels and an alif creates a noun meaning the result of punishment. This word comes after لَهُمْ (they have) in the ayah.

steps.m: a punishment (subject of لَهُمْ - they have)
steps.n: The double dammah (ٌ) at the end shows this is any punishment, not a specific one. It's like saying 'a punishment' instead of 'the punishment'.

steps.m: to humiliate or make light of something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show something that causes humiliation. This word describes the punishment (عَذَابٌ) mentioned in the ayah.
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is any humiliating thing, not a specific one
Adding shadda (double letter mark) on م makes this word stronger/more intense
This is the basic form of the negation particle that makes something negative, like adding 'not' in English. Here it works with كَانَ to say 'was not'.
The shadda (ّ) and fatha (َ) are added to emphasize the negation, making it stronger - like saying 'definitely not' instead of just 'not'.
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in a past tense verb. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to say.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
The و from the root drops because it's a weak letter at the beginning. Added fatha marks to show the present tense form.

steps.m: so that he would leave (the believers) alone
steps.n: The لِ shows purpose here - Allah would not leave the believers in their current state. This connects to مَّا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ (Allah would not) before it.

steps.m: to trust, feel safe, or give safety to others

steps.n: Added مُ at the start to show who does the action (like adding -er in English). The ؤْ comes from the root أ with a sukun mark to connect it.
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to three or more male believers

steps.m: the believers (object of يَذَرَ - to leave)
steps.n:
1. Adding ٱلْ at start makes it specific - these particular believers
2. Adding َ (fatha) at end shows this word is being acted upon by يَذَرَ (to leave)
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of, upon, or about something else. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
Added an alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) at the end. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning or pronunciation.
Basic form of the interrogative/relative pronoun particle
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
The alif is written with madd (آ) to show elongated pronunciation. In this ayah, it's asking about the state or condition that the believers are upon.
Basic form of the pronoun that means 'you'
Adding تُ changes it from singular to plural
Adding مْ makes it stronger and completes the plural form. This pronoun is connected to the word عَلَيْهِ (upon it) in the ayah.
When adding a pronoun to عَلَى, the final ى changes to يْ to make it easier to connect with the next part. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added هِ at the end to show who the action is happening to (him). This connects to the previous word اللَّهُ (Allah) in the ayah, referring back to Allah.
This is a particle that shows a limit or endpoint. The shadda doubles the ت letter to emphasize the stopping point.
The ىٰ (alif maqsurah) at the end shows this word connects to what comes after it. Here it connects to the verb يَمِيزَ (distinguishes), showing the limit or endpoint of the action.
Adding fatha vowels creates the basic past tense form
Changed to present tense form by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra. This comes after حَتَّىٰ (until) which requires the present tense form.
Forms a noun describing someone characterized by impurity/corruption. This word is the object of يَمِيزَ (distinguish/separate) in this ayah.
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific/definite, and the fatha at the end shows it's the object being acted upon
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts. Here it connects يَمِيزَ (distinguish) with ٱلْخَبِيثَ (the evil), showing what is being distinguished from what.
Added fatha at the end to help connect smoothly with the next word. This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
Root letters get vowels and middle letter doubles to make a word meaning 'having good/pure qualities'
Adding kasra because it follows the preposition مِنَ (from) in the ayah

steps.m: the good, pure one (object of مِنَ)
steps.n: Adding definite article to match with الخبيث (the bad) that it's being contrasted with in the ayah
This is a negative particle that makes the statement negative. Here it negates كَانَ (was) to mean 'was not'.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added fathas to make the basic past tense form

The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in a past tense verb. This is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle. In this ayah, the word is negated by ما which comes before it, showing that something was not the case.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Adding 'al' makes it refer to a specific deity rather than any deity
Adding damma shows this word is the subject of the sentence
Connected the root letters together with basic past tense voweling
Changes to present tense and adds causative meaning (making something happen)

steps.m: in order that he reveals (connected to كَانَ)
steps.n: Adds لِ to show purpose, connecting this action as the purpose/result of كَانَ (was) earlier in the verse

steps.m: so that he reveals to you all
steps.n: Adds second person masculine plural object
This is a preposition showing position or relationship. In this ayah, it connects 'to inform you' (لِيُطْلِعَكُمْ) with 'the unseen' (ٱلْغَيْبِ), meaning 'to inform you about/of the unseen'

steps.m: to be absent or hidden from view

steps.n: Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it into a noun meaning 'something that is hidden or unseen'
Added ٱلْ (al-) at the beginning to make it specific and ِ (kasra) at the end because it follows عَلَى (upon) in the ayah
This is a compound particle that introduces a contrast with what came before
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters

steps.m: Allah (as the object being believed in)
steps.n: Added shadda to emphasize the ل, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha to show this is the object of belief in the phrase 'believe in Allah'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added present tense marker يَ and adjusted vowels for present tense form
Adding ت shows he is doing the action for himself
The ى at the end shows this is a special type of verb that ends in a weak letter
This is a preposition showing separation or origin. In this ayah, it's used to show the separation between الْخَبِيثَ (the bad) from الطَّيِّبِ (the good).

steps.m: to send or carry a message

steps.n: The و makes it mean someone who does this action a lot
Changes the word to show multiple messengers
Shows this word is owned by or connected to something
Adds 'his' to show these are Allah's messengers
The letter ر gets doubled (has a shaddah) because it comes after و, making it stronger when pronounced
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to people. In this ayah, it refers to whoever Allah chooses from His messengers.
Changed from basic root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding vowels. The middle letter ي becomes ا when used this way.
Changed from past tense (he wanted) to present tense (he wants) by adding يَ at the start. This word shows Allah's ongoing will/choice.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form for multiple people
Added فَ to connect this command with what came before it in the verse
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. It's a unique word that doesn't come from a typical root.
Added بِ at the beginning to show connection/means, and ِ at the end because it's being acted upon by the verb 'believe'. This word is connected to the command فَـَٔامِنُوا۟ (believe!) in the ayah.
Changed the vowels to make a plural form showing multiple messengers
Added ِ to show this connects to another word
Added هِۦ at the end to show these are Allah's messengers

steps.m: and His messengers (connected to آمِنُوا - believe in)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the command to believe (آمِنُوا) in Allah and His messengers
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects to تُؤْمِنُوا۟ (you believe) to show what happens if you do something.

steps.m: and if (connects this condition to previous statement)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement. This shows this condition is related to what came before it.
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding the vowels a-i-a, showing someone felt safe/secure
Changed from basic 'trust' to stronger religious 'belief/faith' by adding alif at start
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يُ with تُ at start
Added وا at the end to show multiple people are being addressed
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and doubled ق to show 'you' are doing the protecting to yourself
Added وا at the end to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: and you all protect yourselves (connected to تُؤْمِنُوا۟ - believe)
steps.n: Added و at start to connect this action with believing mentioned before it
Starting with the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being addressed
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning the result of the action
Added double dammah to make it indefinite (a reward)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changes the verb into an adjective showing intensity. This word describes the reward (أَجْرٌ) mentioned in the ayah.
This is the basic negative particle that means 'not'. In this ayah, it negates the verb يَحْسَبَنَّ (think/consider).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing last vowel to ُ

steps.m: let him not think (in context of وَلَا before it)
steps.n: Added emphatic نَّ ending and changed final vowel to فتحة because it's in a negative context with وَلَا before it
Added ن at the end to show we're talking about multiple people

steps.m: those who (subject of يَبْخَلُونَ - they withhold)
steps.n: Added ال at beginning with a shadda on the following letter to point to specific people. This word is the subject of يَبْخَلُونَ (they withhold) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they withhold/are withholding (referring to those who received Allah's bounty)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This refers to those mentioned earlier who received Allah's bounty but are withholding it.
Added fatha to show the basic pronunciation of this relative pronoun that means 'what' or 'that which'
Added alif with madda (آ) to show the long vowel sound. This is just a spelling variation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: with what/with that which (connected to خَبِيرٌ - well-acquainted)
steps.n: Added the preposition بِ (with) at the beginning. In this ayah, it connects to the word خَبِيرٌ (well-acquainted), showing Allah is well-acquainted with what you do.
Changed root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding hamza and alif at start
Added alif maksura at the end to show proper spelling of weak letter ending
Added هُمُ at the end to show who received the giving
Changed the root into a noun form that means 'one who is worshipped'

This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite and specific, while the shadda emphasizes its uniqueness. The ُ shows this is the subject of the verb خَبِيرٌ (is All-Aware) in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or something
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under م to show how to pronounce it correctly. In this ayah, it connects to what Allah gave them.

steps.m: to give extra, to favor someone

steps.n: The root letters were made into a noun meaning 'favor' by adding vowel marks (fatha-sukun-nothing)
Added kasra to show this word is being possessed by something after it
Added the pronoun 'his' referring to Allah in this context

steps.m: he/it (refers to being stingy with Allah's bounty)
steps.n: This is a basic pronoun that refers back to something mentioned before

steps.m: he/it (refers to being stingy with Allah's bounty)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) on ه and fatha (َ) on و to show proper pronunciation
Added vowel marks to the root letters to make the word meaning 'good' or 'better'
Shows this word is being acted on by adding -an ending
Starting with the basic combination of ل (for/to) and هم (them)
Adding the shadda on ل and damma on ه to help with pronunciation. The shadda here comes from assimilation with another ل that was originally present.

steps.m: rather/but rather (correcting the assumption that hoarding wealth is good for them)
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces a statement that corrects or contradicts what came before. Here it corrects the wrong assumption that being stingy with Allah's bounties is good for them.

steps.m: he (refers to being stingy with Allah's bounty)
steps.n: This is a standalone pronoun that refers back to the act of being stingy mentioned earlier in the ayah. It connects the subject (being stingy) with what comes after it (خَيْرًا - good) to say that the stingy people shouldn't think their stinginess is good for them.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic word form
The doubled letter with shaddah makes the meaning stronger
Added fatha to make the letter pronounceable
Added shadda for emphasis, showing certainty

steps.m: surely for them (connected to خَيْرًا - good)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who the preposition refers to. This word connects to خَيْرًا (good) in the ayah, showing who the good is for.
Added shadda to show intensified action of wrapping

steps.m: he is being made to wear (around his neck)
steps.n: Changed from 'he wrapped' to 'is being wrapped around him' - shows someone else is doing this to him now

steps.m: they are being made to wear (around their necks)
steps.n: Added plural ending to show this happens to multiple people

steps.m: they will be made to wear (around their necks)
steps.n: Added future prefix to show this will happen later

steps.m: what (refers to what they were stingy with)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that links back to what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it refers to the wealth/blessings that stingy people are withholding. It works with بَخِلُوا (they were stingy) to show what they were stingy with.

steps.m: to withhold or keep something for oneself

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form meaning 'he was stingy/withheld'
Changed the verb to show multiple people did the action (in this ayah, referring to those who withhold what Allah gave them)

steps.m: with it (referring to what they withheld/were stingy with)
steps.n: Connected the preposition ب (with) to the pronoun ه (it). In this ayah, it refers back to what the stingy people withheld from spending in Allah's cause.
Added a small alif (ۦ) after the ه as a pronunciation guide to show it should be read as 'bihi' not 'bih'
Connected the letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun for 'day'

steps.m: day (object of سَيُطَوَّقُونَ - they will be encircled)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of being encircled in the phrase 'they will be encircled on the Day of Resurrection'
Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'the act of standing'
Added ة to make it refer to a specific instance - in this case, the final resurrection
Added ال to specify this is THE resurrection (Day of Judgment)
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to يَوْمَ (day of) in the ayah
This is a unique word in Arabic that refers to God. It's not derived from a root in the usual way.
Added ل at the beginning to show ownership or connection to Allah
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it in the sentence
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it

steps.m: to pass down/receive something from someone who has passed away

steps.n: Added مِ at the start to turn the action (inheriting) into a noun meaning the actual thing that gets passed down
Changed و to ي because it's easier to pronounce, but keeps the same meaning of inheritance

steps.m: inheritance (the subject of وَلِلَّهِ - belongs to Allah)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this is the subject of the phrase 'belongs to Allah' (وَلِلَّهِ). The alif is written as ٰ which is just a different way to write it.

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high up
Changed from single sky/heaven to plural by adding long vowels and -aat ending
Added kasra to show this belongs to Allah in this ayah
Added definite article 'the' and doubled the س with shadda
Basic noun pattern for 'earth'
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Added kasra at the end to show it's possessed by the word ميراث (inheritance)
Added و at the beginning to connect it with السماوات (the heavens)
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. It comes from the root meaning 'deity' but is a unique word that only refers to the One God.
Added shadda on ل to show it is doubled in pronunciation
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb خَبِيرٌ (All-Aware) in this ayah
Added و at the beginning to connect this part to what came before, like saying 'and'
Changed the alif to a different form (ٱ) which is a common writing style in the Quran but doesn't change the meaning
Combination of preposition ب (with) and relative pronoun ما (what)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ون at the end to show multiple people are being addressed

steps.m: to inform or be aware of news/information

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic noun form
Added ي between second and third root letters and kasra on middle letter to show someone who has this quality intensely
Adding tanween shows this is one of Allah's attributes mentioned independently
This is an emphasis particle that adds certainty to statements
Added ل at the beginning to add more emphasis
Added shadda on ل to make the emphasis even stronger
This is the basic concept of hearing or listening
Added vowel marks to show this is a past tense verb. In this ayah, Allah (ٱللَّهُ) is the one who heard.

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun for 'a deity' by adding vowel marks
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Added damma to show this word is the subject
Changed from a root meaning 'to speak' into a noun meaning 'speech' by adding vowel marks
Added fatha at the end to show this is what Allah heard (the object of سَمِعَ/heard)
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and turn 'who' into 'those who'
Added shadda on ل and changed spelling of alif to match Uthmani script. This word is the subject of قَالُوٓا۟ (they said) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels and changed و to ا because it's between two fathas. This makes it a past tense verb.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. The small marks (ٓ and ۟) are just pronunciation helpers.
Adding the shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphatic 'indeed'. This emphasizes what was said (قَالُوٓا۟) in the ayah.
Changed to the unique name Allah by adding shadda and replacing the first letter with ل. This special word change shows this is the one true God, not just any deity.
Added ٱ at start and َ at end because this word comes after إِنَّ (indeed/verily) in the ayah, which makes Allah the object being talked about
Added vowel marks (fatha and kasra) to make it an adjective meaning 'poor' or 'in need'
Added ي to show this describes someone who has this quality strongly or permanently

steps.m: a poor one (in contrast to Allah being called rich in this verse)
steps.n: Added tanween dammah to show this is describing someone in general terms. This word appears in contrast to Allah being called rich in this verse.
This is a personal pronoun meaning 'we'. In this ayah, it's part of what was said by those who claimed Allah is poor while they are rich.

steps.m: and we (subject of قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects their claim about Allah being poor with their boast about themselves being rich.
Root letters got vowels to make a word meaning 'someone who is rich'
Added أَ at the start and changed the word ending to اء to make it mean 'wealthy people' instead of just 'wealthy'

steps.m: wealthy ones (subject of وَنَحْنُ - and we)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this is the subject of the sentence, connected to وَنَحْنُ (and we)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Removed first vowel and changed middle/end vowels to make it present tense
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action
Added سَ at beginning to show this will happen in the future
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' or 'that which'. In this ayah, it refers back to what the disbelievers said, connecting their statement to what will be written.

steps.m: he said (subject of إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ فَقِيرٌ)
steps.n: Added fatha marks to make the past tense form. This shows what happened in the past. The و changes to ا because it's between two vowels.

steps.m: they said (subject of إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ فَقِيرٌ)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people (they) did the action. This refers to those who disbelieved and spoke against Allah.
Changes the action 'to kill' into the noun 'killing' - like how 'to run' becomes 'running'
Adds هُمُ to show who did the killing. In this ayah, it refers to their killing of the prophets mentioned right after this word.

steps.m: and their killing (of الْأَنۢبِيَآءَ - the prophets)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this with what Allah will record: both what they said (قَالُوا۟) and their killing of the prophets

steps.m: someone who tells important news from Allah
steps.n: Changed from 'telling news' to mean 'a person who brings news from Allah (a prophet)'
Changes the word from one prophet to many prophets by adding أ at the start and آء at the end - like changing 'prophet' to 'prophets' in English
Adding ال makes it specific prophets we're talking about
The fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb قَتْلَهُمُ (their killing of)
Added vowel marks to make a noun meaning 'other than'. This word modifies حَقٍّ (right/truth) to mean 'without right'.
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'with/by' and ِ at the end because it owns the next word حَقٍّ (right/truth), making the complete phrase بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ mean 'without right'
The doubled letter (shadda) makes it a strong noun meaning 'truth' instead of the action 'being truthful'
The ٍ ending shows this word is connected to بِغَيْرِ meaning 'without any right/justification'
Changed vowels to make present tense base. The middle ا returns to و.
Added نَ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it in the verse

steps.m: taste/experience! (plural command) (referring to عَذَابَ the punishment)
steps.n: Added وا to make it a command for multiple people. This is Allah commanding a group to taste/experience the punishment mentioned right after this word (عَذَابَ).
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make it a past-tense verb
Added alif after second root letter to make it a noun meaning the result of the action. This word is what is being tasted/experienced in the phrase 'taste the punishment'
Added ي to show this is a strong, lasting quality. This word specifically refers to a painful, burning punishment in this context.
Adds 'ٱلْ' to make it specific and 'ِ' because it follows after the word عَذَابَ (punishment of) in this ayah
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
Added the alif above the ذ to show emphasis
Added لِ to change from 'this' (pointing to something close) to 'that' (pointing to something far away)
Added كَ at the end to complete the demonstrative pronoun, making it specifically point to something far away
This is the basic interrogative particle meaning 'what'

steps.m: because of what (relates to قَدَّمَتْ - sent forth)
steps.n: Adding بِ at the beginning changes 'what' to 'because of what'. This word connects to قَدَّمَتْ (sent forth) in the ayah, explaining why something happened.
This is the basic meaning of the root
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create basic past tense verb
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on middle letter shows doing something with emphasis or repeatedly

steps.m: she sent forward (with emphasis/repeatedly) (subject of أَيْدِيكُمْ)
steps.n: Adding ت with sukun at the end changes the subject from 'he' to 'she'. In this ayah, this verb is describing what 'your hands' (أَيْدِيكُمْ) did in the past.
This root relates to hands and power
Changes from singular 'hand' to plural 'hands' by adding أ at the start - a common way to make plurals for body parts
Adds possessive pronoun suffix meaning 'your (plural)'. In this ayah, it refers to the deeds done by these hands, connecting to قَدَّمَتْ (what you have sent forth/done).
This is a basic particle that emphasizes what comes after it and connects it to what came before
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement. This connects 'that' to the next part which says 'Allah is not unjust' (لَيْسَ بِظَلَّامٍ)
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One God

steps.m: Allah (as the object being referred to)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show this is the object being referred to in the sentence
This is a special verb used for negation in Arabic. It's irregular and only appears in past tense form but gives present meaning.
This special word is used to say something is not true. Here it's saying Allah is not an oppressor to His servants. Even though it looks like a past tense word, it talks about now.
This root relates to injustice and wrongdoing
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
The doubled ل and added ا show this describes someone who does this action intensively or habitually

steps.m: extremely unjust one (in a general sense)
steps.n: The tanween (double kasra) shows this is indefinite, like saying 'an' in English

steps.m: by/with an extremely unjust one (connected to لَيْسَ)
steps.n: Added بِ to show this describes how something is done. This word connects to لَيْسَ (is not) in the ayah.

steps.m: to serve or be devoted to someone/something
steps.n: This root relates to service and worship
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past-tense verb
Changes from singular 'servant' to plural 'slaves' by adding ي in the middle
Adds 'the' to specify these particular servants
Adds 'for/to' at the beginning and changes the ending to match
Adds emphasis to the word by doubling the ل
Added ن to make it refer to multiple people
Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it mean 'those who'. This word is the subject (doer) of قَالُوٓا۟ (they said) in this ayah.
Added fathas and changed و to ا because it's between two fathas
Added وا at the end to show many people did the action
Added a long vowel mark (ٓ) and small و (۟) as pronunciation helpers that don't change the meaning
This is the basic form of the word that can mean 'if' or 'that' depending on context
Adding the shadda makes the word stronger and changes it from 'if/that' to a word that emphasizes what comes after it. Here it emphasizes that 'Allah made a covenant'

steps.m: one who is worshipped, a deity
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Changed to the unique name for The One True God by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows emphasis.

steps.m: Allah (The One True God, as the object of إِنَّ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this word follows إِنَّ (indeed/verily). Also changed the initial alif to a wasl form since it connects with the previous word.

steps.m: to promise, commit, or make a covenant

steps.n: Adding the three vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination
Added sukun on ل and changed ى to ي to prepare for the suffix
Added نا at the end to show this means 'us'
The alif is lengthened to show the end of the word in formal writing
Basic word that connects parts of a sentence, showing that one thing leads to another

steps.m: that not (connected to نُؤْمِنَ - we believe)
steps.n: Added 'not' by combining with لا. The ن and ل merge together with a shadda. This word connects to نُؤْمِنَ (we believe) to show what they said they would not do.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added an alif at the start to change from 'feeling secure' to 'having religious faith'
Changed from 'he believed' in past tense to 'we believe' in present tense
Added the 'a' ending to show this is in the subjunctive mood (used after 'that', 'to', etc.)
Changes the word from 'send' to mean 'one who is sent with a message'. The و after س and ُ marks make it mean someone who does this as their main role.

steps.m: to/for a messenger (connected to قَالُوٓا۟ - what they said)
steps.n: Adds لِ at the beginning to show this messenger is who they were talking to/about in their statement
Adds two dots under the last letter to show it's talking about any messenger (not a specific one)
Added fatha marks to connect the basic letters
Added shadda to strengthen the middle letter, making the meaning stronger

steps.m: until (connects to يَأْتِيَنَا - he brings to us)
steps.n: Added alif maqsura with superscript alif at the end. This shows it connects to what comes next in the sentence - in this case, it means 'until he brings to us'
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and 'he' as the doer. Added ْ (sukun) on أ and ِ (kasra) on ت to complete the present tense pattern.
Added نَا at the end to show who receives the action (us). This verb is part of a condition starting with حَتَّىٰ (until), explaining what must happen before they will believe.
The ان ending makes it mean 'something that is brought near' like an offering
Added بِ at start to mean 'with' and ٍ at end to mean 'an'. This offering is what the verb يَأْتِيَنَا (comes to us) brings.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense (he ate) to present tense (you eat) by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
Added the attached pronoun هُ which shows what is being eaten. In this ayah, it refers to the قُرْبَان (sacrifice) mentioned earlier.
This root relates to light and illumination
Changed و to ا and added fatha to create the noun 'fire' - something that gives light
Added damma to show this is the subject of تَأْكُلُهُ (consumes it) in the ayah
Adding ال makes the word 'fire' specific/definite
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed vowels and dropped و to make it a command. This word is a direct command from Allah to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to respond to what was said before.
This is a particle used to emphasize that something definitely happened in the past. Here it emphasizes that messengers definitely came to them.
Connected the root letters ج-ي-أ with the vowel marks َ (fatha) to make the past tense verb meaning 'he came'
Changed to hollow verb pattern by adding ا after ج to show past tense
The alif is lengthened to add emphasis to the action

steps.m: he indeed came to you all (referring to those who said إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَهِدَ إِلَيْنَآ)
steps.n: Added كم to show the action was directed at multiple people being addressed
The و makes it mean 'someone who does this action a lot'
Changed from singular to plural by changing the vowels inside the word (like many Arabic plurals do)

steps.m: messengers (as the subject of جَآءَكُمْ - came to you)
steps.n: Added tanween damma to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence
Basic form of the word meaning 'from'
The shadda appears because the letter before it (ن in الَّذِينَ) combines with the م of مِن, making it stronger (shown by the shadda)

steps.m: to come before or in front of

steps.n: Changes from verb meaning to noun meaning of 'before'
Added kasra at the end because it comes after مِن (from)
Added ى at the end to show this means 'before me'
Changed from a verb (to make clear) into an adjective (clear) by adding shadda
Added ة to make it a specific instance of evidence
Added ات to make it plural
Added ال to make it specific
Added بِ meaning 'with' and kasra at the end because it follows a preposition
The base word ذِى is a relative pronoun that refers to something mentioned before
Adding ٱلْ and doubling the ذ makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'

steps.m: with that which/with the one who
steps.n: Adding بِ at the beginning shows this is being used with something
Adding وَ connects this phrase to what came before it
This is the basic meaning showing the three root letters separated
Added damma and sukun to make it into a noun meaning 'saying'
The weak letter و drops out because of the vowel pattern
Added تُمْ at the end to show that multiple people (you all) did this action in the past
Starting with the question word ما (what), which gets shortened to مَ when combined with other particles
Added لِ (for/to) before مَ to create the meaning 'why' (literally: for what)
Added فَ (then/so) to connect this question to the previous command قُلْ (say)
Basic meaning of the root letters
Added vowel marks (fathas) to make it mean 'he killed' in the past
Adding و changes it from 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural)
Adding هُمْ shows who was killed (them)
This is a conditional particle that introduces an 'if' clause. Here it connects to the next phrase 'if you are truthful', showing that something depends on a condition being true.
This root represents the basic concept of existing or being in a state
Changed the root 'to be' into a noun by adding the vowels ُ (u) and َ (a)
Changed from the basic word for 'being' to 'I was' by adding ت at the end to show I did it
Changed from 'I' to 'you all' by replacing تُ with تُم at the end
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Changed the word pattern to show who does the action
Added the plural ending ين to show multiple people. The word describes the subject of كُنتُمْ (you were) in the ayah.
This is a combination of two particles: فَ (then/so) and إِن (if). They work together to connect conditions.

steps.m: to strongly reject as false (subject of فَقَدْ)
steps.n: Doubled the middle letter to show this is done with force or repeatedly. This word connects to فَقَدْ (then indeed) which starts the next part of the sentence.
Added وا to show a group of people (they) did this action in the past

Added كَ to show who was being denied (you). This refers to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ being denied by his people, and connects to how previous messengers were also denied as mentioned in the rest of the verse.
This is a particle that adds emphasis to what follows it
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. This connects to the verb كُذِّبَ (were rejected) to mean 'then certainly they were rejected'

steps.m: to deny something or call it false

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: was strongly denied (referring to رُسُلٌ - messengers)
steps.n: Changed the vowels and added shadda to show this happened to someone (passive voice) and happened strongly. This word connects to رُسُلٌ (messengers) who were denied.
Added vowels and و to change from 'to send' into 'someone who delivers messages'
Changed from singular to plural by changing the vowels and making the word shorter
Added double damma to show this is the subject of the verb 'were denied' in the ayah
This is a basic particle that shows where something starts from or comes from. It is not made from a root.
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a vowel sound at the end of the previous word (فَقَدْ). This makes it easier to pronounce when reading quickly.
Root letters got vowels to make it mean 'before' in time or place
Added كَ at the end to mean 'before you'. The ِ (kasra) before كَ shows this word is connected to مِّن (from) that comes before it.
Basic past tense verb pattern with fatha marks

steps.m: they came (subject of كَذَّبُوكَ - they denied you)
steps.n: Added و at the end to show multiple people did the action. This word connects to كَذَّبُوكَ (they denied you) earlier in the verse, showing the same group doing both actions.
Adding vowels to show this is a quality/state of being clear
The feminine plural ending shows multiple pieces of evidence. This word is the object (what was brought) of the verb جاءوا (they came with).
Changed the ending to show this word is being acted upon (like when you say 'the proofs' vs 'with/by the proofs')
Adds 'bi' (with/by) and 'al' (the) to the word

steps.m: to write or inscribe (especially in stone)

steps.n: Root means 'to write' - adding the two u-vowels (zubur) makes it mean 'written books/scriptures'
Added the definite article to specify these particular scriptures
Added و at the beginning to connect it with previous words
Added kasra at the end because it follows the word بِٱلْبَيِّنٰتِ (with clear proofs) in the ayah. Changed alif to hamzat wasl for connecting pronunciation.
This is the basic concept these letters represent
This pattern changes the action of writing into the thing that results from it - a book
Adding 'the' to make it specific
Adding 'and' to connect this with the previous words
The word 'book' gets a kasra (i) ending and is written in formal Quran style with special alif. It connects to 'enlightening' which comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to make it mean 'something that gives light' instead of 'he gave light'
Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it describes الكتاب (the Book) mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: to be complete or encompass something

steps.n: The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
The ُ at the end shows this word is the subject of the sentence, meaning 'every (soul)' is doing something.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the noun form. This word is the subject of ذَآئِقَةُ (taster of) in this ayah.
Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite (any soul) and is being described by what follows it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from a verb (to taste) to a noun meaning 'taster'
Added ة to match with نَفْس (soul) which is feminine in Arabic
Added ُ to show this is the subject. Also, the آ is just a spelling variation of ا.
Added vowel marks to make the noun 'death'. This word is connected to ذَآئِقَةُ (taster) to form 'taster of death'.
ٱلْ makes it specific - not just any death, but the death that everyone experiences
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain
Adding ما changes the meaning from just emphasis to both emphasis and restriction - like saying 'only' in English

steps.m: and indeed only (connects to previous statement while emphasizing and restricting what follows)
steps.n: Adding و at the beginning connects this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Changes from basic root to past tense verb by adding vowels and doubling ف (shows something done completely)
Changed to passive voice to show the action is being done to someone

steps.m: you (all) will be paid in full (your rewards أُجُورَكُمْ)
steps.n: Added plural ending to show it's happening to multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to everyone receiving their full rewards (أُجُورَكُمْ) on the Day of Judgment.
Changed from a root meaning 'payment' to plural form 'rewards' by adding vowels and و

steps.m: your (plural) rewards (what will be given to you all)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show these rewards belong to multiple people being addressed

steps.m: day (a specific period from sunrise to sunset)
steps.n: Connected the root letters to form the basic noun meaning 'day'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is in accusative case (like saying 'on a day' or 'during a day')
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the word to show the action itself rather than someone doing it. The و changes to ي because of the kasra before it
Adding ة makes this refer to a specific type of rising - the resurrection
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to the specific Day of Resurrection
The word gets a line over the ا (called alif) and an -i sound at the end because it comes after the word 'day of' in the sentence
This is a question word that became a conditional word meaning 'whoever'. It introduces the person who might be saved from the Fire (as mentioned in the next part of the ayah).

steps.m: so whoever (subject of زُحْزِحَ - was removed)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what was mentioned before. This connects to the next verb زُحْزِحَ (was removed/kept away) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels

steps.m: was pushed/moved away (from النَّارِ - the Fire)
steps.n: Changed to passive voice to show the action was done to someone/something. In this ayah, it refers to being moved away from the Fire (Hell).
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows movement or distance away from something

steps.m: away from (connects to ٱلنَّارِ - the Fire)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to connect it smoothly to the next word ٱلنَّارِ (the Fire). This shows it's connected to what comes after it.
Root letters were arranged into a simple noun pattern to make the word 'fire'
Added ٱل (the) at the beginning and doubled the ن with a shadda to show it's a specific fire

steps.m: the fire (after the word عَنِ meaning 'away from')
steps.n: Added ِ (kasra) at the end because it comes after the word عَنِ (meaning 'away from')
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice to show the action is being done to someone rather than them doing it themselves

steps.m: and he was made to enter (connected to زُحْزِحَ - was removed)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to the previous verb زُحْزِحَ (was removed), showing these actions happen together
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show complete covering
The ة makes it a noun for a place characterized by complete covering - like a garden covered with plants
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific, ultimate garden - Paradise
The fatha at the end shows this is the object being entered (after وَأُدْخِلَ meaning 'and is made to enter')
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation. قَدْ is used to emphasize what comes next in the sentence.
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement, showing that success follows being saved from fire and entered into paradise.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he succeeded (in relation to entering Paradise)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it comes between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that doesn't affect the meaning.
This is a basic particle that can mean either 'not' or 'what' depending on context. Here it means 'not' because it's negating what comes after it (the worldly life)
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this negative statement to what came before
This root represents the basic concept of living or being alive
Added و between second and third root letters to create a noun meaning 'life'
Added ة at the end to change from the general concept of 'living' to mean a specific 'life'
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Changes from root meaning 'near' to noun meaning 'world' since this life is closer/nearer than afterlife
Added ال to specify we're talking about this specific worldly life, and doubled the د letter because it comes after ال
Added ا at the end to show this word is in the accusative case (when it's the object of a verb or after certain particles)

steps.m: except (what comes after it from what came before)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that shows something is being excluded. It's like saying 'but not' or 'except for' in English.
Basic vowels (fatha) added to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he provided'
Added alif after second root letter to create a noun meaning the result or object of the action
Added damma to show this word is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: deception (the act or result of deceiving)
steps.n: Changed from a basic root meaning 'deceive' into a noun meaning 'deception' by adding the vowel marks ُ (damma) and و
Added ٱلْ to point to a specific deception rather than deception in general
Added ِ at the end to show this word belongs to متٰع (enjoyment), forming the phrase 'enjoyment of deception'

steps.m: to test or try through hardship

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form past tense verb
Changed from 'he tested' to 'you are being tested' by adding تُ at start and changing vowels to show someone else is doing the testing
Added لَ at start to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'

steps.m: you will most certainly be tested
steps.n: Added نَّ at end for extra emphasis and ُ before it to connect the sounds
Basic preposition showing location or containment. Used here to show that the test/trial will happen within their wealth.
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show it's a preposition
Added elongation mark (ٓ) over ى to show proper pronunciation
Root letters م-و-ل became مَال by changing و to ا (alif) to make the basic word for wealth
Changes from single wealth/property to plural form by adding letters to make أَمْوَال (amwaal)
Added kasra to show possession or connection
Added plural possessive pronoun to show ownership by multiple people
The root letters get vowels to make a noun meaning 'one soul/self'
Adds أ at start to make broken plural - a special Arabic way to make plurals by changing the word pattern

steps.m: your selves/souls (referring to the believers being tested)
steps.n: Adds 'your' (plural) to show possession. This word is part of what's being tested (مْوٰلِكُمْ وَأَنفُسِكُمْ - your wealth and yourselves) in this trial mentioned in the ayah.

steps.m: and your selves/souls (connecting to 'your wealth' mentioned before)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this with أَمْوٰلِكُمْ (your wealth) as the two things being tested in this trial
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed to present tense with 'you' as the subject
Added لَ at the beginning to show emphasis and future tense
Added نَّ at the end to add strong emphasis

steps.m: and surely you will definitely hear
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Basic preposition showing source, origin, or starting point
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱلَّذِينَ (those who). This is like adding a connecting sound to make the words flow better.
This is the basic form used to refer to someone or something
Adding ال makes it specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one person
Added vowel marks and changed ال to ٱ to show correct pronunciation. This word describes the people who were given the Book.
This root carries the basic meaning of something coming or being brought
Changed to passive form to show receiving rather than giving

steps.m: they were given (subject of لَتَسْمَعُنَّ - you will surely hear)
steps.n: Changed from 'was given' to 'they were given' by adding وا at the end to show multiple people. The middle letter changed from ي to و.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
This pattern is often used for things that result from an action - a book is the result of writing. In this ayah, the word كِتَاب is the object (what was given) to those who received the book.
Adds 'the' at the beginning and a mark at the end to show it's being acted upon

This is a basic particle that shows where something comes from or its source. In this ayah, it connects who you will hear from (ٱلَّذِينَ - those who) with the verb (لَتَسْمَعُنَّ - you will surely hear).
Added a fatha (َ) at the end because it comes before the word ٱلَّذِينَ which starts with ال. This helps connect the words smoothly when reading.
Root letters got vowels to make a word meaning 'before' - like saying 'earlier' or 'in front of'

steps.m: before you all (referring to those being tested in أَمْوٰلِكُمْ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being addressed. The ِ connects the parts together. This word relates back to أَمْوٰلِكُمْ (your wealth) showing the same group is being addressed.
Added fatha at the end to help connect with the next word smoothly when speaking
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added ال to make it definite. This is a special word (relative pronoun) used to describe people or things, not derived from a root.
Added ين to make it plural. This word is describing the people who were given the Book (scripture) before.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning to make it mean 'to make something a partner' instead of just 'to be a partner'
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action
Word is complete with both the causative prefix أَ and plural suffix وا
Changes from root concept into a concrete noun meaning 'harm'

steps.m: any harm (that you will hear from الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتٰبَ)
steps.n: Added tanween (ً) to show this is any kind of harm, not a specific harm. This harm refers to what will be heard from the People of the Book mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added vowels to make it mean 'it became many' in the past
Changed from a verb (something becoming many) to an adjective (describing something as many) by using the pattern with ي
Added tanween alif to make it describe how something is done. In this ayah, it describes how much harm/insult will be heard.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition - in this ayah, it connects 'if you are patient' with what comes after

steps.m: and if (connects this condition to previous statements)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statements about being tested
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Added تَ at start to show 'you' are doing it, changed vowels to show it's happening now

steps.m: you all endure patiently (connected to وَتَتَّقُوا۟ - and you all fear)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people are doing the action. This verb is connected to وَتَتَّقُوا۟ (and you all fear) in the ayah, showing two actions that go together.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added تَ at start and doubled ق to show 'you' doing the action to protect yourself
Added و at beginning to connect this action with previous one

steps.m: and you (all) protect yourselves (in response to harm mentioned in previous ayah)
steps.n: Changed to plural form to address multiple people, showing this is a command to the whole community
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like adding 'truly' or 'certainly' in English

steps.m: then indeed (connects to وَإِن تَصْبِرُوا۟ وَتَتَّقُوا۟)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement 'if you are patient and fear Allah'. It's like saying 'then' or 'so' in English to connect two ideas.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (alif khanjariya) to show distance
Added ل with kasra which helps connect to the next part
Added ك with fatha to complete the word. This makes it clearly point to something far away.
This is a particle showing something comes from somewhere or someone

steps.m: from (connecting مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ - from before you)
steps.n: Added sukun at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it. In this ayah, it connects to قَبْلِكُمْ (before you) to mean 'from before you'.

steps.m: determination, firm resolve (possessed by الأُمُورِ - matters)
steps.n: Changes from action to the noun meaning the quality itself. This word is owned by الأُمُورِ (matters) in this ayah.
Adds kasra at the end to show this word belongs to الأُمُورِ (matters)
Root meaning 'command' changed to a noun meaning 'one matter/affair'
Changed from singular to plural by adding a 'u' sound in the middle (و)

steps.m: of the matters (in عَزْمِ ٱلْأُمُورِ - determination of matters)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning and kasra at the end to show this word belongs to عَزْمِ (determination)
This is a time particle that introduces a past event - specifically when Allah took a covenant from the People of the Book. It helps set up the historical context for what follows.

steps.m: and when (introducing Allah taking a covenant)
steps.n: The و connects this event to previous events, showing it as part of a sequence of important moments being recalled
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is connected to مِيثٰقَ (covenant) which is what was taken.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added ال to change 'a god' to 'The God', doubled the ل to make it special for Allah's name, and added ُ to show Allah is doing the action

steps.m: to bind firmly or make secure

steps.n: Added مِ at start to show it's a tool or place, and added vowels to connect letters. The و changes to ي after kasra.
Added ا after ث to make it mean a specific type of agreement or covenant
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being taken in the phrase 'Allah took a covenant'
This is the basic form of the word that means 'who'
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding ال at the beginning makes it more specific, like saying 'those who' instead of just 'who'
Final spelling adjustments: 1) The ا becomes ٱ at the start of words, 2) Adding shadda on ل, and 3) Adding fatha at the end to show this is the subject of the verb أُوتُوا۟ (were given)
Changed to 'was given' (passive form) - when someone receives something instead of gives it. The middle letter changed from ي to و
Added وا to show multiple people received the action. This word is connected to لَتُبَيِّنُنَّهُۥ (they will surely make it clear) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The verb 'he wrote' changes to mean 'what was written' (a book). Like how drawing makes a drawing, writing makes a writing (book). This word كِتَاب is what was given in the sentence.

steps.m: the book (that was given to them)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to a specific book

steps.m: the book (object of أُوتُوا - were given)
steps.n: The word changes to show it is receiving the action (it is what was given to them)

steps.m: to make very clear, to explain thoroughly
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more active
Adding ت at the start shows 'you' are doing the action now
The لَ adds emphasis and changes the verb to future tense

steps.m: you will most certainly make clear
steps.n: The نَّ adds extra emphasis, making the statement very strong and definite

steps.m: you will surely make it clear
steps.n: The هُ suffix adds the direct object 'it/him' to the verb

steps.m: to socialize, be friendly with others, or feel at ease with people

steps.n: Root أ-ن-س meaning 'to socialize' becomes 'people' - like changing 'socialize' to 'socializers'
Adds ال to specify we're talking about people in general, not just any people. The ن gets a shadda because it assimilates with the ل
Adding لِ shows this word is receiving the action of 'making clear' (لَتُبَيِّنُنَّهُۥ) - it tells us WHO they should make it clear TO
The kasra at the end shows this word follows the preposition لِ. This is like how in English 'to' requires a specific form of the word after it
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تَكْتُمُونَهُۥ (you conceal it) into 'you do not conceal it'
Adding وَ connects this negative particle to what came before, joining two instructions together: to make clear (لَتُبَيِّنُنَّهُۥ) and not to conceal (وَلَا تَكْتُمُونَهُۥ)
Added basic past tense form by connecting root letters with fatha marks
Adds masculine plural subject marker to indicate multiple doers
Adding هُۥ shows what is being hidden (referring to the truth/scripture mentioned in the ayah)
Connected the root letters with fathas to create the past tense verb meaning 'he threw away'

steps.m: they threw away (referring to those who were given the Book)
steps.n: Adding وا changes 'he' to 'they', showing that a group of people did the action. In this ayah, it refers to those who were given the Book (Scripture).

steps.m: they threw it away (referring to the covenant/مِيثٰقَ mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Adding هُ shows what was thrown away - in this case referring back to the covenant (مِيثٰقَ) that was mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: so they threw it away (showing this happened after they were given the covenant)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this action happened as a result of what came before - after they were given the covenant, they then threw it away
Adds alif to show location/position
The word gets a fatha mark at the end because it shows where something was thrown. The long alif is just a writing style.
Changed the root meaning into a concrete noun for the visible, outer part of something - in this case the back of the body

steps.m: backs (plural, genitive because it follows وَرَآءَ)
steps.n: Makes the word plural to show multiple backs. The word is in genitive case because it comes after وَرَآءَ (behind) in the ayah.

steps.m: their backs (referring to those who were given the Book)
steps.n: Adds 'their' to show these are the backs belonging to those who were given the Book (mentioned earlier in the ayah)
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
This form shows doing something for oneself
Adds the plural masculine past tense suffix to indicate 'they'
Adds conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'
Added kasra to show this is a preposition that connects to what comes after it

steps.m: with it (referring to الْكِتٰبَ - the Book)
steps.n: Added هِ to show what is being referred to (the Book mentioned earlier in the verse)
Added small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation, but doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to set a price, to value something

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added fathas to make the basic noun form meaning 'price'

steps.m: a price (object of اشتروا - they exchanged)
steps.n: Adding ًا (tanween fatha) shows this is any price, not a specific one. This word is what's being exchanged in the phrase 'they exchanged it for a price'
Added ي between the second and third root letters to show this describes something that has the quality of being small in amount
Added tanween alif to show this is the object (what was bought) in the phrase 'they purchased it for a small price'
Changes to a word used to criticize or say something is bad

steps.m: and how bad! (referring to what they purchased مَا يَشْتَرُونَ)
steps.n: Added فَ to connect this judgment with the previous statement about what they did. It shows this is the result or consequence of their actions.
This is a question word that asks about things (not people). In this ayah, it's asking about what they are purchasing.
Basic vowels (fatha) added between root letters to make past tense verb meaning 'he traded'
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show doing action for oneself
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing final vowel
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb يَفْرَحُونَ (they rejoice) into 'they do not rejoice'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action, and changed vowels for present tense

steps.m: you must really think (emphatic negative command)
steps.n: Added نَّ at the end to make the command emphatic. This word comes after لا to make a strong negative command - 'Do not think!'
Starting with the basic demonstrative word and changing it to make it relative
Added ين to make it plural
Added ال to make it definite. This word is the subject of يَفْرَحُونَ (they rejoice) in this ayah.
Added proper diacritical marks for Quranic spelling
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, with ُ at end to show 'he' is doing it

steps.m: they become happy (referring to الَّذِينَ - those who)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people (those who) are doing the action
This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence, like 'what' or 'that which' in English
Added the preposition بِ at the beginning to show this is done 'with' something
The alif is written long (آ) at the end of words for clarity in reading, but doesn't change the meaning
Added vowel marks to make the basic past tense verb meaning 'he came'
Added و to change from 'he came' to 'they came'
Added ا after و to complete the 'they' ending
Added ۟ mark to show this is a plural 'they' ending
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Combined the double letters with a shadda
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action
Word is complete with prefix وَ, present tense marker يُ, and plural suffix ونَ
This is a connecting word that joins two parts of the sentence. Here it connects to the verb يُحْمَدُوا۟ (they be praised) to show what the people love - they love to be praised.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added يُ at start and changed middle vowel to make it passive - changes from 'he praised' to 'he is praised'

steps.m: they are praised (in this ayah, referring to those who love to be praised for what they have not done)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people are being praised. This refers back to الَّذِينَ (those who) from earlier in the verse.
This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb أَتَوا۟ (they brought/did), showing what they are happy about

steps.m: with what/with that which (connects to يَفْرَحُونَ)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this is the thing they are happy with/about. This connects to the verb يَفْرَحُونَ (they rejoice)
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it (يَفْعَلُوا۟) to show it didn't happen in the past. It's like adding 'did not' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the past tense form
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show present/future tense
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
This is the basic negation word that means 'not' in Arabic. It's used to negate what comes after it.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or sequence. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement and negates the verb تَحْسَبَنَّهُم (think of them).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning and changed vowels to make it present tense for 'you'
Added نَّ for strong emphasis

steps.m: you must definitely think them (referring to those who rejoice)
steps.n: Added هُم to show who is being thought about - refers back to those mentioned earlier in the verse who rejoice
Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'success'
Added مَ at the start to show 'place of', added ا after و to make it a place noun, and ة at end to make it specific
Added بِ at start to show location or means

steps.m: in/at a place of success (being acted upon)
steps.n: Added ٍ (tanween kasra) to show this is an indefinite noun
Basic form of the preposition that shows something comes from or is part of something else
The shadda (ّ) doubles the م sound to add emphasis, often used when connecting to the next word
The fatḥa (َ) at the end shows this connects to the next word العَذَابِ (the punishment), making it mean 'from the punishment'
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Adding ا after second root letter turns the action into a noun meaning the result of that action
Added ال to show we're talking about a specific punishment
The ِ shows this word comes after مِنَ (from/of)
Basic conjunction that connects this phrase to what came before
Adds the preposition ل to show something is being given or directed to someone
Adds the pronoun هم to show who is being referred to (them)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Long ا after second root letter makes this a noun meaning the result of punishment. The word is in genitive case because it comes after مِنَ (from) and is possessed by مَفَازَةٍ (escape/safety).
Adding double dammah makes it 'a punishment' instead of 'the punishment'
Changed from basic root meaning 'pain' to adjective meaning 'painful' by adding vowel marks
Added ي to intensify the meaning of pain
Added double dammah to make it indefinite
This is the base word referring to God in Arabic. It's a unique word that doesn't come from a typical root.
Adding ل at the beginning shows something belongs to or is for Allah
The ل is doubled with a shadda because when two identical letters meet, they combine with a shadda
The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it in the sentence
Adding و at the beginning connects this phrase to what came before it. In this ayah, it connects to the word مُلْكُ (dominion/kingdom) that follows.
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the area or thing being ruled/controlled
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject that belongs to Allah, connecting it to لِلَّهِ (to/for Allah) in the ayah
Added vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun for the place that is high up
Changed sky/heaven to its plural form by adding وات at the end
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س
Added kasra at the end to show it's possessed by مُلْكُ (dominion/kingdom) in the phrase مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ (dominion of the heavens)
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters
Adding ال makes it specific, like 'the' in English
Adding وَ means 'and', and the ِ ending shows possession
This is the base word for Allah, the proper name of God in Islam. It's a unique word that doesn't follow normal root patterns.
The alif at the beginning changes to a special form called alif-wasla (ٱ) because it connects with what comes before it in pronunciation.
The ُ (damma) at the end shows that Allah is the subject doing the action in this sentence. Here, Allah is described as being قَدِيرٌ (capable/powerful) over all things.

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of قَدِيرٌ)
steps.n: The وَ at the beginning means 'and', connecting this statement to what came before it.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something is on top of, over, or upon something else
Added the alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show this is the standard written form. In this ayah, it shows Allah's power being 'upon' all things.
Added ُ (damma) on first letter and doubled the ل with ّ (shadda) to show it means absolutely everything
Added ِ (kasra) at the end to show this word is connected to the word after it (شَىْءٍ meaning 'thing')

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: The root letters ش-ي-أ get vowel marks (َ ْ) to make the common noun 'thing'
The hamza at the end changes to ء when it comes after a sukun
The ي changes to ى when it has a sukun
Added kasratin to show this word belongs to كُلِّ (every/all) that comes before it

steps.m: to have power or ability over something

steps.n: Root letters connected with vowels to make an active participle meaning 'one who has power'
Changed to قَدِير pattern to show complete/perfect ability - a form often used for Allah's attributes
Added two dots (tanween) to show this word describes Allah and ends the sentence
This is an indeclinable particle used at the start of sentences to indicate emphasis. It belongs to the family of إنّ وأخواتها
Adding the kasra (إِ) and shadda (نَّ) makes the word stronger. This word introduces an important statement in this ayah about the signs in creation.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under ف to show how to pronounce it correctly. This word connects to خَلْقِ (creation) to tell us we're looking at what's 'in' the creation of things.
Changed the final ي to ى which is just a writing style variation - it means the same thing

steps.m: to create, form, or shape something
creation (the act or result of creating)
steps.n: Adding the vowel marks turns the basic meaning into a noun that means either the act of creating or the thing that was created
The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it (the heavens and earth). It's like saying 'creation of' instead of just 'creation'

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Added vowels (fatha) to make it mean 'it rose up' in the past tense
Added hamza to form the noun meaning 'sky'
Added feminine plural ending to show multiple heavens
Added the definite article ٱل and doubled the س (shown by the shadda)
Added kasra at the end because this word is possessed by 'creation' (خَلْقِ) in the phrase 'creation of the heavens'
Added vowels to the root letters to make the word for 'earth'
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific - like saying THE earth instead of just any earth. This word comes after فِى (in) in the ayah.
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in)
Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect it with السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens) mentioned before it

steps.m: to follow in succession or differ

steps.n: Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Added ٱ and ت to show two or more things differing from each other (not just one thing being different)
Changed from a verb (to differ) into a noun (difference) by adding ا and changing vowels
Added kasra to show this word follows فِى (in) from earlier in the verse
Added و to connect this to the previous phrase about creation of heavens and earth
This turns the concept of darkness into the noun 'night'
Added the definite article ال to specify 'the night' rather than just any night
Changed the initial alif to a special alif that helps pronunciation
The ل merged with ل and got a kasra ending because it's owned by something
Root letters connected with vowels to make the noun 'river/stream'
Added alif to make it a noun meaning the time when light flows
Added shadda to strengthen the ن in preparation for adding ال
Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it belongs to something (the alternation/succession mentioned before it)
Added و to connect with previous word in the phrase 'alternation of the night and the day'

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be apparent

steps.n: Adding ة changes this into a feminine noun meaning 'sign'

steps.m: signs (multiple things that show or prove something)
steps.n: Changes from one sign/miracle to many signs/miracles by adding -aat ending
The two dots under the last letter (tanween) show these are some general signs, not specific ones

steps.m: surely signs (connected to لِّأُو۟لِى - for those of)
steps.n: The لَ adds emphasis to show these are definitely signs. This word connects to لِّأُو۟لِى which comes after it, showing these signs are for a specific group.
This is a special word used to indicate possession or ownership. It's often followed by what is possessed.
Added لِ at the beginning to show purpose or direction - 'for' or 'to'

steps.m: for those who possess/have (connected to ءَايٰتٍ)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل and small high ۟ mark for pronunciation. This word connects to ءَايٰتٍ (signs) to show 'signs for those who possess...'
The root letters are compressed with a shadda and given a damma to make a noun meaning 'the thing that understands'
This is a broken plural form that shows multiple minds/intellects. The pattern أَفْعَال is common for making plurals.

steps.m: the minds/intellects (referring to those who understand)
steps.n: The ال makes it refer to specific minds/intellects - in this context, those who can understand the signs mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: of the minds/intellects (possessed by those who understand Allah's signs)
steps.n: The kasra shows this word follows لِّأُو۟لِى (for those of) in the ayah, forming the phrase 'for those of understanding'
Added ٱلْ at the beginning which makes it specific. The ل gets a shadda (doubled) after ٱلْ.

steps.m: those who (subject of يَذْكُرُونَ - they remember)
