steps.n: Added نَ at the end to make it plural, showing we're talking about multiple people. This word is the subject of يَذْكُرُونَ (they remember) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they remember/mention (subject of الَّذِينَ - those who)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action. This verb describes what 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) are doing in the ayah.

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun for 'one who is worshipped'
Added a fatha mark to show this word is in the accusative case
The middle letter و changes to ي and we add ا to show the name of the action (standing)

steps.m: in a standing position (describing how يَذْكُرُونَ/they remember is done)
steps.n: Changed to show HOW they remember Allah - while standing. The special mark (ٰ) shows a long 'aa' sound.

steps.m: to sit, to remain in a place

steps.n: This form shows the action of sitting as a noun
The ًا at the end shows this word is describing how Allah is being remembered (in relation to قِيٰمًا and عَلَىٰ جُنُوبِهِمْ which describe other positions)

steps.m: and in a sitting position (describing how they remember Allah)
steps.n: Adding و at the start means 'and'
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. على is a preposition showing something is on top of or over something else.
Added alif maksura (ى with small alif above) at the end to show this is the standard written form
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English. This connects to جُنُوبِهِمْ (their sides) in the ayah.
Changes the word to mean multiple sides by adding damma vowels and و, following a common Arabic pattern for making plurals
Adds kasra at the end because it comes after the word عَلَىٰ (upon/on)
Adds possessive pronoun suffix meaning 'their'
Adding ت at start and doubling middle letter shows someone is thinking deeply and reflecting on their own
Adding ي at start and ُ at end shows one male person is doing this action in the present/future
Adding ون at the end shows many people (all men or mixed group) are doing the action together

steps.m: and they reflect deeply (about خَلْقِ ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ - the creation of the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Adding و at start connects this to the previous actions (remembering Allah standing, sitting and lying down). The deep reflection is specifically about Allah's creation.
This is a preposition that connects the verb 'they reflect' (يَتَفَكَّرُونَ) to what they reflect upon - 'the creation' (خَلْقِ). It shows that their reflection happens within or about this topic.
Changed from a verb (to create) into a noun (creation) by adding fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter

steps.m: creation (of السَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ - the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it comes after فِى (in) in the ayah
Changed from root letters to past tense verb meaning 'it rose up' by adding vowels
Changed from 'rising up' (verb) to mean the place that rises up high (noun)
Makes it plural to show multiple heavens
Shows this word is connected to something after it
Makes it specific by adding 'the', and doubles the س because it's a sun letter
Connected the root letters together with basic vowel marks
Added ال to make it specific ('the earth') and ِ to show it follows فِى (in) from the ayah
وَ means 'and', ٱلْ makes it specific ('the earth'), and ِ shows it's affected by فِى

steps.m: to care for and help grow
one who cares for and helps grow (Lord)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does a lot of caring and helping
Added نَا at the end to show this is our Lord. This word is being used as a way to call out to Allah in this ayah.

steps.m: that which (object of خَلَقْتَ - You created)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that links to what comes after it. Here it refers to the things that Allah created, connecting to the verb خَلَقْتَ (You created).
Added fatha after each letter to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: you created (addressing Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Adding تَ at the end changes 'he created' to 'you created'. In this ayah, the believers are directly addressing Allah.
Basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif above ه (called an overbar) extends the sound slightly
The ا at the end shows this points to one masculine thing

steps.m: to be useless, false, or in vain

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to be false' to noun meaning 'that which is false'

steps.m: uselessly, falsely, in vain (describing how something was done)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes how something was done

steps.m: uselessly, falsely, in vain (describing how something was done)
steps.n: No change needed since word already has the correct form

steps.m: to glorify, declare perfection, exalt above any flaw

steps.n: The ان ending makes it more emphatic, showing complete/perfect glorification

steps.m: glory be to You! (addressing Allah in رَبَّنَا)
steps.n: Adding كَ makes this a direct address to Allah, connected to رَبَّنَا (Our Lord) in this ayah. This is a common expression of praise and amazement at Allah's perfect creation.
The root letter و is dropped in the command form. Added ِ (kasra) to show it's a command.
Added نَا at the end to show who is being protected (us)
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this request to the previous statement about Allah's creation not being purposeless
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun meaning 'punishment'. This word is what we're asking to be protected from when we say قِنَا (protect us).
The fatha at the end shows this is the object - it's what we're asking protection from when we say 'protect us'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'fire' - something that gives light
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to عَذَابَ (punishment) in the phrase 'punishment of fire'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ن with shadda
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: one who completely cares for others
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes it more intense - showing complete lordship
Adding 'نا' at the end means 'our', changing 'Lord' to 'our Lord'. The word is being used to call out to Allah.
The extra alif at the end shows this is being called out with strong feeling
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English

steps.m: indeed you (being addressed by رَبَّنَآ - our Lord)
steps.n: Adding كَ shows this is directed at 'you' (when talking to one male). In this ayah, the believers are addressing Allah directly with 'you'
This is a special word that refers to any person or people in general. Here it refers to whoever Allah admits into the Fire.
Changed vowels to make the causative form, meaning to make someone/something enter
Adding تُ makes it mean 'you' or 'she' does the action

steps.m: you cause to enter (connected to مَن who/whoever)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this verb is connected to the word مَن (whoever) in the ayah
The root 'light/fire' becomes 'intense fire' by doubling the first letter with shadda
Adding the definite article ال makes the noun definite
This is a particle that adds emphasis to show something definitely happened
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before. Here it connects to the next word أَخْزَيْتَهُۥ (You have disgraced him)
Adding أ at the start makes it mean causing the action to happen to someone else
Adding تَ shows you (one person) did this in the past
Adding هُ shows who received the action (meaning 'him'). This word is connected to إِنَّكَ (indeed you) in the ayah, showing who did the disgracing.
This is a basic negative particle that makes things negative, like adding 'not' in English

steps.m: and not (for لِلظَّالِمِينَ - for the wrongdoers)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. Here it connects to tell us 'and there are not for the wrongdoers any helpers'

steps.m: to act unfairly or harm others

steps.n: Basic past tense form meaning 'he' did the action of being unfair
Changes from describing the action to describing the person who does it
Makes the word refer to many people instead of just one
Makes it specific which people we're talking about

steps.m: for the people who act unfairly
steps.n: Added 'li' at start to mean 'for/to' the wrongdoers

steps.m: from/of (shows there are no helpers for الظَّالِمِينَ)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to. In this ayah, it's used with أَنصَارٍ (helpers) to show that the wrongdoers (الظَّالِمِينَ) have no helpers.
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Changes the verb into a noun meaning 'someone who helps'
Changes the word to show many helpers instead of just one
Adds the tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this word comes after مِنْ (from/of) in the ayah
Adding fatha marks creates the basic past tense verb meaning 'he did something'
The root رَبَبَ becomes رَبّ (lord/master), then adding نا means 'our'. When calling out to someone we say 'O' before it, making it mean 'O our Lord!'
The extra alif (آ) at the end shows we are calling out to someone, like saying 'O!'
The alif at the end is elongated to show a prolonged call, like when you call out to someone from far away
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain. In this ayah, it emphasizes the statement 'we heard'.

steps.m: indeed we (subject of سَمِعْنَا - we heard)
steps.n: Added نا which means 'we/us'. This connects to the verb سَمِعْنَا (we heard) that comes right after it in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense verb

steps.m: we heard (referring to the believers speaking to their Lord)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. In this ayah, this refers to the believers speaking to Allah about hearing the caller to faith.
Basic past tense verb ending in ى
Added مُ at start and changed middle vowel to show this is someone doing the action

steps.m: one who calls out (object of سَمِعْنَا - we heard)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is the object being heard in the phrase 'we heard a caller'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to show interaction with others

steps.m: he is being called out to
steps.n: Changed from 'he called' to 'he is being called' by adding يُ at start and changing vowels - shows someone else is doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: belief/faith (the state of feeling secure in truth)
steps.n: Changed pattern to make an abstract noun showing the state of believing
Added the definite article to specify this particular faith

steps.m: to/for the faith (called to by مُنَادِيًا)
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose/direction and kasra at end to show connection. This word shows what the caller (مُنَادِيًا) was calling people to.

This is a particle that connects what was heard (سَمِعْنَا) to what was said (ءَامِنُوا۟). It's like quotation marks in English showing the exact words that were heard: 'we heard a caller calling to faith saying "Believe in your Lord"'
The root 'trust' becomes 'believe' by adding a hamza and long alif at the start. Like going from feeling safe to putting your trust in something.
Changed from 'he believed' to 'you believe!' by changing the vowels - like telling someone to do the action
Added plural marker to make the command address multiple people

steps.m: lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and cares completely
Added kasra to show this word connects to something after it
Added كُمْ to show this belongs to multiple people being spoken to
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is what you believe in. This connects to the command ءَامِنُوا۟ (believe!) in the ayah.

steps.m: to trust or feel secure in something

steps.n: Root 'secure/trust' changed to show 'making oneself believe/trust' - like going from feeling safe to choosing to believe
Added فَ to show this belief happened as a result of hearing the call mentioned before
The doubled letter at the end makes the belief stronger and more emphatic

steps.m: and so we definitely believed (in response to hearing the caller)
steps.n: Added نا to show this is 'we' speaking, referring to the believers responding to the call to faith
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: one who completely cares for others
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows intensity, indicating complete mastery and lordship
This word is being used to call out to Allah at the start of a prayer, like saying 'O our Lord!'
Changes verb into a command to forgive

steps.m: so forgive! (command in response to رَبَّنَا)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this is a response to calling upon Allah (رَبَّنَا)

steps.m: so forgive! (command in response to رَبَّنَا)
steps.n: Changed regular alif to wasl alif for proper pronunciation when connecting words
Combined two parts: ل meaning 'for/to' and نا meaning 'us'. This word shows who is receiving the action of فَٱغْفِرْ (forgive).
Connected the root letters together to form the basic noun for a single sin
Changes from single sin (ذَنْب) to multiple sins (ذُنُوب) by adding 'u' sounds inside the word
Adding first person plural possessive pronoun suffix to indicate possession. This word is the direct object (what is being forgiven) of the verb اغفر (forgive).
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Doubling the middle letter makes the action stronger and more complete
Removing the last vowel and adding sukun makes it a command

steps.m: and remove! (command to Allah in prayer)
steps.n: This is part of a prayer asking Allah to remove sins, connected to the previous request فَٱغْفِرْ (so forgive)
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'away from'

steps.m: from us (object of فَٱغْفِرْ - so forgive)
steps.n: Added نا (meaning 'us') at the end. The ن from عَنْ combines with the ن from نا to make a shadda (ّ). This word connects to فَٱغْفِرْ (so forgive) to show what to forgive from.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (doing harm) to an adjective (harmful) with doubled middle letter making it stronger
Adding ة changes it from abstract adjective to concrete noun meaning 'bad deed'
Changes it to plural form to show multiple harmful deeds
Adds نا to show these harmful deeds belong to us or were done by us

steps.m: to take in full, to complete

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning more complete and thorough
Adding ت changes it to mean doing something to oneself or for oneself, like receiving or taking something
Adding نا makes this about 'us' - in this context referring to taking souls in death
Adds و meaning 'and' to connect this request with the previous ones in the prayer
Basic form of the preposition that shows accompaniment
Added fatha marks to show correct pronunciation. This word connects to 'the righteous ones' (ٱلْأَبْرَارِ) that comes after it, showing who we want to be with.
Added shadda to show emphasis and combined root letters with fatha on first letter
Forms plural pattern أَفْعَال to show multiple righteous people
Adding ال makes it refer to specific righteous people
Adding kasra at the end shows this word follows مَعَ (with) in the ayah

steps.m: to take care of, raise, nurture to completion

steps.n: The doubled ب (shown by ّ) makes this word mean 'the one who does the complete nurturing', which is why it means 'Lord'

steps.m: Our Lord (the one we're calling out to)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show we're talking about our Lord. In this ayah, it's being used to call out to Allah directly.
Changed to Form IV (causative) to mean 'give' rather than just 'come'
Changed to command form
Added 'us' as the object of the command

steps.m: and give us! (command to Allah in رَبَّنَا)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect this request with the previous one to Allah
This is a relative pronoun connecting 'give us' (وَءَاتِنَا) with 'you promised' (وَعَدتَّنَا). It works like saying 'Give us what you promised' in English.

steps.m: to promise or make a commitment

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at the end to change from 'he promised' to 'you promised'
Added نَا at the end to show who was promised (us)
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or upon something else. In this ayah, it connects to رُسُلِكَ (your messengers) to show what was promised through them.
Added و and vowels to create a noun meaning someone who carries messages. This is like changing 'send' to 'sender' in English, but with a more special meaning.
Changed the vowels to make it mean more than one messenger. The و is removed because we don't need it for multiple messengers.
Added كَ at the end to show these are your messengers. The ِ before كَ shows this comes after the word 'upon' (عَلَىٰ) in the sentence.
This is a negative particle that prevents something from happening. Here it's used with تُخْزِنَا to say 'do not disgrace us'

steps.m: and do not (negates تُخْزِنَا - disgrace us)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this phrase to what came before. The full meaning is 'and do not disgrace us'
Root letters got vowels to make past tense verb meaning 'he was disgraced'
Changed from 'he was disgraced' to 'disgrace!' as a command, making it shorter by removing letters
Added تُ to make it 'you' form in present tense
Added نَا to show we are receiving the action
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is in the accusative case
Changed the و to ي and added ا to make it a noun meaning the action itself
Added ة to make it refer to one specific instance - in this case, the Day of Resurrection
Added ٱلْ to show we're talking about THE Day of Resurrection
Added ِ to show this word belongs to يَوْمَ (day), forming the phrase 'Day of Resurrection'
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English
Added كَ at the end to show this is talking about 'you'. This word connects to لَا تُخْلِفُ (you do not fail) in the next part of the ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تُخْزِنَا (disgrace us) into 'do not disgrace us'. It's used here in a prayer or request form.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense and second person (you) by adding تُ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end

steps.m: to promise or set a time/place to meet

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'promise' by adding vowels
Added مِ at the start to make it mean 'place/time of' and changed the word structure to show when/where the promise happens
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: the appointed time/place of promise (object of تُخْلِفُ - you break)
steps.n: Added fatha to show this is the object of the verb تُخْلِفُ (you break/fail to fulfill)
Changed from basic root letters to past tense verb meaning 'he answered' by adding vowels
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or responding to a request

steps.m: and so he responded (to لَهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ - to them, their Lord)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this is a result or consequence of something before it. The word is followed by لَهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ meaning 'to them, their Lord' showing who responded.
Added fatha to show the basic preposition meaning

steps.m: for them/to them (object of فَٱسْتَجَابَ - responded)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who the preposition is directed to. This word shows who Allah responded to in فَٱسْتَجَابَ (He responded).
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and sustains in a complete and perfect way
The damma ending shows this word is the subject doing the action of responding (ٱسْتَجَابَ) in this verse
Adding هُمْ shows this Lord belongs to or is associated with them. This word is the subject doing the action of responding (ٱسْتَجَابَ) to them in this verse
This is the basic emphatic particle that confirms what comes after it. The shadda (ّ) on ن shows extra emphasis.
The ى at the end shows this is first person (I/me). The kasra (ِ) before ى is added for pronunciation. In this ayah, this word introduces what Allah is saying in response to the believers' prayers.
Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added madd (lengthening mark) over alif to show extended pronunciation. This word negates the verb أُضِيعُ (I will waste/lose) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: I waste/lose (negated by لا before it)
steps.n: Changed to first person present tense. The word is negated by لا before it, so it means 'I do not waste/lose'
Adding vowels makes it a past tense verb
Changes from verb (to work) to noun (worker)

steps.m: any worker/doer (being acted upon by أُضِيعُ)
steps.n: Adds tanween kasra to show this means 'any' worker and that it's being affected by another word
Added shadda (doubling mark) on م for pronunciation because it comes after the word أَنِّى. This is a sound rule called assimilation where similar sounds blend together.

steps.m: from you all (referring to the workers/doers mentioned in عَمَلَ عٰمِلٍ)
steps.n: Added the pronoun suffix كُم which means 'you all'. This connects to the previous mention of workers/doers (عَمَلَ عٰمِلٍ) in the ayah.
Basic preposition indicating source, origin, or belonging
Added shadda to help connect smoothly with the next word كُم (you all). This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
Adding vowel marks creates the basic past tense verb form
Changed from a verb (remembered) to a noun (male) by changing the vowels, then added tanwin kasra to match عٰمِلٍ (worker) that it describes
This is a connecting word that gives a choice between male (ذَكَرٍ) or female (أُنثَىٰ). It shows that Allah doesn't waste the work of any believer, whether male or female.

steps.m: female (contrasted with ذَكَرٍ meaning male in this ayah)
steps.n: Changes the root into a noun meaning female. This word is being contrasted with ذَكَرٍ (male) in the ayah to show that both males and females are equal in their deeds.
Added vowels to make a noun meaning 'some' or 'part'. This word works with another بَعْض later in the ayah to show how people are connected to each other.
Added كُم at the end to show this refers to multiple people being addressed

steps.m: from/of (showing origin or part of something)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or that it's part of something bigger. Like saying 'some of the cookies' or 'she came from home'.

steps.m: from/of (showing origin or part of something)
steps.n: Added a shadda (ّ) on the م to show it connects strongly with the word before it in pronunciation.

steps.m: from/of (showing origin or part of something)

Root letters got vowel marks (fatHa and sukoon) to make it mean 'some' or 'part'
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this word is being described by the word مِّنۢ (from) in the phrase 'some of you are from others'
Added ن at the end to make it refer to multiple people instead of just one person
Added ال at the beginning to make it point to specific people being talked about

steps.m: so those who (connecting to what was said before)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement. This shows these people are being mentioned as a result of or in connection to what was just said.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Adding alif changes it from just 'leaving' to specifically 'migrating'

steps.m: they migrated (referring to the believers who left their homes for Allah's cause)

This is the basic past tense form of the verb
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding hamza at beginning makes it mean 'cause someone to do the action'
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it
Added وا at the end to show many people were affected

steps.m: and they were made to exit

steps.m: from (shows where something starts or comes from)
steps.n: This is a basic word that shows where something starts or comes from. It's like saying 'from' in English, as in 'from home' or 'made from wood'.

steps.m: from (shows where something starts or comes from)
steps.n: Added the vowel mark ِ (kasra) under the م to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'min'

Changing the vowel mark to kasra (ِ) creates a variation of the word

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic word
Changed to passive form to show receiving harm rather than causing it
Added plural ending to show multiple people were harmed
Added و at beginning to connect with previous phrase about those who emigrated
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it connects to سَبِيلِى (my path/way) to show who was in Allah's path/way.

steps.m: to make a path or way
steps.n: These letters together represent the basic idea of making or following a path
Adding ي and vowels changes it from a verb into a noun meaning 'path'
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

Added و with fatha at start to connect this action with previous actions in the verse
وا at the end shows multiple men did this in the past
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it
Added وا to show multiple people were affected

Adding أُ at start makes this 'I will' do the action
Added لَ at start to show emphasis and certainty

steps.n: Added نَّ at end for extra emphasis and certainty

steps.m: away from (preposition showing separation from سَيِّـَٔات evil deeds)

steps.m: away from them (referring to removing their سَيِّـَٔات evil deeds)

Adding ة transforms abstract quality into specific instance/deed
Changes the word from singular 'bad deed' to plural 'bad deeds'
Adding -i ending to show these bad deeds belong to someone
Adds هِم to show these bad deeds belong to them

steps.m: to cover or hide (like a garden covers the ground)

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger

steps.n: Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show this is the object being entered into
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Added ت at start to make it present tense
Basic particle showing where something starts or comes from

steps.m: from (showing where they were driven out from in دِيٰرِهِمْ)
steps.n: Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it. This word connects to دِيٰرِهِمْ (their homes) to show they were driven out from their homes.

Added kasra at the end because it follows the preposition مِن
Added ها to show what is beneath (referring to gardens/جَنَّات mentioned earlier)

The suffix وا changes the subject from 'he' to 'they (masculine plural)'. These are the people whose movement/activity should not deceive the Prophet.

steps.n: Basic preposition showing location or place. In this ayah, it connects 'movement' (تَقَلُّبُ) with 'the lands' (ٱلْبِلٰدِ) to show where the movement happens.

steps.m: in (location word connecting تَقَلُّبُ with ٱلْبِلٰدِ)

steps.n: Basic meaning of something that points to or indicates something else

Adds kasra at the end to show connection to the next word (Allah)

Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced
Added هُ (him) to show who the preposition is directed to
Added م with sukun to change 'him' to 'them'

Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: to be present, to be near
steps.n: This word is used to show location or possession, like saying something is 'at' or 'with' someone
Adding kasra and sukun creates a noun meaning the place or state of being present

steps.m: with, at, near (of رَبِّهِمْ - their Lord)
steps.n: Adding fatha at the end makes this word work as a preposition showing location or possession. In this ayah, it's used with رَبِّهِمْ (their Lord) to show something is 'with' or 'near' their Lord.
Basic meaning of having authority or lordship over something
Doubling the middle letter shows this is someone who completely embodies the qualities of nurturing and sustaining
The kasra shows this word is connected to something coming after it

steps.m: their Lord (the Lord of the People of the Book)
steps.n: Adding هِمْ shows who the Lord belongs to - in this case referring back to the People of the Book mentioned earlier in the verse
This is a special word that adds emphasis and certainty to what follows. Here it emphasizes that Allah (اللَّهَ) is swift in reckoning.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding definite article and merging letters

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يُؤْمِنُ - believes in)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb يُؤْمِنُ (believes in). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form.
Basic meaning of being fast or rapid
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb

Added ي between second and third root letters and changed vowels to make an adjective. The ُ at the end shows this word owns or describes what comes after it (الْحِسَابِ - the reckoning), forming the phrase 'swift of reckoning'.
Basic meaning of counting or keeping track of numbers
Changes from a verb (to count) into a noun (calculation) by adding a long ا
Adds 'the' to make it specific and shows it belongs to Allah

steps.m: to point to or indicate something

steps.n: The root letters combine with a shadda (doubled letter) to make a question word meaning 'which'
Added يٰٓ at the beginning to call attention, like saying 'hey!' to someone
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added ها to make it address a group of people
This is the basic form of the word used to point to someone or something specific
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people
Added ال at the beginning to point to specific people, and added the shadda to strengthen the sound. This word describes the believers who are being addressed in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to show causing someone else to feel safety/security, which came to mean believing/trusting
Added وا at the end to show many people (they) did the action
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding اِ at start makes it a command form

steps.m: all of you be patient! (command to a group)
steps.n: Changed from commanding one person to commanding many people by adding وا at the end
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding alif makes it Form III, showing more effort and intensity in the patience

steps.m: you all try to match others in endurance
steps.n: Adding وا changes it from 'he endured' to 'you all endure' - like telling a team to be patient together

steps.m: and you all try to match others in endurance (connected to اِصْبِرُوا)
steps.n: Added و with fatha to connect this command with the previous command اِصْبِرُوا (be patient)
Added alif after first root letter and vowels to create a form showing ongoing action
Added وا at the end to make it a command for a group

steps.m: and maintain bonds! (all of you) (connected to وَصَابِرُوا)
steps.n: Added و (and) at the start to connect this command with the previous command وَصَابِرُوا (and be patient)
Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding vowels (fatha) - means 'he protected'
Added اِتَّ at the beginning to show doing the action to oneself (like saying 'protected himself' instead of just 'protected'). The ت has a shadda because two letters merged together.
Changed from past tense 'he was mindful' to a command telling many people 'be mindful!' by adding وا
Added و at beginning to mean 'and', and changed initial alif to ٱ (hamzat al-wasl)
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' by adding ال. The first letter changes to ل with shadda because of a special rule for this word.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being feared in وَٱتَّقُوا۟)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because Allah is the object of the verb 'fear/be mindful of' (وَٱتَّقُوا۟). The alif at the start is written with a waṣla mark showing it connects with the previous word.
This is a word that shows hope or expectation for something. Here it expresses hope for success (تُفْلِحُونَ)

steps.m: perhaps you all (expressing hope that you all will succeed)
steps.n: Adds كُمْ at the end to show this hope applies to multiple people being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense verb
Added تُ prefix for present tense 'you' and changed vowels to present tense pattern

steps.m: you all achieve success (addressed to the believers)
steps.n: Added plural marker ون to show multiple people are being addressed. This is addressing the believers (الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا) mentioned at the start of the verse.
